Task instruction sent to agent 
# Task: Rename NodeAbstractResourceInstance to NodeResourceInstanceBase

## Objective
Rename `NodeAbstractResourceInstance` to `NodeResourceInstanceBase` in Terraform's
internal graph node hierarchy to follow Go naming conventions (avoiding "Abstract").

## Requirements

1. **Rename the struct** in `internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go`:
   - `type NodeAbstractResourceInstance struct` → `type NodeResourceInstanceBase struct`
   - Rename the file to `node_resource_instance_base.go` (optional)

2. **Update all references** (10+ call sites):
   - Embedding in other node types
   - Method receivers
   - Type assertions and casts
   - Graph builder functions

3. **Update receiver methods** on the struct

## Key Reference Files
- `internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go` — struct definition
- `internal/terraform/node_resource_apply_instance.go` — embeds the struct
- `internal/terraform/node_resource_plan_instance.go` — embeds the struct
- `internal/terraform/node_resource_destroy.go` — references

## Success Criteria
- `NodeAbstractResourceInstance` no longer used as struct name
- `NodeResourceInstanceBase` used instead
- Embedding sites updated
- Method receivers updated

    20→// NodeApplyableResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is
    21→// "applyable": it is ready to be applied and is represented by a diff.
    22→//
    23→// This node is for a specific instance of a resource. It will usually be
    24→// accompanied in the graph by a NodeApplyableResource representing its
    25→// containing resource, and should depend on that node to ensure that the
    26→// state is properly prepared to receive changes to instances.
    27→type NodeApplyableResourceInstance struct {
    28→	*NodeAbstractResourceInstance
    29→
    30→	graphNodeDeposer // implementation of GraphNodeDeposerConfig
    31→
    32→	// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to
    33→	// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so
    34→	// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource
    35→	// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.
    36→	forceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    37→}
    38→
    39→var (
    40→	_ GraphNodeConfigResource     = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)
    
... (truncated)

     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"log"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    12→)
    13→
    14→// OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds orphans
    15→// for an expanded count to the graph. The determination of this depends
    16→// on the count argument given.
    17→//
    18→// Orphans are found by comparing the count to what is found in the state.
    19→// This transform assumes that if an element in the state is within the count
    20→// bounds given, that it is not an orphan.
    21→type OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer struct {
    22→	Concrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc
    23→
    24→	Addr          addrs.AbsResource           // Addr of the resource to look for orphans
    25→	InstanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addresses that currently exist in config
    26→	State         *states.State               // Full global state
    27→}
    28→
    29→func (t *OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
    30→	rs := t.State.Resource(t.Addr)
    31→	if rs == nil {
    32→		return nil // Resource doesn't exist in state, so nothing to do!
    33→	}
    34→
    35→	// This is an O(n*m) analysis, which we accept for now because the
    36→	// number of instances of a single resource ought to always be small in any
    37→	// reasonable Terraform configuration.
    38→Have:
    39→	for key, inst := range rs.Instances {
    40→		// Instances which have no current objects (only one or more
    41→		// deposed objects) will be taken care of 
... (truncated)

    19→
    20→// nodeExpandPlannableResource represents an addrs.ConfigResource and implements
    21→// DynamicExpand to a subgraph containing all of the addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    22→// resulting from both the containing module and resource-specific expansion.
    23→type nodeExpandPlannableResource struct {
    24→	*NodeAbstractResource
    25→
    26→	// ForceCreateBeforeDestroy might be set via our GraphNodeDestroyerCBD
    27→	// during graph construction, if dependencies require us to force this
    28→	// on regardless of what the configuration says.
    29→	ForceCreateBeforeDestroy *bool
    30→
    31→	// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances
    32→	skipRefresh bool
    33→
    34→	preDestroyRefresh bool
    35→
    36→	// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions
    37→	// for any instances.
    38→	skipPlanChanges bool
    39→
    40→	// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to
    41→	// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so
    42→	// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource
    43→	// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.
    44→	forceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceIn
... (truncated)

     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    13→)
    14→
    15→// nodeApplyableDeferredInstance is a node that represents a deferred instance
    16→// in the apply graph. This node is targetable and helps maintain the correct
    17→// ordering of the apply graph.
    18→//
    19→// When executed during the apply phase, this transfers the planned change we
    20→// got from the plan's deferrals *back* into the EvalContext's Deferred struct,
    21→// so downstream references to deferred objects can get partial values (of
    22→// varying quality).
    23→type nodeApplyableDeferredInstance struct {
    24→	*NodeAbstractResourceInstance
    25→
    26→	Reason    providers.DeferredReason
    27→	ChangeSrc *plans.ResourceInstanceChangeSrc
    28→}
    29→
    30→func (n *nodeApplyableDeferredInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, _ walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
    31→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
    32→	change, err := n.ChangeSrc.Decode(n.Schema.ImpliedType())
    33→	if err != nil {
    34→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(tfdiags.Error, "Failed to decode ", fmt.Sprintf("Terraform failed to decode a deferred change: %v\n\nThis is a bug in Terraform; please report it!", err)))
    35→	}
    36→
    37→	switch n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {
    38→	case addrs.ManagedResourceMode:
    39→		ctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, n.Reason, change)
    40→	case addrs.DataResourceMode:
    41→		ctx
... (truncated)

    16→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    17→)
    18→
    19→// ConcreteResourceNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert an
    20→// abstract resource to a concrete one of some type.
    21→type ConcreteResourceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex
    22→
    23→// GraphNodeConfigResource is implemented by any nodes that represent a resource.
    24→// The type of operation cannot be assumed, only that this node represents
    25→// the given resource.
    26→type GraphNodeConfigResource interface {
    27→	ResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource
    28→}
    29→
    30→// ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert an
    31→// abstract resource instance to a concrete one of some type.
    32→type ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex
    33→
    34→// GraphNodeResourceInstance is implemented by any nodes that represent
    35→// a resource instance. A single resource may have multiple instances if,
    36→// for example, the "count" or "for_each" argument is used for it in
    37→// configuration.
    38→type GraphNodeResourceInstance interface {
    39→	ResourceInstanceAddr() addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    40→
    41→	// StateDependencies return
... (truncated)

    22→
    23→// nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource is a graph node that stands in for
    24→// an unbounded set of potential resource instances that we don't yet know.
    25→//
    26→// Its job is to check the configuration as much as we can with the information
    27→// that's available (so we can raise an error early if something is clearly
    28→// wrong across _all_ potential instances) and to record a placeholder value
    29→// for use when evaluating other objects that refer to this resource.
    30→//
    31→// This is the partial-expanded equivalent of NodePlannableResourceInstance.
    32→type nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource struct {
    33→	addr             addrs.PartialExpandedResource
    34→	config           *configs.Resource
    35→	resolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig
    36→	skipPlanChanges  bool
    37→}
    38→
    39→var (
    40→	_ graphNodeEvalContextScope = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)
    41→	_ GraphNodeConfigResource   = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)
    42→	_ GraphNodeExecutable     
... (truncated)

    14→
    15→// This file is a temporary split from the node_resource_plan.go file. We handle
    16→// the unknown instances branch of execution within here, while this is still
    17→// being developed.
    18→//
    19→// We have split the files to make structuring the code easier, eventually once
    20→// the functions within this file are production ready, we will merge them back
    21→// into the node_resource_plan.go file.
    22→
    23→// dynamicExpandPartial is a variant of dynamicExpand that we use when deferred
    24→// actions are enabled for the current plan.
    25→//
    26→// Once deferred actions are more stable and robust in the stacks runtime, it
    27→// would be nice to integrate this logic a little better with the main
    28→// DynamicExpand logic, but it's separate for now to minimize the risk of
    29→// stacks-specific behavior impacting configurations that are not opted into it.
    30→func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpandPartial(ctx EvalContext, knownModules []addrs.ModuleInstance, partialModules addrs.Set[addrs.PartialExpandedModule], knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
    31→	var g Graph
    32→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
    33→
    34→	knownResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()
    35→	partialResources := addrs.MakeS
... (truncated)

     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"log"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    12→)
    13→
    14→// OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds orphaned
    15→// resource instances to the graph. An "orphan" is an instance that is present
    16→// in the state but belongs to a resource that is no longer present in the
    17→// configuration.
    18→//
    19→// This is not the transformer that deals with "count orphans" (instances that
    20→// are no longer covered by a resource's "count" or "for_each" setting); that's
    21→// handled instead by OrphanResourceCountTransformer.
    22→type OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer struct {
    23→	Concrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc
    24→
    25→	// State is the global state. We require the global state to
    26→	// properly find module orphans at our path.
    27→	State *states.State
    28→
    29→	// Config is the root node in the configuration tree. We'll look up
    30→	// the appropriate note in this tree using the path in each node.
    31→	Config *configs.Config
    32→
    33→	// Do not apply this transformer
    34→	skip bool
    35→}
    36→
    37→func (t *OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
    38→	if t.skip {
    39→		return nil
    40→	}
    41→
    42→	if t.State == nil {
    43→		// If the entire state is nil, there can't be any orphans
    44→		return nil
    45→	}
    46→	if t.Config == nil {
    47→		// Should never happen: we can't be doing any Terraform operations
    48→		// without at lea
... (truncated)

     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    13→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    14→)
    15→
    16→type graphNodeImportState struct {
    17→	Addr             addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addr is the resource address to import into
    18→	ID               string                    // ID is the ID to import as
    19→	ProviderAddr     addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // Provider address given by the user, or implied by the resource type
    20→	ResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // provider node address after resolution
    21→
    22→	states []providers.ImportedResource
    23→}
    24→
    25→var (
    26→	_ GraphNodeModulePath        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)
    27→	_ GraphNodeExecutable        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)
    28→	_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer  = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)
    29→	_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)
    30→)
    31→
    32→func (n *graphNodeImportState) Name() string {
    33→	return fmt.Sprintf("%s (import id %q)", n.Addr, n.ID)
    34→}
    35→
    36→// GraphNodeProviderConsumer
    37→func (n *graphNodeImportState) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {
    38→	// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.
    39→	// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState
    40→	// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the
    41→	// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type
    
... (truncated)

    12→// nodeExpandApplyableResource handles the first layer of resource

    15→// NodeApplyableResource nodes into their respective modules.
    16→type nodeExpandApplyableResource struct {
    17→	*NodeAbstractResource
    18→
    19→	PartialExpansions []addrs.PartialExpandedResource

    30→)

    33→}
    34→

    38→	// The expand node needs to connect to the individual resource instances it

    40→	// cycles. It would be preferable to entirely d
... (truncated)

     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"

     9→
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
    13→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans"

    16→)
    17→
    18→// DiffTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing
    19→// each of the resource changes described in the given Changes object.
    20→type DiffTransformer struct {
    21→	Concrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc
    22→	State    *states.State
    23→	Changes  *plans.ChangesSrc
    24→	Config   *configs.Config
    25→}
    26→
    27→// return true if the given resource instance has either Preconditions or
    28→// Postconditions defined in the configuration.
    29→func (t *DiffTransformer) hasConfigConditions(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) bool {
    30→	// unit tests may have no config
    31→	if t.Config == nil {
    32→		return false
    33→	}
    34→
    35→	cfg := t.Config.DescendantForInstance(addr.Module)
    36→	if cfg == nil {
    37→		return false
    38→	}
    39→
    40→	res := cfg.Module.ResourceByAddr(addr.ConfigResource().Resource)
    41→	if res == nil {
    42→		return false
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	return len(res.Preconditions) > 0 || len(res.Postconditions) > 0
    46→}
    47→
    48→func (t *DiffTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
    49→	if t.Changes == nil || len(t.Changes.Resources) == 0 {
    50→		// Nothing to do!
    51→		return nil
    52→	}
    53→
    54→	// Go through all 
... (truncated)

     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"log"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// StateTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds the elements of
    13→// the state to the graph.
    14→//
    15→// This transform is used for example by the DestroyPlanGraphBuilder to ensure
    16→// that only resources that are in the state are represented in the graph.
    17→type StateTransformer struct {
    18→	// ConcreteCurrent and ConcreteDeposed are used to specialize the abstract
    19→	// resource instance nodes that this transformer will create.
    20→	//
    21→	// If either of these is nil, the objects of that type will be skipped and
    22→	// not added to the graph at all. It doesn't make sense to use this
    23→	// transformer without setting at least one of these, since that would
    24→	// skip everything and thus be a no-op.
    25→	ConcreteCurrent ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc
    26→	ConcreteDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc
    27→
    28→	State *states.State
    29→}
    30→
    31→func (t *StateTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
    32→	if t.State == nil {
    33→		log.Printf("[TRACE] StateTransformer: state is nil, so nothing to do")
    34→		return nil
    35→	}
    36→
    37→	switch {
    38→	case t.ConcreteCurrent != nil && t.ConcreteDeposed != nil:
    39→		log.Printf("[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for both current and deposed instance objects")
    40→	case t.ConcreteCurrent != nil:
    41→		log.Printf("[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for current instance objects only")
    42→	case t.ConcreteDeposed != nil:
    43→		log.Printf("[TRACE] StateTr
... (truncated)

     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
     8→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans"
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
    11→)
    12→
    13→// DeferredTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing
    14→// each of the deferred changes to the graph.
    15→//
    16→// Deferred changes are not executed during the apply phase, but they are
    17→// tracked in the graph to ensure that the correct ordering is maintained and
    18→// the target flags are correctly applied.
    19→type DeferredTransformer struct {
    20→	DeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc
    21→}
    22→
    23→func (t *DeferredTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
    24→	if len(t.DeferredChanges) == 0 {
    25→		return nil
    26→	}
    27→
    28→	// As with the DiffTransformer, DeferredTransformer creates resource
    29→	// instance nodes. If there are any whole-resource nodes already in the
    30→	// graph, we must ensure they get evaluated before any of the corresponding
    31→	// instances by creating dependency edges.
    32→	resourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()
    33→	for _, node := range g.Vertices() {
    34→		rn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)
    35→		if !ok {
    36→			continue
    37→		}
    38→		// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement
    39→		// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they
    40→		// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.
    41→		if _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResource
... (truncated)

    18→)
    19→
    20→func TestDestroyEdgeTransformer_basic(t *testing.T) {
    21→	g := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}
    22→	g.Add(testDestroyNode("test_object.A"))
    23→	g.Add(testDestroyNode("test_object.B"))
    24→
    25→	state := states.NewState()
    26→	root := state.EnsureModule(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
    27→	root.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(
    28→		mustResourceInstanceAddr("test_object.A").Resource,
    29→		&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{
    30→			Status:    states.ObjectReady,
    31→			AttrsJSON: []byte(`{"id":"A"}`),
    32→		},
    33→		mustProviderConfig(`provider["registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test"]`),
    34→	)
    35→	root.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(
    36→		mustResourceInstanceAddr("test_object.B").Resource,
    37→		&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{
    38→			Status:       states.ObjectReady,
    39→			AttrsJSON:    []byte(`{"id":"B","test_string":"x"}`),
    40→			Dependencies: []addrs.ConfigResource{mustConfigResourceAddr("test_object.A")},
    41→		},
    42→		mustProviderConfig(`provider["registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test"]`),
    43→	)
    44→	if err := (&AttachStateTransformer{State: state}).Transform(&g); err != nil {
    45→		t.Fatal(err)
    46→	}
    47→
    48→	tf := &DestroyEdgeTransformer{}
    49→	if err
... (truncated)

     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"log"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
    12→)
    13→
    14→// ResourceCountTransformer is a GraphTransformer that expands the count
    15→// out for a specific resource.
    16→//
    17→// This assumes that the count is already interpolated.
    18→type ResourceCountTransformer struct {
    19→	Concrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc
    20→	Schema   *configschema.Block
    21→
    22→	Addr          addrs.ConfigResource
    23→	InstanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    24→}
    25→
    26→func (t *ResourceCountTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
    27→	for _, addr := range t.InstanceAddrs {
    28→		abstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)
    29→		abstract.Schema = t.Schema
    30→		var node dag.Vertex = abstract
    31→		if f := t.Concrete; f != nil {
    32→			node = f(abstract)
    33→		}
    34→
    35→		log.Printf("[TRACE] ResourceCountTransformer: adding %s as %T", addr, node)
    36→		g.Add(node)
    37→	}
    38→	return nil
    39→}
    40→

     3→

     5→

    16→// NodeForgetResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is to be

    33→func (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {
    34→	return &n.Addr
    35→}
    36→
    37→func (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) Name() string {
    38→	return n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String() + " (forget)"
    39→}

    44→		return nil, t

     4→package terraform

     7→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"

    16→
    17→// ApplyGraphBuilder implements GraphBuilder and is responsible for building
    18→// a graph for applying a Terraform diff.
    19→//
    20→// Because the graph is built from the diff (vs. the config or state),
    21→// this helps ensure that the apply-time graph doesn't modify any resources
    22→// that aren't explicitly in the diff. There are other scenarios where the
    23→// diff can be deviated, so this is just one layer of protection.
    24→type ApplyGraphBuilder struct {
    25→	// Config is the configuration tree that the diff was built from.
    26→	Config *configs.Config
    27→
    28→	// Changes describes the changes that we need apply.
    29→	Changes *plans.ChangesSrc
    30→
    31→	// DeferredChanges describes the changes that were deferred during the plan
    32→	// and should not be applied.
    33→	DeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc
    34→
    35→	// State is the current state
    36→	State *states.State
    37→
    38→	// RootVariableValues are the root module input variables captured as
    39→	// part of the plan object, which we must reproduce in the apply step
    40→	// to get a consistent re
... (truncated)

    20→	*NodeAbstractResourceInstance

    36→}

     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"log"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance represents a resource that is "destroyable":
    13→// it is ready to be destroyed.
    14→type NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance struct {
    15→	*NodeAbstractResourceInstance
    16→}
    17→
    18→var (
    19→	_ GraphNodeExecutable = (*NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance)(nil)
    20→)
    21→
    22→// GraphNodeExecutable
    23→func (n *NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
    24→	log.Printf("[TRACE] NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance: removing state object for %s", n.Addr)
    25→	ctx.State().SetResourceInstanceCurrent(n.Addr, nil, n.ResolvedProvider)
    26→	return nil
    27→}
    28→

    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking"

    15→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    16→)
    17→
    18→// PlanGraphBuilder is a GraphBuilder implementation that builds a graph for
    19→// planning and for other "plan-like" operations which don't require an
    20→// already-calculated plan as input.
    21→//
    22→// Unlike the apply graph builder, this graph builder:
    23→//
    24→//   - Makes its decisions primarily based on the given configuration, which
    25→//     represents the desired state.
    26→//
    27→//   - Ignores certain lifecycle concerns like create_before_destroy, because
    28→//     those are only important once we already know what action we're planning
    29→//     to take against a particular resource instance.
    30→type PlanGraphBuilder struct {
    31→	// Config is the configuration tree to build a plan from.
    32→	Config *configs.Config
    33→
    34→	// State is the current state
    35→	State *states.State
    36→
    37→	// RootVariableValues are the raw input values for root input variables
    38→	// given by the caller, which we'll resolve into final values as part
    39→	// of the plan walk.
    40→	RootVariableValues InputValues
    41→
    42→	// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider
    43→	// configura
... (truncated)

     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"testing"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    11→)
    12→
    13→func TestNodeDataDestroyExecute(t *testing.T) {
    14→	state := states.NewState()
    15→	state.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceCurrent(
    16→		addrs.Resource{
    17→			Mode: addrs.DataResourceMode,
    18→			Type: "test_instance",
    19→			Name: "foo",
    20→		}.Instance(addrs.NoKey),
    21→		&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{
    22→			Status:    states.ObjectReady,
    23→			AttrsJSON: []byte(`{"dynamic":{"type":"string","value":"hello"}}`),
    24→		},
    25→		addrs.AbsProviderConfig{
    26→			Provider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider("test"),
    27→			Module:   addrs.RootModule,
    28→		},
    29→	)
    30→	ctx := &MockEvalContext{
    31→		StateState: state.SyncWrapper(),
    32→	}
    33→
    34→	node := NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance{&NodeAbstractResourceInstance{
    35→		Addr: addrs.Resource{
    36→			Mode: addrs.DataResourceMode,
    37→			Type: "test_instance",
    38→			Name: "foo",
    39→		}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),
    40→	}}
    41→
    42→	diags := node.Execute(ctx, walkApply)
    43→	if diags.HasErrors() {
    44→		t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", diags.Err())
    45→	}
    46→
    47→	// verify resource removed from state
    48→	if state.HasManagedResourceInstanceObjects() {
    49→		t.Fatal("resources still in state after NodeDataDestroy.Execute")
    50→	}
    51→}
    52→

     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"

     9→	"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty"

    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"

    13→)
    14→
    15→// This file is a temporary split from the node_resource_plan.go file. We handle
    16→// the unknown instances branch of execution within here, while this is still
    17→// being developed.
    18→//
    19→// We have split the files to make structuring the code easier, eventually once
    20→// the functions within this file are production ready, we will merge them back
    21→// into the node_resource_plan.go file.
    22→
    23→// dynamicExpandPartial is a variant of dynamicExpand that we use when deferred
    24→// actions are enabled for the current plan.
    25→//
    26→// Once deferred actions are more stable and robust in the stacks runtime, it
    27→// would be nice to integrate this logic a little better with the main
    28→// DynamicExpand logic, but it's separate for now to minimize the risk of
    29→// stacks-specific behavior impacting configurations that are not opted into it.
    30→func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpandPartial(ctx EvalContext, knownModules []addrs.ModuleInstance, partialModules addrs.Set[addrs.PartialExpandedModule], knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
    31→	var g Graph
    32→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
    33→
    34→	knownResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()
    35→	partialResources := addrs.MakeS
... (truncated)
194-assistanttool_useRead
195-usertool_resultRead
   340→func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   341→	var g Graph
   342→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   343→
   344→	orphans := n.findOrphans(ctx, moduleInstances)
   345→
   346→	for _, res := range orphans {
   347→		for key := range res.Instances {
   348→			addr := res.Addr.Instance(key)
   349→			abs := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)
   350→			abs.AttachResourceState(res)
   351→			n := n.concreteResourceOrphan(abs)
   352→			g.Add(n)
   353→		}
   354→	}
   355→
   356→	// The above dealt with the expansion of the containing module, so now
   357→	// we need to deal with the expansion of the resource itself across all
   358→	// instances of the module.
   359→	//
   360→	// We'll gather up all of the leaf instances we learn about along the way
   361→	// so that we can inform the checks subsystem of which instances it should
   362→	// be expecting check results for, below.
   363→
   364→	expandedInstances := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.Checkable]()
   365→	for _, module := range moduleInstances {

   371→		}
   372→	}
   373→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   374→		return nil, diags
   375→	}
   376→
   377→	diags = diags.Append(n.validateExpandedImportTargets(imports, expandedInstances))

   221→
   222→	// The analyses done in the destroy edge transformer are between
   223→	// not-yet-expanded objects, which is conservative and so it will generate
   224→	// edges that aren't strictly necessary. As a special case we filter out
   225→	// any edges that are between resources instances that are in different
   226→	// instances of the same module, because those edges are never needed
   227→	// (one instance of a module cannot depend on another instance of the
   228→	// same module) and including them can, in complex cases, cause cycles due
   229→	// to unnecessary interactions between destroyed and created module
   230→	// instances in the same plan.
   231→	//
   232→	// Therefore below we expect to see the dependencies within each instance
   233→	// of module.child reflected, but we should not see any dependencies
   234→	// _between_ instances of module.child.

   112→		// A deposed instance may only have a change of Delete, Forget, or NoOp.

   100→

   102→		return &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{
   103→			NodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,
   104→			forceReplace:                 b.ForceReplace,
   105→		}
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	steps := []GraphTransformer{
   109→		// Creates all the resources represented in the config. During apply,
   110→		// we use this just to ensure that the whole-resource metadata is
   111→		// updated to reflect things such as whether the count argument is
   112→		// set in config, or which provider configuration manages each resource.
   113→		&ConfigTransformer{
   114→			Concrete: concreteResource,
   115→			Config:   b.Config,
   116→		},
   117→
   118→		// Add dynamic values
   119→		&RootVariableTransformer{
   120→			Config:       b.Config,
   121→			RawValues:    b.RootVariableValues,
   122→			DestroyApply: b.Operation == walkDestroy,
   123→		},
   124→		&ModuleVariableTransformer{
   125→			Config:       b.Config,
   126→			DestroyApply: b.Operation == walkDestroy,
   127→		},
   128→		&variableValidationTransformer{},
   129→		&LocalTransformer{Config: b.Config},
   130→		&OutputTransformer{
   131→			Config:     b.Config,
   132→			Destroying: b.Operation == walkDestroy,
   133→			Overrides:  b.Overrides,
   134→		},
   135→
   136→		// Creates all the resource instances represented in the diff, along
   137→		// with dependency edges against the whole-resource nodes added by
   138→		// ConfigTransformer above.
   139→		&DiffTransformer{
   140→			Concrete: concreteResourceInstance,

    21→type ConcreteResourceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex
    22→
    23→// GraphNodeConfigResource is implemented by any nodes that represent a resource.
    24→// The type of operation cannot be assumed, only that this node represents
    25→// the given resource.
    26→type GraphNodeConfigResource interface {
    27→	ResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource
    28→}
    29→
    30→// ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert an
    31→// abstract resource instance to a concrete one of some type.
    32→type ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex
    33→
    34→// GraphNodeResourceInstance is implemented by any nodes that represent
    35→// a resource instance. A single resource may have multiple instances if,
    36→// for example, the "count" or "for_each" argument is used for it in
    37→// configuration.
    38→type GraphNodeResourceInstance interface {
    39→	ResourceInstanceAddr() addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    40→
    41→	// StateDependencies return

   563→		if importID, ok := knownImports.GetOk(a.Addr); ok {

   565→		} else {
   566→			// We're going to check now if this resource instance *might* be
   567→			// targeted by one of the unknown imports. If it is, we'll set the
   568→			// import target to an unknown value so that the import operation
   569→			// will be deferred.
   570→			for _, unknownImport := range unknownImports.Elems {

   572→					if unknownImport.Value.Has(a.Addr) {
   573→						// This means that this particular instance already
   574→						// exists within the state. `import` blocks that target
   575→						// instances that already exist are ignored by
   576→						// Terraform. This means that even if this unknown
   577→						// import does eventually resolve to this instance then
   578→						// it would be ignored anyway. So for this instance we
   579→						// won't set the import target.

    20→// nodeExpandPlannableResource represents an addrs.ConfigResource and implements
    21→// DynamicExpand to a subgraph containing all of the addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    22→// resulting from both the containing module and resource-specific expansion.
    23→type nodeExpandPlannableResource struct {
    24→	*NodeAbstractResource
    25→
    26→
    27→	// ForceCreateBeforeDestroy might be set via our GraphNodeDestroyerCBD
    28→	// during graph construction, if dependencies require us to force this
    29→	// on regardless of what the configuration says.
    30→	ForceCreateBeforeDestroy *bool
    31→
    32→	// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances
    33→	skipRefresh bool
    34→
    35→	preDestroyRefresh bool
    36→
    37→	// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions
    38→	// for any instances.
    39→	skipPlanChanges bool
    40→
    41→	// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to
    42→	// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so
    43→	// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource
    44→	// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.
    45→	forceReplace []addrs.AbsRe
... (truncated)

     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
    13→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans"
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    15→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    16→)
    17→
    18→// DiffTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing
    19→// each of the resource changes described in the given Changes object.
    20→type DiffTransformer struct {
    21→	Concrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc
    22→	State    *states.State
    23→	Changes  *plans.ChangesSrc
    24→	Config   *configs.Config
    25→}
    26→
    27→// return true if the given resource instance has either Preconditions or
    28→// Postconditions defined in the configuration.
    29→func (t *DiffTransformer) hasConfigConditions(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) bool {
    30→	// unit tests may have no config
    31→	if t.Config == nil {
    32→		return false
    33→	}
    34→
    35→	cfg := t.Config.DescendantForInstance(addr.Module)
    36→	if cfg == nil {
    37→		return false
    38→	}
    39→
    40→	res := cfg.Module.ResourceByAddr(addr.ConfigResource().Resource)
    41→	if res == nil {
    42→		return false
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	return len(res.Preconditions) > 0 || len(res.Postconditions) > 0
    46→}
    47→
    48→func (t *DiffTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
    49→	if t.Changes == nil || len(t.Changes.Resources) == 0 {
    50→		// Nothing to do!
    51→		return nil
    52→	}
    53→
    54→	// Go through all 
... (truncated)

    20→func TestDestroyEdgeTransformer_basic(t *testing.T) {
    21→	g := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}
    22→	g.Add(testDestroyNode("test_object.A"))
    23→	g.Add(testDestroyNode("test_object.B"))
    24→
    25→	state := states.NewState()
    26→	root := state.EnsureModule(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
    27→	root.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(
    28→		mustResourceInstanceAddr("test_object.A").Resource,
    29→		&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{
    30→			Status:    states.ObjectReady,
    31→			AttrsJSON: []byte(`{"id":"A"}`),
    32→		},
    33→		mustProviderConfig(`provider["registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test"]`),
    34→	)
    35→	root.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(
    36→		mustResourceInstanceAddr("test_object.B").Resource,
    37→		&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{
    38→			Status:       states.ObjectReady,
    39→			AttrsJSON:    []byte(`{"id":"B","test_string":"x"}`),
    40→			Dependencies: []addrs.ConfigResource{mustConfigResourceAddr("test_object.A")},
    41→		},
    42→		mustProviderConfig(`provider["registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test"]`),
    43→	)
    44→	if err := (&AttachStateTransformer{State: state}).Transform(&g); err != nil {
    45→		t.Fatal(err)
    46→	}
    47→
    48→	tf := &DestroyEdgeTransformer{}
    49→	if err
... (truncated)

     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"log"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking"
    13→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    15→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    16→)
    17→
    18→// PlanGraphBuilder is a GraphBuilder implementation that builds a graph for
    19→// planning and for other "plan-like" operations which don't require an
    20→// already-calculated plan as input.
    21→//
    22→// Unlike the apply graph builder, this graph builder:
    23→//
    24→//   - Makes its decisions primarily based on the given configuration, which
    25→//     represents the desired state.
    26→//
    27→//   - Ignores certain lifecycle concerns like create_before_destroy, because
    28→//     those are only important once we already know what action we're planning
    29→//     to take against a particular resource instance.
    30→type PlanGraphBuilder struct {
    31→	// Config is the configuration tree to build a plan from.
    32→	Config *configs.Config
    33→
    34→	// State is the current state
    35→	State *states.State
    36→
    37→	// RootVariableValues are the raw input values for root input variables
    38→	// given by the caller, which we'll resolve into final values as part
    39→	// of the plan walk.
    40→	RootVariableValues InputValues
    41→
    42→	// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider
    43→	// configura
... (truncated)

     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
     8→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans"
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
    13→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    15→)
    16→
    17→// ApplyGraphBuilder implements GraphBuilder and is responsible for building
    18→// a graph for applying a Terraform diff.
    19→//
    20→// Because the graph is built from the diff (vs. the config or state),
    21→// this helps ensure that the apply-time graph doesn't modify any resources
    22→// that aren't explicitly in the diff. There are other scenarios where the
    23→// diff can be deviated, so this is just one layer of protection.
    24→type ApplyGraphBuilder struct {
    25→	// Config is the configuration tree that the diff was built from.
    26→	Config *configs.Config
    27→
    28→	// Changes describes the changes that we need apply.
    29→	Changes *plans.ChangesSrc
    30→
    31→	// DeferredChanges describes the changes that were deferred during the plan
    32→	// and should not be applied.
    33→	DeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc
    34→
    35→	// State is the current state
    36→	State *states.State
    37→
    38→	// RootVariableValues are the root module input variables captured as
    39→	// part of the plan object, which we must reproduce in the apply step
    40→	// to get a consistent re
... (truncated)

    10→)
    11→
    12→// nodeExpandApplyableResource handles the first layer of resource
    13→// expansion during apply. Even though the resource instances themselves are
    14→// already expanded from the plan, we still need to expand the
    15→// NodeApplyableResource nodes into their respective modules.
    16→type nodeExpandApplyableResource struct {
    17→	*NodeAbstractResource
    18→
    19→	PartialExpansions []addrs.PartialExpandedResource
    20→}
    21→
    22→var (
    23→	_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)
    24→	_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)
    25→	_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)
    26→	_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)
    27→	_ graphNodeExpandsInstances     = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)
    28→	_ GraphNodeTargetable           = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)
    29→	_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable    = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)
    30→)
    31→
    32→func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) expandsInstances() {
    33→}
    34→
    35→func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) References() []*addrs.Reference {
    36→	refs := n.NodeAbstractResource.References()
    37→
    38→	// The expand node needs to connect to the individual resource instances it
    39→	// references, but cannot refer to it's own instances without causing
    40→	// cycles. It would be preferable to entirely d
... (truncated)

    16→type graphNodeImportState struct {
    17→	Addr             addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addr is the resource address to import into
    18→	ID               string                    // ID is the ID to import as
    19→	ProviderAddr     addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // Provider address given by the user, or implied by the resource type
    20→	ResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // provider node address after resolution
    21→
    22→	states []providers.ImportedResource
    23→}
    24→
    25→var (
    26→	_ GraphNodeModulePath        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)
    27→	_ GraphNodeExecutable        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)
    28→	_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer  = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)
    29→	_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)
    30→)
    31→
    32→func (n *graphNodeImportState) Name() string {
    33→	return fmt.Sprintf("%s (import id %q)", n.Addr, n.ID)
    34→}
    35→
    36→// GraphNodeProviderConsumer
    37→func (n *graphNodeImportState) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {
    38→	// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.
    39→	// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState
    40→	// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the
    41→	// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type

     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.

     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log"
     9→	"strings"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
    15→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances"
    16→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/marks"
    17→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans"
    18→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange"
    19→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
    20→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    21→)
    22→
    23→// nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource is a graph node that stands in for
    24→// an unbounded set of potential resource instances that we don't yet know.
    25→//
    26→// Its job is to check the configuration as much as we can with the information
    27→// that's available (so we can raise an error early if something is clearly
    28→// wrong across _all_ potential instances) and to record a placeholder value
    29→// for use when evaluating other objects that refer to this resource.
    30→//
    31→// This is the partial-expanded equivalent of NodePlannableResourceInstance.
    32→type nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource struct {
    33→	addr             addrs.PartialExpandedResource
    34→	config           *configs.Resource
    35→	resolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig
    36→	skipPlanChanges  bool
    37→}
    38→
    39→var (
    40→	_ graphNodeEvalContextScope = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)
    41→	_ GraphNodeConfigResource   = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)
    42→	_ GraphNodeExecutable     
... (truncated)

		NodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,

		// to force on CreateBeforeDestroy due to dependencies on other
		// nodes that have it.
		ForceCreateBeforeDestroy: n.CreateBeforeDestroy(),
		skipRefresh:              n.skipRefresh,
		skipPlanChanges:          skipPlanChanges,

{
  "file": {
    "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\"\n\ttfaddr \"github.com/hashicorp/terraform-registry-address\"\n\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/checks\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/marks\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/provisioners\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// NodeAbstractResourceInstance represents a resource instance with no\n// associated operations. It embeds NodeAbstractResource but additionally\n// contains an instance key, used to identify one of potentially many\n// instances that were created from a resource in configuration, e.g. using\n// the \"count\" or \"for_each\" arguments.\ntype NodeAbstractResourceInstance struct {\n\tNodeAbstractResource\n\tAddr addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// These are set via the AttachState method.\n\tinstanceState *states.ResourceInstance\n\n\tDependencies []addrs.ConfigResource\n\n\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n\n\t// During import we may generate configuration for a resource, which needs\n\t// to be stored in the final change.\n\tgeneratedConfigHCL string\n\n\t// override is set by the graph itself, just before this node executes.\n\toverride *configs.Override\n}\n\n// NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance creates an abstract resource instance graph\n// node for the given absolute resource instance address.\nfunc NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) *NodeAbstractResourceInstance {\n\t// Due to the fact that we embed NodeAbstractResource, the given address\n\t// actually ends up split between the resource address in the embedded\n\t// object and the InstanceKey field in our own struct. The\n\t// ResourceInstanceAddr method will stick these back together again on\n\t// request.\n\tr := NewNodeAbstractResource(addr.ContainingResource().Config())\n\treturn &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n\t\tNodeAbstractResource: *r,\n\t\tAddr:                 addr,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) Name() string {\n\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String()\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\n\treturn n.Addr.Module\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) HookResourceIdentity() HookResourceIdentity {\n\treturn HookResourceIdentity{\n\t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\n\t}\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferenceable\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\treturn []addrs.Referenceable{\n\t\taddr.Resource,\n\n\t\t// A resource instance can also be referenced by the address of its\n\t\t// containing resource, so that e.g. a reference to aws_instance.foo\n\t\t// would match both aws_instance.foo[0] and aws_instance.foo[1].\n\t\taddr.ContainingResource().Resource,\n\t}\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferencer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\t// If we have a configuration attached then we'll delegate to our",
    "filePath": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 2859
  },

}

  "content": "24-// accompanied in the graph by a NodeApplyableResource representing its\n25-// containing resource, and should depend on that node to ensure that the\n26-// state is properly prepared to receive changes to instances.\n27:type NodeApplyableResourceInstance struct {\n28:\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n29-\n30-\tgraphNodeDeposer // implementation of GraphNodeDeposerConfig\n31-\n--\n52-\treturn &addr\n53-}\n54-\n55:// GraphNodeReferencer, overriding NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n56-func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n57-\t// Start with the usual resource instance implementation\n58:\tret := n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.References()\n59-\n60-\t// Applying a resource must also depend on the destruction of any of its\n61-\t// dependencies, since this may for example affect the outcome of",
  "filenames": [],
  "mode": "content",
  "numFiles": 0,
  "numLines": 19
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go",
  "limit": 300
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\"\n\ttfaddr \"github.com/hashicorp/terraform-registry-address\"\n\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/checks\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/marks\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/provisioners\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// NodeAbstractResourceInstance represents a resource instance with no\n// associated operations. It embeds NodeAbstractResource but additionally\n// contains an instance key, used to identify one of potentially many\n// instances that were created from a resource in configuration, e.g. using\n// the \"count\" or \"for_each\" arguments.\ntype NodeAbstractResourceInstance struct {\n\tNodeAbstractResource\n\tAddr addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// These are set via the AttachState method.\n\tinstanceState *states.ResourceInstance\n\n\tDependencies []addrs.ConfigResource\n\n\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n\n\t// During import we may generate configuration for a resource, which needs\n\t// to be stored in the final change.\n\tgeneratedConfigHCL string\n\n\t// override is set by the graph itself, just before this node executes.\n\toverride *configs.Override\n}\n\n// NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance creates an abstract resource instance graph\n// node for the given absolute resource instance address.\nfunc NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) *NodeAbstractResourceInstance {\n\t// Due to the fact that we embed NodeAbstractResource, the given address\n\t// actually ends up split between the resource address in the embedded\n\t// object and the InstanceKey field in our own struct. The\n\t// ResourceInstanceAddr method will stick these back together again on\n\t// request.\n\tr := NewNodeAbstractResource(addr.ContainingResource().Config())\n\treturn &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n\t\tNodeAbstractResource: *r,\n\t\tAddr:                 addr,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) Name() string {\n\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String()\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\n\treturn n.Addr.Module\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) HookResourceIdentity() HookResourceIdentity {\n\treturn HookResourceIdentity{\n\t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\n\t}\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferenceable\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\treturn []addrs.Referenceable{\n\t\taddr.Resource,\n\n\t\t// A resource instance can also be referenced by the address of its\n\t\t// containing resource, so that e.g. a reference to aws_instance.foo\n\t\t// would match both aws_instance.foo[0] and aws_instance.foo[1].\n\t\taddr.ContainingResource().Resource,\n\t}\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferencer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\t// If we have a configuration attached then we'll delegate to our\n\t// embedded abstract resource, which knows how to extract dependencies\n\t// from configuration. If there is no config, then the dependencies will\n\t// be connected during destroy from those stored in the state.\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\tif n.Schema == nil {\n\t\t\t// We'll produce a log message about this out here so that\n\t\t\t// we can include the full instance address, since the equivalent\n\t\t\t// message in NodeAbstractResource.References cannot see it.\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] no schema is attached to %s, so config references cannot be detected\", n.Name())\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.References()\n\t}\n\n\t// If we have neither config nor state then we have no references.\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// StateDependencies returns the dependencies which will be saved in the state\n// for managed resources, or the most current dependencies for data resources.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) StateDependencies() []addrs.ConfigResource {\n\t// Managed resources prefer the stored dependencies, to avoid possible\n\t// conflicts in ordering when refactoring configuration.\n\tif s := n.instanceState; s != nil {\n\t\tif s.Current != nil {\n\t\t\treturn s.Current.Dependencies\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If there are no stored dependencies, this is either a newly created\n\t// managed resource, or a data source, and we can use the most recently\n\t// calculated dependencies.\n\treturn n.Dependencies\n}\n\n// GraphNodeResourceInstance\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) ResourceInstanceAddr() addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\treturn n.Addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachResourceState\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) AttachResourceState(s *states.Resource) {\n\tif s == nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] attaching nil state to %s\", n.Addr)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.AttachResourceState for %s\", n.Addr)\n\tn.instanceState = s.Instance(n.Addr.Resource.Key)\n\tn.storedProviderConfig = s.ProviderConfig\n}\n\n// GraphNodeOverridable\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) ConfigProvider() addrs.AbsProviderConfig {\n\treturn n.ResolvedProvider\n}\n\n// GraphNodeOverridable\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) SetOverride(override *configs.Override) {\n\tn.override = override\n}\n\n// readDiff returns the planned change for a particular resource instance\n// object.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) readDiff(ctx EvalContext, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, error) {\n\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.Resource)\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type %q\", addr.Resource.Resource.Type)\n\t}\n\n\tchange := changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(addr, addrs.NotDeposed)\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readDiff: Read %s change from plan for %s\", change.Action, n.Addr)\n\n\treturn change, nil\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) checkPreventDestroy(change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tif change == nil || n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tpreventDestroy := n.Config.Managed.PreventDestroy\n\n\tif (change.Action == plans.Delete || change.Action.IsReplace()) && preventDestroy {\n\t\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\tSummary:  \"Instance cannot be destroyed\",\n\t\t\tDetail: fmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"Resource %s has lifecycle.prevent_destroy set, but the plan calls for this resource to be destroyed. To avoid this error and continue with the plan, either disable lifecycle.prevent_destroy or reduce the scope of the plan using the -target option.\",\n\t\t\t\tn.Addr.String(),\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t\tSubject: &n.Config.DeclRange,\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// preApplyHook calls the pre-Apply hook\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) preApplyHook(ctx EvalContext, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif change == nil {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"preApplyHook for %s called with nil Change\", n.Addr))\n\t}\n\n\t// Only managed resources have user-visible apply actions.\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.ManagedResourceMode {\n\t\tpriorState := change.Before\n\t\tplannedNewState := change.After\n\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\t\treturn h.PreApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), change.DeposedKey, change.Action, priorState, plannedNewState)\n\t\t}))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// postApplyHook calls the post-Apply hook\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) postApplyHook(ctx EvalContext, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, err error) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// Only managed resources have user-visible apply actions.\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.ManagedResourceMode {\n\t\tvar newState cty.Value\n\t\tif state != nil {\n\t\t\tnewState = state.Value\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnewState = cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\t\treturn h.PostApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, newState, err)\n\t\t}))\n\t}\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\ntype phaseState int\n\nconst (\n\tworkingState phaseState = iota\n\trefreshState\n\tprevRunState\n)\n\n//go:generate go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/stringer -type phaseState\n\n// writeResourceInstanceState saves the given object as the current object for\n// the selected resource instance.\n//\n// dependencies is a parameter, instead of those directly attacted to the\n// NodeAbstractResourceInstance, because we don't write dependencies for\n// datasources.\n//\n// targetState determines which context state we're writing to during plan. The\n// default is the global working state.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n\treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, states.NotDeposed, obj, targetState)\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t// Bail out to avoid silently doing something other than what the\n\t\t// caller seems to have intended.\n\t\tpanic(\"trying to write current state object using writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\")\n\t}\n\treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, deposedKey, obj, targetState)\n}\n\n// (this is the private common body of both writeResourceInstanceState and\n// writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed. Don't call it directly; instead, use\n// one of the two wrappers to be explicit about which of the instance's\n// objects you are intending to write.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n\tabsAddr := n.Addr\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tlogFuncName := \"NodeAbstractResouceInstance.writeResourceInstanceState\"\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlogFuncName = \"NodeAbstractResouceInstance.writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\"\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s (deposed key %s)\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr, deposedKey)\n\t}\n\n\tvar state *states.SyncState\n\tswitch targetState {",
    "filePath": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go",
    "numLines": 300,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 2859
  },
  "type": "text"
}

(n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) ConfigProvider() addrs.AbsProviderConfig {\n\treturn n.ResolvedProvider\n}\n\n// GraphNodeOverridable\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) SetOverride(override *configs.Override) {\n\tn.override = override\n}\n\n// readDiff returns the planned change for a particular resource instance\n// object.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) readDiff(ctx EvalContext, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, error) {\n\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tschema, _ := readDiff: Read %s change from plan for %s\", change.Action, n.Addr)\n\n\treturn change, nil\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) checkPreventDestroy(change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) plans.Delete || change.Action.IsReplace()) && preventDestroy {\n\t\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\tSummary:  \"Instance To avoid this error and continue with the plan, either disable lifecycle.prevent_destroy or reduce the scope of the plan using the -target option.\",\n\t\t\t\tn.Addr.String(),\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t\tSubject: &n.Config.DeclRange,\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// preApplyHook calls the pre-Apply hook\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) preApplyHook(ctx EvalContext, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif change == nil {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"preApplyHook for %s called with nil Change\", n.Addr))\n\t}\n\n\t// Only managed resources have user-visible apply actions.\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.ManagedResourceMode {\n\t\tpriorState := change.Before\n\t\tplannedNewState := change.After\n\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\t\treturn h.PreApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), change.DeposedKey, change.Action, priorState, plannedNewState)\n\t\t}))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// postApplyHook calls the post-Apply hook\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) postApplyHook(ctx EvalContext, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, err error) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// Only managed resources have user-visible apply actions.\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.ManagedResourceMode {\n\t\tvar newState cty.Value\n\t\tif state != nil {\n\t\t\tnewState = state.Value\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnewState = cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\t\treturn h.PostApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, newState, err)\n\t\t}))\n\t}\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\ntype phaseState int\n\nconst (\n\tworkingState phaseState = iota\n\trefreshState\n\tprevRunState\n)\n\n//go:generate go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/stringer -type phaseState\n\n// writeResourceInstanceState saves the given object as the current object for\n// the selected resource instance.\n//\n// dependencies is a parameter, instead of those directly attacted to the\n// NodeAbstractResourceInstance, because we don't write dependencies for\n// datasources.\n//\n// targetState determines which context state we're writing to during plan. The\n// default is the global working state.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n\treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, states.NotDeposed, obj, targetState)\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t// Bail out to avoid silently doing something other than what the\n\t\t// caller seems to have intended.\n\t\tpanic(\"trying to write current state object using writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\")\n\t}\n\treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, deposedKey, obj, targetState)\n}\n\n// (this is the private common body of both writeResourceInstanceState and\n// writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed. Don't call it directly; instead, use\n// one of the two wrappers to be explicit about which of the instance's\n// objects you are intending to write.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n\tabsAddr := n.Addr\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tlogFuncName := \"NodeAbstractResouceInstance.writeResourceInstanceState\"\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlogFuncName = \"NodeAbstractResouceInstance.writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\"\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s (deposed key %s)\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr, deposedKey)\n\t}\n\n\tvar state *states.SyncState\n\tswitch targetState {\n\tcase workingState:\n\t\tstate = ctx.State()\n\tcase refreshState:\n\t\tstate = ctx.RefreshState()\n\tcase prevRunState:\n\t\tstate = ctx.PrevRunState()\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"unsupported phaseState value %#v\", targetState))\n\t}\n\tif state == nil {\n\t\t// Should not happen, because we shouldn't ever try to write to\n\t\t// a state that isn't applicable to the current operation.\n\t\t// (We can also get in here for unit tests which are using\n\t\t// EvalContextMock but not populating PrevRunStateState with\n\t\t// a suitable state object.)\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"state of type %s is not applicable to the current operation; this is a bug in Terraform\", targetState)\n\t}\n\n\t// In spite of the name, this function also handles the non-deposed case\n\t// via the writeResourceInstanceState wrapper, by setting deposedKey to\n\t// the NotDeposed value (the zero value of DeposedKey).\n\tvar write func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc)\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\twrite = func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) {\n\t\t\tstate.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(absAddr, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\twrite = func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) {\n\t\t\tstate.SetResourceInstanceDeposed(absAddr, deposedKey, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif obj == nil || obj.Value.IsNull() {\n\t\t// No need to encode anything: we'll just write it directly.\n\t\twrite(nil)\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s: removing state object for %s\", logFuncName, absAddr)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s: writing state object for %s\", logFuncName, absAddr)\n\n\tschema, currentVersion := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(absAddr.ContainingResource().Resource)\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// It shouldn't be possible to get this far in any real scenario\n\t\t// without a schema, but we might end up here in contrived tests that\n\t\t// fail to set up their world properly.\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode %s in state: no resource type schema available\", absAddr)\n\t}\n\n\tsrc, err := obj.Encode(schema.ImpliedType(), currentVersion)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode %s in state: %s\", absAddr, err)\n\t}\n\n\twrite(src)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// planDestroy returns a plain destroy diff.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) planDestroy(ctx EvalContext, currentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject, deposedKey states.DeposedKey) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n\tvar plan *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n\n\tabsAddr := n.Addr\n\tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\n\n\tif n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.Type == \"\" {\n\t\tif deposedKey == \"\" {\n\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"planDestroy for %s does not have ProviderAddr set\", absAddr))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"planDestroy for %s (deposed %s) does not have ProviderAddr set\", absAddr, deposedKey))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If there is no state or our attributes object is null then we're already\n\t// destroyed.\n\tif currentState == nil || currentState.Value.IsNull() {\n\t\t// We still need to generate a NoOp change, because that allows\n\t\t// outside consumers of the plan to distinguish between us affirming\n\t\t// that we checked something and concluded no changes were needed\n\t\t// vs. that something being entirely excluded e.g. due to -target.\n\t\tnoop := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\t\tAddr:        absAddr,\n\t\t\tPrevRunAddr: n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n\t\t\tDeposedKey:  deposedKey,\n\t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\t\tAction: plans.NoOp,\n\t\t\t\tBefore: cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\n\t\t\t\tAfter:  cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn noop, deferred, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// If we are in a context where we forget instead of destroying, we can\n\t// just return the forget change without consulting the provider.\n\tif ctx.Forget() {\n\t\tforget, diags := n.planForget(ctx, currentState, deposedKey)\n\t\treturn forget, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\tunmarkedPriorVal, _ := currentState.Value.UnmarkDeep()\n\n\t// The config and new value are null to signify that this is a destroy\n\t// operation.\n\tnullVal := cty.NullVal(unmarkedPriorVal.Type())\n\n\tprovider, _, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn plan, deferred, diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn plan, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\tvar resp providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse\n\tif n.override != nil {\n\t\t// If we have an overridden value from the test framework, that means\n\t\t// this value was created without consulting the provider previously.\n\t\t// We can just set the planned state to deleted without consulting the\n\t\t// provider.\n\t\tresp = providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse{\n\t\t\tPlannedState: nullVal,\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Allow the provider to check the destroy plan, and insert any\n\t\t// necessary private data.\n\t\tresp = provider.PlanResourceChange(providers.PlanResourceChangeRequest{\n\t\t\tTypeName:         n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n\t\t\tConfig:           nullVal,\n\t\t\tPriorState:       unmarkedPriorVal,\n\t\t\tProposedNewState: nullVal,\n\t\t\tPriorPrivate:     currentState.Private,\n\t\t\tProviderMeta:     metaConfigVal,\n\t\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n\t\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\n\n\t\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\n\t\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\n\t\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// We may not have a config for all destroys, but we want to reference\n\t\t// it in the diagnostics if we do.\n\t\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\t\tresp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String())\n\t\t}\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn plan, deferred, diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check that the provider returned a null value here, since that is the\n\t\t// only valid value for a destroy plan.\n\t\tif !resp.PlannedState.IsNull() && deferred == nil {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned a non-null destroy value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr),\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t\treturn plan, deferred, diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Plan is always the same for a destroy.\n\tplan = &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\tAddr:        absAddr,\n\t\tPrevRunAddr: n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n\t\tDeposedKey:  deposedKey,\n\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\tAction: plans.Delete,\n\t\t\tBefore: currentState.Value,\n\t\t\tAfter:  nullVal,\n\t\t},\n\t\tPrivate:      resp.PlannedPrivate,\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t}\n\n\treturn plan, deferred, diags\n}\n\n// planForget returns a Forget change.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) planForget(ctx EvalContext, currentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject, deposedKey states.DeposedKey) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tvar plan *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n\n\tabsAddr := n.Addr\n\n\t// If there is no state or our attributes object is null then the resource\n\t// is already removed.\n\tif currentState == nil || currentState.Value.IsNull() {\n\t\t// We still need to generate a NoOp change, because that allows\n\t\t// outside consumers of the plan to distinguish between us affirming\n\t\t// that we checked something and concluded no changes were needed\n\t\t// vs. that something being entirely excluded e.g. due to -target.\n\t\tnoop := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\t\tAddr:        absAddr,\n\t\t\tPrevRunAddr: n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n\t\t\tDeposedKey:  deposedKey,\n\t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\t\tAction: plans.NoOp,\n\t\t\t\tBefore: cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\n\t\t\t\tAfter:  cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn noop, nil\n\t}\n\n\tunmarkedPriorVal, _ := currentState.Value.UnmarkDeep()\n\tnullVal := cty.NullVal(unmarkedPriorVal.Type())\n\n\t// Plan is always the same for a forget.\n\tplan = &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\tAddr:        absAddr,\n\t\tPrevRunAddr: n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n\t\tDeposedKey:  deposedKey,\n\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\tAction: plans.Forget,\n\t\t\tBefore:
