Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_apply_instance.go",
  "newString": "// GraphNodeReferencer, overriding NodeResourceInstanceBase\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\t// Start with the usual resource instance implementation\n\tret := n.NodeResourceInstanceBase.References()",
  "oldString": "// GraphNodeReferencer, overriding NodeAbstractResourceInstance\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\t// Start with the usual resource instance implementation\n\tret := n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.References()",
  "originalFile": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// NodeApplyableResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is\n// \"applyable\": it is ready to be applied and is represented by a diff.\n//\n// This node is for a specific instance of a resource. It will usually be\n// accompanied in the graph by a NodeApplyableResource representing its\n// containing resource, and should depend on that node to ensure that the\n// state is properly prepared to receive changes to instances.\ntype NodeApplyableResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeResourceInstanceBase\n\n\tgraphNodeDeposer // implementation of GraphNodeDeposerConfig\n\n\t// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to\n\t// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\n\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\n\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\n\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource     = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance   = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeCreator            = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer         = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDeposer            = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable         = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachDependencies = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n)\n\n// GraphNodeCreator\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) CreateAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\treturn &addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferencer, overriding NodeAbstractResourceInstance\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\t// Start with the usual resource instance implementation\n\tret := n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.References()\n\n\t// Applying a resource must also depend on the destruction of any of its\n\t// dependencies, since this may for example affect the outcome of\n\t// evaluating an entire list of resources with \"count\" set (by reducing\n\t// the count).\n\t//\n\t// However, we can't do this in create_before_destroy mode because that\n\t// would create a dependency cycle. We make a compromise here of requiring\n\t// changes to be updated across two applies in this case, since the first\n\t// plan will use the old values.\n\tif !n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\n\t\tfor _, ref := range ret {\n\t\t\tswitch tr := ref.Subject.(type) {\n\t\t\tcase addrs.ResourceInstance:\n\t\t\t\tnewRef := *ref // shallow copy so we can mutate\n\t\t\t\tnewRef.Subject = tr.Phase(addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy)\n\t\t\t\tnewRef.Remaining = nil // can't access attributes of something being destroyed\n\t\t\t\tret = append(ret, &newRef)\n\t\t\tcase addrs.Resource:\n\t\t\t\tnewRef := *ref // shallow copy so we can mutate\n\t\t\t\tnewRef.Subject = tr.Phase(addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy)\n\t\t\t\tnewRef.Remaining = nil // can't access attributes of something being destroyed\n\t\t\t\tret = append(ret, &newRef)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn ret\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachDependencies\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) AttachDependencies(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\n\tn.Dependencies = deps\n}\n\n// GraphNodeExecutable\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tif n.Config == nil {\n\t\t// If there is no config, and there is no change, then we have nothing\n\t\t// to do and the change was left in the plan for informational\n\t\t// purposes only.\n\t\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\n\t\tcsrc := changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), addrs.NotDeposed)\n\t\tif csrc == nil || csrc.Action == plans.NoOp {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] NodeApplyableResourceInstance: No config or planned change recorded for %s\", n.Addr)\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Resource node has no configuration attached\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"The graph node for %s has no configuration attached to it. This suggests a bug in Terraform's apply graph builder; please report it!\",\n\t\t\t\taddr,\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Eval info is different depending on what kind of resource this is\n\tswitch n.Config.Mode {\n\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tcase addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported resource mode %s\", n.Config.Mode))\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\t_, diags := ephemeralResourceOpen(ctx, ephemeralResourceInput{\n\t\taddr:           n.Addr,\n\t\tconfig:         n.Config,\n\t\tproviderConfig: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t})\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tchange, err := n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\t// Stop early if we don't actually have a diff\n\tif change == nil {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\tif change.Action != plans.Read && change.Action != plans.NoOp {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"nonsensical planned action %#v for %s; this is a bug in Terraform\", change.Action, n.Addr))\n\t}\n\n\t// In this particular call to applyDataSource we include our planned\n\t// change, which signals that we expect this read to complete fully\n\t// with no unknown values; it'll produce an error if not.\n\tstate, repeatData, applyDiags := n.applyDataSource(ctx, change)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tif state != nil {\n\t\t// If n.applyDataSource returned a nil state object with no accompanying\n\t\t// errors then it determined that the given change doesn't require\n\t\t// actually reading the data (e.g. because it was already read during\n\t\t// the plan phase) and so we're only running through here to get the\n\t\t// extra details like precondition/postcondition checks.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, nil, \"\"))\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\n\n\t// Post-conditions might block further progress. We intentionally do this\n\t// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against\n\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\n\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\n\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n\t\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\n\t\tn.Config.Postconditions,\n\t\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(),\n\t\trepeatData,\n\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t// Declare a bunch of variables that are used for state during\n\t// evaluation. Most of this are written to by-address below.\n\tvar state *states.ResourceInstanceObject\n\tvar createBeforeDestroyEnabled bool\n\tvar deposedKey states.DeposedKey\n\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the saved diff for apply\n\tdiffApply, err := n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// We don't want to do any destroys\n\t// (these are handled by NodeDestroyResourceInstance instead)\n\tif diffApply == nil || diffApply.Action == plans.Delete {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\tif diffApply.Action == plans.Read {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"nonsensical planned action %#v for %s; this is a bug in Terraform\", diffApply.Action, n.Addr))\n\t}\n\n\tdestroy := (diffApply.Action == plans.Delete || diffApply.Action.IsReplace())\n\t// Get the stored action for CBD if we have a plan already\n\tcreateBeforeDestroyEnabled = diffApply.Change.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete\n\n\tif destroy && n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\n\t\tcreateBeforeDestroyEnabled = true\n\t}\n\n\tif createBeforeDestroyEnabled {\n\t\tstate := ctx.State()\n\t\tif n.PreallocatedDeposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\tdeposedKey = state.DeposeResourceInstanceObject(n.Addr)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tdeposedKey = n.PreallocatedDeposedKey\n\t\t\tstate.DeposeResourceInstanceObjectForceKey(n.Addr, deposedKey)\n\t\t}\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] managedResourceExecute: prior object for %s now deposed with key %s\", n.Addr, deposedKey)\n\t}\n\n\tstate, readDiags := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr())\n\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the saved diff\n\tdiff, err := n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Make a new diff, in case we've learned new values in the state\n\t// during apply which we can now incorporate.\n\tdiffApply, _, deferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.plan(ctx, diff, state, false, n.forceReplace)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(planDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tif deferred != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Resource deferred during apply, but not during plan\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"Terraform has encountered a bug where a provider would mark the resource %q as deferred during apply, but not during plan. This is most likely a bug in the provider. Please file an issue with the provider.\", n.Addr,\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Compare the diffs\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.checkPlannedChange(ctx, diff, diffApply, providerSchema))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tdiffApply = reducePlan(addr, diffApply, false)\n\t// reducePlan may have simplified our planned change\n\t// into a NoOp if it only requires destroying, since destroying\n\t// is handled by NodeDestroyResourceInstance. If so, we'll\n\t// still run through most of the logic here because we do still\n\t// need to deal with other book-keeping such as marking the\n\t// change as \"complete\", and running the author's postconditions.\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.preApplyHook(ctx, diffApply))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// If there is no change, there was nothing to apply, and we don't need to\n\t// re-write the state, but we do need to re-evaluate postconditions.\n\tif diffApply.Action == plans.NoOp {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(n.managedResourcePostconditions(ctx, repeatData))\n\t}\n\n\tstate, applyDiags := n.apply(ctx, state, diffApply, n.Config, repeatData, n.CreateBeforeDestroy())\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\n\n\t// We clear the change out here so that future nodes don't see a change\n\t// that is already complete.\n\terr = n.writeChange(ctx, nil, \"\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\tstate = maybeTainted(addr.Absolute(ctx.Path()), state, diffApply, diags.Err())\n\n\tif state != nil {\n\t\t// dependencies are always updated to match the configuration during apply\n\t\tstate.Dependencies = n.Dependencies\n\t}\n\terr = n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Run Provisioners\n\tcreateNew := (diffApply.Action == plans.Create || diffApply.Action.IsReplace())\n\tapplyProvisionersDiags := n.evalApplyProvisioners(ctx, state, createNew, configs.ProvisionerWhenCreate)\n\t// the provisioner errors count as port of the apply error, so we can bundle the diags\n\tdiags = diags.Append(applyProvisionersDiags)\n\n\tstate = maybeTainted(addr.Absolute(ctx.Path()), state, diffApply, diags.Err())\n\n\terr = n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\tif createBeforeDestroyEnabled && diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t// This should never happen, and so it always indicates a bug.\n\t\t\t// We should evaluate this node only if we've previously deposed\n\t\t\t// an object as part of the same operation.\n\t\t\tif diffApply != nil {\n\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Attempt to restore non-existent deposed object\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Terraform has encountered a bug where it would need to restore a deposed object for %s without knowing a deposed object key for that object. This occurred during a %s action. This is a bug in Terraform; please report it!\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\taddr, diffApply.Action,\n\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Attempt to restore non-existent deposed object\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Terraform has encountered a bug where it would need to restore a deposed object for %s without knowing a deposed object key for that object. This is a bug in Terraform; please report it!\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\taddr,\n\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\trestored := ctx.State().MaybeRestoreResourceInstanceDeposed(addr.Absolute(ctx.Path()), deposedKey)\n\t\t\tif restored {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] managedResourceExecute: %s deposed object %s was restored as the current object\", addr, deposedKey)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] managedResourceExecute: %s deposed object %s remains deposed\", addr, deposedKey)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.postApplyHook(ctx, state, diags.Err()))\n\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\n\n\t// Post-conditions might block further progress. We intentionally do this\n\t// _after_ writing the state because we want to check against\n\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\n\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\n\treturn diags.Append(n.managedResourcePostconditions(ctx, repeatData))\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) managedResourcePostconditions(ctx EvalContext, repeatData instances.RepetitionData) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\n\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n\t\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\n\t\tn.Config.Postconditions,\n\t\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), repeatData,\n\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t)\n\treturn diags.Append(checkDiags)\n}\n\n// checkPlannedChange produces errors if the _actual_ expected value is not\n// compatible with what was recorded in the plan.\n//\n// Errors here are most often indicative of a bug in the provider, so our error\n// messages will report with that in mind. It's also possible that there's a bug\n// in Terraform's Core's own \"proposed new value\" code in EvalDiff.\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) checkPlannedChange(ctx EvalContext, plannedChange, actualChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource\n\n\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.ContainingResource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support %q\", addr.Resource.Type))\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tabsAddr := addr.Absolute(ctx.Path())\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] checkPlannedChange: Verifying that actual change (action %s) matches planned change (action %s)\", actualChange.Action, plannedChange.Action)\n\n\tif plannedChange.Action != actualChange.Action {\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase plannedChange.Action == plans.Update && actualChange.Action == plans.NoOp:\n\t\t\t// It's okay for an update to become a NoOp once we've filled in\n\t\t\t// all of the unknown values, since the final values might actually\n\t\t\t// match what was there before after all.\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] After incorporating new values learned so far during apply, %s change has become NoOp\", absAddr)\n\n\t\tcase (plannedChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete && actualChange.Action == plans.DeleteThenCreate) ||\n\t\t\t(plannedChange.Action == plans.DeleteThenCreate && actualChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete):\n\t\t\t// If the order of replacement changed, then that is a bug in terraform\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Terraform produced inconsistent final plan\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, the planned action changed from %s to %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in Terraform and should be reported.\",\n\t\t\t\t\tabsAddr, plannedChange.Action, actualChange.Action,\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced inconsistent final plan\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, provider %q changed the planned action from %s to %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\t\tabsAddr, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tplannedChange.Action, actualChange.Action,\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\terrs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, plannedChange.After, actualChange.After)\n\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Provider produced inconsistent final plan\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, provider %q produced an invalid new value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\tabsAddr, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(), tfdiags.FormatError(err),\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t}\n\treturn diags\n}\n\n// maybeTainted takes the resource addr, new value, planned change, and possible\n// error from an apply operation and return a new instance object marked as\n// tainted if it appears that a create operation has failed.\nfunc maybeTainted(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, err error) *states.ResourceInstanceObject {\n\tif state == nil || change == nil || err == nil {\n\t\treturn state\n\t}\n\tif state.Status == states.ObjectTainted {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] maybeTainted: %s was already tainted, so nothing to do\", addr)\n\t\treturn state\n\t}\n\tif change.Action == plans.Create {\n\t\t// If there are errors during a _create_ then the object is\n\t\t// in an undefined state, and so we'll mark it as tainted so\n\t\t// we can try again on the next run.\n\t\t//\n\t\t// We don't do this for other change actions because errors\n\t\t// during updates will often not change the remote object at all.\n\t\t// If there _were_ changes prior to the error, it's the provider's\n\t\t// responsibility to record the effect of those changes in the\n\t\t// object value it returned.\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] maybeTainted: %s encountered an error during creation, so it is now marked as tainted\", addr)\n\t\treturn state.AsTainted()\n\t}\n\treturn state\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": true,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "   return &addr",
        " }",
        " ",
        "-// GraphNodeReferencer, overriding NodeAbstractResourceInstance",
        "+// GraphNodeReferencer, overriding NodeResourceInstanceBase",
        " func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {",
        "   // Start with the usual resource instance implementation",
        "-  ret := n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.References()",
        "+  ret := n.NodeResourceInstanceBase.References()",
        " ",
        "   // Applying a resource must also depend on the destruction of any of its",
        "   // dependencies, since this may for example affect the outcome of"
      ],
      "newLines": 10,
      "newStart": 52,
      "oldLines": 10,
      "oldStart": 52
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\t\"path/filepath\"\n    10\u2192\t\"sort\"\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2/hclsyntax\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    17\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n    18\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n    19\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/genconfig\"\n    20\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n    21\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n    22\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    23\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n    24\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    25\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    26\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    27\u2192)\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192// NodePlannableResourceInstance represents a _single_ resource\n    30\u2192// instance that is plannable. This means this represents a single\n    31\u2192// count index, for example.\n    32\u2192type NodePlannableResourceInstance struct {\n    33\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n    34\u2192\tForceCreateBeforeDestroy bool\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances\n    37\u2192\tskipRefresh bool\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192\t// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions\n    40\u2192\t// for any instances.\n    41\u2192\tskipPlanChanges bool\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192\t// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to\n    44\u2192\t// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\n    45\u2192\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\n    46\u2192\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\n    47\u2192\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192\t// replaceTriggeredBy stores references from replace_triggered_by which\n    50\u2192\t// triggered this instance to be replaced.\n    51\u2192\treplaceTriggeredBy []*addrs.Reference\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\t// importTarget, if populated, contains the information necessary to plan\n    54\u2192\t// an import of this resource.\n    55\u2192\timportTarget cty.Value\n    56\u2192}\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192var (\n    59\u2192\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance       = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    60\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    61\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    62\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    63\u2192\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance     = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    64\u2192\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    65\u2192\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceState  = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    66\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable           = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    67\u2192)\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192// GraphNodeEvalable\n    70\u2192func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n    71\u2192\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\t// Eval info is different depending on what kind of resource this is\n    74\u2192\tswitch addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {\n    75\u2192\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n    76\u2192\t\treturn n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\n    77\u2192\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n    78\u2192\t\treturn n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\n    79\u2192\tcase addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:\n    80\u2192\t\treturn n.ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx)\n    81\u2192\tdefault:\n    82\u2192\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported resource mode %s\", n.Config.Mode))\n    83\u2192\t}\n    84\u2192}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    87\u2192\tconfig := n.Config\n    88\u2192\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192\tvar change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n    93\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n    94\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    95\u2192\t\treturn diags\n    96\u2192\t}\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(validateSelfRef(addr.Resource, config.Config, providerSchema))\n    99\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   100\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   101\u2192\t}\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192\tcheckRuleSeverity := tfdiags.Error\n   104\u2192\tif n.skipPlanChanges || n.preDestroyRefresh {\n   105\u2192\t\tcheckRuleSeverity = tfdiags.Warning\n   106\u2192\t}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192\tdeferrals := ctx.Deferrals()\n   109\u2192\tchange, state, deferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.planDataSource(ctx, checkRuleSeverity, n.skipPlanChanges, deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(addr, n.Dependencies))\n   110\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(planDiags)\n   111\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   112\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   113\u2192\t}\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192\t// A nil change here indicates that Terraform is deciding NOT to make a\n   116\u2192\t// change at all. In which case even if we wanted to try and defer it\n   117\u2192\t// (because of a dependency) we can't as there is no change to defer.\n   118\u2192\t//\n   119\u2192\t// The most common case for this is when the data source is being refreshed\n   120\u2192\t// but depends on unknown values or dependencies which means we just skip\n   121\u2192\t// refreshing the data source. We maintain that behaviour here.\n   122\u2192\tif change != nil && deferred != nil {\n   123\u2192\t\t// Then this data source got deferred by the provider during planning.\n   124\u2192\t\tdeferrals.ReportDataSourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, change)\n   125\u2192\t} else {\n   126\u2192\t\t// Not deferred; business as usual.\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192\t\t// write the data source into both the refresh state and the\n   129\u2192\t\t// working state\n   130\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, refreshState))\n   131\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   132\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   133\u2192\t\t}\n   134\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))\n   135\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   136\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   137\u2192\t\t}\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, \"\"))\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192\t\t// Post-conditions might block further progress. We intentionally do this\n   142\u2192\t\t// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against\n   143\u2192\t\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\n   144\u2192\t\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\n   145\u2192\t\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n   146\u2192\t\t\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\n   147\u2192\t\t\tn.Config.Postconditions,\n   148\u2192\t\t\tctx, addr, repeatData,\n   149\u2192\t\t\tcheckRuleSeverity,\n   150\u2192\t\t)\n   151\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n   152\u2192\t}\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192\treturn diags\n   155\u2192}\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   158\u2192\tdeferrals := ctx.Deferrals()\n   159\u2192\t// For deferred ephemeral resources, we don't need to do anything here.\n   160\u2192\tif deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(n.Addr, n.Dependencies) {\n   161\u2192\t\tdeferrals.ReportEphemeralResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq)\n   162\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   163\u2192\t}\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192\tdeferred, diags := ephemeralResourceOpen(ctx, ephemeralResourceInput{\n   166\u2192\t\taddr:           n.Addr,\n   167\u2192\t\tconfig:         n.Config,\n   168\u2192\t\tproviderConfig: n.ResolvedProvider,\n   169\u2192\t})\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192\tif deferred != nil {\n   172\u2192\t\t// Then this ephemeral resource has been deferred while opening.\n   173\u2192\t\tdeferrals.ReportEphemeralResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, deferred.Reason)\n   174\u2192\t}\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192\treturn diags\n   177\u2192}\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   180\u2192\tconfig := n.Config\n   181\u2192\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192\tvar instanceRefreshState *states.ResourceInstanceObject\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192\tcheckRuleSeverity := tfdiags.Error\n   186\u2192\tif n.skipPlanChanges || n.preDestroyRefresh {\n   187\u2192\t\tcheckRuleSeverity = tfdiags.Warning\n   188\u2192\t}\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n   191\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n   192\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   193\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   194\u2192\t}\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192\tif config != nil {\n   197\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateSelfRef(addr.Resource, config.Config, providerSchema))\n   198\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   199\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   200\u2192\t\t}\n   201\u2192\t}\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192\timporting := n.importTarget != cty.NilVal && !n.preDestroyRefresh\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192\t// If the resource is to be imported, we now ask the provider for an Import\n   208\u2192\t// and a Refresh, and save the resulting state to instanceRefreshState.\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192\tif importing {\n   211\u2192\t\tif n.importTarget.IsKnown() {\n   212\u2192\t\t\tvar importDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   213\u2192\t\t\tinstanceRefreshState, deferred, importDiags = n.importState(ctx, addr, n.importTarget.AsString(), provider, providerSchema)\n   214\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\n   215\u2192\t\t} else {\n   216\u2192\t\t\t// Otherwise, just mark the resource as deferred without trying to\n   217\u2192\t\t\t// import it.\n   218\u2192\t\t\tdeferred = &providers.Deferred{\n   219\u2192\t\t\t\tReason: providers.DeferredReasonResourceConfigUnknown,\n   220\u2192\t\t\t}\n   221\u2192\t\t\tif n.Config == nil && len(n.generateConfigPath) > 0 {\n   222\u2192\t\t\t\t// Then we're supposed to be generating configuration for this\n   223\u2192\t\t\t\t// resource, but we can't because the configuration is unknown.\n   224\u2192\t\t\t\t//\n   225\u2192\t\t\t\t// Normally, the rest of this function would just be about\n   226\u2192\t\t\t\t// planning the known configuration to make sure everything we\n   227\u2192\t\t\t\t// do know about it is correct, but we can't even do that here.\n   228\u2192\t\t\t\t//\n   229\u2192\t\t\t\t// What we'll do is write out the address as being deferred with\n   230\u2192\t\t\t\t// an entirely unknown value. Then we'll skip the rest of this\n   231\u2192\t\t\t\t// function. (a) We're going to panic later when it complains\n   232\u2192\t\t\t\t// about having no configuration, and (b) the rest of the\n   233\u2192\t\t\t\t// function isn't doing anything as there is no configuration\n   234\u2192\t\t\t\t// to validate.\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192\t\t\t\timpliedType := providerSchema.ResourceTypes[addr.Resource.Resource.Type].Block.ImpliedType()\n   237\u2192\t\t\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, providers.DeferredReasonResourceConfigUnknown, &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n   238\u2192\t\t\t\t\tAddr:         addr,\n   239\u2192\t\t\t\t\tPrevRunAddr:  addr,\n   240\u2192\t\t\t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n   241\u2192\t\t\t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n   242\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tAction: plans.NoOp, // assume we'll get the config generation correct.\n   243\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tBefore: cty.NullVal(impliedType),\n   244\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tAfter:  cty.UnknownVal(impliedType),\n   245\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tImporting: &plans.Importing{\n   246\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tID: n.importTarget,\n   247\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n   248\u2192\t\t\t\t\t},\n   249\u2192\t\t\t\t})\n   250\u2192\t\t\t\treturn diags\n   251\u2192\t\t\t}\n   252\u2192\t\t}\n   253\u2192\t} else {\n   254\u2192\t\tvar readDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   255\u2192\t\tinstanceRefreshState, readDiags = n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, addr)\n   256\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\n   257\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   258\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   259\u2192\t\t}\n   260\u2192\t}\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192\tif deferred == nil {\n   263\u2192\t\t// We'll save a snapshot of what we just read from the state into the\n   264\u2192\t\t// prevRunState before we do anything else, since this will capture the\n   265\u2192\t\t// result of any schema upgrading that readResourceInstanceState just did,\n   266\u2192\t\t// but not include any out-of-band changes we might detect in in the\n   267\u2192\t\t// refresh step below.\n   268\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, prevRunState))\n   269\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   270\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   271\u2192\t\t}\n   272\u2192\t\t// Also the refreshState, because that should still reflect schema upgrades\n   273\u2192\t\t// even if it doesn't reflect upstream changes.\n   274\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\n   275\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   276\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   277\u2192\t\t}\n   278\u2192\t}\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192\t// we may need to detect a change in CreateBeforeDestroy to ensure it's\n   281\u2192\t// stored when we are not refreshing\n   282\u2192\tupdatedCBD := false\n   283\u2192\tif n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil && instanceRefreshState != nil {\n   284\u2192\t\tnewCBD := n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy || n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy\n   285\u2192\t\tupdatedCBD = instanceRefreshState.CreateBeforeDestroy != newCBD\n   286\u2192\t\tinstanceRefreshState.CreateBeforeDestroy = newCBD\n   287\u2192\t}\n   288\u2192\n   289\u2192\tvar refreshDeferred *providers.Deferred\n   290\u2192\t// This is the state of the resource before we refresh the value, we need to keep track\n   291\u2192\t// of this to report this as the before value if the refresh is deferred.\n   292\u2192\tpriorInstanceRefreshState := instanceRefreshState\n   293\u2192\n   294\u2192\t// Refresh, maybe\n   295\u2192\t// The import process handles its own refresh\n   296\u2192\tif !n.skipRefresh && !importing {\n   297\u2192\t\tvar refreshDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   298\u2192\t\tinstanceRefreshState, refreshDeferred, refreshDiags = n.refresh(ctx, states.NotDeposed, instanceRefreshState, ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed())\n   299\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)\n   300\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   301\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   302\u2192\t\t}\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192\t\tif instanceRefreshState != nil {\n   305\u2192\t\t\t// When refreshing we start by merging the stored dependencies and\n   306\u2192\t\t\t// the configured dependencies. The configured dependencies will be\n   307\u2192\t\t\t// stored to state once the changes are applied. If the plan\n   308\u2192\t\t\t// results in no changes, we will re-write these dependencies\n   309\u2192\t\t\t// below.\n   310\u2192\t\t\tinstanceRefreshState.Dependencies = mergeDeps(n.Dependencies, instanceRefreshState.Dependencies)\n   311\u2192\t\t}\n   312\u2192\n   313\u2192\t\tif deferred == nil && refreshDeferred != nil {\n   314\u2192\t\t\tdeferred = refreshDeferred\n   315\u2192\t\t}\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192\t\tif deferred == nil {\n   318\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\n   319\u2192\t\t}\n   320\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   321\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   322\u2192\t\t}\n   323\u2192\t}\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192\tif n.skipRefresh && !importing && updatedCBD {\n   326\u2192\t\t// CreateBeforeDestroy must be set correctly in the state which is used\n   327\u2192\t\t// to create the apply graph, so if we did not refresh the state make\n   328\u2192\t\t// sure we still update any changes to CreateBeforeDestroy.\n   329\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\n   330\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   331\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   332\u2192\t\t}\n   333\u2192\t}\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192\t// Plan the instance, unless we're in the refresh-only mode\n   336\u2192\tif !n.skipPlanChanges {\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192\t\t// add this instance to n.forceReplace if replacement is triggered by\n   339\u2192\t\t// another change\n   340\u2192\t\trepData := instances.RepetitionData{}\n   341\u2192\t\tswitch k := addr.Resource.Key.(type) {\n   342\u2192\t\tcase addrs.IntKey:\n   343\u2192\t\t\trepData.CountIndex = k.Value()\n   344\u2192\t\tcase addrs.StringKey:\n   345\u2192\t\t\trepData.EachKey = k.Value()\n   346\u2192\t\t\trepData.EachValue = cty.DynamicVal\n   347\u2192\t\t}\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.replaceTriggered(ctx, repData))\n   350\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   351\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   352\u2192\t\t}\n   353\u2192\n   354\u2192\t\tchange, instancePlanState, planDeferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.plan(\n   355\u2192\t\t\tctx, nil, instanceRefreshState, n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy, n.forceReplace,\n   356\u2192\t\t)\n   357\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(planDiags)\n   358\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   359\u2192\t\t\t// If we are importing and generating a configuration, we need to\n   360\u2192\t\t\t// ensure the change is written out so the configuration can be\n   361\u2192\t\t\t// captured.\n   362\u2192\t\t\tif planDeferred == nil && len(n.generateConfigPath) > 0 {\n   363\u2192\t\t\t\t// Update our return plan\n   364\u2192\t\t\t\tchange := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n   365\u2192\t\t\t\t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n   366\u2192\t\t\t\t\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n   367\u2192\t\t\t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n   368\u2192\t\t\t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n   369\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t// we only need a placeholder, so this will be a NoOp\n   370\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tAction:          plans.NoOp,\n   371\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tBefore:          instanceRefreshState.Value,\n   372\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tAfter:           instanceRefreshState.Value,\n   373\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tGeneratedConfig: n.generatedConfigHCL,\n   374\u2192\t\t\t\t\t},\n   375\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   376\u2192\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, \"\"))\n   377\u2192\t\t\t}\n   378\u2192\n   379\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   380\u2192\t\t}\n   381\u2192\n   382\u2192\t\tif deferred == nil && planDeferred != nil {\n   383\u2192\t\t\tdeferred = planDeferred\n   384\u2192\t\t}\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192\t\tif importing {\n   387\u2192\t\t\tchange.Importing = &plans.Importing{ID: n.importTarget}\n   388\u2192\t\t}\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192\t\t// FIXME: here we udpate the change to reflect the reason for\n   391\u2192\t\t// replacement, but we still overload forceReplace to get the correct\n   392\u2192\t\t// change planned.\n   393\u2192\t\tif len(n.replaceTriggeredBy) > 0 {\n   394\u2192\t\t\tchange.ActionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceByTriggers\n   395\u2192\t\t}\n   396\u2192\n   397\u2192\t\tdeferrals := ctx.Deferrals()\n   398\u2192\t\tif deferred != nil {\n   399\u2192\t\t\t// Then this resource has been deferred either during the import,\n   400\u2192\t\t\t// refresh or planning stage. We'll report the deferral and\n   401\u2192\t\t\t// store what we could produce in the deferral tracker.\n   402\u2192\t\t\tdeferrals.ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, change)\n   403\u2192\t\t} else if !deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(n.Addr, n.Dependencies) {\n   404\u2192\t\t\t// We intentionally write the change before the subsequent checks, because\n   405\u2192\t\t\t// all of the checks below this point are for problems caused by the\n   406\u2192\t\t\t// context surrounding the change, rather than the change itself, and\n   407\u2192\t\t\t// so it's helpful to still include the valid-in-isolation change as\n   408\u2192\t\t\t// part of the plan as additional context in our error output.\n   409\u2192\t\t\t//\n   410\u2192\t\t\t// FIXME: it is currently important that we write resource changes to\n   411\u2192\t\t\t// the plan (n.writeChange) before we write the corresponding state\n   412\u2192\t\t\t// (n.writeResourceInstanceState).\n   413\u2192\t\t\t//\n   414\u2192\t\t\t// This is because the planned resource state will normally have the\n   415\u2192\t\t\t// status of states.ObjectPlanned, which causes later logic to refer to\n   416\u2192\t\t\t// the contents of the plan to retrieve the resource data. Because\n   417\u2192\t\t\t// there is no shared lock between these two data structures, reversing\n   418\u2192\t\t\t// the order of these writes will cause a brief window of inconsistency\n   419\u2192\t\t\t// which can lead to a failed safety check.\n   420\u2192\t\t\t//\n   421\u2192\t\t\t// Future work should adjust these APIs such that it is impossible to\n   422\u2192\t\t\t// update these two data structures incorrectly through any objects\n   423\u2192\t\t\t// reachable via the terraform.EvalContext API.\n   424\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, \"\"))\n   425\u2192\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   426\u2192\t\t\t\treturn diags\n   427\u2192\t\t\t}\n   428\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instancePlanState, workingState))\n   429\u2192\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   430\u2192\t\t\t\treturn diags\n   431\u2192\t\t\t}\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.checkPreventDestroy(change))\n   434\u2192\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   435\u2192\t\t\t\treturn diags\n   436\u2192\t\t\t}\n   437\u2192\n   438\u2192\t\t\t// If this plan resulted in a NoOp, then apply won't have a chance to make\n   439\u2192\t\t\t// any changes to the stored dependencies. Since this is a NoOp we know\n   440\u2192\t\t\t// that the stored dependencies will have no effect during apply, and we can\n   441\u2192\t\t\t// write them out now.\n   442\u2192\t\t\tif change.Action == plans.NoOp && !depsEqual(instanceRefreshState.Dependencies, n.Dependencies) {\n   443\u2192\t\t\t\t// the refresh state will be the final state for this resource, so\n   444\u2192\t\t\t\t// finalize the dependencies here if they need to be updated.\n   445\u2192\t\t\t\tinstanceRefreshState.Dependencies = n.Dependencies\n   446\u2192\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\n   447\u2192\t\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   448\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn diags\n   449\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   450\u2192\t\t\t}\n   451\u2192\n   452\u2192\t\t\t// Post-conditions might block completion. We intentionally do this\n   453\u2192\t\t\t// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against\n   454\u2192\t\t\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\n   455\u2192\t\t\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\n   456\u2192\t\t\t// (Note that some preconditions will end up being skipped during\n   457\u2192\t\t\t// planning, because their conditions depend on values not yet known.)\n   458\u2192\t\t\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n   459\u2192\t\t\t\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\n   460\u2192\t\t\t\tn.Config.Postconditions,\n   461\u2192\t\t\t\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), repeatData,\n   462\u2192\t\t\t\tcheckRuleSeverity,\n   463\u2192\t\t\t)\n   464\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n   465\u2192\t\t} else {\n   466\u2192\t\t\t// The deferrals tracker says that we must defer changes for\n   467\u2192\t\t\t// this resource instance, presumably due to a dependency on an\n   468\u2192\t\t\t// upstream object that was already deferred. Therefore we just\n   469\u2192\t\t\t// report our own deferral (capturing a placeholder value in the\n   470\u2192\t\t\t// deferral tracker) and don't add anything to the plan or\n   471\u2192\t\t\t// working state.\n   472\u2192\t\t\t// In this case, the expression evaluator should use the placeholder\n   473\u2192\t\t\t// value registered here as the value of this resource instance,\n   474\u2192\t\t\t// instead of using the plan.\n   475\u2192\t\t\tdeferrals.ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq, change)\n   476\u2192\t\t}\n   477\u2192\t} else {\n   478\u2192\t\t// In refresh-only mode we need to evaluate the for-each expression in\n   479\u2192\t\t// order to supply the value to the pre- and post-condition check\n   480\u2192\t\t// blocks. This has the unfortunate edge case of a refresh-only plan\n   481\u2192\t\t// executing with a for-each map which has the same keys but different\n   482\u2192\t\t// values, which could result in a post-condition check relying on that\n   483\u2192\t\t// value being inaccurate. Unless we decide to store the value of the\n   484\u2192\t\t// for-each expression in state, this is unavoidable.\n   485\u2192\t\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\n   486\u2192\t\trepeatData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\n   487\u2192\n   488\u2192\t\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n   489\u2192\t\t\taddrs.ResourcePrecondition,\n   490\u2192\t\t\tn.Config.Preconditions,\n   491\u2192\t\t\tctx, addr, repeatData,\n   492\u2192\t\t\tcheckRuleSeverity,\n   493\u2192\t\t)\n   494\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n   495\u2192\n   496\u2192\t\t// Even if we don't plan changes, we do still need to at least update\n   497\u2192\t\t// the working state to reflect the refresh result. If not, then e.g.\n   498\u2192\t\t// any output values refering to this will not react to the drift.\n   499\u2192\t\t// (Even if we didn't actually refresh above, this will still save\n   500\u2192\t\t// the result of any schema upgrading we did in readResourceInstanceState.)\n   501\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, workingState))\n   502\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   503\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   504\u2192\t\t}\n   505\u2192\n   506\u2192\t\t// Here we also evaluate post-conditions after updating the working\n   507\u2192\t\t// state, because we want to check against the result of the refresh.\n   508\u2192\t\t// Unlike in normal planning mode, these checks are still evaluated\n   509\u2192\t\t// even if pre-conditions generated diagnostics, because we have no\n   510\u2192\t\t// planned changes to block.\n   511\u2192\t\tcheckDiags = evalCheckRules(\n   512\u2192\t\t\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\n   513\u2192\t\t\tn.Config.Postconditions,\n   514\u2192\t\t\tctx, addr, repeatData,\n   515\u2192\t\t\tcheckRuleSeverity,\n   516\u2192\t\t)\n   517\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n   518\u2192\n   519\u2192\t\t// In this case we skipped planning changes and therefore need to report the deferral\n   520\u2192\t\t// here, if there was one.\n   521\u2192\t\tif refreshDeferred != nil {\n   522\u2192\t\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n   523\u2192\t\t\t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n   524\u2192\t\t\t\tPrevRunAddr:  n.Addr,\n   525\u2192\t\t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n   526\u2192\t\t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n   527\u2192\t\t\t\t\tAction: plans.Read,\n   528\u2192\t\t\t\t\tBefore: priorInstanceRefreshState.Value,\n   529\u2192\t\t\t\t\tAfter:  instanceRefreshState.Value,\n   530\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   531\u2192\t\t\t})\n   532\u2192\t\t}\n   533\u2192\t}\n   534\u2192\n   535\u2192\treturn diags\n   536\u2192}\n   537\u2192\n   538\u2192// replaceTriggered checks if this instance needs to be replace due to a change\n   539\u2192// in a replace_triggered_by reference. If replacement is required, the\n   540\u2192// instance address is added to forceReplace\n   541\u2192func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) replaceTriggered(ctx EvalContext, repData instances.RepetitionData) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n   542\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   543\u2192\tif n.Config == nil {\n   544\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   545\u2192\t}\n   546\u2192\n   547\u2192\tfor _, expr := range n.Config.TriggersReplacement {\n   548\u2192\t\tref, replace, evalDiags := ctx.EvaluateReplaceTriggeredBy(expr, repData)\n   549\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n   550\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   551\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   552\u2192\t\t}\n   553\u2192\n   554\u2192\t\tif replace {\n   555\u2192\t\t\t// FIXME: forceReplace accomplishes the same goal, however we may\n   556\u2192\t\t\t// want to communicate more information about which resource\n   557\u2192\t\t\t// triggered the replacement in the plan.\n   558\u2192\t\t\t// Rather than further complicating the plan method with more\n   559\u2192\t\t\t// options, we can refactor both of these features later.\n   560\u2192\t\t\tn.forceReplace = append(n.forceReplace, n.Addr)\n   561\u2192\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] ReplaceTriggeredBy forcing replacement of %s due to change in %s\", n.Addr, ref.DisplayString())\n   562\u2192\n   563\u2192\t\t\tn.replaceTriggeredBy = append(n.replaceTriggeredBy, ref)\n   564\u2192\t\t\tbreak\n   565\u2192\t\t}\n   566\u2192\t}\n   567\u2192\n   568\u2192\treturn diags\n   569\u2192}\n   570\u2192\n   571\u2192func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) importState(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, importId string, provider providers.Interface, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   572\u2192\tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\n   573\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   574\u2192\tabsAddr := addr.Resource.Absolute(ctx.Path())\n   575\u2192\thookResourceID := HookResourceIdentity{\n   576\u2192\t\tAddr:         absAddr,\n   577\u2192\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\n   578\u2192\t}\n   579\u2192\n   580\u2192\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n   581\u2192\n   582\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n   583\u2192\t\treturn h.PrePlanImport(hookResourceID, importId)\n   584\u2192\t}))\n   585\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   586\u2192\t\treturn nil, deferred, diags\n   587\u2192\t}\n   588\u2192\n   589\u2192\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.Resource.Resource)\n   590\u2192\tif schema == nil {\n   591\u2192\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n   592\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type for %q\", n.Addr))\n   593\u2192\t\treturn nil, deferred, diags\n   594\u2192\t}\n   595\u2192\n   596\u2192\tvar resp providers.ImportResourceStateResponse\n   597\u2192\tif n.override != nil {\n   598\u2192\t\t// For overriding resources that are being imported, we cheat a little\n   599\u2192\t\t// bit and look ahead at the configuration the user has provided and\n   600\u2192\t\t// we'll use that as the basis for the resource we're going to make up\n   601\u2192\t\t// that is due to be overridden.\n   602\u2192\n   603\u2192\t\t// Note, we know we have configuration as it's impossible to enable\n   604\u2192\t\t// config generation during tests, and the validation that config exists\n   605\u2192\t\t// if configuration generation is off has already happened.\n   606\u2192\t\tif n.Config == nil {\n   607\u2192\t\t\t// But, just in case we change this at some point in the future,\n   608\u2192\t\t\t// let's add a specific error message here we can test for to\n   609\u2192\t\t\t// document the expectation somewhere. This shouldn't happen in\n   610\u2192\t\t\t// production, so we don't bother with a pretty error.\n   611\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"override blocks do not support config generation\"))\n   612\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, deferred, diags\n   613\u2192\t\t}\n   614\u2192\n   615\u2192\t\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\n   616\u2192\t\tkeyData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\n   617\u2192\t\tconfigVal, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(n.Config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\n   618\u2192\t\tif configDiags.HasErrors() {\n   619\u2192\t\t\t// We have an overridden resource so we're definitely in a test and\n   620\u2192\t\t\t// the users config is not valid. So give up and just report the\n   621\u2192\t\t\t// problems in the users configuration. Normally, we'd import the\n   622\u2192\t\t\t// resource before giving up but for a test it doesn't matter, the\n   623\u2192\t\t\t// test fails in the same way and the state is just lost anyway.\n   624\u2192\t\t\t//\n   625\u2192\t\t\t// If there were only warnings from the config then we'll duplicate\n   626\u2192\t\t\t// them if we include them (as the config will be loaded again\n   627\u2192\t\t\t// later), so only add the configDiags into the main diags if we\n   628\u2192\t\t\t// found actual errors.\n   629\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\n   630\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, deferred, diags\n   631\u2192\t\t}\n   632\u2192\t\tconfigVal, _ = configVal.UnmarkDeep()\n   633\u2192\n   634\u2192\t\t// Let's pretend we're reading the value as a data source so we\n   635\u2192\t\t// pre-compute values now as if the resource has already been created.\n   636\u2192\t\toverride, overrideDiags := mocking.ComputedValuesForDataSource(configVal, mocking.MockedData{\n   637\u2192\t\t\tValue: n.override.Values,\n   638\u2192\t\t\tRange: n.override.ValuesRange,\n   639\u2192\t\t}, schema)\n   640\u2192\t\tresp = providers.ImportResourceStateResponse{\n   641\u2192\t\t\tImportedResources: []providers.ImportedResource{\n   642\u2192\t\t\t\t{\n   643\u2192\t\t\t\t\tTypeName: addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n   644\u2192\t\t\t\t\tState:    override,\n   645\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   646\u2192\t\t\t},\n   647\u2192\t\t\tDiagnostics: overrideDiags.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, absAddr.String()),\n   648\u2192\t\t}\n   649\u2192\t} else {\n   650\u2192\t\tresp = provider.ImportResourceState(providers.ImportResourceStateRequest{\n   651\u2192\t\t\tTypeName: addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n   652\u2192\t\t\tID:       importId,\n   653\u2192\t\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n   654\u2192\t\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\n   655\u2192\t\t\t},\n   656\u2192\t\t})\n   657\u2192\t}\n   658\u2192\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\n   659\u2192\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\n   660\u2192\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n   661\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\n   662\u2192\t}\n   663\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)\n   664\u2192\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\n   665\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   666\u2192\t\treturn nil, deferred, diags\n   667\u2192\t}\n   668\u2192\n   669\u2192\timported := resp.ImportedResources\n   670\u2192\n   671\u2192\tif len(imported) > 1 {\n   672\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   673\u2192\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n   674\u2192\t\t\t\"Multiple import states not supported\",\n   675\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"While attempting to import with ID %s, the provider \"+\n   676\u2192\t\t\t\t\"returned multiple resource instance states. This \"+\n   677\u2192\t\t\t\t\"is not currently supported.\",\n   678\u2192\t\t\t\timportId,\n   679\u2192\t\t\t),\n   680\u2192\t\t))\n   681\u2192\t}\n   682\u2192\n   683\u2192\tif len(imported) == 0 {\n   684\u2192\n   685\u2192\t\t// Sanity check against the providers. If the provider defers the response, it may not have been able to return a state, so we'll only error if no deferral was returned.\n   686\u2192\t\tif deferred == nil {\n   687\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   688\u2192\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n   689\u2192\t\t\t\t\"Import returned no resources\",\n   690\u2192\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"While attempting to import with ID %s, the provider\"+\n   691\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"returned no instance states.\",\n   692\u2192\t\t\t\t\timportId,\n   693\u2192\t\t\t\t),\n   694\u2192\t\t\t))\n   695\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, deferred, diags\n   696\u2192\t\t}\n   697\u2192\n   698\u2192\t\t// If we were deferred, then let's make up a resource to represent the\n   699\u2192\t\t// state we're going to import.\n   700\u2192\t\tstate := providers.ImportedResource{\n   701\u2192\t\t\tTypeName: addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n   702\u2192\t\t\tState:    cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType()),\n   703\u2192\t\t}\n   704\u2192\n   705\u2192\t\t// We skip the read and further validation since we make up the state\n   706\u2192\t\t// of the imported resource anyways.\u0010\n   707\u2192\t\treturn state.AsInstanceObject(), deferred, diags\n   708\u2192\t}\n   709\u2192\n   710\u2192\tfor _, obj := range imported {\n   711\u2192\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] graphNodeImportState: import %s %q produced instance object of type %s\", absAddr.String(), importId, obj.TypeName)\n   712\u2192\t}\n   713\u2192\n   714\u2192\timportedState := imported[0].AsInstanceObject()\n   715\u2192\n   716\u2192\t// We can only call the hooks and validate the imported state if we have\n   717\u2192\t// actually done the import.\n   718\u2192\tif resp.Deferred == nil {\n   719\u2192\t\t// call post-import hook\n   720\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n   721\u2192\t\t\treturn h.PostPlanImport(hookResourceID, imported)\n   722\u2192\t\t}))\n   723\u2192\t}\n   724\u2192\n   725\u2192\tif imported[0].TypeName == \"\" {\n   726\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"import of %s didn't set type\", n.Addr.String()))\n   727\u2192\t\treturn nil, deferred, diags\n   728\u2192\t}\n   729\u2192\n   730\u2192\tif deferred == nil && importedState.Value.IsNull() {\n   731\u2192\t\t// It's actually okay for a deferred import to have returned a null.\n   732\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   733\u2192\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n   734\u2192\t\t\t\"Import returned null resource\",\n   735\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"While attempting to import with ID %s, the provider\"+\n   736\u2192\t\t\t\t\"returned an instance with no state.\",\n   737\u2192\t\t\t\timportId,\n   738\u2192\t\t\t),\n   739\u2192\t\t))\n   740\u2192\n   741\u2192\t}\n   742\u2192\n   743\u2192\t// refresh\n   744\u2192\triNode := &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n   745\u2192\t\tAddr: n.Addr,\n   746\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n   747\u2192\t\t\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n   748\u2192\t\t},\n   749\u2192\t\toverride: n.override,\n   750\u2192\t}\n   751\u2192\tinstanceRefreshState, refreshDeferred, refreshDiags := riNode.refresh(ctx, states.NotDeposed, importedState, ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed())\n   752\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)\n   753\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   754\u2192\t\treturn instanceRefreshState, deferred, diags\n   755\u2192\t}\n   756\u2192\n   757\u2192\t// report the refresh was deferred, we don't need to error since the import step succeeded\n   758\u2192\tif deferred == nil && refreshDeferred != nil {\n   759\u2192\t\tdeferred = refreshDeferred\n   760\u2192\t}\n   761\u2192\n   762\u2192\t// verify the existence of the imported resource\n   763\u2192\tif refreshDeferred == nil && instanceRefreshState.Value.IsNull() {\n   764\u2192\t\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   765\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   766\u2192\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n   767\u2192\t\t\t\"Cannot import non-existent remote object\",\n   768\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n   769\u2192\t\t\t\t\"While attempting to import an existing object to %q, \"+\n   770\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"the provider detected that no object exists with the given id. \"+\n   771\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"Only pre-existing objects can be imported; check that the id \"+\n   772\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"is correct and that it is associated with the provider's \"+\n   773\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"configured region or endpoint, or use \\\"terraform apply\\\" to \"+\n   774\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"create a new remote object for this resource.\",\n   775\u2192\t\t\t\tn.Addr,\n   776\u2192\t\t\t),\n   777\u2192\t\t))\n   778\u2192\t\treturn instanceRefreshState, deferred, diags\n   779\u2192\t}\n   780\u2192\n   781\u2192\t// If we're importing and generating config, generate it now. We only\n   782\u2192\t// generate config if the import isn't being deferred. We should generate\n   783\u2192\t// the configuration in the plan that the import is actually happening in.\n   784\u2192\tif deferred == nil && len(n.generateConfigPath) > 0 {\n   785\u2192\t\tif n.Config != nil {\n   786\u2192\t\t\treturn instanceRefreshState, nil, diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"tried to generate config for %s, but it already exists\", n.Addr))\n   787\u2192\t\t}\n   788\u2192\n   789\u2192\t\t// Generate the HCL string first, then parse the HCL body from it.\n   790\u2192\t\t// First we generate the contents of the resource block for use within\n   791\u2192\t\t// the planning node. Then we wrap it in an enclosing resource block to\n   792\u2192\t\t// pass into the plan for rendering.\n   793\u2192\t\tgeneratedHCLAttributes, generatedDiags := n.generateHCLStringAttributes(n.Addr, instanceRefreshState, schema)\n   794\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(generatedDiags)\n   795\u2192\n   796\u2192\t\tn.generatedConfigHCL = genconfig.WrapResourceContents(n.Addr, generatedHCLAttributes)\n   797\u2192\n   798\u2192\t\t// parse the \"file\" as HCL to get the hcl.Body\n   799\u2192\t\tsynthHCLFile, hclDiags := hclsyntax.ParseConfig([]byte(generatedHCLAttributes), filepath.Base(n.generateConfigPath), hcl.Pos{Byte: 0, Line: 1, Column: 1})\n   800\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(hclDiags)\n   801\u2192\t\tif hclDiags.HasErrors() {\n   802\u2192\t\t\treturn instanceRefreshState, nil, diags\n   803\u2192\t\t}\n   804\u2192\n   805\u2192\t\t// We have to do a kind of mini parsing of the content here to correctly\n   806\u2192\t\t// mark attributes like 'provider' as hidden. We only care about the\n   807\u2192\t\t// resulting content, so it's remain that gets passed into the resource\n   808\u2192\t\t// as the config.\n   809\u2192\t\t_, remain, resourceDiags := synthHCLFile.Body.PartialContent(configs.ResourceBlockSchema)\n   810\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(resourceDiags)\n   811\u2192\t\tif resourceDiags.HasErrors() {\n   812\u2192\t\t\treturn instanceRefreshState, nil, diags\n   813\u2192\t\t}\n   814\u2192\n   815\u2192\t\tn.Config = &configs.Resource{\n   816\u2192\t\t\tMode:     addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n   817\u2192\t\t\tType:     n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n   818\u2192\t\t\tName:     n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Name,\n   819\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   remain,\n   820\u2192\t\t\tManaged:  &configs.ManagedResource{},\n   821\u2192\t\t\tProvider: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\n   822\u2192\t\t}\n   823\u2192\t}\n   824\u2192\n   825\u2192\tif deferred == nil {\n   826\u2192\t\t// Only write the state if the change isn't being deferred. We're also\n   827\u2192\t\t// reporting the deferred status to the caller, so they should know\n   828\u2192\t\t// not to read from the state.\n   829\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(riNode.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\n   830\u2192\t}\n   831\u2192\treturn instanceRefreshState, deferred, diags\n   832\u2192}\n   833\u2192\n   834\u2192// generateHCLStringAttributes produces a string in HCL format for the given\n   835\u2192// resource state and schema without the surrounding block.\n   836\u2192func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) generateHCLStringAttributes(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, schema *configschema.Block) (string, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   837\u2192\tfilteredSchema := schema.Filter(\n   838\u2192\t\tconfigschema.FilterOr(\n   839\u2192\t\t\tconfigschema.FilterReadOnlyAttribute,\n   840\u2192\t\t\tconfigschema.FilterDeprecatedAttribute,\n   841\u2192\n   842\u2192\t\t\t// The legacy SDK adds an Optional+Computed \"id\" attribute to the\n   843\u2192\t\t\t// resource schema even if not defined in provider code.\n   844\u2192\t\t\t// During validation, however, the presence of an extraneous \"id\"\n   845\u2192\t\t\t// attribute in config will cause an error.\n   846\u2192\t\t\t// Remove this attribute so we do not generate an \"id\" attribute\n   847\u2192\t\t\t// where there is a risk that it is not in the real resource schema.\n   848\u2192\t\t\t//\n   849\u2192\t\t\t// TRADEOFF: Resources in which there actually is an\n   850\u2192\t\t\t// Optional+Computed \"id\" attribute in the schema will have that\n   851\u2192\t\t\t// attribute missing from generated config.\n   852\u2192\t\t\tconfigschema.FilterHelperSchemaIdAttribute,\n   853\u2192\t\t),\n   854\u2192\t\tconfigschema.FilterDeprecatedBlock,\n   855\u2192\t)\n   856\u2192\n   857\u2192\tproviderAddr := addrs.LocalProviderConfig{\n   858\u2192\t\tLocalName: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.Type,\n   859\u2192\t\tAlias:     n.ResolvedProvider.Alias,\n   860\u2192\t}\n   861\u2192\n   862\u2192\treturn genconfig.GenerateResourceContents(addr, filteredSchema, providerAddr, state.Value)\n   863\u2192}\n   864\u2192\n   865\u2192// mergeDeps returns the union of 2 sets of dependencies\n   866\u2192func mergeDeps(a, b []addrs.ConfigResource) []addrs.ConfigResource {\n   867\u2192\tswitch {\n   868\u2192\tcase len(a) == 0:\n   869\u2192\t\treturn b\n   870\u2192\tcase len(b) == 0:\n   871\u2192\t\treturn a\n   872\u2192\t}\n   873\u2192\n   874\u2192\tset := make(map[string]addrs.ConfigResource)\n   875\u2192\n   876\u2192\tfor _, dep := range a {\n   877\u2192\t\tset[dep.String()] = dep\n   878\u2192\t}\n   879\u2192\n   880\u2192\tfor _, dep := range b {\n   881\u2192\t\tset[dep.String()] = dep\n   882\u2192\t}\n   883\u2192\n   884\u2192\tnewDeps := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, 0, len(set))\n   885\u2192\tfor _, dep := range set {\n   886\u2192\t\tnewDeps = append(newDeps, dep)\n   887\u2192\t}\n   888\u2192\n   889\u2192\treturn newDeps\n   890\u2192}\n   891\u2192\n   892\u2192func depsEqual(a, b []addrs.ConfigResource) bool {\n   893\u2192\tif len(a) != len(b) {\n   894\u2192\t\treturn false\n   895\u2192\t}\n   896\u2192\n   897\u2192\t// Because we need to sort the deps to compare equality, make shallow\n   898\u2192\t// copies to prevent concurrently modifying the array values on\n   899\u2192\t// dependencies shared between expanded instances.\n   900\u2192\tcopyA, copyB := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, len(a)), make([]addrs.ConfigResource, len(b))\n   901\u2192\tcopy(copyA, a)\n   902\u2192\tcopy(copyB, b)\n   903\u2192\ta, b = copyA, copyB\n   904\u2192\n   905\u2192\tless := func(s []addrs.ConfigResource) func(i, j int) bool {\n   906\u2192\t\treturn func(i, j int) bool {\n   907\u2192\t\t\treturn s[i].String() < s[j].String()\n   908\u2192\t\t}\n   909\u2192\t}\n   910\u2192\n   911\u2192\tsort.Slice(a, less(a))\n   912\u2192\tsort.Slice(b, less(b))\n   913\u2192\n   914\u2192\tfor i := range a {\n   915\u2192\t\tif !a[i].Equal(b[i]) {\n   916\u2192\t\t\treturn false\n   917\u2192\t\t}\n   918\u2192\t}\n   919\u2192\treturn true\n   920\u2192}\n   921\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
23. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_orphan_count.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"log\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    12\u2192)\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192// OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds orphans\n    15\u2192// for an expanded count to the graph. The determination of this depends\n    16\u2192// on the count argument given.\n    17\u2192//\n    18\u2192// Orphans are found by comparing the count to what is found in the state.\n    19\u2192// This transform assumes that if an element in the state is within the count\n    20\u2192// bounds given, that it is not an orphan.\n    21\u2192type OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer struct {\n    22\u2192\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192\tAddr          addrs.AbsResource           // Addr of the resource to look for orphans\n    25\u2192\tInstanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addresses that currently exist in config\n    26\u2192\tState         *states.State               // Full global state\n    27\u2192}\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192func (t *OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n    30\u2192\trs := t.State.Resource(t.Addr)\n    31\u2192\tif rs == nil {\n    32\u2192\t\treturn nil // Resource doesn't exist in state, so nothing to do!\n    33\u2192\t}\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\t// This is an O(n*m) analysis, which we accept for now because the\n    36\u2192\t// number of instances of a single resource ought to always be small in any\n    37\u2192\t// reasonable Terraform configuration.\n    38\u2192Have:\n    39\u2192\tfor key, inst := range rs.Instances {\n    40\u2192\t\t// Instances which have no current objects (only one or more\n    41\u2192\t\t// deposed objects) will be taken care of separately\n    42\u2192\t\tif inst.Current == nil {\n    43\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    44\u2192\t\t}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\t\tthisAddr := rs.Addr.Instance(key)\n    47\u2192\t\tfor _, wantAddr := range t.InstanceAddrs {\n    48\u2192\t\t\tif wantAddr.Equal(thisAddr) {\n    49\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue Have\n    50\u2192\t\t\t}\n    51\u2192\t\t}\n    52\u2192\t\t// If thisAddr is not in t.InstanceAddrs then we've found an \"orphan\"\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(thisAddr)\n    55\u2192\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n    56\u2192\t\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\n    57\u2192\t\t\tnode = f(abstract)\n    58\u2192\t\t}\n    59\u2192\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer: adding %s as %T\", thisAddr, node)\n    60\u2192\t\tg.Add(node)\n    61\u2192\t}\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192\treturn nil\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
24. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_plan_orphan_test.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"testing\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    17\u2192)\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192func TestNodeResourcePlanOrphanExecute(t *testing.T) {\n    20\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n    21\u2192\tstate.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n    22\u2192\t\taddrs.Resource{\n    23\u2192\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n    24\u2192\t\t\tType: \"test_object\",\n    25\u2192\t\t\tName: \"foo\",\n    26\u2192\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey),\n    27\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    28\u2192\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n    29\u2192\t\t\t\t\"test_string\": \"foo\",\n    30\u2192\t\t\t},\n    31\u2192\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n    32\u2192\t\t},\n    33\u2192\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n    34\u2192\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n    35\u2192\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n    36\u2192\t\t},\n    37\u2192\t)\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192\tp := simpleMockProvider()\n    40\u2192\tp.ConfigureProvider(providers.ConfigureProviderRequest{})\n    41\u2192\tctx := &MockEvalContext{\n    42\u2192\t\tStateState:               state.SyncWrapper(),\n    43\u2192\t\tRefreshStateState:        state.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper(),\n    44\u2192\t\tPrevRunStateState:        state.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper(),\n    45\u2192\t\tInstanceExpanderExpander: instances.NewExpander(nil),\n    46\u2192\t\tProviderProvider:         p,\n    47\u2192\t\tProviderSchemaSchema: providers.ProviderSchema{\n    48\u2192\t\t\tResourceTypes: map[string]providers.Schema{\n    49\u2192\t\t\t\t\"test_object\": {\n    50\u2192\t\t\t\t\tBlock: simpleTestSchema(),\n    51\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    52\u2192\t\t\t},\n    53\u2192\t\t},\n    54\u2192\t\tChangesChanges: plans.NewChanges().SyncWrapper(),\n    55\u2192\t\tDeferralsState: deferring.NewDeferred(false),\n    56\u2192\t}\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192\tnode := NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\n    59\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n    60\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n    61\u2192\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n    62\u2192\t\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n    63\u2192\t\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n    64\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    65\u2192\t\t\t},\n    66\u2192\t\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.foo\"),\n    67\u2192\t\t},\n    68\u2192\t}\n    69\u2192\tdiags := node.Execute(ctx, walkApply)\n    70\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    71\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", diags.Err())\n    72\u2192\t}\n    73\u2192\tif !state.Empty() {\n    74\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected empty state, got %s\", state.String())\n    75\u2192\t}\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192func TestNodeResourcePlanOrphanExecute_alreadyDeleted(t *testing.T) {\n    79\u2192\taddr := addrs.Resource{\n    80\u2192\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n    81\u2192\t\tType: \"test_object\",\n    82\u2192\t\tName: \"foo\",\n    83\u2192\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n    86\u2192\tstate.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n    87\u2192\t\taddr.Resource,\n    88\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    89\u2192\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n    90\u2192\t\t\t\t\"test_string\": \"foo\",\n    91\u2192\t\t\t},\n    92\u2192\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n    93\u2192\t\t},\n    94\u2192\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n    95\u2192\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n    96\u2192\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n    97\u2192\t\t},\n    98\u2192\t)\n    99\u2192\trefreshState := state.DeepCopy()\n   100\u2192\tprevRunState := state.DeepCopy()\n   101\u2192\tchanges := plans.NewChanges()\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192\tp := simpleMockProvider()\n   104\u2192\tp.ConfigureProvider(providers.ConfigureProviderRequest{})\n   105\u2192\tp.ReadResourceResponse = &providers.ReadResourceResponse{\n   106\u2192\t\tNewState: cty.NullVal(p.GetProviderSchemaResponse.ResourceTypes[\"test_string\"].Block.ImpliedType()),\n   107\u2192\t}\n   108\u2192\tctx := &MockEvalContext{\n   109\u2192\t\tStateState:               state.SyncWrapper(),\n   110\u2192\t\tRefreshStateState:        refreshState.SyncWrapper(),\n   111\u2192\t\tPrevRunStateState:        prevRunState.SyncWrapper(),\n   112\u2192\t\tInstanceExpanderExpander: instances.NewExpander(nil),\n   113\u2192\t\tProviderProvider:         p,\n   114\u2192\t\tProviderSchemaSchema: providers.ProviderSchema{\n   115\u2192\t\t\tResourceTypes: map[string]providers.Schema{\n   116\u2192\t\t\t\t\"test_object\": {\n   117\u2192\t\t\t\t\tBlock: simpleTestSchema(),\n   118\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   119\u2192\t\t\t},\n   120\u2192\t\t},\n   121\u2192\t\tChangesChanges: changes.SyncWrapper(),\n   122\u2192\t\tDeferralsState: deferring.NewDeferred(false),\n   123\u2192\t}\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192\tnode := NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\n   126\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n   127\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n   128\u2192\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   129\u2192\t\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n   130\u2192\t\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n   131\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   132\u2192\t\t\t},\n   133\u2192\t\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.foo\"),\n   134\u2192\t\t},\n   135\u2192\t}\n   136\u2192\tdiags := node.Execute(ctx, walkPlan)\n   137\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   138\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", diags.Err())\n   139\u2192\t}\n   140\u2192\tif !state.Empty() {\n
