7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"strings\"\n     9\u2192\t\"testing\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    14\u2192)\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192func TestOrphanResourceInstanceTransformer(t *testing.T) {\n    17\u2192\tmod := testModule(t, \"transform-orphan-basic\")\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192\tstate := states.BuildState(func(s *states.SyncState) {\n    20\u2192\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n    21\u2192\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n    22\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n    23\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n    24\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"web\",\n    25\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n    26\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    27\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n    28\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n    29\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    30\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n    31\u2192\t\t\t},\n    32\u2192\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n    33\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n    34\u2192\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n    35\u2192\t\t\t},\n    36\u2192\t\t)\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192\t\t// The orphan\n    39\u2192\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n    40\u2192\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n    41\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n    42\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n    43\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"db\",\n    44\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n    45\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    46\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n    47\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n    48\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    49\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n    50\u2192\t\t\t},\n    51\u2192\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n    52\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n    53\u2192\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n    54\u2192\t\t\t},\n    55\u2192\t\t)\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192\t\t// A deposed orphan should not be handled by this transformer\n    58\u2192\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceDeposed(\n    59\u2192\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n    60\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n    61\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"test_instance\",\n    62\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"deposed\",\n    63\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n    64\u2192\t\t\tstates.NewDeposedKey(),\n    65\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    66\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n    67\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n    68\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    69\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n    70\u2192\t\t\t},\n    71\u2192\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n    72\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n    73\u2192\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n    74\u2192\t\t\t},\n    75\u2192\t\t)\n    76\u2192\t})\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n    79\u2192\t{\n    80\u2192\t\ttf := &ConfigTransformer{Config: mod}\n    81\u2192\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n    82\u2192\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n    83\u2192\t\t}\n    84\u2192\t}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192\t{\n    87\u2192\t\ttf := &OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{\n    88\u2192\t\t\tConcrete: testOrphanResourceConcreteFunc,\n    89\u2192\t\t\tState:    state,\n    90\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   mod,\n    91\u2192\t\t}\n    92\u2192\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n    93\u2192\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n    94\u2192\t\t}\n    95\u2192\t}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n    98\u2192\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(testTransformOrphanResourceBasicStr)\n    99\u2192\tif actual != expected {\n   100\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"bad:\\n\\n%s\", actual)\n   101\u2192\t}\n   102\u2192}\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192func TestOrphanResourceInstanceTransformer_countGood(t *testing.T) {\n   105\u2192\tmod := testModule(t, \"transform-orphan-count\")\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192\tstate := states.BuildState(func(s *states.SyncState) {\n   108\u2192\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   109\u2192\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n   110\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n   111\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n   112\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"foo\",\n   113\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.IntKey(0)).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n   114\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   115\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n   116\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n   117\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   118\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n   119\u2192\t\t\t},\n   120\u2192\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   121\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n   122\u2192\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n   123\u2192\t\t\t},\n   124\u2192\t\t)\n   125\u2192\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   126\u2192\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n   127\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n   128\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n   129\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"foo\",\n   130\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.IntKey(1)).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n   131\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   132\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n   133\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n   134\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   135\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n   136\u2192\t\t\t},\n   137\u2192\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   138\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n   139\u2192\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n   140\u2192\t\t\t},\n   141\u2192\t\t)\n   142\u2192\t})\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   145\u2192\t{\n   146\u2192\t\ttf := &ConfigTransformer{Config: mod}\n   147\u2192\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   148\u2192\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   149\u2192\t\t}\n   150\u2192\t}\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192\t{\n   153\u2192\t\ttf := &OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{\n   154\u2192\t\t\tConcrete: testOrphanResourceConcreteFunc,\n   155\u2192\t\t\tState:    state,\n   156\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   mod,\n   157\u2192\t\t}\n   158\u2192\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   159\u2192\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   160\u2192\t\t}\n   161\u2192\t}\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n   164\u2192\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(testTransformOrphanResourceCountStr)\n   165\u2192\tif actual != expected {\n   166\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"bad:\\n\\n%s\", actual)\n   167\u2192\t}\n   168\u2192}\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192func TestOrphanResourceInstanceTransformer_countBad(t *testing.T) {\n   171\u2192\tmod := testModule(t, \"transform-orphan-count-empty\")\n   172\u2192\tstate := states.BuildState(func(s *states.SyncState) {\n   173\u2192\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   174\u2192\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n   175\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n   176\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n   177\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"foo\",\n   178\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.IntKey(0)).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n   179\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   180\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n   181\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n   182\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   183\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n   184\u2192\t\t\t},\n   185\u2192\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   186\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n   187\u2192\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n   188\u2192\t\t\t},\n   189\u2192\t\t)\n   190\u2192\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   191\u2192\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n   192\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n   193\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n   194\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"foo\",\n   195\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.IntKey(1)).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n   196\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   197\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n   198\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n   199\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   200\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n   201\u2192\t\t\t},\n   202\u2192\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   203\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n   204\u2192\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n   205\u2192\t\t\t},\n   206\u2192\t\t)\n   207\u2192\t})\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   210\u2192\t{\n   211\u2192\t\ttf := &ConfigTransformer{Config: mod}\n   212\u2192\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   213\u2192\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   214\u2192\t\t}\n   215\u2192\t}\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192\t{\n   218\u2192\t\ttf := &OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{\n   219\u2192\t\t\tConcrete: testOrphanResourceConcreteFunc,\n   220\u2192\t\t\tState:    state,\n   221\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   mod,\n   222\u2192\t\t}\n   223\u2192\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   224\u2192\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   225\u2192\t\t}\n   226\u2192\t}\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n   229\u2192\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(testTransformOrphanResourceCountBadStr)\n   230\u2192\tif actual != expected {\n   231\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"bad:\\n\\n%s\", actual)\n   232\u2192\t}\n   233\u2192}\n   234\u2192\n   235\u2192func TestOrphanResourceInstanceTransformer_modules(t *testing.T) {\n   236\u2192\tmod := testModule(t, \"transform-orphan-modules\")\n   237\u2192\tstate := states.BuildState(func(s *states.SyncState) {\n   238\u2192\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   239\u2192\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n   240\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n   241\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n   242\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"foo\",\n   243\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n   244\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   245\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n   246\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n   247\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   248\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n   249\u2192\t\t\t},\n   250\u2192\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   251\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n   252\u2192\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n   253\u2192\t\t\t},\n   254\u2192\t\t)\n   255\u2192\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   256\u2192\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n   257\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n   258\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n   259\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"web\",\n   260\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance.Child(\"child\", addrs.NoKey)),\n   261\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   262\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n   263\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n   264\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   265\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n   266\u2192\t\t\t},\n   267\u2192\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   268\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n   269\u2192\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n   270\u2192\t\t\t},\n   271\u2192\t\t)\n   272\u2192\t})\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   275\u2192\t{\n   276\u2192\t\ttf := &ConfigTransformer{Config: mod}\n   277\u2192\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   278\u2192\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   279\u2192\t\t}\n   280\u2192\t}\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192\t{\n   283\u2192\t\ttf := &OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{\n   284\u2192\t\t\tConcrete: testOrphanResourceConcreteFunc,\n   285\u2192\t\t\tState:    state,\n   286\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   mod,\n   287\u2192\t\t}\n   288\u2192\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   289\u2192\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   290\u2192\t\t}\n   291\u2192\t}\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192\tgot := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n   294\u2192\twant := strings.TrimSpace(testTransformOrphanResourceModulesStr)\n   295\u2192\tif got != want {\n   296\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong state result\\ngot:\\n%s\\n\\nwant:\\n%s\", got, want)\n   297\u2192\t}\n   298\u2192}\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192const testTransformOrphanResourceBasicStr = `\n   301\u2192aws_instance.db (orphan)\n   302\u2192aws_instance.web\n   303\u2192`\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192const testTransformOrphanResourceCountStr = `\n   306\u2192aws_instance.foo\n   307\u2192`\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192const testTransformOrphanResourceCountBadStr = `\n   310\u2192aws_instance.foo[0] (orphan)\n   311\u2192aws_instance.foo[1] (orphan)\n   312\u2192`\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192const testTransformOrphanResourceModulesStr = `\n   315\u2192aws_instance.foo\n   316\u2192module.child.aws_instance.web (orphan)\n   317\u2192`\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192func testOrphanResourceConcreteFunc(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n   320\u2192\treturn &testOrphanResourceInstanceConcrete{a}\n   321\u2192}\n   322\u2192\n   323\u2192type testOrphanResourceInstanceConcrete struct {\n   324\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n   325\u2192}\n   326\u2192\n   327\u2192func (n *testOrphanResourceInstanceConcrete) Name() string {\n   328\u2192\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (orphan)\", n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.Name())\n   329\u2192}\n   330\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
28. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_apply_deferred.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    13\u2192)\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192// nodeApplyableDeferredInstance is a node that represents a deferred instance\n    16\u2192// in the apply graph. This node is targetable and helps maintain the correct\n    17\u2192// ordering of the apply graph.\n    18\u2192//\n    19\u2192// When executed during the apply phase, this transfers the planned change we\n    20\u2192// got from the plan's deferrals *back* into the EvalContext's Deferred struct,\n    21\u2192// so downstream references to deferred objects can get partial values (of\n    22\u2192// varying quality).\n    23\u2192type nodeApplyableDeferredInstance struct {\n    24\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192\tReason    providers.DeferredReason\n    27\u2192\tChangeSrc *plans.ResourceInstanceChangeSrc\n    28\u2192}\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192func (n *nodeApplyableDeferredInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, _ walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n    31\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    32\u2192\tchange, err := n.ChangeSrc.Decode(n.Schema.ImpliedType())\n    33\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    34\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(tfdiags.Error, \"Failed to decode \", fmt.Sprintf(\"Terraform failed to decode a deferred change: %v\\n\\nThis is a bug in Terraform; please report it!\", err)))\n    35\u2192\t}\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192\tswitch n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {\n    38\u2192\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n    39\u2192\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, n.Reason, change)\n    40\u2192\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n    41\u2192\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportDataSourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, n.Reason, change)\n    42\u2192\t}\n    43\u2192\treturn diags\n    44\u2192}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192// nodeApplyableDeferredPartialInstance is a node that represents a deferred\n    47\u2192// partial instance in the apply graph. This simply adds a method  to get the\n    48\u2192// partial address on top of the regular behaviour of\n    49\u2192// nodeApplyableDeferredInstance.\n    50\u2192type nodeApplyableDeferredPartialInstance struct {\n    51\u2192\t*nodeApplyableDeferredInstance\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\tPartialAddr addrs.PartialExpandedResource\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192func (n *nodeApplyableDeferredPartialInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, _ walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n    57\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    58\u2192\tchange, err := n.ChangeSrc.Decode(n.Schema.ImpliedType())\n    59\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    60\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(tfdiags.Error, \"Failed to decode \", fmt.Sprintf(\"Terraform failed to decode a deferred change: %v\\n\\nThis is a bug in Terraform; please report it!\", err)))\n    61\u2192\t}\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192\tswitch n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {\n    64\u2192\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n    65\u2192\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceExpansionDeferred(n.PartialAddr, change)\n    66\u2192\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n    67\u2192\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportDataSourceExpansionDeferred(n.PartialAddr, change)\n    68\u2192\t}\n    69\u2192\treturn diags\n    70\u2192}\n    71\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
29. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/langrefs\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    17\u2192)\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192// ConcreteResourceNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert an\n    20\u2192// abstract resource to a concrete one of some type.\n    21\u2192type ConcreteResourceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192// GraphNodeConfigResource is implemented by any nodes that represent a resource.\n    24\u2192// The type of operation cannot be assumed, only that this node represents\n    25\u2192// the given resource.\n    26\u2192type GraphNodeConfigResource interface {\n    27\u2192\tResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource\n    28\u2192}\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192// ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert an\n    31\u2192// abstract resource instance to a concrete one of some type.\n    32\u2192type ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192// GraphNodeResourceInstance is implemented by any nodes that represent\n    35\u2192// a resource instance. A single resource may have multiple instances if,\n    36\u2192// for example, the \"count\" or \"for_each\" argument is used for it in\n    37\u2192// configuration.\n    38\u2192type GraphNodeResourceInstance interface {\n    39\u2192\tResourceInstanceAddr() addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192\t// StateDependencies returns any inter-resource dependencies that are\n    42\u2192\t// stored in the state.\n    43\u2192\tStateDependencies() []addrs.ConfigResource\n    44\u2192}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192// NodeAbstractResource represents a resource that has no associated\n    47\u2192// operations. It registers all the interfaces for a resource that common\n    48\u2192// across multiple operation types.\n    49\u2192type NodeAbstractResource struct {\n    50\u2192\tAddr addrs.ConfigResource\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192\t// The fields below will be automatically set using the Attach\n    53\u2192\t// interfaces if you're running those transforms, but also be explicitly\n    54\u2192\t// set if you already have that information.\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192\tSchema        *configschema.Block // Schema for processing the configuration body\n    57\u2192\tSchemaVersion uint64              // Schema version of \"Schema\", as decided by the provider\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192\t// Config and RemovedConfig are mutally-exclusive, because a\n    60\u2192\t// resource can't be both declared and removed at the same time.\n    61\u2192\tConfig        *configs.Resource // Config is the resource in the config, if any\n    62\u2192\tRemovedConfig *configs.Removed  // RemovedConfig is the \"removed\" block for this resource, if any\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192\t// ProviderMetas is the provider_meta configs for the module this resource belongs to\n    65\u2192\tProviderMetas map[addrs.Provider]*configs.ProviderMeta\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192\tProvisionerSchemas map[string]*configschema.Block\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192\t// Set from GraphNodeTargetable\n    70\u2192\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192\t// Set from AttachDataResourceDependsOn\n    73\u2192\tdependsOn []addrs.ConfigResource\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192\t// The address of the provider this resource will use\n    76\u2192\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n    77\u2192\t// storedProviderConfig is the provider address retrieved from the\n    78\u2192\t// state. This is defined here for access within the ProvidedBy method, but\n    79\u2192\t// will be set from the embedding instance type when the state is attached.\n    80\u2192\tstoredProviderConfig addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192\t// This resource may expand into instances which need to be imported.\n    83\u2192\timportTargets []*ImportTarget\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192\t// generateConfigPath tells this node which file to write generated config\n    86\u2192\t// into. If empty, then config should not be generated.\n    87\u2192\tgenerateConfigPath string\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192\tforceCreateBeforeDestroy bool\n    90\u2192}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192var (\n    93\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable               = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n    94\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer                  = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n    95\u2192\t_ GraphNodeImportReferencer            = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n    96\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer            = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n    97\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer         = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n    98\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource              = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n    99\u2192\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig        = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n   100\u2192\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceSchema        = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
30. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_plan_partialexp.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/marks\"\n    17\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    18\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange\"\n    19\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    20\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    21\u2192)\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192// nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource is a graph node that stands in for\n    24\u2192// an unbounded set of potential resource instances that we don't yet know.\n    25\u2192//\n    26\u2192// Its job is to check the configuration as much as we can with the information\n    27\u2192// that's available (so we can raise an error early if something is clearly\n    28\u2192// wrong across _all_ potential instances) and to record a placeholder value\n    29\u2192// for use when evaluating other objects that refer to this resource.\n    30\u2192//\n    31\u2192// This is the partial-expanded equivalent of NodePlannableResourceInstance.\n    32\u2192type nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource struct {\n    33\u2192\taddr             addrs.PartialExpandedResource\n    34\u2192\tconfig           *configs.Resource\n    35\u2192\tresolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n    36\u2192\tskipPlanChanges  bool\n    37\u2192}\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192var (\n    40\u2192\t_ graphNodeEvalContextScope = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)\n    41\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource   = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)\n    42\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable       = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)\n    43\u2192)\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192// Name implements [dag.NamedVertex].\n    46\u2192func (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) Name() string {\n    47\u2192\treturn n.addr.String()\n    48\u2192}\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192// Path implements graphNodeEvalContextScope.\n    51\u2192func (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) Path() evalContextScope {\n    52\u2192\tif moduleAddr, ok := n.addr.ModuleInstance(); ok {\n    53\u2192\t\treturn evalContextModuleInstance{Addr: moduleAddr}\n    54\u2192\t} else if moduleAddr, ok := n.addr.PartialExpandedModule(); ok {\n    55\u2192\t\treturn evalContextPartialExpandedModule{Addr: moduleAddr}\n    56\u2192\t} else {\n    57\u2192\t\t// Should not get here: at least one of the two cases above\n    58\u2192\t\t// should always be true for any valid addrs.PartialExpandedResource\n    59\u2192\t\tpanic(\"addrs.PartialExpandedResource has neither a partial-expanded or a fully-expanded module instance address\")\n    60\u2192\t}\n    61\u2192}\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192// ResourceAddr implements GraphNodeConfigResource.\n    64\u2192func (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) ResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource {\n    65\u2192\treturn n.addr.ConfigResource()\n    66\u2192}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192// Execute implements GraphNodeExecutable.\n    69\u2192func (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n    70\u2192\t// Because this node type implements [graphNodeEvalContextScope], the\n    71\u2192\t// given EvalContext could either be for a fully-expanded module instance\n    72\u2192\t// or an unbounded set of potential module instances sharing a common\n    73\u2192\t// prefix. The logic here doesn't need to vary between the two because\n    74\u2192\t// the differences are encapsulated in the EvalContext abstraction,\n    75\u2192\t// but if you're unsure which of the two is being used then look for\n    76\u2192\t// the following line in the logs to see if there's a [*] marker on\n    77\u2192\t// any of the module instance steps, or if the [*] is applied only to\n    78\u2192\t// the resource itself.\n    79\u2192\t//\n    80\u2192\t// Fully-expanded module example:\n    81\u2192\t//\n    82\u2192\t//     module.foo[\"a\"].type.name[*]\n    83\u2192\t//\n    84\u2192\t// Partial-expanded module example:\n    85\u2192\t//\n    86\u2192\t//     module.foo[*].type.name[*]\n    87\u2192\t//\n    88\u2192\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource: checking all of %s\", n.addr.String())\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    91\u2192\tswitch n.addr.Resource().Mode {\n    92\u2192\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n    93\u2192\t\tchange, changeDiags := n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\n    94\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(changeDiags)\n    95\u2192\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceExpansionDeferred(n.addr, change)\n    96\u2192\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n    97\u2192\t\tchange, changeDiags := n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\n    98\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(changeDiags)\n    99\u2192\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportDataSourceExpansionDeferred(n.addr, change)\n   100\u2192\tcase addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_partial_plan.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    13\u2192)\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192// This file is a temporary split from the node_resource_plan.go file. We handle\n    16\u2192// the unknown instances branch of execution within here, while this is still\n    17\u2192// being developed.\n    18\u2192//\n    19\u2192// We have split the files to make structuring the code easier, eventually once\n    20\u2192// the functions within this file are production ready, we will merge them back\n    21\u2192// into the node_resource_plan.go file.\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192// dynamicExpandPartial is a variant of dynamicExpand that we use when deferred\n    24\u2192// actions are enabled for the current plan.\n    25\u2192//\n    26\u2192// Once deferred actions are more stable and robust in the stacks runtime, it\n    27\u2192// would be nice to integrate this logic a little better with the main\n    28\u2192// DynamicExpand logic, but it's separate for now to minimize the risk of\n    29\u2192// stacks-specific behavior impacting configurations that are not opted into it.\n    30\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpandPartial(ctx EvalContext, knownModules []addrs.ModuleInstance, partialModules addrs.Set[addrs.PartialExpandedModule], knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    31\u2192\tvar g Graph\n    32\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192\tknownResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()\n    35\u2192\tpartialResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.PartialExpandedResource]()\n    36\u2192\tmaybeOrphanResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192\tfor _, moduleAddr := range knownModules {\n    39\u2192\t\tresourceAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(moduleAddr)\n    40\u2192\t\tresources, partials, maybeOrphans, moreDiags := n.expandKnownModule(ctx, resourceAddr, knownImports, unknownImports, &g)\n    41\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192\t\t// Track all the resources we know about.\n    44\u2192\t\tknownResources = knownResources.Union(resources)\n    45\u2192\t\tpartialResources = partialResources.Union(partials)\n    46\u2192\t\tmaybeOrphanResources = maybeOrphanResources.Union(maybeOrphans)\n    47\u2192\t}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192\tfor _, moduleAddr := range partialModules {\n    50\u2192\t\tresourceAddr := moduleAddr.Resource(n.Addr.Resource)\n    51\u2192\t\tpartialResources.Add(resourceAddr)\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\t\t// And add a node to the graph for this resource.\n    54\u2192\t\tg.Add(&nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource{\n    55\u2192\t\t\taddr:             resourceAddr,\n    56\u2192\t\t\tconfig:           n.Config,\n    57\u2192\t\t\tresolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n    58\u2192\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:  n.skipPlanChanges,\n    59\u2192\t\t})\n    60\u2192\t}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192\tfunc() {\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192\t\tss := ctx.PrevRunState()\n    65\u2192\t\tif ss == nil {\n    66\u2192\t\t\treturn // No previous state, so nothing to do here.\n    67\u2192\t\t}\n    68\u2192\t\tstate := ss.Lock()\n    69\u2192\t\tdefer ss.Unlock()\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192\tResources:\n    72\u2192\t\tfor _, res := range state.Resources(n.Addr) {\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192\t\t\tfor _, knownModule := range knownModules {\n    75\u2192\t\t\t\tif knownModule.Equal(res.Addr.Module) {\n    76\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// Then we handled this resource as part of the known\n    77\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// modules processing.\n    78\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcontinue Resources\n    79\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n    80\u2192\t\t\t}\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192\t\t\tfor _, partialResource := range partialResources {\n    83\u2192\t\t\t\tif partialResource.MatchesResource(res.Addr) {\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192\t\t\t\t\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n    86\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t// Then each of the instances is a \"maybe orphan\"\n    87\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t// instance, and we need to add a node for that.\n    88\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tmaybeOrphanResources.Add(res.Addr.Instance(key))\n    89\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tg.Add(n.concreteResource(ctx, addrs.MakeMap[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value](), addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]](), true)(NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(res.Addr.Instance(key))))\n    90\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// Move onto the next resource.\n    93\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcontinue Resources\n    94\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n    95\u2192\t\t\t}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192\t\t\t// Otherwise, everything in here is just a simple orphaned instance.\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192\t\t\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n   100\u2192\t\t\t\tinst := res.Addr.Instance(key)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

{\n    22\u2192\t\tAddr                 addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n    23\u2192\t\tConfig               *configs.Resource\n    24\u2192\t\tStoredProviderConfig addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n    25\u2192\t\tWant                 addrs.Provider\n    26\u2192\t}{\n    27\u2192\t\t{\n    28\u2192\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n    29\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n    30\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n    31\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n    32\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n    33\u2192\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n    34\u2192\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n    35\u2192\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"hashicorp\",\n    36\u2192\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n    37\u2192\t\t\t},\n    38\u2192\t\t},\n    39\u2192\t\t{\n    40\u2192\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n    41\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n    42\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"terraform_remote_state\",\n    43\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n    44\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n    45\u2192\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n    46\u2192\t\t\t\t// As a special case, the type prefix \"terraform_\" maps to\n    47\u2192\t\t\t\t// the builtin provider, not the default one.\n    48\u2192\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.BuiltInProviderHost,\n    49\u2192\t\t\t\tNamespace: addrs.BuiltInProviderNamespace,\n    50\u2192\t\t\t\tType:      \"terraform\",\n    51\u2192\t\t\t},\n    52\u2192\t\t},\n    53\u2192\t\t{\n    54\u2192\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n    55\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n    56\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n    57\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n    58\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n    59\u2192\t\t\tConfig: &configs.Resource{\n    60\u2192\t\t\t\t// Just enough configs.Resource for the Provider method. Not\n    61\u2192\t\t\t\t// actually valid for general use.\n    62\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n    63\u2192\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n    64\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n    65\u2192\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n    66\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    67\u2192\t\t\t},\n    68\u2192\t\t\t// The config overrides the default behavior.\n    69\u2192\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n    70\u2192\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n    71\u2192\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n    72\u2192\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n    73\u2192\t\t\t},\n    74\u2192\t\t},\n    75\u2192\t\t{\n    76\u2192\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n    77\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n    78\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"terraform_remote_state\",\n    79\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n    80\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n    81\u2192\t\t\tConfig: &configs.Resource{\n    82\u2192\t\t\t\t// Just enough configs.Resource for the Provider method. Not\n    83\u2192\t\t\t\t// actually valid for general use.\n    84\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n    85\u2192\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n    86\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n    87\u2192\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n    88\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    89\u2192\t\t\t},\n    90\u2192\t\t\t// The config overrides the default behavior.\n    91\u2192\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n    92\u2192\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n    93\u2192\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n    94\u2192\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n    95\u2192\t\t\t},\n    96\u2192\t\t},\n    97\u2192\t\t{\n    98\u2192\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n    99\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n   100\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n   101\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n   102\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n   103\u2192\t\t\tConfig: nil,\n   104\u2192\t\t\tStoredProviderConfig: addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   105\u2192\t\t\t\tModule: addrs.RootModule,\n   106\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n   107\u2192\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n   108\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n   109\u2192\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n   110\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   111\u2192\t\t\t},\n   112\u2192\t\t\t// The stored provider config overrides the default behavior.\n   113\u2192\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n   114\u2192\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n   115\u2192\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n   116\u2192\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n   117\u2192\t\t\t},\n   118\u2192\t\t},\n   119\u2192\t}\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192\tfor _, test := range tests {\n   122\u2192\t\tvar name string\n   123\u2192\t\tif test.Config != nil {\n   124\u2192\t\t\tname = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s with configured %s\", test.Addr, test.Config.Provider)\n   125\u2192\t\t} else {\n   126\u2192\t\t\tname = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s with no configuration\", test.Addr)\n   127\u2192\t\t}\n   128\u2192\t\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\n   129\u2192\t\t\tnode := &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n   130\u2192\t\t\t\t// Just enough NodeAbstractResourceInstance for the Provider\n   131\u2192\t\t\t\t// function. (This would not be valid for some other functions.)\n   132\u2192\t\t\t\tAddr: test.Addr,\n   133\u2192\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n   134\u2192\t\t\t\t\tAddr:                 test.Addr.ConfigResource(),\n   135\u2192\t\t\t\t\tConfig:               test.Config,\n   136\u2192\t\t\t\t\tstoredProviderConfig: test.StoredProviderConfig,\n   137\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   138\u2192\t\t\t}\n   139\u2192\t\t\tgot := node.Provider()\n   140\u2192\t\t\tif got != test.Want {\n   141\u2192\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"wrong result\\naddr:  %s\\nconfig: %#v\\ngot:   %s\\nwant:  %s\", test.Addr, test.Config, got, test.Want)\n   142\u2192\t\t\t}\n   143\u2192\t\t})\n   144\u2192\t}\n   145\u2192}\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192func TestNodeAbstractResourceInstance_WriteResourceInstanceState(t *testing.T) {\n   148\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n   149\u2192\tctx := new(MockEvalContext)\n   150\u2192\tctx.StateState = state.SyncWrapper()\n   151\u2192\tctx.Scope = evalContextModuleInstance{Addr: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192\tmockProvider := mockProviderWithResourceTypeSchema(\"aws_instance\", &configschema.Block{\n   154\u2192\t\tAttributes: map[string]*configschema.Attribute{\n   155\u2192\t\t\t\"id\": {\n   156\u2192\t\t\t\tType:     cty.String,\n   157\u2192\t\t\t\tOptional: true,\n   158\u2192\t\t\t},\n   159\u2192\t\t},\n   160\u2192\t})\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192\tobj := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n   163\u2192\t\tValue: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n   164\u2192\t\t\t\"id\": cty.StringVal(\"i-abc123\"),\n   165\u2192\t\t}),\n   166\u2192\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n   167\u2192\t}\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192\tnode := &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n   170\u2192\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"aws_instance.foo\"),\n   171\u2192\t\t// instanceState:        obj,\n   172\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n   173\u2192\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws\"]`),\n   174\u2192\t\t},\n   175\u2192\t}\n   176\u2192\tctx.ProviderProvider = mockProvider\n   177\u2192\tctx.ProviderSchemaSchema = mockProvider.GetProviderSchema()\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192\terr := node.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, obj, workingState)\n   180\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   181\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", err.Error())\n   182\u2192\t}\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192\tcheckStateString(t, state, `\n   185\u2192aws_instance.foo:\n   186\u2192  ID = i-abc123\n   187\u2192  provider = provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws\"]\n   188\u2192\t`)\n   189\u2192}\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192func TestNodeAbstractResourceInstance_refresh_with_deferred_read(t *testing.T) {\n   192\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n   193\u2192\tevalCtx := &MockEvalContext{}\n   194\u2192\tevalCtx.StateState = state.SyncWrapper()\n   195\u2192\tevalCtx.Scope = evalContextModuleInstance{Addr: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192\tmockProvider := mockProviderWithResourceTypeSchema(\"aws_instance\", &configschema.Block{\n   198\u2192\t\tAttributes: map[string]*configschema.Attribute{\n   199\u2192\t\t\t\"id\": {\n   200\u2192\t\t\t\tType:     cty.String,\n   201\u2192\t\t\t\tOptional: true,\n   202\u2192\t\t\t},\n   203\u2192\t\t},\n   204\u2192\t})\n   205\u2192\tmockProvider.ConfigureProviderCalled = true\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192\tmockProvider.ReadResourceFn = func(providers.ReadResourceRequest) providers.ReadResourceResponse {\n   208\u2192\t\treturn providers.ReadResourceResponse{\n   209\u2192\t\t\tNewState: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n   210\u2192\t\t\t\t\"id\": cty.UnknownVal(cty.String),\n   211\u2192\t\t\t}),\n   212\u2192\t\t\tDeferred: &providers.Deferred{\n   213\u2192\t\t\t\tReason: providers.DeferredReasonAbsentPrereq,\n   214\u2192\t\t\t},\n   215\u2192\t\t}\n   216\u2192\t}\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192\tobj := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n   219\u2192\t\tValue: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n   220\u2192\t\t\t\"id\": cty.StringVal(\"i-abc123\"),\n   221\u2192\t\t}),\n   222\u2192\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n   223\u2192\t}\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192\tnode := &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n   226\u2192\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"aws_instance.foo\"),\n   227\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n   228\u2192\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws\"]`),\n   229\u2192\t\t},\n   230\u2192\t}\n   231\u2192\tevalCtx.ProviderProvider = mockProvider\n   232\u2192\tevalCtx.ProviderSchemaSchema = mockProvider.GetProviderSchema()\n   233\u2192\tevalCtx.DeferralsState = deferring.NewDeferred(true)\n   234\u2192\n   235\u2192\trio, deferred, diags := node.refresh(evalCtx, states.NotDeposed, obj, true)\n   236\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   237\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(diags.Err())\n   238\u2192\t}\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192\tvalue := rio.Value\n   241\u2192\tif value.IsWhollyKnown() {\n   242\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"value was known: %v\", value)\n   243\u2192\t}\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192\tif deferred == nil {\n   246\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected deferral to be present\")\n   247\u2192\t}\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192\tif deferred.Reason != providers.DeferredReasonAbsentPrereq {\n   250\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected deferral to be AbsentPrereq, got %s\", deferred.Reason)\n   251\u2192\t}\n   252\u2192}\n   253\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
40. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_orphan_resource.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"log\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    12\u2192)\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192// OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds orphaned\n    15\u2192// resource instances to the graph. An \"orphan\" is an instance that is present\n    16\u2192// in the state but belongs to a resource that is no longer present in the\n    17\u2192// configuration.\n    18\u2192//\n    19\u2192// This is not the transformer that deals with \"count orphans\" (instances that\n    20\u2192// are no longer covered by a resource's \"count\" or \"for_each\" setting); that's\n    21\u2192// handled instead by OrphanResourceCountTransformer.\n    22\u2192type OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer struct {\n    23\u2192\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\t// State is the global state. We require the global state to\n    26\u2192\t// properly find module orphans at our path.\n    27\u2192\tState *states.State\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\t// Config is the root node in the configuration tree. We'll look up\n    30\u2192\t// the appropriate note in this tree using the path in each node.\n    31\u2192\tConfig *configs.Config\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192\t// Do not apply this transformer\n    34\u2192\tskip bool\n    35\u2192}\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192func (t *OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n    38\u2192\tif t.skip {\n    39\u2192\t\treturn nil\n    40\u2192\t}\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192\tif t.State == nil {\n    43\u2192\t\t// If the entire state is nil, there can't be any orphans\n    44\u2192\t\treturn nil\n    45\u2192\t}\n    46\u2192\tif t.Config == nil {\n    47\u2192\t\t// Should never happen: we can't be doing any Terraform operations\n    48\u2192\t\t// without at least an empty configuration.\n    49\u2192\t\tpanic(\"OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer used without setting Config\")\n    50\u2192\t}\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192\t// Go through the modules and for each module transform in order\n    53\u2192\t// to add the orphan.\n    54\u2192\tfor _, ms := range t.State.Modules {\n    55\u2192\t\tif err := t.transform(g, ms); err != nil {\n    56\u2192\t\t\treturn err\n    57\u2192\t\t}\n    58\u2192\t}\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192\treturn nil\n    61\u2192}\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192func (t *OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer) transform(g *Graph, ms *states.Module) error {\n    64\u2192\tif ms == nil {\n    65\u2192\t\treturn nil\n    66\u2192\t}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192\tmoduleAddr := ms.Addr\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192\t// Get the configuration for this module. The configuration might be\n    71\u2192\t// nil if the module was removed from the configuration. This is okay,\n    72\u2192\t// this just means that every resource is an orphan.\n    73\u2192\tvar m *configs.Module\n    74\u2192\tif c := t.Config.DescendantForInstance(moduleAddr); c != nil {\n    75\u2192\t\tm = c.Module\n    76\u2192\t}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192\t// An \"orphan\" is a resource that is in the state but not the configuration,\n    79\u2192\t// so we'll walk the state resources and try to correlate each of them\n    80\u2192\t// with a configuration block. Each orphan gets a node in the graph whose\n    81\u2192\t// type is decided by t.Concrete.\n    82\u2192\t//\n    83\u2192\t// We don't handle orphans related to changes in the \"count\" and \"for_each\"\n    84\u2192\t// pseudo-arguments here. They are handled by OrphanResourceCountTransformer.\n    85\u2192\tfor _, rs := range ms.Resources {\n    86\u2192\t\tif m != nil {\n    87\u2192\t\t\tif r := m.ResourceByAddr(rs.Addr.Resource); r != nil {\n    88\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n    89\u2192\t\t\t}\n    90\u2192\t\t}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192\t\tfor key, inst := range rs.Instances {\n    93\u2192\t\t\t// Instances which have no current objects (only one or more\n    94\u2192\t\t\t// deposed objects) will be taken care of separately\n    95\u2192\t\t\tif inst.Current == nil {\n    96\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n    97\u2192\t\t\t}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192\t\t\taddr := rs.Addr.Instance(key)\n   100\u2192\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n   101\u2192\t\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n   102\u2192\t\t\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\n   103\u2192\t\t\t\tnode = f(abstract)\n   104\u2192\t\t\t}\n   105\u2192\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer: adding single-instance orphan node for %s\", addr)\n   106\u2192\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n   107\u2192\t\t}\n   108\u2192\t}\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192\treturn nil\n   111\u2192}\n   112\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
41. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_import.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    14\u2192)\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192type graphNodeImportState struct {\n    17\u2192\tAddr             addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addr is the resource address to import into\n    18\u2192\tID               string                    // ID is the ID to import as\n    19\u2192\tProviderAddr     addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // Provider address given by the user, or implied by the resource type\n    20\u2192\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // provider node address after resolution\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192\tstates []providers.ImportedResource\n    23\u2192}\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192var (\n    26\u2192\t_ GraphNodeModulePath        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n    27\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n    28\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer  = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n    29\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n    30\u2192)\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) Name() string {\n    33\u2192\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (import id %q)\", n.Addr, n.ID)\n    34\u2192}\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\n    37\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {\n    38\u2192\t// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.\n    39\u2192\t// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState\n    40\u2192\t// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the\n    41\u2192\t// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type\n    42\u2192\t// name.\n    43\u2192\treturn n.ProviderAddr, false\n    44\u2192}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\n    47\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) Provider() addrs.Provider {\n    48\u2192\t// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.\n    49\u2192\t// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState\n    50\u2192\t// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the\n    51\u2192\t// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type\n    52\u2192\t// name.\n    53\u2192\treturn n.ProviderAddr.Provider\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\n    57\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) SetProvider(addr addrs.AbsProviderConfig) {\n    58\u2192\tn.ResolvedProvider = addr\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192// GraphNodeModuleInstance\n    62\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\n    63\u2192\treturn n.Addr.Module\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192// GraphNodeModulePath\n    67\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) ModulePath() addrs.Module {\n    68\u2192\treturn n.Addr.Module.Module()\n    69\u2192}\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192// GraphNodeExecutable impl.\n    72\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    73\u2192\t// Reset our states\n    74\u2192\tn.states = nil\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192\tprovider, _, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n    77\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n    78\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    79\u2192\t\treturn diags\n    80\u2192\t}\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192\t// import state\n    83\u2192\tabsAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(ctx.Path())\n    84\u2192\thookResourceID := HookResourceIdentity{\n    85\u2192\t\tAddr:         absAddr,\n    86\u2192\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\n    87\u2192\t}\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192\t// Call pre-import hook\n    90\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n    91\u2192\t\treturn h.PreImportState(hookResourceID, n.ID)\n    92\u2192\t}))\n    93\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    94\u2192\t\treturn diags\n    95\u2192\t}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192\tresp := provider.ImportResourceState(providers.ImportResourceStateRequest{\n    98\u2192\t\tTypeName: n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n    99\u2192\t\tID:       n.ID,\n   100\u2192\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance_test.go",
  "new_string": "func TestNodeResourceInstanceBaseProvider(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tAddr                 addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\t\tConfig               *configs.Resource\n\t\tStoredProviderConfig addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n\t\tWant                 addrs.Provider\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"hashicorp\",\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"terraform_remote_state\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\t// As a special case, the type prefix \"terraform_\" maps to\n\t\t\t\t// the builtin provider, not the default one.\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.BuiltInProviderHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: addrs.BuiltInProviderNamespace,\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"terraform\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tConfig: &configs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\t// Just enough configs.Resource for the Provider method. Not\n\t\t\t\t// actually valid for general use.\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t// The config overrides the default behavior.\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"terraform_remote_state\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tConfig: &configs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\t// Just enough configs.Resource for the Provider method. Not\n\t\t\t\t// actually valid for general use.\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t// The config overrides the default behavior.\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tConfig: nil,\n\t\t\tStoredProviderConfig: addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tModule: addrs.RootModule,\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t// The stored provider config overrides the default behavior.\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, test := range tests {\n\t\tvar name string\n\t\tif test.Config != nil {\n\t\t\tname = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s with configured %s\", test.Addr, test.Config.Provider)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tname = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s with no configuration\", test.Addr)\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tnode := &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n\t\t\t\t// Just enough NodeResourceInstanceBase for the Provider\n\t\t\t\t// function. (This would not be valid for some other functions.)\n\t\t\t\tAddr: test.Addr,\n\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\t\t\t\tAddr:                 test.Addr.ConfigResource(),\n\t\t\t\t\tConfig:               test.Config,\n\t\t\t\t\tstoredProviderConfig: test.StoredProviderConfig,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tgot := node.Provider()\n\t\t\tif got != test.Want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"wrong result\\naddr:  %s\\nconfig: %#v\\ngot:   %s\\nwant:  %s\", test.Addr, test.Config, got, test.Want)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}",
  "old_string": "func TestNodeAbstractResourceInstanceProvider(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tAddr                 addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\t\tConfig               *configs.Resource\n\t\tStoredProviderConfig addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n\t\tWant                 addrs.Provider\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"hashicorp\",\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"terraform_remote_state\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\t// As a special case, the type prefix \"terraform_\" maps to\n\t\t\t\t// the builtin provider, not the default one.\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.BuiltInProviderHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: addrs.BuiltInProviderNamespace,\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"terraform\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tConfig: &configs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\t// Just enough configs.Resource for the Provider method. Not\n\t\t\t\t// actually valid for general use.\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t// The config overrides the default behavior.\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"terraform_remote_state\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tConfig: &configs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\t// Just enough configs.Resource for the Provider method. Not\n\t\t\t\t// actually valid for general use.\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t// The config overrides the default behavior.\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tConfig: nil,\n\t\t\tStoredProviderConfig: addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tModule: addrs.RootModule,\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t// The stored provider config overrides the default behavior.\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, test := range tests {\n\t\tvar name string\n\t\tif test.Config != nil {\n\t\t\tname = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s with configured %s\", test.Addr, test.Config.Provider)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tname = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s with no configuration\", test.Addr)\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tnode := &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n\t\t\t\t// Just enough NodeResourceInstanceBase for the Provider\n\t\t\t\t// function. (This would not be valid for some other functions.)\n\t\t\t\tAddr: test.Addr,\n\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\t\t\t\tAddr:                 test.Addr.ConfigResource(),\n\t\t\t\t\tConfig:               test.Config,\n\t\t\t\t\tstoredProviderConfig: test.StoredProviderConfig,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tgot := node.Provider()\n\t\t\tif got != test.Want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"wrong result\\naddr:  %s\\nconfig: %#v\\ngot:   %s\\nwant:  %s\", test.Addr, test.Config, got, test.Want)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}",

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_destroy.go",
