Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    17\u2192)\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192// NodeDestroyResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is to be\n    20\u2192// destroyed.\n    21\u2192type NodeDestroyResourceInstance struct {\n    22\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n    23\u2192}\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192var (\n    26\u2192\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance      = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n    27\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource      = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n    28\u2192\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance    = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n    29\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer           = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n    30\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD        = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n    31\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable       = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n    32\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer          = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n    33\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable          = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n    34\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer    = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n    35\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n    36\u2192)\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) Name() string {\n    39\u2192\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String() + \" (destroy)\"\n    40\u2192}\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) ProvidedBy() (addr addrs.ProviderConfig, exact bool) {\n    43\u2192\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\n    44\u2192\t\t// indicate that this node does not require a configured provider\n    45\u2192\t\treturn nil, true\n    46\u2192\t}\n    47\u2192\treturn n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ProvidedBy()\n    48\u2192}\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192// GraphNodeDestroyer\n    51\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n    52\u2192\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n    53\u2192\treturn &addr\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\n    57\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\n    58\u2192\t// State takes precedence during destroy.\n    59\u2192\t// If the resource was removed, there is no config to check.\n    60\u2192\t// If CBD was forced from descendant, it should be saved in the state\n    61\u2192\t// already.\n    62\u2192\tif s := n.instanceState; s != nil {\n    63\u2192\t\tif s.Current != nil {\n    64\u2192\t\t\treturn s.Current.CreateBeforeDestroy\n    65\u2192\t\t}\n    66\u2192\t}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192\tif n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil {\n    69\u2192\t\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\n    70\u2192\t}\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192\treturn false\n    73\u2192}\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\n    76\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\n    77\u2192\treturn nil\n    78\u2192}\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192// GraphNodeReferenceable, overriding NodeAbstractResource\n    81\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n    82\u2192\tnormalAddrs := n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ReferenceableAddrs()\n    83\u2192\tdestroyAddrs := make([]addrs.Referenceable, len(normalAddrs))\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192\tphaseType := addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy\n    86\u2192\tif n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\n    87\u2192\t\tphaseType = addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroyCBD\n    88\u2192\t}\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192\tfor i, normalAddr := range normalAddrs {\n    91\u2192\t\tswitch ta := normalAddr.(type) {\n    92\u2192\t\tcase addrs.Resource:\n    93\u2192\t\t\tdestroyAddrs[i] = ta.Phase(phaseType)\n    94\u2192\t\tcase addrs.ResourceInstance:\n    95\u2192\t\t\tdestroyAddrs[i] = ta.Phase(phaseType)\n    96\u2192\t\tdefault:\n    97\u2192\t\t\tdestroyAddrs[i] = normalAddr\n    98\u2192\t\t}\n    99\u2192\t}\n   100\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
53. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_apply.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n     8\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    10\u2192)\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192// nodeExpandApplyableResource handles the first layer of resource\n    13\u2192// expansion during apply. Even though the resource instances themselves are\n    14\u2192// already expanded from the plan, we still need to expand the\n    15\u2192// NodeApplyableResource nodes into their respective modules.\n    16\u2192type nodeExpandApplyableResource struct {\n    17\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResource\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192\tPartialExpansions []addrs.PartialExpandedResource\n    20\u2192}\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192var (\n    23\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    24\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    25\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    26\u2192\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    27\u2192\t_ graphNodeExpandsInstances     = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    28\u2192\t_ GraphNodeTargetable           = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    29\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable    = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    30\u2192)\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) expandsInstances() {\n    33\u2192}\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n    36\u2192\trefs := n.NodeAbstractResource.References()\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192\t// The expand node needs to connect to the individual resource instances it\n    39\u2192\t// references, but cannot refer to it's own instances without causing\n    40\u2192\t// cycles. It would be preferable to entirely disallow self references\n    41\u2192\t// without the `self` identifier, but those were allowed in provisioners\n    42\u2192\t// for compatibility with legacy configuration. We also can't always just\n    43\u2192\t// filter them out for all resource node types, because the only method we\n    44\u2192\t// have for catching certain invalid configurations are the cycles that\n    45\u2192\t// result from these inter-instance references.\n    46\u2192\treturn filterSelfRefs(n.Addr.Resource, refs)\n    47\u2192}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) Name() string {\n    50\u2192\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.Name() + \" (expand)\"\n    51\u2192}\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    54\u2192\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n    55\u2192\t\treturn n.dynamicExpandEphemeral(ctx)\n    56\u2192\t}\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    59\u2192\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n    60\u2192\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\n    61\u2192\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n    62\u2192\t\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(ctx, module)\n    63\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)))\n    64\u2192\t}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192\treturn nil, diags\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192// We need to expand the ephemeral resources mostly the same as we do during\n    70\u2192// planning. There a lot of options than happen during planning which aren't\n    71\u2192// applicable to apply however, and we have to make sure we don't re-register\n    72\u2192// checks which already recorded in the plan, so we create a pared down version\n    73\u2192// of the plan expansion here.\n    74\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) dynamicExpandEphemeral(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    75\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    76\u2192\tvar g Graph\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n    79\u2192\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n    82\u2192\t\tresAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)\n    83\u2192\t\texpDiags := n.expandEphemeralResourceInstances(ctx, resAddr, &g)\n    84\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(expDiags)\n    85\u2192\t}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192\treturn &g, diags\n    88\u2192}\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) expandEphemeralResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, g *Graph) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n    91\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192\t// The rest of our work here needs to know which module instance it's\n    94\u2192\t// working in, so that it can evaluate expressions in the appropriate scope.\n    95\u2192\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\n    98\u2192\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\n    99\u2192\t// to work below.\n   100\u2192\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
54. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_plan_destroy.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    16\u2192)\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192// NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance represents a resource that is ready\n    19\u2192// to be planned for destruction.\n    20\u2192type NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance struct {\n    21\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing\n    24\u2192\tskipRefresh bool\n    25\u2192}\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192var (\n    28\u2192\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance       = (*NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    29\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    30\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    31\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer            = (*NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    32\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    33\u2192\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance     = (*NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    34\u2192\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    35\u2192\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceState  = (*NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    36\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable           = (*NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    37\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer     = (*NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n    38\u2192)\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192func (n *NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance) Name() string {\n    41\u2192\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.Name() + \" (destroy)\"\n    42\u2192}\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192// GraphNodeDestroyer\n    45\u2192func (n *NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n    46\u2192\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n    47\u2192\treturn &addr\n    48\u2192}\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192// GraphNodeEvalable\n    51\u2192func (n *NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    52\u2192\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192\tswitch addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {\n    55\u2192\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n    56\u2192\t\treturn n.managedResourceExecute(ctx, op)\n    57\u2192\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n    58\u2192\t\treturn n.dataResourceExecute(ctx, op)\n    59\u2192\tdefault:\n    60\u2192\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported resource mode %s\", n.Config.Mode))\n    61\u2192\t}\n    62\u2192}\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192func (n *NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    65\u2192\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192\t// Declare a bunch of variables that are used for state during\n    68\u2192\t// evaluation. These are written to by address in the EvalNodes we\n    69\u2192\t// declare below.\n    70\u2192\tvar change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n    71\u2192\tvar state *states.ResourceInstanceObject\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\tstate, err := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, addr)\n    74\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n    75\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    76\u2192\t\treturn diags\n    77\u2192\t}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192\t// If we are in the \"skip refresh\" mode then we will have skipped over our\n    80\u2192\t// usual opportunity to update the previous run state and refresh state\n    81\u2192\t// with the result of any provider schema upgrades, so we'll compensate\n    82\u2192\t// by doing that here.\n    83\u2192\t//\n    84\u2192\t// NOTE: this is coupled with logic in Context.destroyPlan which skips\n    85\u2192\t// running a normal plan walk when refresh is enabled. These two\n    86\u2192\t// conditionals must agree (be exactly opposite) in order to get the\n    87\u2192\t// correct behavior in both cases.\n    88\u2192\tif n.skipRefresh {\n    89\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, prevRunState))\n    90\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    91\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n    92\u2192\t\t}\n    93\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, refreshState))\n    94\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    95\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n    96\u2192\t\t}\n    97\u2192\t}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192\tchange, deferred, destroyPlanDiags := n.planDestroy(ctx, state, \"\")\n   100\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(destroyPlanDiags)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
55. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_diff.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    16\u2192)\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192// DiffTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing\n    19\u2192// each of the resource changes described in the given Changes object.\n    20\u2192type DiffTransformer struct {\n    21\u2192\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n    22\u2192\tState    *states.State\n    23\u2192\tChanges  *plans.ChangesSrc\n    24\u2192\tConfig   *configs.Config\n    25\u2192}\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192// return true if the given resource instance has either Preconditions or\n    28\u2192// Postconditions defined in the configuration.\n    29\u2192func (t *DiffTransformer) hasConfigConditions(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) bool {\n    30\u2192\t// unit tests may have no config\n    31\u2192\tif t.Config == nil {\n    32\u2192\t\treturn false\n    33\u2192\t}\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\tcfg := t.Config.DescendantForInstance(addr.Module)\n    36\u2192\tif cfg == nil {\n    37\u2192\t\treturn false\n    38\u2192\t}\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192\tres := cfg.Module.ResourceByAddr(addr.ConfigResource().Resource)\n    41\u2192\tif res == nil {\n    42\u2192\t\treturn false\n    43\u2192\t}\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192\treturn len(res.Preconditions) > 0 || len(res.Postconditions) > 0\n    46\u2192}\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192func (t *DiffTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n    49\u2192\tif t.Changes == nil || len(t.Changes.Resources) == 0 {\n    50\u2192\t\t// Nothing to do!\n    51\u2192\t\treturn nil\n    52\u2192\t}\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192\t// Go through all the modules in the diff.\n    55\u2192\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer starting\")\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    58\u2192\tstate := t.State\n    59\u2192\tchanges := t.Changes\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192\t// DiffTransformer creates resource _instance_ nodes. If there are any\n    62\u2192\t// whole-resource nodes already in the graph, we must ensure that they\n    63\u2192\t// get evaluated before any of the corresponding instances by creating\n    64\u2192\t// dependency edges, so we'll do some prep work here to ensure we'll only\n    65\u2192\t// create connections to nodes that existed before we started here.\n    66\u2192\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()\n    67\u2192\tfor _, node := range g.Vertices() {\n    68\u2192\t\trn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)\n    69\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n    70\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    71\u2192\t\t}\n    72\u2192\t\t// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\n    73\u2192\t\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\n    74\u2192\t\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.\n    75\u2192\t\tif _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResourceInstance); ok {\n    76\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    77\u2192\t\t}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192\t\trAddr := rn.ResourceAddr()\n    80\u2192\t\tresourceNodes.Put(rAddr, append(resourceNodes.Get(rAddr), rn))\n    81\u2192\t}\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192\tfor _, rc := range changes.Resources {\n    84\u2192\t\taddr := rc.Addr\n    85\u2192\t\tdk := rc.DeposedKey\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: found %s change for %s %s\", rc.Action, addr, dk)\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192\t\t// Depending on the action we'll need some different combinations of\n    90\u2192\t\t// nodes, because destroying uses a special node type separate from\n    91\u2192\t\t// other actions.\n    92\u2192\t\tvar update, delete, forget, createBeforeDestroy bool\n    93\u2192\t\tswitch rc.Action {\n    94\u2192\t\tcase plans.NoOp:\n    95\u2192\t\t\t// For a no-op change we don't take any action but we still\n    96\u2192\t\t\t// run any condition checks associated with the object, to\n    97\u2192\t\t\t// make sure that they still hold when considering the\n    98\u2192\t\t\t// results of other changes.\n    99\u2192\t\t\tupdate = t.hasConfigConditions(addr)\n   100\u2192\t\tcase plans.Delete:\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
56. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_state.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"log\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    10\u2192)\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192// StateTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds the elements of\n    13\u2192// the state to the graph.\n    14\u2192//\n    15\u2192// This transform is used for example by the DestroyPlanGraphBuilder to ensure\n    16\u2192// that only resources that are in the state are represented in the graph.\n    17\u2192type StateTransformer struct {\n    18\u2192\t// ConcreteCurrent and ConcreteDeposed are used to specialize the abstract\n    19\u2192\t// resource instance nodes that this transformer will create.\n    20\u2192\t//\n    21\u2192\t// If either of these is nil, the objects of that type will be skipped and\n    22\u2192\t// not added to the graph at all. It doesn't make sense to use this\n    23\u2192\t// transformer without setting at least one of these, since that would\n    24\u2192\t// skip everything and thus be a no-op.\n    25\u2192\tConcreteCurrent ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n    26\u2192\tConcreteDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\tState *states.State\n    29\u2192}\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192func (t *StateTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n    32\u2192\tif t.State == nil {\n    33\u2192\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: state is nil, so nothing to do\")\n    34\u2192\t\treturn nil\n    35\u2192\t}\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192\tswitch {\n    38\u2192\tcase t.ConcreteCurrent != nil && t.ConcreteDeposed != nil:\n    39\u2192\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for both current and deposed instance objects\")\n    40\u2192\tcase t.ConcreteCurrent != nil:\n    41\u2192\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for current instance objects only\")\n    42\u2192\tcase t.ConcreteDeposed != nil:\n    43\u2192\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for deposed instance objects only\")\n    44\u2192\tdefault:\n    45\u2192\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: pointless no-op call, creating no nodes at all\")\n    46\u2192\t}\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192\tfor _, ms := range t.State.Modules {\n    49\u2192\t\tfor _, rs := range ms.Resources {\n    50\u2192\t\t\tresourceAddr := rs.Addr\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192\t\t\tfor key, is := range rs.Instances {\n    53\u2192\t\t\t\taddr := resourceAddr.Instance(key)\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192\t\t\t\tif obj := is.Current; obj != nil && t.ConcreteCurrent != nil {\n    56\u2192\t\t\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n    57\u2192\t\t\t\t\tnode := t.ConcreteCurrent(abstract)\n    58\u2192\t\t\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n    59\u2192\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: added %T for %s current object\", node, addr)\n    60\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192\t\t\t\tif t.ConcreteDeposed != nil {\n    63\u2192\t\t\t\t\tfor dk := range is.Deposed {\n    64\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n    65\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tnode := t.ConcreteDeposed(abstract, dk)\n    66\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n    67\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: added %T for %s deposed object %s\", node, addr, dk)\n    68\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n    69\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n    70\u2192\t\t\t}\n    71\u2192\t\t}\n    72\u2192\t}\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192\treturn nil\n    75\u2192}\n    76\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
57. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_destroy.go",
  "new_string": "type NodeDestroyResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeResourceInstanceBase\n}",
  "old_string": "type NodeDestroyResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n}",
  "replace_all": true
}
Output
"The file /workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_destroy.go has been updated. All occurrences of 'type NodeDestroyResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n}' were successfully replaced with 'type NodeDestroyResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeResourceInstanceBase\n}'."
58. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_destroy.go",
  "new_string": "\treturn n.NodeResourceInstanceBase.ProvidedBy()",
  "old_string": "\treturn n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ProvidedBy()",
  "replace_all": true
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_deferred.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n     8\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    11\u2192)\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192// DeferredTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing\n    14\u2192// each of the deferred changes to the graph.\n    15\u2192//\n    16\u2192// Deferred changes are not executed during the apply phase, but they are\n    17\u2192// tracked in the graph to ensure that the correct ordering is maintained and\n    18\u2192// the target flags are correctly applied.\n    19\u2192type DeferredTransformer struct {\n    20\u2192\tDeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc\n    21\u2192}\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192func (t *DeferredTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n    24\u2192\tif len(t.DeferredChanges) == 0 {\n    25\u2192\t\treturn nil\n    26\u2192\t}\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\t// As with the DiffTransformer, DeferredTransformer creates resource\n    29\u2192\t// instance nodes. If there are any whole-resource nodes already in the\n    30\u2192\t// graph, we must ensure they get evaluated before any of the corresponding\n    31\u2192\t// instances by creating dependency edges.\n    32\u2192\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()\n    33\u2192\tfor _, node := range g.Vertices() {\n    34\u2192\t\trn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)\n    35\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n    36\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    37\u2192\t\t}\n    38\u2192\t\t// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\n    39\u2192\t\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\n    40\u2192\t\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.\n    41\u2192\t\tif _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResourceInstance); ok {\n    42\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    43\u2192\t\t}\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192\t\trAddr := rn.ResourceAddr()\n    46\u2192\t\tresourceNodes.Put(rAddr, append(resourceNodes.Get(rAddr), rn))\n    47\u2192\t}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192\tfor _, change := range t.DeferredChanges {\n    50\u2192\t\tnode := &nodeApplyableDeferredInstance{\n    51\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(change.ChangeSrc.Addr),\n    52\u2192\t\t\tReason:                       change.DeferredReason,\n    53\u2192\t\t\tChangeSrc:                    change.ChangeSrc,\n    54\u2192\t\t}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192\t\t// Create a special node for partial instances, that handles the\n    57\u2192\t\t// addresses a little differently.\n    58\u2192\t\tif change.DeferredReason == providers.DeferredReasonInstanceCountUnknown {\n    59\u2192\t\t\tper := change.ChangeSrc.Addr.PartialResource()\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192\t\t\t// This is a partial instance, so we need to create a partial node\n    62\u2192\t\t\t// instead of a full instance node.\n    63\u2192\t\t\tnode := &nodeApplyableDeferredPartialInstance{\n    64\u2192\t\t\t\tnodeApplyableDeferredInstance: node,\n    65\u2192\t\t\t\tPartialAddr:                   per,\n    66\u2192\t\t\t}\n    67\u2192\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192\t\t\t// Now we want to find the expansion node that would be applied for\n    70\u2192\t\t\t// this resource, and tell it that it is performing a partial\n    71\u2192\t\t\t// expansion.\n    72\u2192\t\t\tfor _, v := range g.Vertices() {\n    73\u2192\t\t\t\tif n, ok := v.(*nodeExpandApplyableResource); ok {\n    74\u2192\t\t\t\t\tif per.ConfigResource().Equal(n.Addr) {\n    75\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tn.PartialExpansions = append(n.PartialExpansions, per)\n    76\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n    77\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n    78\u2192\t\t\t}\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192\t\t\t// Also connect the deferred instance node to the underlying\n    81\u2192\t\t\t// resource node to make sure any expansion happens first.\n    82\u2192\t\t\tfor _, resourceNode := range resourceNodes.Get(node.Addr.ConfigResource()) {\n    83\u2192\t\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, resourceNode))\n    84\u2192\t\t\t}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    87\u2192\t\t}\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192\t\t// Otherwise, just add the normal deferred instance node.\n    90\u2192\t\tg.Add(node)\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192\t\t// Still connect the deferred instance node to the underlying resource\n    93\u2192\t\t// node.\n    94\u2192\t\tfor _, resourceNode := range resourceNodes.Get(node.Addr.ConfigResource()) {\n    95\u2192\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, resourceNode))\n    96\u2192\t\t}\n    97\u2192\t}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192\treturn nil\n   100\u2192}\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"strings\"\n     9\u2192\t\"testing\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/davecgh/go-spew/spew\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    17\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    18\u2192)\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192func TestDestroyEdgeTransformer_basic(t *testing.T) {\n    21\u2192\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n    22\u2192\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(\"test_object.A\"))\n    23\u2192\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(\"test_object.B\"))\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n    26\u2192\troot := state.EnsureModule(addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n    27\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n    28\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.A\").Resource,\n    29\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    30\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n    31\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"A\"}`),\n    32\u2192\t\t},\n    33\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n    34\u2192\t)\n    35\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n    36\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.B\").Resource,\n    37\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    38\u2192\t\t\tStatus:       states.ObjectReady,\n    39\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON:    []byte(`{\"id\":\"B\",\"test_string\":\"x\"}`),\n    40\u2192\t\t\tDependencies: []addrs.ConfigResource{mustConfigResourceAddr(\"test_object.A\")},\n    41\u2192\t\t},\n    42\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n    43\u2192\t)\n    44\u2192\tif err := (&AttachStateTransformer{State: state}).Transform(&g); err != nil {\n    45\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n    46\u2192\t}\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192\ttf := &DestroyEdgeTransformer{}\n    49\u2192\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n    50\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n    51\u2192\t}\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n    54\u2192\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(testTransformDestroyEdgeBasicStr)\n    55\u2192\tif actual != expected {\n    56\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong result\\n\\ngot:\\n%s\\n\\nwant:\\n%s\", actual, expected)\n    57\u2192\t}\n    58\u2192}\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192func TestDestroyEdgeTransformer_multi(t *testing.T) {\n    61\u2192\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n    62\u2192\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(\"test_object.A\"))\n    63\u2192\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(\"test_object.B\"))\n    64\u2192\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(\"test_object.C\"))\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n    67\u2192\troot := state.EnsureModule(addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n    68\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n    69\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.A\").Resource,\n    70\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    71\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n    72\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"A\"}`),\n    73\u2192\t\t},\n    74\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n    75\u2192\t)\n    76\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n    77\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.B\").Resource,\n    78\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    79\u2192\t\t\tStatus:       states.ObjectReady,\n    80\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON:    []byte(`{\"id\":\"B\",\"test_string\":\"x\"}`),\n    81\u2192\t\t\tDependencies: []addrs.ConfigResource{mustConfigResourceAddr(\"test_object.A\")},\n    82\u2192\t\t},\n    83\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n    84\u2192\t)\n    85\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n    86\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.C\").Resource,\n    87\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    88\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n    89\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"C\",\"test_string\":\"x\"}`),\n    90\u2192\t\t\tDependencies: []addrs.ConfigResource{\n    91\u2192\t\t\t\tmustConfigResourceAddr(\"test_object.A\"),\n    92\u2192\t\t\t\tmustConfigResourceAddr(\"test_object.B\"),\n    93\u2192\t\t\t},\n    94\u2192\t\t},\n    95\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n    96\u2192\t)\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192\tif err := (&AttachStateTransformer{State: state}).Transform(&g); err != nil {\n    99\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   100\u2192\t}\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 67. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_resource_count.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"log\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    12\u2192)\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192// ResourceCountTransformer is a GraphTransformer that expands the count\n    15\u2192// out for a specific resource.\n    16\u2192//\n    17\u2192// This assumes that the count is already interpolated.\n    18\u2192type ResourceCountTransformer struct {\n    19\u2192\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n    20\u2192\tSchema   *configschema.Block\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192\tAddr          addrs.ConfigResource\n    23\u2192\tInstanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n    24\u2192}\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192func (t *ResourceCountTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n    27\u2192\tfor _, addr := range t.InstanceAddrs {\n    28\u2192\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n    29\u2192\t\tabstract.Schema = t.Schema\n    30\u2192\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n    31\u2192\t\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\n    32\u2192\t\t\tnode = f(abstract)\n    33\u2192\t\t}\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] ResourceCountTransformer: adding %s as %T\", addr, node)\n    36\u2192\t\tg.Add(node)\n    37\u2192\t}\n    38\u2192\treturn nil\n    39\u2192}\n    40\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
68. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_forget.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"log\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    14\u2192)\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192// NodeForgetResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is to be\n    17\u2192// removed from state.\n    18\u2192type NodeForgetResourceInstance struct {\n    19\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n    20\u2192}\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192var (\n    23\u2192\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance      = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n    24\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource      = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n    25\u2192\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance    = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n    26\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer          = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n    27\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable          = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n    28\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer    = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n    29\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n    30\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer           = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n    31\u2192)\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192func (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n    34\u2192\treturn &n.Addr\n    35\u2192}\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192func (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) Name() string {\n    38\u2192\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String() + \" (forget)\"\n    39\u2192}\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192func (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) ProvidedBy() (addr addrs.ProviderConfig, exact bool) {\n    42\u2192\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\n    43\u2192\t\t// Indicate that this node does not require a configured provider\n    44\u2192\t\treturn nil, true\n    45\u2192\t}\n    46\u2192\treturn n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ProvidedBy()\n    47\u2192}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192// GraphNodeExecutable\n    50\u2192func (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    51\u2192\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\tis := n.instanceState\n    54\u2192\tif is == nil {\n    55\u2192\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] NodeForgetResourceInstance for %s with no state\", addr)\n    56\u2192\t}\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192\tvar changeApply *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n    59\u2192\tvar state *states.ResourceInstanceObject\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n    62\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n    63\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    64\u2192\t\treturn diags\n    65\u2192\t}\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192\tchangeApply, err = n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n    68\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n    69\u2192\tif changeApply == nil || diags.HasErrors() {\n    70\u2192\t\treturn diags\n    71\u2192\t}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\tstate, readDiags := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, addr)\n    74\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\n    75\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    76\u2192\t\treturn diags\n    77\u2192\t}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192\t// Exit early if state is already null\n    80\u2192\tif state == nil || state.Value.IsNull() {\n    81\u2192\t\treturn diags\n    82\u2192\t}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192\tctx.State().ForgetResourceInstanceCurrent(n.Addr)\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\n    87\u2192\treturn diags\n    88\u2192}\n    89\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
69. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/graph_builder_apply.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n     8\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    15\u2192)\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192// ApplyGraphBuilder implements GraphBuilder and is responsible for building\n    18\u2192// a graph for applying a Terraform diff.\n    19\u2192//\n    20\u2192// Because the graph is built from the diff (vs. the config or state),\n    21\u2192// this helps ensure that the apply-time graph doesn't modify any resources\n    22\u2192// that aren't explicitly in the diff. There are other scenarios where the\n    23\u2192// diff can be deviated, so this is just one layer of protection.\n    24\u2192type ApplyGraphBuilder struct {\n    25\u2192\t// Config is the configuration tree that the diff was built from.\n    26\u2192\tConfig *configs.Config\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\t// Changes describes the changes that we need apply.\n    29\u2192\tChanges *plans.ChangesSrc\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192\t// DeferredChanges describes the changes that were deferred during the plan\n    32\u2192\t// and should not be applied.\n    33\u2192\tDeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\t// State is the current state\n    36\u2192\tState *states.State\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192\t// RootVariableValues are the root module input variables captured as\n    39\u2192\t// part of the plan object, which we must reproduce in the apply step\n    40\u2192\t// to get a consistent result.\n    41\u2192\tRootVariableValues InputValues\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\n    44\u2192\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\n    45\u2192\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\n    46\u2192\t// initialization step.\n    47\u2192\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192\t// Plugins is a library of the plug-in components (providers and\n    50\u2192\t// provisioners) available for use.\n    51\u2192\tPlugins *contextPlugins\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\t// Targets are resources to target. This is only required to make sure\n    54\u2192\t// unnecessary outputs aren't included in the apply graph. The plan\n    55\u2192\t// builder successfully handles targeting resources. In the future,\n    56\u2192\t// outputs should go into the diff so that this is unnecessary.\n    57\u2192\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192\t// ForceReplace are the resource instance addresses that the user\n    60\u2192\t// requested to force replacement for when creating the plan, if any.\n    61\u2192\t// The apply step refers to these as part of verifying that the planned\n    62\u2192\t// actions remain consistent between plan and apply.\n    63\u2192\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\n    66\u2192\tOperation walkOperation\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\n    69\u2192\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\n    70\u2192\t// the actual graph.\n    71\u2192\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\n    74\u2192\t// framework.\n    75\u2192\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192// See GraphBuilder\n    79\u2192func (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    80\u2192\treturn (&BasicGraphBuilder{\n    81\u2192\t\tSteps: b.Steps(),\n    82\u2192\t\tName:  \"ApplyGraphBuilder\",\n    83\u2192\t}).Build(path)\n    84\u2192}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192// See GraphBuilder\n    87\u2192func (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {\n    88\u2192\t// Custom factory for creating providers.\n    89\u2192\tconcreteProvider := func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n    90\u2192\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n    91\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n    92\u2192\t\t}\n    93\u2192\t}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192\tconcreteResource := func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n    96\u2192\t\treturn &nodeExpandApplyableResource{\n    97\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n    98\u2192\t\t}\n    99\u2192\t}\n   100\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

74. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_destroy_deposed.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    17\u2192)\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192// ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert\n    20\u2192// an abstract resource instance to a concrete one of some type that has\n    21\u2192// an associated deposed object key.\n    22\u2192type ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance, states.DeposedKey) dag.Vertex\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192type GraphNodeDeposedResourceInstanceObject interface {\n    25\u2192\tDeposedInstanceObjectKey() states.DeposedKey\n    26\u2192}\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192// NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject represents deposed resource\n    29\u2192// instance objects during plan. These are distinct from the primary object\n    30\u2192// for each resource instance since they must be either destroyed or forgotten.\n    31\u2192//\n    32\u2192// This node type is also used during the refresh walk to ensure that the\n    33\u2192// record of a deposed object is up to date before we plan to destroy or forget\n    34\u2192// it.\n    35\u2192type NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject struct {\n    36\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n    37\u2192\tDeposedKey states.DeposedKey\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances\n    40\u2192\tskipRefresh bool\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192\t// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions\n    43\u2192\t// for any instances.\n    44\u2192\tskipPlanChanges bool\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\t// forgetResources lists resources that should not be destroyed, only removed\n    47\u2192\t// from state.\n    48\u2192\tforgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192\t// forgetModules lists modules that should not be destroyed, only removed\n    51\u2192\t// from state.\n    52\u2192\tforgetModules []addrs.Module\n    53\u2192}\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192var (\n    56\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDeposedResourceInstanceObject = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    57\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource                = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    58\u2192\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance              = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    59\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable                 = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    60\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer                    = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    61\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable                    = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    62\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer              = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    63\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer           = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    64\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer                     = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    65\u2192)\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192func (n *NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n    68\u2192\treturn &n.Addr\n    69\u2192}\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192func (n *NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject) Name() string {\n    72\u2192\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (deposed %s)\", n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String(), n.DeposedKey)\n    73\u2192}\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192func (n *NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject) DeposedInstanceObjectKey() states.DeposedKey {\n    76\u2192\treturn n.DeposedKey\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192// GraphNodeReferenceable implementation, overriding the one from NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n    80\u2192func (n *NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n    81\u2192\t// Deposed objects don't participate in references.\n    82\u2192\treturn nil\n    83\u2192}\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192// GraphNodeReferencer implementation, overriding the one from NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n    86\u2192func (n *NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n    87\u2192\t// We don't evaluate configuration for deposed objects, so they effectively\n    88\u2192\t// make no references.\n    89\u2192\treturn nil\n    90\u2192}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192// GraphNodeEvalable impl.\n    93\u2192func (n *NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    94\u2192\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject: planning %s deposed object %s\", n.Addr, n.DeposedKey)\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192\t// Read the state for the deposed resource instance\n    99\u2192\tstate, err := n.readResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx, n.Addr, n.DeposedKey)\n   100\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_plan_orphan.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    15\u2192)\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192// NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan represents a resource that is \"applyable\":\n    18\u2192// it is ready to be applied and is represented by a diff.\n    19\u2192type NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan struct {\n    20\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances\n    23\u2192\tskipRefresh bool\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\t// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions\n    26\u2192\t// for any instances.\n    27\u2192\tskipPlanChanges bool\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\t// forgetResources lists resources that should not be destroyed, only removed\n    30\u2192\t// from state.\n    31\u2192\tforgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192\t// forgetModules lists modules that should not be destroyed, only removed\n    34\u2192\t// from state.\n    35\u2192\tforgetModules []addrs.Module\n    36\u2192}\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192var (\n    39\u2192\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance       = (*NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan)(nil)\n    40\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan)(nil)\n    41\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan)(nil)\n    42\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan)(nil)\n    43\u2192\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance     = (*NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan)(nil)\n    44\u2192\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan)(nil)\n    45\u2192\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceState  = (*NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan)(nil)\n    46\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable           = (*NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan)(nil)\n    47\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer     = (*NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan)(nil)\n    48\u2192)\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan) Name() string {\n    51\u2192\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String() + \" (orphan)\"\n    52\u2192}\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192// GraphNodeExecutable\n    55\u2192func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n    56\u2192\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192\t// Eval info is different depending on what kind of resource this is\n    59\u2192\tswitch addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {\n    60\u2192\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n    61\u2192\t\treturn n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\n    62\u2192\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n    63\u2192\t\treturn n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\n    64\u2192\tdefault:\n    65\u2192\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported resource mode %s\", n.Config.Mode))\n    66\u2192\t}\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan) ProvidedBy() (addr addrs.ProviderConfig, exact bool) {\n    70\u2192\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\n    71\u2192\t\t// indicate that this node does not require a configured provider\n    72\u2192\t\treturn nil, true\n    73\u2192\t}\n    74\u2192\treturn n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ProvidedBy()\n    75\u2192}\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n    78\u2192\t// A data source that is no longer in the config is removed from the state\n    79\u2192\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan: removing state object for %s\", n.Addr)\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\t// we need to update both the refresh state to refresh the current data\n    82\u2192\t// source, and the working state for plan-time evaluations.\n    83\u2192\trefreshState := ctx.RefreshState()\n    84\u2192\trefreshState.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(n.Addr, nil, n.ResolvedProvider)\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192\tworkingState := ctx.State()\n    87\u2192\tworkingState.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(n.Addr, nil, n.ResolvedProvider)\n    88\u2192\treturn nil\n    89\u2192}\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    92\u2192\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192\toldState, readDiags := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, addr)\n    95\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\n    96\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    97\u2192\t\treturn diags\n    98\u2192\t}\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192\t// Note any upgrades that readResourceInstanceState might've done in the\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"log\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    10\u2192)\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192// NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance represents a resource that is \"destroyable\":\n    13\u2192// it is ready to be destroyed.\n    14\u2192type NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance struct {\n    15\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n    16\u2192}\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192var (\n    19\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable = (*NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance)(nil)\n    20\u2192)\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192// GraphNodeExecutable\n    23\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n    24\u2192\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance: removing state object for %s\", n.Addr)\n    25\u2192\tctx.State().SetResourceInstanceCurrent(n.Addr, nil, n.ResolvedProvider)\n    26\u2192\treturn nil\n    27\u2192}\n    28\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go",

}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"log\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    16\u2192)\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192// PlanGraphBuilder is a GraphBuilder implementation that builds a graph for\n    19\u2192// planning and for other \"plan-like\" operations which don't require an\n    20\u2192// already-calculated plan as input.\n    21\u2192//\n    22\u2192// Unlike the apply graph builder, this graph builder:\n    23\u2192//\n    24\u2192//   - Makes its decisions primarily based on the given configuration, which\n    25\u2192//     represents the desired state.\n    26\u2192//\n    27\u2192//   - Ignores certain lifecycle concerns like create_before_destroy, because\n    28\u2192//     those are only important once we already know what action we're planning\n    29\u2192//     to take against a particular resource instance.\n    30\u2192type PlanGraphBuilder struct {\n    31\u2192\t// Config is the configuration tree to build a plan from.\n    32\u2192\tConfig *configs.Config\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192\t// State is the current state\n    35\u2192\tState *states.State\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192\t// RootVariableValues are the raw input values for root input variables\n    38\u2192\t// given by the caller, which we'll resolve into final values as part\n    39\u2192\t// of the plan walk.\n    40\u2192\tRootVariableValues InputValues\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\n    43\u2192\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\n    44\u2192\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\n    45\u2192\t// initialization step.\n    46\u2192\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192\t// Plugins is a library of plug-in components (providers and\n    49\u2192\t// provisioners) available for use.\n    50\u2192\tPlugins *contextPlugins\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192\t// Targets are resources to target\n    53\u2192\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192\t// ForceReplace are resource instances where if we would normally have\n    56\u2192\t// generated a NoOp or Update action then we'll force generating a replace\n    57\u2192\t// action instead. Create and Delete actions are not affected.\n    58\u2192\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing managed resources\n    61\u2192\tskipRefresh bool\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192\t// preDestroyRefresh indicates that we are executing the refresh which\n    64\u2192\t// happens immediately before a destroy plan, which happens to use the\n    65\u2192\t// normal planing mode so skipPlanChanges cannot be set.\n    66\u2192\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192\t// skipPlanChanges indicates that we should skip the step of comparing\n    69\u2192\t// prior state with configuration and generating planned changes to\n    70\u2192\t// resource instances. (This is for the \"refresh only\" planning mode,\n    71\u2192\t// where we _only_ do the refresh step.)\n    72\u2192\tskipPlanChanges bool\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192\tConcreteProvider                ConcreteProviderNodeFunc\n    75\u2192\tConcreteResource                ConcreteResourceNodeFunc\n    76\u2192\tConcreteResourceInstance        ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n    77\u2192\tConcreteResourceOrphan          ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n    78\u2192\tConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc\n    79\u2192\tConcreteModule                  ConcreteModuleNodeFunc\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\n    82\u2192\tOperation walkOperation\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\n    85\u2192\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\n    86\u2192\t// the actual graph.\n    87\u2192\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\n    90\u2192\t// framework.\n    91\u2192\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192\t// ImportTargets are the list of resources to import.\n    94\u2192\tImportTargets []*ImportTarget\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192\t// forgetResources lists the resources that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed\n    97\u2192\t// from state without destroying.\n    98\u2192\tforgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192\t// forgetModules lists the modules that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"testing\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    17\u2192)\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192func TestNodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject_Execute(t *testing.T) {\n    20\u2192\tdeposedKey := states.NewDeposedKey()\n    21\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n    22\u2192\tabsResource := mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_instance.foo\")\n    23\u2192\tstate.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceDeposed(\n    24\u2192\t\tabsResource.Resource,\n    25\u2192\t\tdeposedKey,\n    26\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    27\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectTainted,\n    28\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"bar\"}`),\n    29\u2192\t\t},\n    30\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n    31\u2192\t)\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192\tp := testProvider(\"test\")\n    34\u2192\tp.ConfigureProvider(providers.ConfigureProviderRequest{})\n    35\u2192\tp.UpgradeResourceStateResponse = &providers.UpgradeResourceStateResponse{\n    36\u2192\t\tUpgradedState: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n    37\u2192\t\t\t\"id\": cty.StringVal(\"bar\"),\n    38\u2192\t\t}),\n    39\u2192\t}\n    40\u2192\tctx := &MockEvalContext{\n    41\u2192\t\tStateState:        state.SyncWrapper(),\n    42\u2192\t\tPrevRunStateState: state.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper(),\n    43\u2192\t\tRefreshStateState: state.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper(),\n    44\u2192\t\tProviderProvider:  p,\n    45\u2192\t\tProviderSchemaSchema: providers.ProviderSchema{\n    46\u2192\t\t\tResourceTypes: map[string]providers.Schema{\n    47\u2192\t\t\t\t\"test_instance\": {\n    48\u2192\t\t\t\t\tBlock: &configschema.Block{\n    49\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tAttributes: map[string]*configschema.Attribute{\n    50\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": {\n    51\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tType:     cty.String,\n    52\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tComputed: true,\n    53\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n    54\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n    55\u2192\t\t\t\t\t},\n    56\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    57\u2192\t\t\t},\n    58\u2192\t\t},\n    59\u2192\t\tChangesChanges: plans.NewChanges().SyncWrapper(),\n    60\u2192\t}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192\tnode := NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n    63\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n    64\u2192\t\t\tAddr: absResource,\n    65\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n    66\u2192\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n    67\u2192\t\t\t},\n    68\u2192\t\t},\n    69\u2192\t\tDeposedKey: deposedKey,\n    70\u2192\t}\n    71\u2192\terr := node.Execute(ctx, walkPlan)\n    72\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    73\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", err)\n    74\u2192\t}\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192\tif !p.UpgradeResourceStateCalled {\n    77\u2192\t\tt.Errorf(\"UpgradeResourceState wasn't called; should've been called to upgrade the previous run's object\")\n    78\u2192\t}\n    79\u2192\tif !p.ReadResourceCalled {\n    80\u2192\t\tt.Errorf(\"ReadResource wasn't called; should've been called to refresh the deposed object\")\n    81\u2192\t}\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192\tchange := ctx.Changes().GetResourceInstanceChange(absResource, deposedKey)\n    84\u2192\tif got, want := change.Change.Action, plans.Delete; got != want {\n    85\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong planned action\\ngot:  %s\\nwant: %s\", got, want)\n    86\u2192\t}\n    87\u2192}\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192func TestNodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject_Execute(t *testing.T) {\n    90\u2192\tdeposedKey := states.NewDeposedKey()\n    91\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n    92\u2192\tabsResource := mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_instance.foo\")\n    93\u2192\tstate.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceDeposed(\n    94\u2192\t\tabsResource.Resource,\n    95\u2192\t\tdeposedKey,\n    96\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    97\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectTainted,\n    98\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"bar\"}`),\n    99\u2192\t\t},\n   100\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"testing\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    11\u2192)\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192func TestNodeDataDestroyExecute(t *testing.T) {\n    14\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n    15\u2192\tstate.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n    16\u2192\t\taddrs.Resource{\n    17\u2192\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n    18\u2192\t\t\tType: \"test_instance\",\n    19\u2192\t\t\tName: \"foo\",\n    20\u2192\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey),\n    21\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    22\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n    23\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"dynamic\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"value\":\"hello\"}}`),\n    24\u2192\t\t},\n    25\u2192\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n    26\u2192\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n    27\u2192\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n    28\u2192\t\t},\n    29\u2192\t)\n    30\u2192\tctx := &MockEvalContext{\n    31\u2192\t\tStateState: state.SyncWrapper(),\n    32\u2192\t}\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192\tnode := NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance{&NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n    35\u2192\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n    36\u2192\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n    37\u2192\t\t\tType: \"test_instance\",\n    38\u2192\t\t\tName: \"foo\",\n    39\u2192\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n    40\u2192\t}}\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192\tdiags := node.Execute(ctx, walkApply)\n    43\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    44\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %v\", diags.Err())\n    45\u2192\t}\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192\t// verify resource removed from state\n    48\u2192\tif state.HasManagedResourceInstanceObjects() {\n    49\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(\"resources still in state after NodeDataDestroy.Execute\")\n    50\u2192\t}\n    51\u2192}\n    52\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
