"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    13\u2192)\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192// This file is a temporary split from the node_resource_plan.go file. We handle\n    16\u2192// the unknown instances branch of execution within here, while this is still\n    17\u2192// being developed.\n    18\u2192//\n    19\u2192// We have split the files to make structuring the code easier, eventually once\n    20\u2192// the functions within this file are production ready, we will merge them back\n    21\u2192// into the node_resource_plan.go file.\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192// dynamicExpandPartial is a variant of dynamicExpand that we use when deferred\n    24\u2192// actions are enabled for the current plan.\n    25\u2192//\n    26\u2192// Once deferred actions are more stable and robust in the stacks runtime, it\n    27\u2192// would be nice to integrate this logic a little better with the main\n    28\u2192// DynamicExpand logic, but it's separate for now to minimize the risk of\n    29\u2192// stacks-specific behavior impacting configurations that are not opted into it.\n    30\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpandPartial(ctx EvalContext, knownModules []addrs.ModuleInstance, partialModules addrs.Set[addrs.PartialExpandedModule], knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    31\u2192\tvar g Graph\n    32\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192\tknownResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()\n    35\u2192\tpartialResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.PartialExpandedResource]()\n    36\u2192\tmaybeOrphanResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192\tfor _, moduleAddr := range knownModules {\n    39\u2192\t\tresourceAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(moduleAddr)\n    40\u2192\t\tresources, partials, maybeOrphans, moreDiags := n.expandKnownModule(ctx, resourceAddr, knownImports, unknownImports, &g)\n    41\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192\t\t// Track all the resources we know about.\n    44\u2192\t\tknownResources = knownResources.Union(resources)\n    45\u2192\t\tpartialResources = partialResources.Union(partials)\n    46\u2192\t\tmaybeOrphanResources = maybeOrphanResources.Union(maybeOrphans)\n    47\u2192\t}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192\tfor _, moduleAddr := range partialModules {\n    50\u2192\t\tresourceAddr := moduleAddr.Resource(n.Addr.Resource)\n    51\u2192\t\tpartialResources.Add(resourceAddr)\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\t\t// And add a node to the graph for this resource.\n    54\u2192\t\tg.Add(&nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource{\n    55\u2192\t\t\taddr:             resourceAddr,\n    56\u2192\t\t\tconfig:           n.Config,\n    57\u2192\t\t\tresolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n    58\u2192\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:  n.skipPlanChanges,\n    59\u2192\t\t})\n    60\u2192\t}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192\tfunc() {\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192\t\tss := ctx.PrevRunState()\n    65\u2192\t\tif ss == nil {\n    66\u2192\t\t\treturn // No previous state, so nothing to do here.\n    67\u2192\t\t}\n    68\u2192\t\tstate := ss.Lock()\n    69\u2192\t\tdefer ss.Unlock()\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192\tResources:\n    72\u2192\t\tfor _, res := range state.Resources(n.Addr) {\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192\t\t\tfor _, knownModule := range knownModules {\n    75\u2192\t\t\t\tif knownModule.Equal(res.Addr.Module) {\n    76\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// Then we handled this resource as part of the known\n    77\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// modules processing.\n    78\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcontinue Resources\n    79\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n    80\u2192\t\t\t}\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192\t\t\tfor _, partialResource := range partialResources {\n    83\u2192\t\t\t\tif partialResource.MatchesResource(res.Addr) {\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192\t\t\t\t\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n    86\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t// Then each of the instances is a \"maybe orphan\"\n    87\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t// instance, and we need to add a node for that.\n    88\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tmaybeOrphanResources.Add(res.Addr.Instance(key))\n    89\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tg.Add(n.concreteResource(ctx, addrs.MakeMap[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value](), addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]](), true)(NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(res.Addr.Instance(key))))\n    90\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// Move onto the next resource.\n    93\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcontinue Resources\n    94\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n    95\u2192\t\t\t}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192\t\t\t// Otherwise, everything in here is just a simple orphaned instance.\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192\t\t\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n   100\u2192\t\t\t\tinst := res.Addr.Instance(key)\n   101\u2192\t\t\t\tabs := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(inst)\n   102\u2192\t\t\t\tabs.AttachResourceState(res)\n   103\u2192\t\t\t\tg.Add(n.concreteResourceOrphan(abs))\n   104\u2192\t\t\t}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192\t\t}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192\t}()\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192\t// We need to ensure that all of the expanded import targets are actually\n   111\u2192\t// present in the configuration, because we can't import something that\n   112\u2192\t// doesn't exist.\n   113\u2192\t//\n   114\u2192\t// See the validateExpandedImportTargets function for the equivalent of\n   115\u2192\t// this for the known resources path.\n   116\u2192ImportValidationKnown:\n   117\u2192\tfor _, addr := range knownImports.Keys() {\n   118\u2192\t\tif knownResources.Has(addr) {\n   119\u2192\t\t\t// Simple case, this is known to be in the configuration so we\n   120\u2192\t\t\t// skip it.\n   121\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   122\u2192\t\t}\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192\t\tfor _, partialAddr := range partialResources {\n   125\u2192\t\t\tif partialAddr.MatchesInstance(addr) {\n   126\u2192\t\t\t\t// This is a partial-expanded address, so we can't yet know\n   127\u2192\t\t\t\t// whether it's in the configuration or not, and so we'll\n   128\u2192\t\t\t\t// defer dealing with it to a future round.\n   129\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue ImportValidationKnown\n   130\u2192\t\t\t}\n   131\u2192\t\t}\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192\t\tif maybeOrphanResources.Has(addr) {\n   134\u2192\t\t\t// This is in the previous state but we can't yet know whether\n   135\u2192\t\t\t// it's still desired, so we'll defer dealing with it to a future\n   136\u2192\t\t\t// round.\n   137\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   138\u2192\t\t}\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192\t\t// If we get here then the import target is not in the configuration\n   141\u2192\t\t// at all, and so we'll report an error.\n   142\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   143\u2192\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n   144\u2192\t\t\t\"Configuration for import target does not exist\",\n   145\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The configuration for the given import %s does not exist. All target instances must have an associated configuration to be imported.\", addr),\n   146\u2192\t\t))\n   147\u2192\t}\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192\t// We'll also perform the same kind of validation on our unknown imports.\n   150\u2192\t// This will be less precise because we don't have the full state to\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_plan.go",
  "limit": 150,

Output
"   340\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   341\u2192\tvar g Graph\n   342\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192\torphans := n.findOrphans(ctx, moduleInstances)\n   345\u2192\n   346\u2192\tfor _, res := range orphans {\n   347\u2192\t\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n   348\u2192\t\t\taddr := res.Addr.Instance(key)\n   349\u2192\t\t\tabs := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n   350\u2192\t\t\tabs.AttachResourceState(res)\n   351\u2192\t\t\tn := n.concreteResourceOrphan(abs)\n   352\u2192\t\t\tg.Add(n)\n   353\u2192\t\t}\n   354\u2192\t}\n   355\u2192\n   356\u2192\t// The above dealt with the expansion of the containing module, so now\n   357\u2192\t// we need to deal with the expansion of the resource itself across all\n   358\u2192\t// instances of the module.\n   359\u2192\t//\n   360\u2192\t// We'll gather up all of the leaf instances we learn about along the way\n   361\u2192\t// so that we can inform the checks subsystem of which instances it should\n   362\u2192\t// be expecting check results for, below.\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192\texpandedInstances := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.Checkable]()\n   365\u2192\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n   366\u2192\t\tresAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)\n   367\u2192\t\tinstances, err := n.expandResourceInstances(ctx, resAddr, imports, &g)\n   368\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n   369\u2192\t\tfor _, instance := range instances {\n   370\u2192\t\t\texpandedInstances.Add(instance)\n   371\u2192\t\t}\n   372\u2192\t}\n   373\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   374\u2192\t\treturn nil, diags\n   375\u2192\t}\n   376\u2192\n   377\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validateExpandedImportTargets(imports, expandedInstances))\n   378\u2192\n   379\u2192\t// If this is a resource that participates in custom condition checks\n   380\u2192\t// (i.e. it has preconditions or postconditions) then the check state\n   381\u2192\t// wants to know the addresses of the checkable objects so that it can\n   382\u2192\t// treat them as unknown status if we encounter an error before actually\n   383\u2192\t// visiting the checks.\n   384\u2192\tif checkState := ctx.Checks(); checkState.ConfigHasChecks(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr) {\n   385\u2192\t\tcheckState.ReportCheckableObjects(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr, expandedInstances)\n   386\u2192\t}\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192\treturn &g, diags\n   391\u2192}\n   392\u2192\n   393\u2192// expandResourceInstances calculates the dynamic expansion for the resource\n   394\u2192// itself in the context of a particular module instance.\n   395\u2192//\n   396\u2192// It has several side-effects:\n   397\u2192//   - Adds a node to Graph g for each leaf resource instance it discovers, whether present or orphaned.\n   398\u2192//   - Registers the expansion of the resource in the \"expander\" object embedded inside EvalContext globalCtx.\n   399\u2192//   - Adds each present (non-orphaned) resource instance address to checkableAddrs (guaranteed to always be addrs.AbsResourceInstance, despite being declared as addrs.Checkable).\n   400\u2192//\n   401\u2192// After calling this for each of the module instances the resource appears\n   402\u2192// within, the caller must register the final superset instAddrs with the\n   403\u2192// checks subsystem so that it knows the fully expanded set of checkable\n   404\u2192// object instances for this resource instance.\n   405\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], g *Graph) ([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   406\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   407\u2192\n   408\u2192\t// The rest of our work here needs to know which module instance it's\n   409\u2192\t// working in, so that it can evaluate expressions in the appropriate scope.\n   410\u2192\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\n   411\u2192\n   412\u2192\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\n   413\u2192\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\n   414\u2192\t// to work below.\n   415\u2192\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n   416\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n   417\u2192\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\n   418\u2192\t\treturn nil, diags\n   419\u2192\t}\n   420\u2192\n   421\u2192\t// Before we expand our resource into potentially many resource instances,\n   422\u2192\t// we'll verify that any mention of this resource in n.forceReplace is\n   423\u2192\t// consistent with the repetition mode of the resource. In other words,\n   424\u2192\t// we're aiming to catch a situation where naming a particular resource\n   425\u2192\t// instance would require an instance key but the given address has none.\n   426\u2192\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\n   427\u2192\tinstanceAddrs := expander.ExpandResource(resAddr)\n   428\u2192\n   429\u2192\t// If there's a number of instances other than 1 then we definitely need\n   430\u2192\t// an index.\n   431\u2192\tmustHaveIndex := len(instanceAddrs) != 1\n   432\u2192\t// If there's only one instance then we might still need an index, if the\n   433\u2192\t// instance address has one.\n   434\u2192\tif len(instanceAddrs) == 1 && instanceAddrs[0].Resource.Key != addrs.NoKey {\n   435\u2192\t\tmustHaveIndex = true\n   436\u2192\t}\n   437\u2192\tif mustHaveIndex {\n   438\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs))\n   439\u2192\t}\n   440\u2192\t// NOTE: The actual interpretation of n.forceReplace to produce replace\n   441\u2192\t// actions is in the per-instance function we're about to call, because\n   442\u2192\t// we need to evaluate it on a per-instance basis.\n   443\u2192\n   444\u2192\t// Our graph builder mechanism expects to always be constructing new\n   445\u2192\t// graphs rather than adding to existing ones, so we'll first\n   446\u2192\t// construct a subgraph just for this individual modules's instances and\n   447\u2192\t// then we'll steal all of its nodes and edges to incorporate into our\n   448\u2192\t// main graph which contains all of the resource instances together.\n   449\u2192\tinstG, instDiags := n.resourceInstanceSubgraph(moduleCtx, resAddr, instanceAddrs, imports)\n   450\u2192\tif instDiags.HasErrors() {\n   451\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(instDiags)\n   452\u2192\t\treturn nil, diags\n   453\u2192\t}\n   454\u2192\tg.Subsume(&instG.AcyclicGraph.Graph)\n   455\u2192\n   456\u2192\treturn instanceAddrs, diags\n   457\u2192}\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) resourceInstanceSubgraph(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource, instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   460\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   461\u2192\n   462\u2192\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath != \"\" && imports.Len() == 0 {\n   463\u2192\t\t// We're generating configuration, but there's nothing to import, which\n   464\u2192\t\t// means the import block must have expanded to zero instances.\n   465\u2192\t\t// the instance expander will always return a single instance because\n   466\u2192\t\t// we have assumed there will eventually be a configuration for this\n   467\u2192\t\t// resource, so return here before we add that to the graph.\n   468\u2192\t\treturn &Graph{}, diags\n   469\u2192\t}\n   470\u2192\n   471\u2192\t// Our graph transformers require access to the full state, so we'll\n   472\u2192\t// temporarily lock it while we work on this.\n   473\u2192\tstate := ctx.State().Lock()\n   474\u2192\tdefer ctx.State().Unlock()\n   475\u2192\n   476\u2192\t// Start creating the steps\n   477\u2192\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n   478\u2192\t\t// Expand the count or for_each (if present)\n   479\u2192\t\t&ResourceCountTransformer{\n   480\u2192\t\t\tConcrete:      n.concreteResource(ctx, imports, addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]](), n.skipPlanChanges),\n   481\u2192\t\t\tSchema:        n.Schema,\n   482\u2192\t\t\tAddr:          n.ResourceAddr(),\n   483\u2192\t\t\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\n   484\u2192\t\t},\n   485\u2192\n   486\u2192\t\t// Add the count/for_each orphans\n   487\u2192\t\t&OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer{\n   488\u2192\t\t\tConcrete:      n.concreteResourceOrphan,\n   489\u2192\t\t\tAddr:          addr,\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"   200\u2192\t// visiting the checks.\n   201\u2192\tif checkState := ctx.Checks(); checkState.ConfigHasChecks(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr) {\n   202\u2192\t\tcheckables := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.Checkable]()\n   203\u2192\t\tfor _, addr := range knownResources {\n   204\u2192\t\t\tcheckables.Add(addr)\n   205\u2192\t\t}\n   206\u2192\t\tfor _, addr := range maybeOrphanResources {\n   207\u2192\t\t\tcheckables.Add(addr)\n   208\u2192\t\t}\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192\t\tcheckState.ReportCheckableObjects(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr, checkables)\n   211\u2192\t}\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\n   214\u2192\treturn &g, diags\n   215\u2192}\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandKnownModule(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], g *Graph) (addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance], addrs.Set[addrs.PartialExpandedResource], addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance], tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   218\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n   223\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n   224\u2192\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\n   225\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, nil, diags\n   226\u2192\t}\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\n   229\u2192\t_, knownInstKeys, haveUnknownKeys := expander.ResourceInstanceKeys(resAddr)\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192\tknownResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()\n   232\u2192\tpartialResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.PartialExpandedResource]()\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192\tfor _, key := range knownInstKeys {\n   235\u2192\t\tknownResources.Add(resAddr.Instance(key))\n   236\u2192\t}\n   237\u2192\tif haveUnknownKeys {\n   238\u2192\t\tpartialResources.Add(resAddr.Module.UnexpandedResource(resAddr.Resource))\n   239\u2192\t}\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192\tmustHaveIndex := len(knownInstKeys) != 1 || haveUnknownKeys\n   242\u2192\tif len(knownInstKeys) == 1 && knownInstKeys[0] != addrs.NoKey {\n   243\u2192\t\tmustHaveIndex = true\n   244\u2192\t}\n   245\u2192\tif mustHaveIndex {\n   246\u2192\t\tvar instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n   247\u2192\t\tfor _, key := range knownInstKeys {\n   248\u2192\t\t\tinstanceAddrs = append(instanceAddrs, resAddr.Instance(key))\n   249\u2192\t\t}\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Output
"   200\u2192\t\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.c\").Resource,\n   201\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   202\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n   203\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"c\",\"test_string\":\"x\"}`),\n   204\u2192\t\t\t\tDependencies: []addrs.ConfigResource{\n   205\u2192\t\t\t\t\tmustConfigResourceAddr(\"module.child.test_object.a\"),\n   206\u2192\t\t\t\t\tmustConfigResourceAddr(\"module.child.test_object.b\"),\n   207\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   208\u2192\t\t\t},\n   209\u2192\t\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   210\u2192\t\t)\n   211\u2192\t}\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192\tif err := (&AttachStateTransformer{State: state}).Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   214\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   215\u2192\t}\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192\ttf := &DestroyEdgeTransformer{}\n   218\u2192\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   219\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   220\u2192\t}\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192\t// The analyses done in the destroy edge transformer are between\n   223\u2192\t// not-yet-expanded objects, which is conservative and so it will generate\n   224\u2192\t// edges that aren't strictly necessary. As a special case we filter out\n   225\u2192\t// any edges that are between resources instances that are in different\n   226\u2192\t// instances of the same module, because those edges are never needed\n   227\u2192\t// (one instance of a module cannot depend on another instance of the\n   228\u2192\t// same module) and including them can, in complex cases, cause cycles due\n   229\u2192\t// to unnecessary interactions between destroyed and created module\n   230\u2192\t// instances in the same plan.\n   231\u2192\t//\n   232\u2192\t// Therefore below we expect to see the dependencies within each instance\n   233\u2192\t// of module.child reflected, but we should not see any dependencies\n   234\u2192\t// _between_ instances of module.child.\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n   237\u2192\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(`\n   238\u2192module.child[0].test_object.a (destroy)\n   239\u2192  module.child[0].test_object.b (destroy)\n   240\u2192  module.child[0].test_object.c (destroy)\n   241\u2192module.child[0].test_object.b (destroy)\n   242\u2192  module.child[0].test_object.c (destroy)\n   243\u2192module.child[0].test_object.c (destroy)\n   244\u2192module.child[1].test_object.a (destroy)\n   245\u2192  module.child[1].test_object.b (destroy)\n   246\u2192  module.child[1].test_object.c (destroy)\n   247\u2192module.child[1].test_object.b (destroy)\n   248\u2192  module.child[1].test_object.c (destroy)\n   249\u2192module.child[1].test_object.c (destroy)\n   250\u2192`)\n   251\u2192\tif actual != expected {\n   252\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong result\\n\\ngot:\\n%s\\n\\nwant:\\n%s\", actual, expected)\n   253\u2192\t}\n   254\u2192}\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192func TestDestroyEdgeTransformer_destroyThenUpdate(t *testing.T) {\n   257\u2192\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   258\u2192\tg.Add(testUpdateNode(\"test_object.A\"))\n   259\u2192\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(\"test_object.B\"))\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n   262\u2192\troot := state.EnsureModule(addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n   263\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   264\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.A\").Resource,\n   265\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   266\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n   267\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"A\",\"test_string\":\"old\"}`),\n   268\u2192\t\t},\n   269\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   270\u2192\t)\n   271\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   272\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.B\").Resource,\n   273\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   274\u2192\t\t\tStatus:       states.ObjectReady,\n   275\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON:    []byte(`{\"id\":\"B\",\"test_string\":\"x\"}`),\n   276\u2192\t\t\tDependencies: []addrs.ConfigResource{mustConfigResourceAddr(\"test_object.A\")},\n   277\u2192\t\t},\n   278\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   279\u2192\t)\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192\tif err := (&AttachStateTransformer{State: state}).Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   282\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   283\u2192\t}\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192\ttf := &DestroyEdgeTransformer{}\n   286\u2192\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   287\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   288\u2192\t}\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(`\n   291\u2192test_object.A\n   292\u2192  test_object.B (destroy)\n   293\u2192test_object.B (destroy)\n   294\u2192`)\n   295\u2192\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192\tif actual != expected {\n   298\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong result\\n\\ngot:\\n%s\\n\\nwant:\\n%s\", actual, expected)\n   299\u2192\t}\n   300\u2192}\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192func TestPruneUnusedNodesTransformer_rootModuleOutputValues(t *testing.T) {\n   303\u2192\t// This is a kinda-weird test case covering the very narrow situation\n   304\u2192\t// where a root module output value depends on a resource, where we\n   305\u2192\t// need to make sure that the output value doesn't block pruning of\n   306\u2192\t// the resource from the graph. This special case exists because although\n   307\u2192\t// root module objects are \"expanders\", they in practice always expand\n   308\u2192\t// to exactly one instance and so don't have the usual requirement of\n   309\u2192\t// needing to stick around in order to support downstream expanders\n   310\u2192\t// when there are e.g. nested expanding modules.\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192\t// In order to keep this test focused on the pruneUnusedNodesTransformer\n   313\u2192\t// as much as possible we're using a minimal graph construction here which\n   314\u2192\t// is just enough to get the nodes we need, but this does mean that this\n   315\u2192\t// test might be invalidated by future changes to the apply graph builder,\n   316\u2192\t// and so if something seems off here it might help to compare the\n   317\u2192\t// following with the real apply graph transformer and verify whether\n   318\u2192\t// this smaller construction is still realistic enough to be a valid test.\n   319\u2192\t// It might be valid to change or remove this test to \"make it work\", as\n   320\u2192\t// long as you verify that there is still _something_ upholding the\n   321\u2192\t// invariant that a root module output value should not block a resource\n   322\u2192\t// node from being pruned from the graph.\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192\tconcreteResource := func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n   325\u2192\t\treturn &nodeExpandApplyableResource{\n   326\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n   327\u2192\t\t}\n   328\u2192\t}\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192\tconcreteResourceInstance := func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n   331\u2192\t\treturn &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{\n   332\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   333\u2192\t\t}\n   334\u2192\t}\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192\tresourceInstAddr := mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test.a\")\n   337\u2192\tproviderCfgAddr := addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   338\u2192\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n   339\u2192\t\tProvider: addrs.MustParseProviderSourceString(\"foo/test\"),\n   340\u2192\t}\n   341\u2192\temptyObjDynamicVal, err := plans.NewDynamicValue(cty.EmptyObjectVal, cty.EmptyObject)\n   342\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   343\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   344\u2192\t}\n   345\u2192\tnullObjDynamicVal, err := plans.NewDynamicValue(cty.NullVal(cty.EmptyObject), cty.EmptyObject)\n   346\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   347\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   348\u2192\t}\n   349\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

abstract,\ninternal/terraform/transform_diff.go:\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\ninternal/terraform/node_resource_apply.go:\tconcreteEphemeral := func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\ninternal/terraform/node_resource_apply.go:\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\ninternal/terraform/node_resource_destroy_deposed_test.go:\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\ninternal/terraform/node_resource_destroy_deposed_test.go:\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\ninternal/terraform/node_resource_destroy_deposed_test.go:\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\ninternal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go:\tb.ConcreteResourceOrphan = func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\ninternal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go:\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\ninternal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go:\tb.ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed = func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance, key states.DeposedKey) dag.Vertex {\ninternal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go:\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\ninternal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go:\tb.ConcreteResourceInstance = func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\ninternal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go:\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\ninternal/terraform/node_resource_destroy_deposed.go:// GraphNodeReferenceable implementation, overriding the one from NodeAbstractResourceInstance\ninternal/terraform/node_resource_destroy_deposed.go:// GraphNodeReferencer implementation, overriding the {
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_diff.go",
  "limit": 150,
  "offset": 100

Output
"   100\u2192\t\tcase plans.Delete:\n   101\u2192\t\t\tdelete = true\n   102\u2192\t\tcase plans.DeleteThenCreate, plans.CreateThenDelete:\n   103\u2192\t\t\tupdate = true\n   104\u2192\t\t\tdelete = true\n   105\u2192\t\t\tcreateBeforeDestroy = (rc.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete)\n   106\u2192\t\tcase plans.Forget:\n   107\u2192\t\t\tforget = true\n   108\u2192\t\tdefault:\n   109\u2192\t\t\tupdate = true\n   110\u2192\t\t}\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192\t\t// A deposed instance may only have a change of Delete, Forget, or NoOp.\n   113\u2192\t\t// A NoOp can happen if the provider shows it no longer exists during\n   114\u2192\t\t// the most recent ReadResource operation.\n   115\u2192\t\tif dk != states.NotDeposed && !(rc.Action == plans.Delete || rc.Action == plans.Forget || rc.Action == plans.NoOp) {\n   116\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   117\u2192\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n   118\u2192\t\t\t\t\"Invalid planned change for deposed object\",\n   119\u2192\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The plan contains a non-remove change for %s deposed object %s. The only valid actions for a deposed object are to destroy it or remove it from state, so this is a bug in Terraform.\", addr, dk),\n   120\u2192\t\t\t))\n   121\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   122\u2192\t\t}\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192\t\t// If we're going to do a create_before_destroy Replace operation then\n   125\u2192\t\t// we need to allocate a DeposedKey to use to retain the\n   126\u2192\t\t// not-yet-destroyed prior object, so that the delete node can destroy\n   127\u2192\t\t// _that_ rather than the newly-created node, which will be current\n   128\u2192\t\t// by the time the delete node is visited.\n   129\u2192\t\tif update && delete && createBeforeDestroy {\n   130\u2192\t\t\t// In this case, variable dk will be the _pre-assigned_ DeposedKey\n   131\u2192\t\t\t// that must be used if the update graph node deposes the current\n   132\u2192\t\t\t// instance, which will then align with the same key we pass\n   133\u2192\t\t\t// into the destroy node to ensure we destroy exactly the deposed\n   134\u2192\t\t\t// object we expect.\n   135\u2192\t\t\tif state != nil {\n   136\u2192\t\t\t\tris := state.ResourceInstance(addr)\n   137\u2192\t\t\t\tif ris == nil {\n   138\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// Should never happen, since we don't plan to replace an\n   139\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// instance that doesn't exist yet.\n   140\u2192\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   141\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n   142\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Invalid planned change\",\n   143\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The plan contains a replace change for %s, which doesn't exist yet. This is a bug in Terraform.\", addr),\n   144\u2192\t\t\t\t\t))\n   145\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   146\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192\t\t\t\t// Allocating a deposed key separately from using it can be racy\n   149\u2192\t\t\t\t// in general, but we assume here that nothing except the apply\n   150\u2192\t\t\t\t// node we instantiate below will actually make new deposed objects\n   151\u2192\t\t\t\t// in practice, and so the set of already-used keys will not change\n   152\u2192\t\t\t\t// between now and then.\n   153\u2192\t\t\t\tdk = ris.FindUnusedDeposedKey()\n   154\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   155\u2192\t\t\t\t// If we have no state at all yet then we can use _any_\n   156\u2192\t\t\t\t// DeposedKey.\n   157\u2192\t\t\t\tdk = states.NewDeposedKey()\n   158\u2192\t\t\t}\n   159\u2192\t\t}\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192\t\tif update {\n   162\u2192\t\t\t// All actions except destroying the node type chosen by t.Concrete\n   163\u2192\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n   164\u2192\t\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n   165\u2192\t\t\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\n   166\u2192\t\t\t\tnode = f(abstract)\n   167\u2192\t\t\t}\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192\t\t\tif createBeforeDestroy {\n   170\u2192\t\t\t\t// We'll attach our pre-allocated DeposedKey to the node if\n   171\u2192\t\t\t\t// it supports that. NodeApplyableResourceInstance is the\n   172\u2192\t\t\t\t// specific concrete node type we are looking for here really,\n   173\u2192\t\t\t\t// since that's the only node type that might depose objects.\n   174\u2192\t\t\t\tif dn, ok := node.(GraphNodeDeposer); ok {\n   175\u2192\t\t\t\t\tdn.SetPreallocatedDeposedKey(dk)\n   176\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   177\u2192\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented by %s, deposing prior object to %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node), dk)\n   178\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   179\u2192\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n   180\u2192\t\t\t}\n   181\u2192\n   182\u2192\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n   183\u2192\t\t\tfor _, rsrcNode := range resourceNodes.Get(addr.ConfigResource()) {\n   184\u2192\t\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, rsrcNode))\n   185\u2192\t\t\t}\n   186\u2192\t\t}\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192\t\tif delete {\n   189\u2192\t\t\t// Destroying always uses a destroy-specific node type, though\n   190\u2192\t\t\t// which one depends on whether we're destroying a current object\n   191\u2192\t\t\t// or a deposed object.\n   192\u2192\t\t\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\n   193\u2192\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n   194\u2192\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n   195\u2192\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{\n   196\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n   197\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   198\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   199\u2192\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n   200\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n   201\u2192\t\t\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\n   202\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   203\u2192\t\t\t}\n   204\u2192\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n   205\u2192\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented for destruction by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n   206\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   207\u2192\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s deposed object %s will be represented for destruction by %s\", addr, dk, dag.VertexName(node))\n   208\u2192\t\t\t}\n   209\u2192\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n   210\u2192\t\t}\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192\t\tif forget {\n   213\u2192\t\t\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\n   214\u2192\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n   215\u2192\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n   216\u2192\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeForgetResourceInstance{\n   217\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n   218\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   219\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   220\u2192\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n   221\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n   222\u2192\t\t\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\n   223\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   224\u2192\t\t\t}\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented for forgetting by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n   227\u2192\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n   228\u2192\t\t}\n   229\u2192\n   230\u2192\t}\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer complete\")\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192\treturn diags.Err()\n   235\u2192}\n   236\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"   100\u2192\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n   101\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n   102\u2192\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\n   103\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   104\u2192\t}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\n   107\u2192\tinstanceAddrs := expander.ExpandResource(resAddr)\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192\tinstG, instDiags := n.ephemeralResourceInstanceSubgraph(resAddr, instanceAddrs)\n   110\u2192\tif instDiags.HasErrors() {\n   111\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(instDiags)\n   112\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   113\u2192\t}\n   114\u2192\tg.Subsume(&instG.AcyclicGraph.Graph)\n   115\u2192\treturn diags\n   116\u2192}\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) ephemeralResourceInstanceSubgraph(addr addrs.AbsResource, instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   119\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192\tconcreteEphemeral := func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n   122\u2192\t\ta.Config = n.Config\n   123\u2192\t\ta.ResolvedProvider = n.ResolvedProvider\n   124\u2192\t\ta.Schema = n.Schema\n   125\u2192\t\ta.ProvisionerSchemas = n.ProvisionerSchemas\n   126\u2192\t\ta.ProviderMetas = n.ProviderMetas\n   127\u2192\t\ta.dependsOn = n.dependsOn\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192\t\t// we still need the Plannable resource instance\n   130\u2192\t\treturn &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{\n   131\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   132\u2192\t\t}\n   133\u2192\t}\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192\t// Start creating the steps\n   136\u2192\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n   137\u2192\t\t// Expand the count or for_each (if present)\n   138\u2192\t\t&ResourceCountTransformer{\n   139\u2192\t\t\tConcrete:      concreteEphemeral,\n   140\u2192\t\t\tSchema:        n.Schema,\n   141\u2192\t\t\tAddr:          n.ResourceAddr(),\n   142\u2192\t\t\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\n   143\u2192\t\t},\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192\t\t// Targeting\n   146\u2192\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: n.Targets},\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192\t\t// Connect references so ordering is correct\n   149\u2192\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192\t\t// Make sure there is a single root\n   152\u2192\t\t&RootTransformer{},\n   153\u2192\t}\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192\t// Build the graph\n   156\u2192\tb := &BasicGraphBuilder{\n   157\u2192\t\tSteps: steps,\n   158\u2192\t\tName:  \"nodeExpandApplyEphemeralResource\",\n   159\u2192\t}\n   160\u2192\tgraph, graphDiags := b.Build(addr.Module)\n   161\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(graphDiags)\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192\treturn graph, diags\n   164\u2192}\n   165\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"   200\u2192\t\t\tConcreteDeposed: b.ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed,\n   201\u2192\t\t\tState:           b.State,\n   202\u2192\t\t},\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192\t\t// Attach the state\n   205\u2192\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: b.State},\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192\t\t// Create orphan output nodes\n   208\u2192\t\t&OrphanOutputTransformer{\n   209\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n   210\u2192\t\t\tState:    b.State,\n   211\u2192\t\t\tPlanning: true,\n   212\u2192\t\t},\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192\t\t// Attach the configuration to any resources\n   215\u2192\t\t&AttachResourceConfigTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192\t\t// add providers\n   218\u2192\t\ttransformProviders(b.ConcreteProvider, b.Config, b.ExternalProviderConfigs),\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192\t\t// Remove modules no longer present in the config\n   221\u2192\t\t&RemovedModuleTransformer{Config: b.Config, State: b.State},\n   222\u2192\n   223\u2192\t\t// Must attach schemas before ReferenceTransformer so that we can\n   224\u2192\t\t// analyze the configuration to find references.\n   225\u2192\t\t&AttachSchemaTransformer{Plugins: b.Plugins, Config: b.Config},\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192\t\t// Create expansion nodes for all of the module calls. This must\n   228\u2192\t\t// come after all other transformers that create nodes representing\n   229\u2192\t\t// objects that can belong to modules.\n   230\u2192\t\t&ModuleExpansionTransformer{Concrete: b.ConcreteModule, Config: b.Config},\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192\t\t// Plug in any external references.\n   233\u2192\t\t&ExternalReferenceTransformer{\n   234\u2192\t\t\tExternalReferences: b.ExternalReferences,\n   235\u2192\t\t},\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192\t\t&AttachDependenciesTransformer{},\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192\t\t// Make sure data sources are aware of any depends_on from the\n   242\u2192\t\t// configuration\n   243\u2192\t\t&attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer{},\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192\t\t// DestroyEdgeTransformer is only required during a plan so that the\n   246\u2192\t\t// TargetsTransformer can determine which nodes to keep in the graph.\n   247\u2192\t\t&DestroyEdgeTransformer{\n   248\u2192\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n   249\u2192\t\t},\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192\t\t&pruneUnusedNodesTransformer{\n   252\u2192\t\t\tskip: b.Operation != walkPlanDestroy,\n   253\u2192\t\t},\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192\t\t// Target\n   256\u2192\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: b.Targets},\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192\t\t// Detect when create_before_destroy must be forced on for a particular\n   259\u2192\t\t// node due to dependency edges, to avoid graph cycles during apply.\n   260\u2192\t\t&ForcedCBDTransformer{},\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192\t\t// Close any ephemeral resource instances.\n   263\u2192\t\t&ephemeralResourceCloseTransformer{skip: b.Operation == walkValidate},\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192\t\t// Close opened plugin connections\n   266\u2192\t\t&CloseProviderTransformer{},\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192\t\t// Close the root module\n   269\u2192\t\t&CloseRootModuleTransformer{},\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192\t\t// Perform the transitive reduction to make our graph a bit\n   272\u2192\t\t// more understandable if possible (it usually is possible).\n   273\u2192\t\t&TransitiveReductionTransformer{},\n   274\u2192\t}\n   275\u2192\n   276\u2192\treturn steps\n   277\u2192}\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) initPlan() {\n   280\u2192\tb.ConcreteProvider = func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n   281\u2192\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n   282\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n   283\u2192\t\t}\n   284\u2192\t}\n   285\u2192\n   286\u2192\tb.ConcreteResource = func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n   287\u2192\t\treturn &nodeExpandPlannableResource{\n   288\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n   289\u2192\t\t\tskipRefresh:          b.skipRefresh,\n   290\u2192\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:      b.skipPlanChanges,\n   291\u2192\t\t\tpreDestroyRefresh:    b.preDestroyRefresh,\n   292\u2192\t\t\tforceReplace:         b.ForceReplace,\n   293\u2192\t\t}\n   294\u2192\t}\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192\tb.ConcreteResourceOrphan = func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n   297\u2192\t\treturn &NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\n   298\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   299\u2192\t\t\tskipRefresh:                  b.skipRefresh,\n   300\u2192\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:              b.skipPlanChanges,\n   301\u2192\t\t\tforgetResources:              b.forgetResources,\n   302\u2192\t\t\tforgetModules:                b.forgetModules,\n   303\u2192\t\t}\n   304\u2192\t}\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192\tb.ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed = func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance, key states.DeposedKey) dag.Vertex {\n   307\u2192\t\treturn &NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n   308\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   309\u2192\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   key,\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192\t\t\tskipRefresh:     b.skipRefresh,\n   312\u2192\t\t\tskipPlanChanges: b.skipPlanChanges,\n   313\u2192\t\t\tforgetResources: b.forgetResources,\n   314\u2192\t\t\tforgetModules:   b.forgetModules,\n   315\u2192\t\t}\n   316\u2192\t}\n   317\u2192}\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) initDestroy() {\n   320\u2192\tb.initPlan()\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192\tb.ConcreteResourceInstance = func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n   323\u2192\t\treturn &NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance{\n   324\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   325\u2192\t\t\tskipRefresh:                  b.skipRefresh,\n   326\u2192\t\t}\n   327\u2192\t}\n   328\u2192}\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) initValidate() {\n   331\u2192\t// Set the provider to the normal provider. This will ask for input.\n   332\u2192\tb.ConcreteProvider = func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n   333\u2192\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n   334\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n   335\u2192\t\t}\n   336\u2192\t}\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192\tb.ConcreteResource = func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n   339\u2192\t\treturn &NodeValidatableResource{\n   340\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n   341\u2192\t\t}\n   342\u2192\t}\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192\tb.ConcreteModule = func(n *nodeExpandModule) dag.Vertex {\n   345\u2192\t\treturn &nodeValidateModule{\n   346\u2192\t\t\tnodeExpandModule: *n,\n   347\u2192\t\t}\n   348\u2192\t}\n   349\u2192}\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"   100\u2192\n   101\u2192\tconcreteResourceInstance := func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n   102\u2192\t\treturn &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{\n   103\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   104\u2192\t\t\tforceReplace:                 b.ForceReplace,\n   105\u2192\t\t}\n   106\u2192\t}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n   109\u2192\t\t// Creates all the resources represented in the config. During apply,\n   110\u2192\t\t// we use this just to ensure that the whole-resource metadata is\n   111\u2192\t\t// updated to reflect things such as whether the count argument is\n   112\u2192\t\t// set in config, or which provider configuration manages each resource.\n   113\u2192\t\t&ConfigTransformer{\n   114\u2192\t\t\tConcrete: concreteResource,\n   115\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n   116\u2192\t\t},\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192\t\t// Add dynamic values\n   119\u2192\t\t&RootVariableTransformer{\n   120\u2192\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n   121\u2192\t\t\tRawValues:    b.RootVariableValues,\n   122\u2192\t\t\tDestroyApply: b.Operation == walkDestroy,\n   123\u2192\t\t},\n   124\u2192\t\t&ModuleVariableTransformer{\n   125\u2192\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n   126\u2192\t\t\tDestroyApply: b.Operation == walkDestroy,\n   127\u2192\t\t},\n   128\u2192\t\t&variableValidationTransformer{},\n   129\u2192\t\t&LocalTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n   130\u2192\t\t&OutputTransformer{\n   131\u2192\t\t\tConfig:     b.Config,\n   132\u2192\t\t\tDestroying: b.Operation == walkDestroy,\n   133\u2192\t\t\tOverrides:  b.Overrides,\n   134\u2192\t\t},\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192\t\t// Creates all the resource instances represented in the diff, along\n   137\u2192\t\t// with dependency edges against the whole-resource nodes added by\n   138\u2192\t\t// ConfigTransformer above.\n   139\u2192\t\t&DiffTransformer{\n   140\u2192\t\t\tConcrete: concreteResourceInstance,\n   141\u2192\t\t\tState:    b.State,\n   142\u2192\t\t\tChanges:  b.Changes,\n   143\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n   144\u2192\t\t},\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192\t\t// Creates nodes for all the deferred changes.\n   147\u2192\t\t&DeferredTransformer{\n   148\u2192\t\t\tDeferredChanges: b.DeferredChanges,\n   149\u2192\t\t},\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

  "file": {
    "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/langrefs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// ConcreteResourceNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert an\n// abstract resource to a concrete one of some type.\ntype ConcreteResourceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex\n\n// GraphNodeConfigResource is implemented by any nodes that represent a resource.\n// The type of operation cannot be assumed, only that this node represents\n// the given resource.\ntype GraphNodeConfigResource interface {\n\tResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource\n}\n\n// ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert an\n// abstract resource instance to a concrete one of some type.\ntype ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex\n\n// GraphNodeResourceInstance is implemented by any nodes that represent\n// a resource instance. A single resource may have multiple instances if,\n// for example, the \"count\" or \"for_each\" argument is used for it in\n// configuration.\ntype GraphNodeResourceInstance interface {\n\tResourceInstanceAddr() addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// StateDependencies returns any inter-resource dependencies that are\n\t// stored in the state.\n\tStateDependencies() []addrs.ConfigResource\n}\n\n// NodeAbstractResource represents a resource that has no associated\n// operations. It registers all the interfaces for a resource that common\n// across multiple operation types.\ntype NodeAbstractResource struct {\n\tAddr addrs.ConfigResource\n\n\t// The fields below will be automatically set using the Attach\n\t// interfaces if you're running those transforms, but also be explicitly\n\t// set if you already have that information.\n\n\tSchema        *configschema.Block // Schema for processing the configuration body\n\tSchemaVersion uint64              // Schema version of \"Schema\", as decided by the provider\n\n\t// Config and RemovedConfig are mutally-exclusive, because a\n\t// resource can't be both declared and removed at the same time.\n\tConfig        *configs.Resource // Config is the resource in the config, if any\n\tRemovedConfig *configs.Removed  // RemovedConfig is the \"removed\" block for this resource, if any\n\n\t// ProviderMetas is the provider_meta configs for the module this resource belongs to\n\tProviderMetas map[addrs.Provider]*configs.ProviderMeta\n\n\tProvisionerSchemas map[string]*configschema.Block\n\n\t// Set from GraphNodeTargetable\n\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n\n\t// Set from AttachDataResourceDependsOn\n\tdependsOn []addrs.ConfigResource\n\n\t// The address of the provider this resource will use\n\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n\t// storedProviderConfig is the provider address retrieved from the\n\t// state. This is defined here for access within the ProvidedBy method, but\n\t// will be set from the embedding instance type when the state is attached.\n\tstoredProviderConfig addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n\n\t// This resource may expand into instances which need to be imported.\n\timportTargets []*ImportTarget\n\n\t// generateConfigPath tells this node which file to write generated config\n\t// into. If empty, then config should not be generated.\n\tgenerateConfigPath string\n\n\tforceCreateBeforeDestroy bool\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable               = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer                  = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeImportReferencer            = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer            = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer         = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource              = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig        = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceSchema        = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachProvisionerSchema     = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachProviderMetaConfigs   = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeTargetable                  = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ graphNodeAttachDataResourceDependsOn = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ dag.GraphNodeDotter                  = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD                = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n)\n\n// NewNodeAbstractResource creates an abstract resource graph node for\n// the given absolute resource address.\nfunc NewNodeAbstractResource(addr addrs.ConfigResource) *NodeAbstractResource {\n\treturn &NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\tAddr: addr,\n\t}\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance            = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable             = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer                = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer          = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer       = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource            = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance          = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceState       = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig      = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceSchema      = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachProvisionerSchema   = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachProviderMetaConfigs = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeTargetable                = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeOverridable               = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ dag.GraphNodeDotter                = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) Name() string {\n\treturn n.ResourceAddr().String()\n}\n\n// GraphNodeModulePath\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ModulePath() addrs.Module {\n\treturn n.Addr.Module\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferenceable\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n\treturn []addrs.Referenceable{n.Addr.Resource}\n}\n\n// CreateBeforeDestroy returns this node's CreateBeforeDestroy status.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {",

  "originalFile": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/langrefs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// ConcreteResourceNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert an\n// abstract resource to a concrete one of some type.\ntype ConcreteResourceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex\n\n// GraphNodeConfigResource is implemented by any nodes that represent a resource.\n// The type of operation cannot be assumed, only that this node represents\n// the given resource.\ntype GraphNodeConfigResource interface {\n\tResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource\n}\n\n// ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert an\n// abstract resource instance to a concrete one of some type.\ntype ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex\n\n// GraphNodeResourceInstance is implemented by any nodes that represent\n// a resource instance. A single resource may have multiple instances if,\n// for example, the \"count\" or \"for_each\" argument is used for it in\n// configuration.\ntype GraphNodeResourceInstance interface {\n\tResourceInstanceAddr() addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// StateDependencies returns any inter-resource dependencies that are\n\t// stored in the state.\n\tStateDependencies() []addrs.ConfigResource\n}\n\n// NodeAbstractResource represents a resource that has no associated\n// operations. It registers all the interfaces for a resource that common\n// across multiple operation types.\ntype NodeAbstractResource struct {\n\tAddr addrs.ConfigResource\n\n\t// The fields below will be automatically set using the Attach\n\t// interfaces if you're running those transforms, but also be explicitly\n\t// set if you already have that information.\n\n\tSchema        *configschema.Block // Schema for processing the configuration body\n\tSchemaVersion uint64              // Schema version of \"Schema\", as decided by the provider\n\n\t// Config and RemovedConfig are mutally-exclusive, because a\n\t// resource can't be both declared and removed at the same time.\n\tConfig        *configs.Resource // Config is the resource in the config, if any\n\tRemovedConfig *configs.Removed  // RemovedConfig is the \"removed\" block for this resource, if any\n\n\t// ProviderMetas is the provider_meta configs for the module this resource belongs to\n\tProviderMetas map[addrs.Provider]*configs.ProviderMeta\n\n\tProvisionerSchemas map[string]*configschema.Block\n\n\t// Set from GraphNodeTargetable\n\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n\n\t// Set from AttachDataResourceDependsOn\n\tdependsOn []addrs.ConfigResource\n\n\t// The address of the provider this resource will use\n\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n\t// storedProviderConfig is the provider address retrieved from the\n\t// state. This is defined here for access within the ProvidedBy method, but\n\t// will be set from the embedding instance type when the state is attached.\n\tstoredProviderConfig addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n\n\t// This resource may expand into instances which need to be imported.\n\timportTargets []*ImportTarget\n\n\t// generateConfigPath tells this node which file to write generated config\n\t// into. If empty, then config should not be generated.\n\tgenerateConfigPath string\n\n\tforceCreateBeforeDestroy bool\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable               = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer                  = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeImportReferencer            = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer            = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer         = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource              = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig        = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceSchema        = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachProvisionerSchema     = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachProviderMetaConfigs   = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeTargetable                  = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ graphNodeAttachDataResourceDependsOn = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ dag.GraphNodeDotter                  = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD                = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\n)\n\n// NewNodeAbstractResource creates an abstract resource graph node for\n// the given absolute resource address.\nfunc NewNodeAbstractResource(addr addrs.ConfigResource) *NodeAbstractResource {\n\treturn &NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\tAddr: addr,\n\t}\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance            = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable             = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer                = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer          = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer       = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource            = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance          = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceState       = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig      = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceSchema      = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachProvisionerSchema   = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachProviderMetaConfigs = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeTargetable                = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeOverridable               = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ dag.GraphNodeDotter                = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) Name() string {\n\treturn n.ResourceAddr().String()\n}\n\n// GraphNodeModulePath\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ModulePath() addrs.Module {\n\treturn n.Addr.Module\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferenceable\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n\treturn []addrs.Referenceable{n.Addr.Resource}\n}\n\n// CreateBeforeDestroy returns this node's CreateBeforeDestroy status.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\n\tif n.forceCreateBeforeDestroy {\n\t\treturn n.forceCreateBeforeDestroy\n\t}\n\n\tif n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil {\n\t\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\n\tn.forceCreateBeforeDestroy = v\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferencer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\n\t// If we have a config then we prefer to use that.\n\tif c := n.Config; c != nil {\n\t\tresult = append(result, n.DependsOn()...)\n\n\t\tif n.Schema == nil {\n\t\t\t// Should never happen, but we'll log if it does so that we can\n\t\t\t// see this easily when debugging.\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] no schema is attached to %s, so config references cannot be detected\", n.Name())\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, c.Count)\n\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, c.ForEach)\n\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\n\t\tfor _, expr := range c.TriggersReplacement {\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, expr)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// ReferencesInBlock() requires a schema\n\t\tif n.Schema != nil {\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, c.Config, n.Schema)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif c.Managed != nil {\n\t\t\tif c.Managed.Connection != nil {\n\t\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, c.Managed.Connection.Config, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)\n\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tfor _, p := range c.Managed.Provisioners {\n\t\t\t\tif p.When != configs.ProvisionerWhenCreate {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif p.Connection != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, p.Connection.Config, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)\n\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tschema := n.ProvisionerSchemas[p.Type]\n\t\t\t\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] no schema for provisioner %q is attached to %s, so provisioner block references cannot be detected\", p.Type, n.Name())\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, p.Config, schema)\n\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, check := range c.Preconditions {\n\t\t\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.Condition)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.ErrorMessage)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor _, check := range c.Postconditions {\n\t\t\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.Condition)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.ErrorMessage)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ImportReferences() []*addrs.Reference {\n\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\n\tfor _, importTarget := range n.importTargets {\n\t\t// legacy import won't have any config\n\t\tif importTarget.Config == nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, importTarget.Config.ID)\n\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, importTarget.Config.ForEach)\n\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) DependsOn() []*addrs.Reference {\n\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\n\tif c := n.Config; c != nil {\n\n\t\tfor _, traversal := range c.DependsOn {\n\t\t\tref, diags := addrs.ParseRef(traversal)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\t// We ignore this here, because this isn't a suitable place to return\n\t\t\t\t// errors. This situation should be caught and rejected during\n\t\t\t\t// validation.\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Can't parse %#v from depends_on as reference: %s\", traversal, diags.Err())\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, ref)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) SetProvider(p addrs.AbsProviderConfig) {\n\tn.ResolvedProvider = p\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {\n\t// Once the provider is fully resolved, we can return the known value.\n\tif n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn n.ResolvedProvider, true\n\t}\n\n\t// If we have a config we prefer that above all else\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\trelAddr := n.Config.ProviderConfigAddr()\n\t\treturn addrs.LocalProviderConfig{\n\t\t\tLocalName: relAddr.LocalName,\n\t\t\tAlias:     relAddr.Alias,\n\t\t}, false\n\t}\n\n\t// See if we have a valid provider config from the state.\n\tif n.storedProviderConfig.Provider.Type != \"\" {\n\t\t// An address from the state must match exactly, since we must ensure\n\t\t// we refresh/destroy a resource with the same provider configuration\n\t\t// that created it.\n\t\treturn n.storedProviderConfig, true\n\t}\n\n\t// We might have an import target that is providing a specific provider,\n\t// this is okay as we know there is nothing else potentially providing a\n\t// provider configuration.\n\tif len(n.importTargets) > 0 {\n\t\t// The import targets should either all be defined via config or none\n\t\t// of them should be. They should also all have the same provider, so it\n\t\t// shouldn't matter which we check here, as they'll all give the same.\n\t\tif n.importTargets[0].Config != nil && n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef != nil {\n\t\t\treturn addrs.LocalProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tLocalName: n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef.Name,\n\t\t\t\tAlias:     n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef.Alias,\n\t\t\t}, false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// No provider configuration found; return a default address\n\treturn addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\tProvider: n.Provider(),\n\t\tModule:   n.ModulePath(),\n\t}, false\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) Provider() addrs.Provider {\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\treturn n.Config.Provider\n\t}\n\tif n.storedProviderConfig.Provider.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn n.storedProviderConfig.Provider\n\t}\n\n\tif len(n.importTargets) > 0 {\n\t\t// The import targets should either all be defined via config or none\n\t\t// of them should be. They should also all have the same provider, so it\n\t\t// shouldn't matter which we check here, as they'll all give the same.\n\t\tif n.importTargets[0].Config != nil {\n\t\t\treturn n.importTargets[0].Config.Provider\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn addrs.ImpliedProviderForUnqualifiedType(n.Addr.Resource.ImpliedProvider())\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ProvisionedBy() []string {\n\t// If we have no configuration, then we have no provisioners\n\tif n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Build the list of provisioners we need based on the configuration.\n\t// It is okay to have duplicates here.\n\tresult := make([]string, len(n.Config.Managed.Provisioners))\n\tfor i, p := range n.Config.Managed.Provisioners {\n\t\tresult[i] = p.Type\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachProvisionerSchema(name string, schema *configschema.Block) {\n\tif n.ProvisionerSchemas == nil {\n\t\tn.ProvisionerSchemas = make(map[string]*configschema.Block)\n\t}\n\tn.ProvisionerSchemas[name] = schema\n}\n\n// GraphNodeResource\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource {\n\treturn n.Addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeTargetable\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) SetTargets(targets []addrs.Targetable) {\n\tn.Targets = targets\n}\n\n// graphNodeAttachDataResourceDependsOn\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachDataResourceDependsOn(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\n\tn.dependsOn = deps\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachResourceConfig(c *configs.Resource, rc *configs.Removed) {\n\tn.Config = c\n\tn.RemovedConfig = rc\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachResourceSchema impl\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachResourceSchema(schema *configschema.Block, version uint64) {\n\tn.Schema = schema\n\tn.SchemaVersion = version\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachProviderMetaConfigs impl\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachProviderMetaConfigs(c map[addrs.Provider]*configs.ProviderMeta) {\n\tn.ProviderMetas = c\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDotter impl.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) DotNode(name string, opts *dag.DotOpts) *dag.DotNode {\n\treturn &dag.DotNode{\n\t\tName: name,\n\t\tAttrs: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"label\": n.Name(),\n\t\t\t\"shape\": \"box\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// recordResourceData records some metadata for the resource as a whole in\n// various locations. This currently includes adding resource expansion info to\n// the instance expander, and recording the provider used in the state.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) recordResourceData(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\n\t// We'll record our expansion decision in the shared \"expander\" object\n\t// so that later operations (i.e. DynamicExpand and expression evaluation)\n\t// can refer to it. Since this node represents the abstract module, we need\n\t// to expand the module here to create all resources.\n\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n\n\t// Allowing unknown values in count and for_each is a top-level plan option.\n\t//\n\t// If this is false then the codepaths that handle unknown values below\n\t// become unreachable, because the evaluate functions will reject unknown\n\t// values as an error.\n\tallowUnknown := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.Count != nil:\n\t\tcount, countDiags := evaluateCountExpression(n.Config.Count, ctx, allowUnknown)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(countDiags)\n\t\tif countDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif count >= 0 {\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceCount(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource, count)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// -1 represents \"unknown\"\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceCountUnknown(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.ForEach != nil:\n\t\tforEach, known, forEachDiags := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, allowUnknown)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(forEachDiags)\n\t\tif forEachDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// This method takes care of all of the business logic of updating this\n\t\t// while ensuring that any existing instances are preserved, etc.\n\t\tif known {\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceForEach(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource, forEach)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceForEachUnknown(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\n\t\t}\n\n\tdefault:\n\t\texpander.SetResourceSingle(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\n\t}\n\n\tif addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n\t\t// ephemeral resources are not included in the state\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tstate := ctx.State()\n\tstate.SetResourceProvider(addr, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\n// readResourceInstanceState reads the current object for a specific instance in\n// the state.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) readResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceState: reading state for %s\", addr)\n\n\tsrc := ctx.State().ResourceInstanceObject(addr, addrs.NotDeposed)\n\tif src == nil {\n\t\t// Presumably we only have deposed objects, then.\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceState: no state present for %s\", addr)\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\tschema, currentVersion := (providerSchema).SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.ContainingResource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Shouldn't happen since we should've failed long ago if no schema is present\n\t\treturn nil, diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"no schema available for %s while reading state; this is a bug in Terraform and should be reported\", addr))\n\t}\n\tsrc, upgradeDiags := upgradeResourceState(addr, provider, src, schema, currentVersion)\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\tupgradeDiags = upgradeDiags.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, addr.String())\n\t}\n\tdiags = diags.Append(upgradeDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tobj, err := src.Decode(schema.ImpliedType())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn obj, diags\n}\n\n// readResourceInstanceStateDeposed reads the deposed object for a specific\n// instance in the state.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) readResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, key states.DeposedKey) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tif key == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\treturn nil, diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"readResourceInstanceStateDeposed used with no instance key; this is a bug in Terraform and should be reported\"))\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceStateDeposed: reading state for %s deposed object %s\", addr, key)\n\n\tsrc := ctx.State().ResourceInstanceObject(addr, key)\n\tif src == nil {\n\t\t// Presumably we only have deposed objects, then.\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceStateDeposed: no state present for %s deposed object %s\", addr, key)\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tschema, currentVersion := (providerSchema).SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.ContainingResource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Shouldn't happen since we should've failed long ago if no schema is present\n\t\treturn nil, diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"no schema available for %s while reading state; this is a bug in Terraform and should be reported\", addr))\n\n\t}\n\n\tsrc, upgradeDiags := upgradeResourceState(addr, provider, src, schema, currentVersion)\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\tupgradeDiags = upgradeDiags.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, addr.String())\n\t}\n\tdiags = diags.Append(upgradeDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t// Note that we don't have any channel to return warnings here. We'll\n\t\t// accept that for now since warnings during a schema upgrade would\n\t\t// be pretty weird anyway, since this operation is supposed to seem\n\t\t// invisible to the user.\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tobj, err := src.Decode(schema.ImpliedType())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn obj, diags\n}\n\n// graphNodesAreResourceInstancesInDifferentInstancesOfSameModule is an\n// annoyingly-task-specific helper function that returns true if and only if\n// the following conditions hold:\n//   - Both of the given vertices represent specific resource instances, as\n//     opposed to unexpanded resources or any other non-resource-related object.\n//   - The module instance addresses for both of the resource instances belong\n//     to the same static module.\n//   - The module instance addresses for both of the resource instances are\n//     not equal, indicating that they belong to different instances of the\n//     same module.\n//\n// This result can be used as a way to compensate for the effects of\n// conservative analysis passes in our graph builders which make their\n// decisions based only on unexpanded addresses, often so that they can behave\n// correctly for interactions between expanded and not-yet-expanded objects.\n//\n// Callers of this helper function will typically skip adding an edge between\n// the two given nodes if this function returns true.\nfunc graphNodesAreResourceInstancesInDifferentInstancesOfSameModule(a, b dag.Vertex) bool {\n\taRI, aOK := a.(GraphNodeResourceInstance)\n\tbRI, bOK := b.(GraphNodeResourceInstance)\n\tif !(aOK && bOK) {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\taModInst := aRI.ResourceInstanceAddr().Module\n\tbModInst := bRI.ResourceInstanceAddr().Module\n\taMod := aModInst.Module()\n\tbMod := bModInst.Module()\n\tif !aMod.Equal(bMod) {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\treturn !aModInst.Equal(bModInst)\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
