"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    17\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    18\u2192)\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192// nodeExpandPlannableResource represents an addrs.ConfigResource and implements\n    21\u2192// DynamicExpand to a subgraph containing all of the addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n    22\u2192// resulting from both the containing module and resource-specific expansion.\n    23\u2192type nodeExpandPlannableResource struct {\n    24\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResource\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192\t// ForceCreateBeforeDestroy might be set via our GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\n    28\u2192\t// during graph construction, if dependencies require us to force this\n    29\u2192\t// on regardless of what the configuration says.\n    30\u2192\tForceCreateBeforeDestroy *bool\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances\n    33\u2192\tskipRefresh bool\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192\t// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions\n    38\u2192\t// for any instances.\n    39\u2192\tskipPlanChanges bool\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192\t// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to\n    42\u2192\t// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\n    43\u2192\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\n    44\u2192\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\n    45\u2192\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192\t// We attach dependencies to the Resource during refresh, since the\n    48\u2192\t// instances are instantiated during DynamicExpand.\n    49\u2192\t// FIXME: These would be better off converted to a generic Set data\n    50\u2192\t// structure in the future, as we need to compare for equality and take the\n    51\u2192\t// union of multiple groups of dependencies.\n    52\u2192\tdependencies []addrs.ConfigResource\n    53\u2192}\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192var (\n    56\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD         = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n    57\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable    = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n    58\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n    59\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n    60\u2192\t_ GraphNodeImportReferencer     = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n    61\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n    62\u2192\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n    63\u2192\t_ GraphNodeAttachDependencies   = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n    64\u2192\t_ GraphNodeTargetable           = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n    65\u2192\t_ graphNodeExpandsInstances     = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n    66\u2192)\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) Name() string {\n    69\u2192\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.Name() + \" (expand)\"\n    70\u2192}\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandsInstances() {\n    73\u2192}\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192// GraphNodeAttachDependencies\n    76\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) AttachDependencies(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\n    77\u2192\tn.dependencies = deps\n    78\u2192}\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\n    81\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\n    82\u2192\tif n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy != nil {\n    83\u2192\t\treturn *n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy\n    84\u2192\t}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192\t// If we have no config, we just assume no\n    87\u2192\tif n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n    88\u2192\t\treturn false\n    89\u2192\t}\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\n    92\u2192}\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\n    95\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\n    96\u2192\tn.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy = &v\n    97\u2192\treturn nil\n    98\u2192}\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   101\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192\t// First, make sure the count and the foreach don't refer to the same\n   104\u2192\t// resource. The config maybe nil if we are generating configuration, or\n   105\u2192\t// deleting a resource.\n   106\u2192\tif n.Config != nil {\n   107\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateMetaSelfRef(n.Addr.Resource, n.Config.Count))\n   108\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateMetaSelfRef(n.Addr.Resource, n.Config.ForEach))\n   109\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   110\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, diags\n   111\u2192\t\t}\n   112\u2192\t}\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192\t// Expand the current module.\n   115\u2192\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n   116\u2192\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192\t// The possibility of partial-expanded modules and resources is guarded by a\n   119\u2192\t// top-level option for the whole plan, so that we can preserve mainline\n   120\u2192\t// behavior for the modules runtime. So, we currently branch off into an\n   121\u2192\t// entirely-separate codepath in those situations, at the expense of\n   122\u2192\t// duplicating some of the logic for behavior this method would normally\n   123\u2192\t// handle.\n   124\u2192\tif ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed() { // Expand the imports for this resource.\n   125\u2192\t\tknownImports, unknownImports, importDiags := n.expandResourceImports(ctx, true)\n   126\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192\t\tpem := expander.UnknownModuleInstances(n.Addr.Module, false)\n   129\u2192\t\tg, expandDiags := n.dynamicExpandPartial(ctx, moduleInstances, pem, knownImports, unknownImports)\n   130\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(expandDiags)\n   131\u2192\t\treturn g, diags\n   132\u2192\t}\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192\t// Expand the imports for this resource.\n   135\u2192\timports, unknownImports, importDiags := n.expandResourceImports(ctx, false)\n   136\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192\t// Since allowUnknown was set to false in expandResourceImports, we should\n   139\u2192\t// not have any unknown imports.\n   140\u2192\tif unknownImports.Len() > 0 {\n   141\u2192\t\tpanic(\"unexpected unknown imports\")\n   142\u2192\t}\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192\tg, expandDiags := n.dynamicExpand(ctx, moduleInstances, imports)\n   145\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(expandDiags)\n   146\u2192\treturn g, diags\n   147\u2192}\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192// Import blocks are expanded in conjunction with their associated resource block.\n   150\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceImports(ctx EvalContext, allowUnknown bool) (addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   151\u2192\t// Imports maps the target address to an import ID.\n   152\u2192\tknownImports := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]()\n   153\u2192\tunknownImports := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]]()\n   154\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192\tif len(n.importTargets) == 0 {\n   157\u2192\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n   158\u2192\t}\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192\t// Import blocks are only valid within the root module, and must be\n   161\u2192\t// evaluated within that context\n   162\u2192\tctx = evalContextForModuleInstance(ctx, addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192\tstate := ctx.State()\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192\tfor _, imp := range n.importTargets {\n   167\u2192\t\tif imp.Config == nil {\n   168\u2192\t\t\t// if we have a legacy addr, it was supplied on the commandline so\n   169\u2192\t\t\t// there is nothing to expand\n   170\u2192\t\t\tif !imp.LegacyAddr.Equal(addrs.AbsResourceInstance{}) {\n   171\u2192\t\t\t\tknownImports.Put(imp.LegacyAddr, cty.StringVal(imp.IDString))\n   172\u2192\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n   173\u2192\t\t\t}\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192\t\t\t// legacy import tests may have no configuration\n   176\u2192\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] no configuration for import target %#v\", imp)\n   177\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   178\u2192\t\t}\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192\t\tif imp.Config.ForEach == nil {\n   181\u2192\t\t\ttraversal, hds := hcl.AbsTraversalForExpr(imp.Config.To)\n   182\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(hds)\n   183\u2192\t\t\tto, tds := addrs.ParseAbsResourceInstance(traversal)\n   184\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tds)\n   185\u2192\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   186\u2192\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n   187\u2192\t\t\t}\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateImportTargetExpansion(n.Config, to, imp.Config.To))\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192\t\t\timportID, evalDiags := evaluateImportIdExpression(imp.Config.ID, ctx, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey, allowUnknown)\n   192\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n   193\u2192\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   194\u2192\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n   195\u2192\t\t\t}\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192\t\t\tknownImports.Put(to, importID)\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] expandResourceImports: found single import target %s\", to)\n   200\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   201\u2192\t\t}\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192\t\tforEachData, known, forEachDiags := newForEachEvaluator(imp.Config.ForEach, ctx, allowUnknown).ImportValues()\n   204\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(forEachDiags)\n   205\u2192\t\tif forEachDiags.HasErrors() {\n   206\u2192\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n   207\u2192\t\t}\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192\t\tif !known {\n   210\u2192\t\t\t// Then we need to parse the target address as a PartialResource\n   211\u2192\t\t\t// instead of a known resource.\n   212\u2192\t\t\taddr, evalDiags := evalImportUnknownToExpression(imp.Config.To)\n   213\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n   214\u2192\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   215\u2192\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n   216\u2192\t\t\t}\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192\t\t\t// We're going to work out which instances this import block might\n   219\u2192\t\t\t// target actually already exist.\n   220\u2192\t\t\tknownInstances := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192\t\t\tcfg := addr.ConfigResource()\n   223\u2192\t\t\tmodInsts := state.ModuleInstances(cfg.Module)\n   224\u2192\t\t\tfor _, modInst := range modInsts {\n   225\u2192\t\t\t\tabs := cfg.Absolute(modInst)\n   226\u2192\t\t\t\tresource := state.Resource(cfg.Absolute(modInst))\n   227\u2192\t\t\t\tif resource == nil {\n   228\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// Then we are creating every instance of this resource.\n   229\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   230\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192\t\t\t\tfor inst := range resource.Instances {\n   233\u2192\t\t\t\t\tknownInstances.Add(abs.Instance(inst))\n   234\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   235\u2192\t\t\t}\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192\t\t\tunknownImports.Put(addr, knownInstances)\n   238\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   239\u2192\t\t}\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192\t\tfor _, keyData := range forEachData {\n   242\u2192\t\t\tres, evalDiags := evalImportToExpression(imp.Config.To, keyData)\n   243\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n   244\u2192\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   245\u2192\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n   246\u2192\t\t\t}\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateImportTargetExpansion(n.Config, res, imp.Config.To))\n   249\u2192\n   250\u2192\t\t\timportID, evalDiags := evaluateImportIdExpression(imp.Config.ID, ctx, keyData, allowUnknown)\n   251\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n   252\u2192\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   253\u2192\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n   254\u2192\t\t\t}\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192\t\t\tknownImports.Put(res, importID)\n   257\u2192\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] expandResourceImports: expanded import target %s\", res)\n   258\u2192\t\t}\n   259\u2192\t}\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192\t// filter out any known import which already exist in state\n   262\u2192\tfor _, el := range knownImports.Elements() {\n   263\u2192\t\tif state.ResourceInstance(el.Key) != nil {\n   264\u2192\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] expandResourceImports: skipping import address %s already in state\", el.Key)\n   265\u2192\t\t\tknownImports.Remove(el.Key)\n   266\u2192\t\t}\n   267\u2192\t}\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n   270\u2192}\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192// validateExpandedImportTargets checks that all expanded imports correspond to\n   273\u2192// a configured instance.\n   274\u2192//\n   275\u2192// This function is only called from within the dynamicExpand method, the\n   276\u2192// import validation is inlined within the dynamicExpandPartial method for the\n   277\u2192// alternate code path.\n   278\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) validateExpandedImportTargets(expandedImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], expandedInstances addrs.Set[addrs.Checkable]) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n   279\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192\tfor _, addr := range expandedImports.Keys() {\n   282\u2192\t\tif !expandedInstances.Has(addr) {\n   283\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   284\u2192\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n   285\u2192\t\t\t\t\"Configuration for import target does not exist\",\n   286\u2192\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The configuration for the given import %s does not exist. All target instances must have an associated configuration to be imported.\", addr),\n   287\u2192\t\t\t))\n   288\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   289\u2192\t\t}\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192\t\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath == \"\" && len(n.importTargets) == 1 {\n   292\u2192\t\t\t// we have no config and aren't generating any. This isn't caught during\n   293\u2192\t\t\t// validation because generateConfigPath is only a plan option. If we\n   294\u2192\t\t\t// got this far however, it means this node is eligible for config\n   295\u2192\t\t\t// generation, so suggest it to the user.\n   296\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n   297\u2192\t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n   298\u2192\t\t\t\tSummary:  \"Configuration for import target does not exist\",\n   299\u2192\t\t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\"The configuration for the given import target %s does not exist. If you wish to automatically generate config for this resource, use the -generate-config-out option within terraform plan. Otherwise, make sure the target resource exists within your configuration. For example:\\n\\n  terraform plan -generate-config-out=generated.tf\", n.Addr),\n   300\u2192\t\t\t\tSubject:  n.importTargets[0].Config.To.Range().Ptr(),\n   301\u2192\t\t\t})\n   302\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   303\u2192\t\t}\n   304\u2192\t}\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192\treturn diags\n   307\u2192}\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) findOrphans(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance) []*states.Resource {\n   310\u2192\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n   311\u2192\t\t// ephemeral resources don't exist in state\n   312\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   313\u2192\t}\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192\tvar orphans []*states.Resource\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192\t// Lock the state while we inspect it\n   318\u2192\tsMgr := ctx.State()\n   319\u2192\tstate := sMgr.Lock()\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192\tfor _, res := range state.Resources(n.Addr) {\n   322\u2192\t\tfound := false\n   323\u2192\t\tfor _, m := range moduleInstances {\n   324\u2192\t\t\tif m.Equal(res.Addr.Module) {\n   325\u2192\t\t\t\tfound = true\n   326\u2192\t\t\t\tbreak\n   327\u2192\t\t\t}\n   328\u2192\t\t}\n   329\u2192\t\t// The module instance of the resource in the state doesn't exist\n   330\u2192\t\t// in the current config, so this whole resource is orphaned.\n   331\u2192\t\tif !found {\n   332\u2192\t\t\torphans = append(orphans, res)\n   333\u2192\t\t}\n   334\u2192\t}\n   335\u2192\tsMgr.Unlock()\n   336\u2192\n   337\u2192\treturn orphans\n   338\u2192}\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   341\u2192\tvar g Graph\n   342\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192\torphans := n.findOrphans(ctx, moduleInstances)\n   345\u2192\n   346\u2192\tfor _, res := range orphans {\n   347\u2192\t\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n   348\u2192\t\t\taddr := res.Addr.Instance(key)\n   349\u2192\t\t\tabs := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addr)\n   350\u2192\t\t\tabs.AttachResourceState(res)\n   351\u2192\t\t\tn := n.concreteResourceOrphan(abs)\n   352\u2192\t\t\tg.Add(n)\n   353\u2192\t\t}\n   354\u2192\t}\n   355\u2192\n   356\u2192\t// The above dealt with the expansion of the containing module, so now\n   357\u2192\t// we need to deal with the expansion of the resource itself across all\n   358\u2192\t// instances of the module.\n   359\u2192\t//\n   360\u2192\t// We'll gather up all of the leaf instances we learn about along the way\n   361\u2192\t// so that we can inform the checks subsystem of which instances it should\n   362\u2192\t// be expecting check results for, below.\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192\texpandedInstances := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.Checkable]()\n   365\u2192\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n   366\u2192\t\tresAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)\n   367\u2192\t\tinstances, err := n.expandResourceInstances(ctx, resAddr, imports, &g)\n   368\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n   369\u2192\t\tfor _, instance := range instances {\n   370\u2192\t\t\texpandedInstances.Add(instance)\n   371\u2192\t\t}\n   372\u2192\t}\n   373\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   374\u2192\t\treturn nil, diags\n   375\u2192\t}\n   376\u2192\n   377\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validateExpandedImportTargets(imports, expandedInstances))\n   378\u2192\n   379\u2192\t// If this is a resource that participates in custom condition checks\n   380\u2192\t// (i.e. it has preconditions or postconditions) then the check state\n   381\u2192\t// wants to know the addresses of the checkable objects so that it can\n   382\u2192\t// treat them as unknown status if we encounter an error before actually\n   383\u2192\t// visiting the checks.\n   384\u2192\tif checkState := ctx.Checks(); checkState.ConfigHasChecks(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr) {\n   385\u2192\t\tcheckState.ReportCheckableObjects(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr, expandedInstances)\n   386\u2192\t}\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192\treturn &g, diags\n   391\u2192}\n   392\u2192\n   393\u2192// expandResourceInstances calculates the dynamic expansion for the resource\n   394\u2192// itself in the context of a particular module instance.\n   395\u2192//\n   396\u2192// It has several side-effects:\n   397\u2192//   - Adds a node to Graph g for each leaf resource instance it discovers, whether present or orphaned.\n   398\u2192//   - Registers the expansion of the resource in the \"expander\" object embedded inside EvalContext globalCtx.\n   399\u2192//   - Adds each present (non-orphaned) resource instance address to checkableAddrs (guaranteed to always be addrs.AbsResourceInstance, despite being declared as addrs.Checkable).\n   400\u2192//\n   401\u2192// After calling this for each of the module instances the resource appears\n   402\u2192// within, the caller must register the final superset instAddrs with the\n   403\u2192// checks subsystem so that it knows the fully expanded set of checkable\n   404\u2192// object instances for this resource instance.\n   405\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], g *Graph) ([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   406\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   407\u2192\n   408\u2192\t// The rest of our work here needs to know which module instance it's\n   409\u2192\t// working in, so that it can evaluate expressions in the appropriate scope.\n   410\u2192\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\n   411\u2192\n   412\u2192\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\n   413\u2192\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\n   414\u2192\t// to work below.\n   415\u2192\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n   416\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n   417\u2192\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\n   418\u2192\t\treturn nil, diags\n   419\u2192\t}\n   420\u2192\n   421\u2192\t// Before we expand our resource into potentially many resource instances,\n   422\u2192\t// we'll verify that any mention of this resource in n.forceReplace is\n   423\u2192\t// consistent with the repetition mode of the resource. In other words,\n   424\u2192\t// we're aiming to catch a situation where naming a particular resource\n   425\u2192\t// instance would require an instance key but the given address has none.\n   426\u2192\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\n   427\u2192\tinstanceAddrs := expander.ExpandResource(resAddr)\n   428\u2192\n   429\u2192\t// If there's a number of instances other than 1 then we definitely need\n   430\u2192\t// an index.\n   431\u2192\tmustHaveIndex := len(instanceAddrs) != 1\n   432\u2192\t// If there's only one instance then we might still need an index, if the\n   433\u2192\t// instance address has one.\n   434\u2192\tif len(instanceAddrs) == 1 && instanceAddrs[0].Resource.Key != addrs.NoKey {\n   435\u2192\t\tmustHaveIndex = true\n   436\u2192\t}\n   437\u2192\tif mustHaveIndex {\n   438\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs))\n   439\u2192\t}\n   440\u2192\t// NOTE: The actual interpretation of n.forceReplace to produce replace\n   441\u2192\t// actions is in the per-instance function we're about to call, because\n   442\u2192\t// we need to evaluate it on a per-instance basis.\n   443\u2192\n   444\u2192\t// Our graph builder mechanism expects to always be constructing new\n   445\u2192\t// graphs rather than adding to existing ones, so we'll first\n   446\u2192\t// construct a subgraph just for this individual modules's instances and\n   447\u2192\t// then we'll steal all of its nodes and edges to incorporate into our\n   448\u2192\t// main graph which contains all of the resource instances together.\n   449\u2192\tinstG, instDiags := n.resourceInstanceSubgraph(moduleCtx, resAddr, instanceAddrs, imports)\n   450\u2192\tif instDiags.HasErrors() {\n   451\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(instDiags)\n   452\u2192\t\treturn nil, diags\n   453\u2192\t}\n   454\u2192\tg.Subsume(&instG.AcyclicGraph.Graph)\n   455\u2192\n   456\u2192\treturn instanceAddrs, diags\n   457\u2192}\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) resourceInstanceSubgraph(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource, instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   460\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   461\u2192\n   462\u2192\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath != \"\" && imports.Len() == 0 {\n   463\u2192\t\t// We're generating configuration, but there's nothing to import, which\n   464\u2192\t\t// means the import block must have expanded to zero instances.\n   465\u2192\t\t// the instance expander will always return a single instance because\n   466\u2192\t\t// we have assumed there will eventually be a configuration for this\n   467\u2192\t\t// resource, so return here before we add that to the graph.\n   468\u2192\t\treturn &Graph{}, diags\n   469\u2192\t}\n   470\u2192\n   471\u2192\t// Our graph transformers require access to the full state, so we'll\n   472\u2192\t// temporarily lock it while we work on this.\n   473\u2192\tstate := ctx.State().Lock()\n   474\u2192\tdefer ctx.State().Unlock()\n   475\u2192\n   476\u2192\t// Start creating the steps\n   477\u2192\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n   478\u2192\t\t// Expand the count or for_each (if present)\n   479\u2192\t\t&ResourceCountTransformer{\n   480\u2192\t\t\tConcrete:      n.concreteResource(ctx, imports, addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]](), n.skipPlanChanges),\n   481\u2192\t\t\tSchema:        n.Schema,\n   482\u2192\t\t\tAddr:          n.ResourceAddr(),\n   483\u2192\t\t\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\n   484\u2192\t\t},\n   485\u2192\n   486\u2192\t\t// Add the count/for_each orphans\n   487\u2192\t\t&OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer{\n   488\u2192\t\t\tConcrete:      n.concreteResourceOrphan,\n   489\u2192\t\t\tAddr:          addr,\n   490\u2192\t\t\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\n   491\u2192\t\t\tState:         state,\n   492\u2192\t\t},\n   493\u2192\n   494\u2192\t\t// Attach the state\n   495\u2192\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: state},\n   496\u2192\n   497\u2192\t\t// Targeting\n   498\u2192\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: n.Targets},\n   499\u2192\n   500\u2192\t\t// Connect references so ordering is correct\n   501\u2192\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n   502\u2192\n   503\u2192\t\t// Make sure there is a single root\n   504\u2192\t\t&RootTransformer{},\n   505\u2192\t}\n   506\u2192\n   507\u2192\t// Build the graph\n   508\u2192\tb := &BasicGraphBuilder{\n   509\u2192\t\tSteps: steps,\n   510\u2192\t\tName:  \"nodeExpandPlannableResource\",\n   511\u2192\t}\n   512\u2192\tgraph, graphDiags := b.Build(addr.Module)\n   513\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(graphDiags)\n   514\u2192\n   515\u2192\treturn graph, diags\n   516\u2192}\n   517\u2192\n   518\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) concreteResource(ctx EvalContext, knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], skipPlanChanges bool) func(*NodeResourceInstanceBase) dag.Vertex {\n   519\u2192\treturn func(a *NodeResourceInstanceBase) dag.Vertex {\n   520\u2192\t\tvar m *NodePlannableResourceInstance\n   521\u2192\n   522\u2192\t\t// If we're in legacy import mode (the import CLI command), we only need\n   523\u2192\t\t// to return the import node, not a plannable resource node.\n   524\u2192\t\tfor _, importTarget := range n.importTargets {\n   525\u2192\t\t\tif importTarget.LegacyAddr.Equal(a.Addr) {\n   526\u2192\n   527\u2192\t\t\t\t// If we're in the legacy import mode, then we should never\n   528\u2192\t\t\t\t// see unknown imports. So, it's fine to just look at the known\n   529\u2192\t\t\t\t// imports here.\n   530\u2192\t\t\t\tidValue := knownImports.Get(importTarget.LegacyAddr)\n   531\u2192\n   532\u2192\t\t\t\treturn &graphNodeImportState{\n   533\u2192\t\t\t\t\tAddr:             importTarget.LegacyAddr,\n   534\u2192\t\t\t\t\tID:               idValue.AsString(),\n   535\u2192\t\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n   536\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   537\u2192\t\t\t}\n   538\u2192\t\t}\n   539\u2192\n   540\u2192\t\t// Add the config and state since we don't do that via transforms\n   541\u2192\t\ta.Config = n.Config\n   542\u2192\t\ta.ResolvedProvider = n.ResolvedProvider\n   543\u2192\t\ta.Schema = n.Schema\n   544\u2192\t\ta.ProvisionerSchemas = n.ProvisionerSchemas\n   545\u2192\t\ta.ProviderMetas = n.ProviderMetas\n   546\u2192\t\ta.dependsOn = n.dependsOn\n   547\u2192\t\ta.Dependencies = n.dependencies\n   548\u2192\t\ta.preDestroyRefresh = n.preDestroyRefresh\n   549\u2192\t\ta.generateConfigPath = n.generateConfigPath\n   550\u2192\n   551\u2192\t\tm = &NodePlannableResourceInstance{\n   552\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   553\u2192\n   554\u2192\t\t\t// By the time we're walking, we've figured out whether we need\n   555\u2192\t\t\t// to force on CreateBeforeDestroy due to dependencies on other\n   556\u2192\t\t\t// nodes that have it.\n   557\u2192\t\t\tForceCreateBeforeDestroy: n.CreateBeforeDestroy(),\n   558\u2192\t\t\tskipRefresh:              n.skipRefresh,\n   559\u2192\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:          skipPlanChanges,\n   560\u2192\t\t\tforceReplace:             n.forceReplace,\n   561\u2192\t\t}\n   562\u2192\n   563\u2192\t\tif importID, ok := knownImports.GetOk(a.Addr); ok {\n   564\u2192\t\t\tm.importTarget = importID\n   565\u2192\t\t} else {\n   566\u2192\t\t\t// We're going to check now if this resource instance *might* be\n   567\u2192\t\t\t// targeted by one of the unknown imports. If it is, we'll set the\n   568\u2192\t\t\t// import target to an unknown value so that the import operation\n   569\u2192\t\t\t// will be deferred.\n   570\u2192\t\t\tfor _, unknownImport := range unknownImports.Elems {\n   571\u2192\t\t\t\tif unknownImport.Key.MatchesInstance(a.Addr) {\n   572\u2192\t\t\t\t\tif unknownImport.Value.Has(a.Addr) {\n   573\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t// This means that this particular instance already\n   574\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t// exists within the state. `import` blocks that target\n   575\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t// instances that already exist are ignored by\n   576\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t// Terraform. This means that even if this unknown\n   577\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t// import does eventually resolve to this instance then\n   578\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t// it would be ignored anyway. So for this instance we\n   579\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t// won't set the import target.\n   580\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   581\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n   582\u2192\n   583\u2192\t\t\t\t\tm.importTarget = cty.UnknownVal(cty.String)\n   584\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   585\u2192\t\t\t}\n   586\u2192\t\t}\n   587\u2192\n   588\u2192\t\treturn m\n   589\u2192\t}\n   590\u2192}\n   591\u2192\n   592\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) concreteResourceOrphan(a *NodeResourceInstanceBase) dag.Vertex {\n   593\u2192\t// Add the config and state since we don't do that via transforms\n   594\u2192\ta.Config = n.Config\n   595\u2192\ta.ResolvedProvider = n.ResolvedProvider\n   596\u2192\ta.Schema = n.Schema\n   597\u2192\ta.ProvisionerSchemas = n.ProvisionerSchemas\n   598\u2192\ta.ProviderMetas = n.ProviderMetas\n   599\u2192\n   600\u2192\treturn &NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\n   601\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   602\u2192\t\tskipRefresh:                  n.skipRefresh,\n   603\u2192\t\tskipPlanChanges:              n.skipPlanChanges,\n   604\u2192\t}\n   605\u2192}\n   606\u2192\n   607\u2192func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n   608\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   609\u2192\n   610\u2192\tfor _, candidateAddr := range n.forceReplace {\n   611\u2192\t\tif candidateAddr.Resource.Key == addrs.NoKey {\n   612\u2192\t\t\tif n.Addr.Resource.Equal(candidateAddr.Resource.Resource) {\n   613\u2192\t\t\t\tswitch {\n   614\u2192\t\t\t\tcase len(instanceAddrs) == 0:\n   615\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// In this case there _are_ no instances to replace, so\n   616\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// there isn't any alternative address for us to suggest.\n   617\u2192\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   618\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Warning,\n   619\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\",\n   620\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n   621\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because this resource doesn't have any instances.\",\n   622\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcandidateAddr,\n   623\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t),\n   624\u2192\t\t\t\t\t))\n   625\u2192\t\t\t\tcase len(instanceAddrs) == 1:\n   626\u2192\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   627\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Warning,\n   628\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\",\n   629\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n   630\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because it lacks an instance key.\\n\\nTo force replacement of the single declared instance, use the following option instead:\\n  -replace=%q\",\n   631\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcandidateAddr, instanceAddrs[0],\n   632\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t),\n   633\u2192\t\t\t\t\t))\n   634\u2192\t\t\t\tdefault:\n   635\u2192\t\t\t\t\tvar possibleValidOptions strings.Builder\n   636\u2192\t\t\t\t\tfor _, addr := range instanceAddrs {\n   637\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&possibleValidOptions, \"\\n  -replace=%q\", addr)\n   638\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n   639\u2192\n   640\u2192\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   641\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Warning,\n   642\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\",\n   643\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n   644\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because it lacks an instance key.\\n\\nTo force replacement of particular instances, use one or more of the following options instead:%s\",\n   645\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcandidateAddr, possibleValidOptions.String(),\n   646\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t),\n   647\u2192\t\t\t\t\t))\n   648\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   649\u2192\t\t\t}\n   650\u2192\t\t}\n   651\u2192\t}\n   652\u2192\n   653\u2192\treturn diags\n   654\u2192}\n   655\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
149. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance_test.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"testing\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    17\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    18\u2192)\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192func TestNodeAbstractResourceInstanceProvider(t *testing.T) {\n    21\u2192\ttests := []struct {\n    22\u2192\t\tAddr                 addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n    23\u2192\t\tConfig               *configs.Resource\n    24\u2192\t\tStoredProviderConfig addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n    25\u2192\t\tWant                 addrs.Provider\n    26\u2192\t}{\n    27\u2192\t\t{\n    28\u2192\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n    29\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n    30\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n    31\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n    32\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n    33\u2192\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n    34\u2192\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n    35\u2192\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"hashicorp\",\n    36\u2192\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n    37\u2192\t\t\t},\n    38\u2192\t\t},\n    39\u2192\t\t{\n    40\u2192\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n    41\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n    42\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"terraform_remote_state\",\n    43\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n    44\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n    45\u2192\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n    46\u2192\t\t\t\t// As a special case, the type prefix \"terraform_\" maps to\n    47\u2192\t\t\t\t// the builtin provider, not the default one.\n    48\u2192\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.BuiltInProviderHost,\n    49\u2192\t\t\t\tNamespace: addrs.BuiltInProviderNamespace,\n    50\u2192\t\t\t\tType:      \"terraform\",\n    51\u2192\t\t\t},\n    52\u2192\t\t},\n    53\u2192\t\t{\n    54\u2192\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n    55\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n    56\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n    57\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n    58\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n    59\u2192\t\t\tConfig: &configs.Resource{\n    60\u2192\t\t\t\t// Just enough configs.Resource for the Provider method. Not\n    61\u2192\t\t\t\t// actually valid for general use.\n    62\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n    63\u2192\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n    64\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n    65\u2192\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n    66\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    67\u2192\t\t\t},\n    68\u2192\t\t\t// The config overrides the default behavior.\n    69\u2192\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n    70\u2192\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n    71\u2192\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n    72\u2192\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n    73\u2192\t\t\t},\n    74\u2192\t\t},\n    75\u2192\t\t{\n    76\u2192\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n    77\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n    78\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"terraform_remote_state\",\n    79\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n    80\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n    81\u2192\t\t\tConfig: &configs.Resource{\n    82\u2192\t\t\t\t// Just enough configs.Resource for the Provider method. Not\n    83\u2192\t\t\t\t// actually valid for general use.\n    84\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n    85\u2192\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n    86\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n    87\u2192\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n    88\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    89\u2192\t\t\t},\n    90\u2192\t\t\t// The config overrides the default behavior.\n    91\u2192\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n    92\u2192\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n    93\u2192\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n    94\u2192\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n    95\u2192\t\t\t},\n    96\u2192\t\t},\n    97\u2192\t\t{\n    98\u2192\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n    99\u2192\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n   100\u2192\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n   101\u2192\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n   102\u2192\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n   103\u2192\t\t\tConfig: nil,\n   104\u2192\t\t\tStoredProviderConfig: addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   105\u2192\t\t\t\tModule: addrs.RootModule,\n   106\u2192\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n   107\u2192\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n   108\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n   109\u2192\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n   110\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   111\u2192\t\t\t},\n   112\u2192\t\t\t// The stored provider config overrides the default behavior.\n   113\u2192\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n   114\u2192\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n   115\u2192\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n   116\u2192\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n   117\u2192\t\t\t},\n   118\u2192\t\t},\n   119\u2192\t}\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192\tfor _, test := range tests {\n   122\u2192\t\tvar name string\n   123\u2192\t\tif test.Config != nil {\n   124\u2192\t\t\tname = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s with configured %s\", test.Addr, test.Config.Provider)\n   125\u2192\t\t} else {\n   126\u2192\t\t\tname = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s with no configuration\", test.Addr)\n   127\u2192\t\t}\n   128\u2192\t\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\n   129\u2192\t\t\tnode := &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n   130\u2192\t\t\t\t// Just enough NodeAbstractResourceInstance for the Provider\n   131\u2192\t\t\t\t// function. (This would not be valid for some other functions.)\n   132\u2192\t\t\t\tAddr: test.Addr,\n   133\u2192\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n   134\u2192\t\t\t\t\tAddr:                 test.Addr.ConfigResource(),\n   135\u2192\t\t\t\t\tConfig:               test.Config,\n   136\u2192\t\t\t\t\tstoredProviderConfig: test.StoredProviderConfig,\n   137\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   138\u2192\t\t\t}\n   139\u2192\t\t\tgot := node.Provider()\n   140\u2192\t\t\tif got != test.Want {\n   141\u2192\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"wrong result\\naddr:  %s\\nconfig: %#v\\ngot:   %s\\nwant:  %s\", test.Addr, test.Config, got, test.Want)\n   142\u2192\t\t\t}\n   143\u2192\t\t})\n   144\u2192\t}\n   145\u2192}\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192func TestNodeResourceInstanceBase_WriteResourceInstanceState(t *testing.T) {\n   148\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n   149\u2192\tctx := new(MockEvalContext)\n   150\u2192\tctx.StateState = state.SyncWrapper()\n   151\u2192\tctx.Scope = evalContextModuleInstance{Addr: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192\tmockProvider := mockProviderWithResourceTypeSchema(\"aws_instance\", &configschema.Block{\n   154\u2192\t\tAttributes: map[string]*configschema.Attribute{\n   155\u2192\t\t\t\"id\": {\n   156\u2192\t\t\t\tType:     cty.String,\n   157\u2192\t\t\t\tOptional: true,\n   158\u2192\t\t\t},\n   159\u2192\t\t},\n   160\u2192\t})\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192\tobj := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n   163\u2192\t\tValue: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n   164\u2192\t\t\t\"id\": cty.StringVal(\"i-abc123\"),\n   165\u2192\t\t}),\n   166\u2192\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n   167\u2192\t}\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192\tnode := &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n   170\u2192\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"aws_instance.foo\"),\n   171\u2192\t\t// instanceState:        obj,\n   172\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n   173\u2192\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws\"]`),\n   174\u2192\t\t},\n   175\u2192\t}\n   176\u2192\tctx.ProviderProvider = mockProvider\n   177\u2192\tctx.ProviderSchemaSchema = mockProvider.GetProviderSchema()\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192\terr := node.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, obj, workingState)\n   180\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   181\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", err.Error())\n   182\u2192\t}\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192\tcheckStateString(t, state, `\n   185\u2192aws_instance.foo:\n   186\u2192  ID = i-abc123\n   187\u2192  provider = provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws\"]\n   188\u2192\t`)\n   189\u2192}\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192func TestNodeResourceInstanceBase_refresh_with_deferred_read(t *testing.T) {\n   192\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n   193\u2192\tevalCtx := &MockEvalContext{}\n   194\u2192\tevalCtx.StateState = state.SyncWrapper()\n   195\u2192\tevalCtx.Scope = evalContextModuleInstance{Addr: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192\tmockProvider := mockProviderWithResourceTypeSchema(\"aws_instance\", &configschema.Block{\n   198\u2192\t\tAttributes: map[string]*configschema.Attribute{\n   199\u2192\t\t\t\"id\": {\n   200\u2192\t\t\t\tType:     cty.String,\n   201\u2192\t\t\t\tOptional: true,\n   202\u2192\t\t\t},\n   203\u2192\t\t},\n   204\u2192\t})\n   205\u2192\tmockProvider.ConfigureProviderCalled = true\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192\tmockProvider.ReadResourceFn = func(providers.ReadResourceRequest) providers.ReadResourceResponse {\n   208\u2192\t\treturn providers.ReadResourceResponse{\n   209\u2192\t\t\tNewState: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n   210\u2192\t\t\t\t\"id\": cty.UnknownVal(cty.String),\n   211\u2192\t\t\t}),\n   212\u2192\t\t\tDeferred: &providers.Deferred{\n   213\u2192\t\t\t\tReason: providers.DeferredReasonAbsentPrereq,\n   214\u2192\t\t\t},\n   215\u2192\t\t}\n   216\u2192\t}\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192\tobj := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n   219\u2192\t\tValue: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n   220\u2192\t\t\t\"id\": cty.StringVal(\"i-abc123\"),\n   221\u2192\t\t}),\n   222\u2192\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n   223\u2192\t}\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192\tnode := &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n   226\u2192\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"aws_instance.foo\"),\n   227\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n   228\u2192\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws\"]`),\n   229\u2192\t\t},\n   230\u2192\t}\n   231\u2192\tevalCtx.ProviderProvider = mockProvider\n   232\u2192\tevalCtx.ProviderSchemaSchema = mockProvider.GetProviderSchema()\n   233\u2192\tevalCtx.DeferralsState = deferring.NewDeferred(true)\n   234\u2192\n   235\u2192\trio, deferred, diags := node.refresh(evalCtx, states.NotDeposed, obj, true)\n   236\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   237\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(diags.Err())\n   238\u2192\t}\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192\tvalue := rio.Value\n   241\u2192\tif value.IsWhollyKnown() {\n   242\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"value was known: %v\", value)\n   243\u2192\t}\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192\tif deferred == nil {\n   246\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected deferral to be present\")\n   247\u2192\t}\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192\tif deferred.Reason != providers.DeferredReasonAbsentPrereq {\n   250\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected deferral to be AbsentPrereq, got %s\", deferred.Reason)\n   251\u2192\t}\n   252\u2192}\n   253\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
150. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_plan_orphan_test.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"testing\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    17\u2192)\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192func TestNodeResourcePlanOrphanExecute(t *testing.T) {\n    20\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n    21\u2192\tstate.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n    22\u2192\t\taddrs.Resource{\n    23\u2192\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n    24\u2192\t\t\tType: \"test_object\",\n    25\u2192\t\t\tName: \"foo\",\n    26\u2192\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey),\n    27\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    28\u2192\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n    29\u2192\t\t\t\t\"test_string\": \"foo\",\n    30\u2192\t\t\t},\n    31\u2192\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n    32\u2192\t\t},\n    33\u2192\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n    34\u2192\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n    35\u2192\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n    36\u2192\t\t},\n    37\u2192\t)\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192\tp := simpleMockProvider()\n    40\u2192\tp.ConfigureProvider(providers.ConfigureProviderRequest{})\n    41\u2192\tctx := &MockEvalContext{\n    42\u2192\t\tStateState:               state.SyncWrapper(),\n    43\u2192\t\tRefreshStateState:        state.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper(),\n    44\u2192\t\tPrevRunStateState:        state.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper(),\n    45\u2192\t\tInstanceExpanderExpander: instances.NewExpander(nil),\n    46\u2192\t\tProviderProvider:         p,\n    47\u2192\t\tProviderSchemaSchema: providers.ProviderSchema{\n    48\u2192\t\t\tResourceTypes: map[string]providers.Schema{\n    49\u2192\t\t\t\t\"test_object\": {\n    50\u2192\t\t\t\t\tBlock: simpleTestSchema(),\n    51\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    52\u2192\t\t\t},\n    53\u2192\t\t},\n    54\u2192\t\tChangesChanges: plans.NewChanges().SyncWrapper(),\n    55\u2192\t\tDeferralsState: deferring.NewDeferred(false),\n    56\u2192\t}\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192\tnode := NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\n    59\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n    60\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n    61\u2192\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n    62\u2192\t\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n    63\u2192\t\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n    64\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    65\u2192\t\t\t},\n    66\u2192\t\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.foo\"),\n    67\u2192\t\t},\n    68\u2192\t}\n    69\u2192\tdiags := node.Execute(ctx, walkApply)\n    70\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    71\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", diags.Err())\n    72\u2192\t}\n    73\u2192\tif !state.Empty() {\n    74\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected empty state, got %s\", state.String())\n    75\u2192\t}\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192func TestNodeResourcePlanOrphanExecute_alreadyDeleted(t *testing.T) {\n    79\u2192\taddr := addrs.Resource{\n    80\u2192\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n    81\u2192\t\tType: \"test_object\",\n    82\u2192\t\tName: \"foo\",\n    83\u2192\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n    86\u2192\tstate.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n    87\u2192\t\taddr.Resource,\n    88\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    89\u2192\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n    90\u2192\t\t\t\t\"test_string\": \"foo\",\n    91\u2192\t\t\t},\n    92\u2192\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n    93\u2192\t\t},\n    94\u2192\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n    95\u2192\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n    96\u2192\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n    97\u2192\t\t},\n    98\u2192\t)\n    99\u2192\trefreshState := state.DeepCopy()\n   100\u2192\tprevRunState := state.DeepCopy()\n   101\u2192\tchanges := plans.NewChanges()\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192\tp := simpleMockProvider()\n   104\u2192\tp.ConfigureProvider(providers.ConfigureProviderRequest{})\n   105\u2192\tp.ReadResourceResponse = &providers.ReadResourceResponse{\n   106\u2192\t\tNewState: cty.NullVal(p.GetProviderSchemaResponse.ResourceTypes[\"test_string\"].Block.ImpliedType()),\n   107\u2192\t}\n   108\u2192\tctx := &MockEvalContext{\n   109\u2192\t\tStateState:               state.SyncWrapper(),\n   110\u2192\t\tRefreshStateState:        refreshState.SyncWrapper(),\n   111\u2192\t\tPrevRunStateState:        prevRunState.SyncWrapper(),\n   112\u2192\t\tInstanceExpanderExpander: instances.NewExpander(nil),\n   113\u2192\t\tProviderProvider:         p,\n   114\u2192\t\tProviderSchemaSchema: providers.ProviderSchema{\n   115\u2192\t\t\tResourceTypes: map[string]providers.Schema{\n   116\u2192\t\t\t\t\"test_object\": {\n   117\u2192\t\t\t\t\tBlock: simpleTestSchema(),\n   118\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   119\u2192\t\t\t},\n   120\u2192\t\t},\n   121\u2192\t\tChangesChanges: changes.SyncWrapper(),\n   122\u2192\t\tDeferralsState: deferring.NewDeferred(false),\n   123\u2192\t}\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192\tnode := NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\n   126\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n   127\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n   128\u2192\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   129\u2192\t\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n   130\u2192\t\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n   131\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   132\u2192\t\t\t},\n   133\u2192\t\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.foo\"),\n   134\u2192\t\t},\n   135\u2192\t}\n   136\u2192\tdiags := node.Execute(ctx, walkPlan)\n   137\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   138\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", diags.Err())\n   139\u2192\t}\n   140\u2192\tif !state.Empty() {\n   141\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected empty state, got %s\", state.String())\n   142\u2192\t}\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192\tif got := prevRunState.ResourceInstance(addr); got == nil {\n   145\u2192\t\tt.Errorf(\"no entry for %s in the prev run state; should still be present\", addr)\n   146\u2192\t}\n   147\u2192\tif got := refreshState.ResourceInstance(addr); got != nil {\n   148\u2192\t\tt.Errorf(\"refresh state has entry for %s; should've been removed\", addr)\n   149\u2192\t}\n   150\u2192\tif got := changes.ResourceInstance(addr); got != nil {\n   151\u2192\t\tt.Errorf(\"there should be no change for the %s instance, got %s\", addr, got.Action)\n   152\u2192\t}\n   153\u2192}\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192// This test describes a situation which should not be possible, as this node\n   156\u2192// should never work on deposed instances. However, a bug elsewhere resulted in\n   157\u2192// this code path being exercised and triggered a panic. As a result, the\n   158\u2192// assertions at the end of the test are minimal, as the behaviour (aside from\n   159\u2192// not panicking) is unspecified.\n   160\u2192func TestNodeResourcePlanOrphanExecute_deposed(t *testing.T) {\n   161\u2192\taddr := addrs.Resource{\n   162\u2192\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n   163\u2192\t\tType: \"test_object\",\n   164\u2192\t\tName: \"foo\",\n   165\u2192\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n   168\u2192\tstate.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceDeposed(\n   169\u2192\t\taddr.Resource,\n   170\u2192\t\tstates.NewDeposedKey(),\n   171\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   172\u2192\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n   173\u2192\t\t\t\t\"test_string\": \"foo\",\n   174\u2192\t\t\t},\n   175\u2192\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n   176\u2192\t\t},\n   177\u2192\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   178\u2192\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n   179\u2192\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n   180\u2192\t\t},\n   181\u2192\t)\n   182\u2192\trefreshState := state.DeepCopy()\n   183\u2192\tprevRunState := state.DeepCopy()\n   184\u2192\tchanges := plans.NewChanges()\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192\tp := simpleMockProvider()\n   187\u2192\tp.ConfigureProvider(providers.ConfigureProviderRequest{})\n   188\u2192\tp.ReadResourceResponse = &providers.ReadResourceResponse{\n   189\u2192\t\tNewState: cty.NullVal(p.GetProviderSchemaResponse.ResourceTypes[\"test_string\"].Block.ImpliedType()),\n   190\u2192\t}\n   191\u2192\tctx := &MockEvalContext{\n   192\u2192\t\tStateState:               state.SyncWrapper(),\n   193\u2192\t\tRefreshStateState:        refreshState.SyncWrapper(),\n   194\u2192\t\tPrevRunStateState:        prevRunState.SyncWrapper(),\n   195\u2192\t\tInstanceExpanderExpander: instances.NewExpander(nil),\n   196\u2192\t\tProviderProvider:         p,\n   197\u2192\t\tProviderSchemaSchema: providers.ProviderSchema{\n   198\u2192\t\t\tResourceTypes: map[string]providers.Schema{\n   199\u2192\t\t\t\t\"test_object\": {\n   200\u2192\t\t\t\t\tBlock: simpleTestSchema(),\n   201\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   202\u2192\t\t\t},\n   203\u2192\t\t},\n   204\u2192\t\tChangesChanges: changes.SyncWrapper(),\n   205\u2192\t\tDeferralsState: deferring.NewDeferred(false),\n   206\u2192\t}\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192\tnode := NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\n   209\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n   210\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n   211\u2192\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   212\u2192\t\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n   213\u2192\t\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n   214\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   215\u2192\t\t\t},\n   216\u2192\t\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.foo\"),\n   217\u2192\t\t},\n   218\u2192\t}\n   219\u2192\tdiags := node.Execute(ctx, walkPlan)\n   220\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   221\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", diags.Err())\n   222\u2192\t}\n   223\u2192}\n   224\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
151. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_diff.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    16\u2192)\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192// DiffTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing\n    19\u2192// each of the resource changes described in the given Changes object.\n    20\u2192type DiffTransformer struct {\n    21\u2192\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n    22\u2192\tState    *states.State\n    23\u2192\tChanges  *plans.ChangesSrc\n    24\u2192\tConfig   *configs.Config\n    25\u2192}\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192// return true if the given resource instance has either Preconditions or\n    28\u2192// Postconditions defined in the configuration.\n    29\u2192func (t *DiffTransformer) hasConfigConditions(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) bool {\n    30\u2192\t// unit tests may have no config\n    31\u2192\tif t.Config == nil {\n    32\u2192\t\treturn false\n    33\u2192\t}\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\tcfg := t.Config.DescendantForInstance(addr.Module)\n    36\u2192\tif cfg == nil {\n    37\u2192\t\treturn false\n    38\u2192\t}\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192\tres := cfg.Module.ResourceByAddr(addr.ConfigResource().Resource)\n    41\u2192\tif res == nil {\n    42\u2192\t\treturn false\n    43\u2192\t}\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192\treturn len(res.Preconditions) > 0 || len(res.Postconditions) > 0\n    46\u2192}\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192func (t *DiffTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n    49\u2192\tif t.Changes == nil || len(t.Changes.Resources) == 0 {\n    50\u2192\t\t// Nothing to do!\n    51\u2192\t\treturn nil\n    52\u2192\t}\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192\t// Go through all the modules in the diff.\n    55\u2192\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer starting\")\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    58\u2192\tstate := t.State\n    59\u2192\tchanges := t.Changes\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192\t// DiffTransformer creates resource _instance_ nodes. If there are any\n    62\u2192\t// whole-resource nodes already in the graph, we must ensure that they\n    63\u2192\t// get evaluated before any of the corresponding instances by creating\n    64\u2192\t// dependency edges, so we'll do some prep work here to ensure we'll only\n    65\u2192\t// create connections to nodes that existed before we started here.\n    66\u2192\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()\n    67\u2192\tfor _, node := range g.Vertices() {\n    68\u2192\t\trn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)\n    69\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n    70\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    71\u2192\t\t}\n    72\u2192\t\t// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\n    73\u2192\t\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\n    74\u2192\t\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.\n    75\u2192\t\tif _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResourceInstance); ok {\n    76\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    77\u2192\t\t}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192\t\trAddr := rn.ResourceAddr()\n    80\u2192\t\tresourceNodes.Put(rAddr, append(resourceNodes.Get(rAddr), rn))\n    81\u2192\t}\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192\tfor _, rc := range changes.Resources {\n    84\u2192\t\taddr := rc.Addr\n    85\u2192\t\tdk := rc.DeposedKey\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: found %s change for %s %s\", rc.Action, addr, dk)\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192\t\t// Depending on the action we'll need some different combinations of\n    90\u2192\t\t// nodes, because destroying uses a special node type separate from\n    91\u2192\t\t// other actions.\n    92\u2192\t\tvar update, delete, forget, createBeforeDestroy bool\n    93\u2192\t\tswitch rc.Action {\n    94\u2192\t\tcase plans.NoOp:\n    95\u2192\t\t\t// For a no-op change we don't take any action but we still\n    96\u2192\t\t\t// run any condition checks associated with the object, to\n    97\u2192\t\t\t// make sure that they still hold when considering the\n    98\u2192\t\t\t// results of other changes.\n    99\u2192\t\t\tupdate = t.hasConfigConditions(addr)\n   100\u2192\t\tcase plans.Delete:\n   101\u2192\t\t\tdelete = true\n   102\u2192\t\tcase plans.DeleteThenCreate, plans.CreateThenDelete:\n   103\u2192\t\t\tupdate = true\n   104\u2192\t\t\tdelete = true\n   105\u2192\t\t\tcreateBeforeDestroy = (rc.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete)\n   106\u2192\t\tcase plans.Forget:\n   107\u2192\t\t\tforget = true\n   108\u2192\t\tdefault:\n   109\u2192\t\t\tupdate = true\n   110\u2192\t\t}\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192\t\t// A deposed instance may only have a change of Delete, Forget, or NoOp.\n   113\u2192\t\t// A NoOp can happen if the provider shows it no longer exists during\n   114\u2192\t\t// the most recent ReadResource operation.\n   115\u2192\t\tif dk != states.NotDeposed && !(rc.Action == plans.Delete || rc.Action == plans.Forget || rc.Action == plans.NoOp) {\n   116\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   117\u2192\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n   118\u2192\t\t\t\t\"Invalid planned change for deposed object\",\n   119\u2192\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The plan contains a non-remove change for %s deposed object %s. The only valid actions for a deposed object are to destroy it or remove it from state, so this is a bug in Terraform.\", addr, dk),\n   120\u2192\t\t\t))\n   121\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   122\u2192\t\t}\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192\t\t// If we're going to do a create_before_destroy Replace operation then\n   125\u2192\t\t// we need to allocate a DeposedKey to use to retain the\n   126\u2192\t\t// not-yet-destroyed prior object, so that the delete node can destroy\n   127\u2192\t\t// _that_ rather than the newly-created node, which will be current\n   128\u2192\t\t// by the time the delete node is visited.\n   129\u2192\t\tif update && delete && createBeforeDestroy {\n   130\u2192\t\t\t// In this case, variable dk will be the _pre-assigned_ DeposedKey\n   131\u2192\t\t\t// that must be used if the update graph node deposes the current\n   132\u2192\t\t\t// instance, which will then align with the same key we pass\n   133\u2192\t\t\t// into the destroy node to ensure we destroy exactly the deposed\n   134\u2192\t\t\t// object we expect.\n   135\u2192\t\t\tif state != nil {\n   136\u2192\t\t\t\tris := state.ResourceInstance(addr)\n   137\u2192\t\t\t\tif ris == nil {\n   138\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// Should never happen, since we don't plan to replace an\n   139\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// instance that doesn't exist yet.\n   140\u2192\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   141\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n   142\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Invalid planned change\",\n   143\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The plan contains a replace change for %s, which doesn't exist yet. This is a bug in Terraform.\", addr),\n   144\u2192\t\t\t\t\t))\n   145\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   146\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192\t\t\t\t// Allocating a deposed key separately from using it can be racy\n   149\u2192\t\t\t\t// in general, but we assume here that nothing except the apply\n   150\u2192\t\t\t\t// node we instantiate below will actually make new deposed objects\n   151\u2192\t\t\t\t// in practice, and so the set of already-used keys will not change\n   152\u2192\t\t\t\t// between now and then.\n   153\u2192\t\t\t\tdk = ris.FindUnusedDeposedKey()\n   154\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   155\u2192\t\t\t\t// If we have no state at all yet then we can use _any_\n   156\u2192\t\t\t\t// DeposedKey.\n   157\u2192\t\t\t\tdk = states.NewDeposedKey()\n   158\u2192\t\t\t}\n   159\u2192\t\t}\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192\t\tif update {\n   162\u2192\t\t\t// All actions except destroying the node type chosen by t.Concrete\n   163\u2192\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addr)\n   164\u2192\t\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n   165\u2192\t\t\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\n   166\u2192\t\t\t\tnode = f(abstract)\n   167\u2192\t\t\t}\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192\t\t\tif createBeforeDestroy {\n   170\u2192\t\t\t\t// We'll attach our pre-allocated DeposedKey to the node if\n   171\u2192\t\t\t\t// it supports that. NodeApplyableResourceInstance is the\n   172\u2192\t\t\t\t// specific concrete node type we are looking for here really,\n   173\u2192\t\t\t\t// since that's the only node type that might depose objects.\n   174\u2192\t\t\t\tif dn, ok := node.(GraphNodeDeposer); ok {\n   175\u2192\t\t\t\t\tdn.SetPreallocatedDeposedKey(dk)\n   176\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   177\u2192\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented by %s, deposing prior object to %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node), dk)\n   178\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   179\u2192\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n   180\u2192\t\t\t}\n   181\u2192\n   182\u2192\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n   183\u2192\t\t\tfor _, rsrcNode := range resourceNodes.Get(addr.ConfigResource()) {\n   184\u2192\t\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, rsrcNode))\n   185\u2192\t\t\t}\n   186\u2192\t\t}\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192\t\tif delete {\n   189\u2192\t\t\t// Destroying always uses a destroy-specific node type, though\n   190\u2192\t\t\t// which one depends on whether we're destroying a current object\n   191\u2192\t\t\t// or a deposed object.\n   192\u2192\t\t\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\n   193\u2192\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addr)\n   194\u2192\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n   195\u2192\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{\n   196\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n   197\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   198\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   199\u2192\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n   200\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n   201\u2192\t\t\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\n   202\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   203\u2192\t\t\t}\n   204\u2192\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n   205\u2192\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented for destruction by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n   206\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   207\u2192\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s deposed object %s will be represented for destruction by %s\", addr, dk, dag.VertexName(node))\n   208\u2192\t\t\t}\n   209\u2192\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n   210\u2192\t\t}\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192\t\tif forget {\n   213\u2192\t\t\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\n   214\u2192\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addr)\n   215\u2192\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n   216\u2192\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeForgetResourceInstance{\n   217\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n   218\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   219\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   220\u2192\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n   221\u2192\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n   222\u2192\t\t\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\n   223\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   224\u2192\t\t\t}\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented for forgetting by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n   227\u2192\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n   228\u2192\t\t}\n   229\u2192\n   230\u2192\t}\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer complete\")\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192\treturn diags.Err()\n   235\u2192}\n   236\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
152. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_destroy_edge_test.go"
}
Output

addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n    22\u2192\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(\"test_object.A\"))\n    23\u2192\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(\"test_object.B\"))\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n 29\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    30\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n    31\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"A\"}`),\n    32\u2192\t\t},\n 36\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.B\").Resource,\n    37\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    38\u2192\t\t\tStatus:       states.ObjectReady,\n    39\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: state.EnsureModule(addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n   135\u2192\tchild := state.EnsureModule(addrs.RootModuleInstance.Child(\"child\", addrs.NoKey))\n   136\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   137\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.a\").Resource,\n   138\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   139\u2192\t\t\tStatus:       states.ObjectReady,\n
