164\u2192\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(testTransformDestroyEdgeModuleStr)\n   165\u2192\tif actual != expected {\n   166\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong result\\n\\ngot:\\n%s\\n\\nwant:\\n%s\", actual, expected)\n   167\u2192\t}\n   168\u2192}\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192func TestDestroyEdgeTransformer_moduleOnly(t *testing.T) {\n   171\u2192\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n   174\u2192\tfor moduleIdx := 0; moduleIdx < 2; moduleIdx++ {\n   175\u2192\t\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(fmt.Sprintf(\"module.child[%d].test_object.a\", moduleIdx)))\n   176\u2192\t\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(fmt.Sprintf(\"module.child[%d].test_object.b\", moduleIdx)))\n   177\u2192\t\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(fmt.Sprintf(\"module.child[%d].test_object.c\", moduleIdx)))\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192\t\tchild := state.EnsureModule(addrs.RootModuleInstance.Child(\"child\", addrs.IntKey(moduleIdx)))\n   180\u2192\t\tchild.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   181\u2192\t\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.a\").Resource,\n   182\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   183\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n   184\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"a\"}`),\n   185\u2192\t\t\t},\n   186\u2192\t\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   187\u2192\t\t)\n   188\u2192\t\tchild.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   189\u2192\t\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.b\").Resource,\n   190\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   191\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n   192\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"b\",\"test_string\":\"x\"}`),\n   193\u2192\t\t\t\tDependencies: []addrs.ConfigResource{\n   194\u2192\t\t\t\t\tmustConfigResourceAddr(\"module.child.test_object.a\"),\n   195\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   196\u2192\t\t\t},\n   197\u2192\t\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   198\u2192\t\t)\n   199\u2192\t\tchild.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   200\u2192\t\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.c\").Resource,\n   201\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   202\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n   203\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"c\",\"test_string\":\"x\"}`),\n   204\u2192\t\t\t\tDependencies: []addrs.ConfigResource{\n   205\u2192\t\t\t\t\tmustConfigResourceAddr(\"module.child.test_object.a\"),\n   206\u2192\t\t\t\t\tmustConfigResourceAddr(\"module.child.test_object.b\"),\n   207\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   208\u2192\t\t\t},\n   209\u2192\t\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   210\u2192\t\t)\n   211\u2192\t}\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192\tif err := (&AttachStateTransformer{State: state}).Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   214\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   215\u2192\t}\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192\ttf := &DestroyEdgeTransformer{}\n   218\u2192\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   219\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   220\u2192\t}\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192\t// The analyses done in the destroy edge transformer are between\n   223\u2192\t// not-yet-expanded objects, which is conservative and so it will generate\n   224\u2192\t// edges that aren't strictly necessary. As a special case we filter out\n   225\u2192\t// any edges that are between resources instances that are in different\n   226\u2192\t// instances of the same module, because those edges are never needed\n   227\u2192\t// (one instance of a module cannot depend on another instance of the\n   228\u2192\t// same module) and including them can, in complex cases, cause cycles due\n   229\u2192\t// to unnecessary interactions between destroyed and created module\n   230\u2192\t// instances in the same plan.\n   231\u2192\t//\n   232\u2192\t// Therefore below we expect to see the dependencies within each instance\n   233\u2192\t// of module.child reflected, but we should not see any dependencies\n   234\u2192\t// _between_ instances of module.child.\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n   237\u2192\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(`\n   238\u2192module.child[0].test_object.a (destroy)\n   239\u2192  module.child[0].test_object.b (destroy)\n   240\u2192  module.child[0].test_object.c (destroy)\n   241\u2192module.child[0].test_object.b (destroy)\n   242\u2192  module.child[0].test_object.c (destroy)\n   243\u2192module.child[0].test_object.c (destroy)\n   244\u2192module.child[1].test_object.a (destroy)\n   245\u2192  module.child[1].test_object.b (destroy)\n   246\u2192  module.child[1].test_object.c (destroy)\n   247\u2192module.child[1].test_object.b (destroy)\n   248\u2192  module.child[1].test_object.c (destroy)\n   249\u2192module.child[1].test_object.c (destroy)\n   250\u2192`)\n   251\u2192\tif actual != expected {\n   252\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong result\\n\\ngot:\\n%s\\n\\nwant:\\n%s\", actual, expected)\n   253\u2192\t}\n   254\u2192}\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192func TestDestroyEdgeTransformer_destroyThenUpdate(t *testing.T) {\n   257\u2192\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   258\u2192\tg.Add(testUpdateNode(\"test_object.A\"))\n   259\u2192\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(\"test_object.B\"))\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n   262\u2192\troot := state.EnsureModule(addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n   263\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   264\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.A\").Resource,\n   265\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   266\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n   267\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"A\",\"test_string\":\"old\"}`),\n   268\u2192\t\t},\n   269\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   270\u2192\t)\n   271\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   272\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.B\").Resource,\n   273\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   274\u2192\t\t\tStatus:       states.ObjectReady,\n   275\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON:    []byte(`{\"id\":\"B\",\"test_string\":\"x\"}`),\n   276\u2192\t\t\tDependencies: []addrs.ConfigResource{mustConfigResourceAddr(\"test_object.A\")},\n   277\u2192\t\t},\n   278\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   279\u2192\t)\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192\tif err := (&AttachStateTransformer{State: state}).Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   282\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   283\u2192\t}\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192\ttf := &DestroyEdgeTransformer{}\n   286\u2192\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   287\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   288\u2192\t}\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(`\n   291\u2192test_object.A\n   292\u2192  test_object.B (destroy)\n   293\u2192test_object.B (destroy)\n   294\u2192`)\n   295\u2192\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192\tif actual != expected {\n   298\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong result\\n\\ngot:\\n%s\\n\\nwant:\\n%s\", actual, expected)\n   299\u2192\t}\n   300\u2192}\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192func TestPruneUnusedNodesTransformer_rootModuleOutputValues(t *testing.T) {\n   303\u2192\t// This is a kinda-weird test case covering the very narrow situation\n   304\u2192\t// where a root module output value depends on a resource, where we\n   305\u2192\t// need to make sure that the output value doesn't block pruning of\n   306\u2192\t// the resource from the graph. This special case exists because although\n   307\u2192\t// root module objects are \"expanders\", they in practice always expand\n   308\u2192\t// to exactly one instance and so don't have the usual requirement of\n   309\u2192\t// needing to stick around in order to support downstream expanders\n   310\u2192\t// when there are e.g. nested expanding modules.\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192\t// In order to keep this test focused on the pruneUnusedNodesTransformer\n   313\u2192\t// as much as possible we're using a minimal graph construction here which\n   314\u2192\t// is just enough to get the nodes we need, but this does mean that this\n   315\u2192\t// test might be invalidated by future changes to the apply graph builder,\n   316\u2192\t// and so if something seems off here it might help to compare the\n   317\u2192\t// following with the real apply graph transformer and verify whether\n   318\u2192\t// this smaller construction is still realistic enough to be a valid test.\n   319\u2192\t// It might be valid to change or remove this test to \"make it work\", as\n   320\u2192\t// long as you verify that there is still _something_ upholding the\n   321\u2192\t// invariant that a root module output value should not block a resource\n   322\u2192\t// node from being pruned from the graph.\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192\tconcreteResource := func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n   325\u2192\t\treturn &nodeExpandApplyableResource{\n   326\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n   327\u2192\t\t}\n   328\u2192\t}\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192\tconcreteResourceInstance := func(a *NodeResourceInstanceBase) dag.Vertex {\n   331\u2192\t\treturn &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{\n   332\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   333\u2192\t\t}\n   334\u2192\t}\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192\tresourceInstAddr := mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test.a\")\n   337\u2192\tproviderCfgAddr := addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n   338\u2192\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n   339\u2192\t\tProvider: addrs.MustParseProviderSourceString(\"foo/test\"),\n   340\u2192\t}\n   341\u2192\temptyObjDynamicVal, err := plans.NewDynamicValue(cty.EmptyObjectVal, cty.EmptyObject)\n   342\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   343\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   344\u2192\t}\n   345\u2192\tnullObjDynamicVal, err := plans.NewDynamicValue(cty.NullVal(cty.EmptyObject), cty.EmptyObject)\n   346\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   347\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   348\u2192\t}\n   349\u2192\n   350\u2192\tconfig := testModuleInline(t, map[string]string{\n   351\u2192\t\t\"main.tf\": `\n   352\u2192\t\t\tresource \"test\" \"a\" {\n   353\u2192\t\t\t}\n   354\u2192\n   355\u2192\t\t\toutput \"test\" {\n   356\u2192\t\t\t\tvalue = test.a.foo\n   357\u2192\t\t\t}\n   358\u2192\t\t`,\n   359\u2192\t})\n   360\u2192\tstate := states.BuildState(func(s *states.SyncState) {\n   361\u2192\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   362\u2192\t\t\tresourceInstAddr,\n   363\u2192\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   364\u2192\t\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n   365\u2192\t\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{}`),\n   366\u2192\t\t\t},\n   367\u2192\t\t\tproviderCfgAddr,\n   368\u2192\t\t)\n   369\u2192\t})\n   370\u2192\tchanges := plans.NewChangesSrc()\n   371\u2192\tchanges.AppendResourceInstanceChange(&plans.ResourceInstanceChangeSrc{\n   372\u2192\t\tAddr:         resourceInstAddr,\n   373\u2192\t\tPrevRunAddr:  resourceInstAddr,\n   374\u2192\t\tProviderAddr: providerCfgAddr,\n   375\u2192\t\tChangeSrc: plans.ChangeSrc{\n   376\u2192\t\t\tAction: plans.Delete,\n   377\u2192\t\t\tBefore: emptyObjDynamicVal,\n   378\u2192\t\t\tAfter:  nullObjDynamicVal,\n   379\u2192\t\t},\n   380\u2192\t})\n   381\u2192\n   382\u2192\tbuilder := &BasicGraphBuilder{\n   383\u2192\t\tSteps: []GraphTransformer{\n   384\u2192\t\t\t&ConfigTransformer{\n   385\u2192\t\t\t\tConcrete: concreteResource,\n   386\u2192\t\t\t\tConfig:   config,\n   387\u2192\t\t\t},\n   388\u2192\t\t\t&OutputTransformer{\n   389\u2192\t\t\t\tConfig: config,\n   390\u2192\t\t\t},\n   391\u2192\t\t\t&DiffTransformer{\n   392\u2192\t\t\t\tConcrete: concreteResourceInstance,\n   393\u2192\t\t\t\tState:    state,\n   394\u2192\t\t\t\tChanges:  changes,\n   395\u2192\t\t\t},\n   396\u2192\t\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n   397\u2192\t\t\t&AttachDependenciesTransformer{},\n   398\u2192\t\t\t&pruneUnusedNodesTransformer{},\n   399\u2192\t\t\t&CloseRootModuleTransformer{},\n   400\u2192\t\t},\n   401\u2192\t}\n   402\u2192\tgraph, diags := builder.Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n   403\u2192\tassertNoDiagnostics(t, diags)\n   404\u2192\n   405\u2192\t// At this point, thanks to pruneUnusedNodesTransformer, we should still\n   406\u2192\t// have the node for the output value, but the \"test.a (expand)\" node\n   407\u2192\t// should've been pruned in recognition of the fact that we're performing\n   408\u2192\t// a destroy and therefore we only need the \"test.a (destroy)\" node.\n   409\u2192\n   410\u2192\tnodesByName := make(map[string]dag.Vertex)\n   411\u2192\tnodesByResourceExpand := make(map[string]dag.Vertex)\n   412\u2192\tfor _, n := range graph.Vertices() {\n   413\u2192\t\tname := dag.VertexName(n)\n   414\u2192\t\tif _, exists := nodesByName[name]; exists {\n   415\u2192\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"multiple nodes have name %q\", name)\n   416\u2192\t\t}\n   417\u2192\t\tnodesByName[name] = n\n   418\u2192\n   419\u2192\t\tif exp, ok := n.(*nodeExpandApplyableResource); ok {\n   420\u2192\t\t\taddr := exp.Addr\n   421\u2192\t\t\tif _, exists := nodesByResourceExpand[addr.String()]; exists {\n   422\u2192\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"multiple nodes are expanders for %s\", addr)\n   423\u2192\t\t\t}\n   424\u2192\t\t\tnodesByResourceExpand[addr.String()] = exp\n   425\u2192\t\t}\n   426\u2192\t}\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192\t// NOTE: The following is sensitive to the current name string formats we\n   429\u2192\t// use for these particular node types. These names are not contractual\n   430\u2192\t// so if this breaks in future it is fine to update these names to the new\n   431\u2192\t// names as long as you verify first that the new names correspond to\n   432\u2192\t// the same meaning as what we're assuming below.\n   433\u2192\tif _, exists := nodesByName[\"test.a (destroy)\"]; !exists {\n   434\u2192\t\tt.Errorf(\"missing destroy node for resource instance test.a\")\n   435\u2192\t}\n   436\u2192\tif _, exists := nodesByName[\"output.test (expand)\"]; !exists {\n   437\u2192\t\tt.Errorf(\"missing expand for output value 'test'\")\n   438\u2192\t}\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192\t// We _must not_ have any node that expands a resource.\n   441\u2192\tif len(nodesByResourceExpand) != 0 {\n   442\u2192\t\tt.Errorf(\"resource expand nodes remain the graph after transform; should've been pruned\\n%s\", spew.Sdump(nodesByResourceExpand))\n   443\u2192\t}\n   444\u2192}\n   445\u2192\n   446\u2192// NoOp changes should not be participating in the destroy sequence\n   447\u2192func TestDestroyEdgeTransformer_noOp(t *testing.T) {\n   448\u2192\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   449\u2192\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(\"test_object.A\"))\n   450\u2192\tg.Add(testUpdateNode(\"test_object.B\"))\n   451\u2192\tg.Add(testDestroyNode(\"test_object.C\"))\n   452\u2192\n   453\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n   454\u2192\troot := state.EnsureModule(addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n   455\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   456\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.A\").Resource,\n   457\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   458\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n   459\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"A\"}`),\n   460\u2192\t\t},\n   461\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   462\u2192\t)\n   463\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   464\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.B\").Resource,\n   465\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   466\u2192\t\t\tStatus:       states.ObjectReady,\n   467\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON:    []byte(`{\"id\":\"B\",\"test_string\":\"x\"}`),\n   468\u2192\t\t\tDependencies: []addrs.ConfigResource{mustConfigResourceAddr(\"test_object.A\")},\n   469\u2192\t\t},\n   470\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   471\u2192\t)\n   472\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   473\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.C\").Resource,\n   474\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   475\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n   476\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"C\",\"test_string\":\"x\"}`),\n   477\u2192\t\t\tDependencies: []addrs.ConfigResource{mustConfigResourceAddr(\"test_object.A\"),\n   478\u2192\t\t\t\tmustConfigResourceAddr(\"test_object.B\")},\n   479\u2192\t\t},\n   480\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   481\u2192\t)\n   482\u2192\n   483\u2192\tif err := (&AttachStateTransformer{State: state}).Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   484\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   485\u2192\t}\n   486\u2192\n   487\u2192\ttf := &DestroyEdgeTransformer{\n   488\u2192\t\t// We only need a minimal object to indicate GraphNodeCreator change is\n   489\u2192\t\t// a NoOp here.\n   490\u2192\t\tChanges: &plans.ChangesSrc{\n   491\u2192\t\t\tResources: []*plans.ResourceInstanceChangeSrc{\n   492\u2192\t\t\t\t{\n   493\u2192\t\t\t\t\tAddr:      mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.B\"),\n   494\u2192\t\t\t\t\tChangeSrc: plans.ChangeSrc{Action: plans.NoOp},\n   495\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   496\u2192\t\t\t},\n   497\u2192\t\t},\n   498\u2192\t}\n   499\u2192\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   500\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   501\u2192\t}\n   502\u2192\n   503\u2192\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(`\n   504\u2192test_object.A (destroy)\n   505\u2192  test_object.C (destroy)\n   506\u2192test_object.B\n   507\u2192test_object.C (destroy)`)\n   508\u2192\n   509\u2192\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n   510\u2192\tif actual != expected {\n   511\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong result\\n\\ngot:\\n%s\\n\\nwant:\\n%s\", actual, expected)\n   512\u2192\t}\n   513\u2192}\n   514\u2192\n   515\u2192func TestDestroyEdgeTransformer_dataDependsOn(t *testing.T) {\n   516\u2192\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   517\u2192\n   518\u2192\taddrA := mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.A\")\n   519\u2192\tinstA := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addrA)\n   520\u2192\ta := &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{NodeAbstractResourceInstance: instA}\n   521\u2192\tg.Add(a)\n   522\u2192\n   523\u2192\t// B here represents a data sources, which is effectively an update during\n   524\u2192\t// apply, but won't have dependencies stored in the state.\n   525\u2192\taddrB := mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.B\")\n   526\u2192\tinstB := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addrB)\n   527\u2192\tinstB.Dependencies = append(instB.Dependencies, addrA.ConfigResource())\n   528\u2192\tb := &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{NodeAbstractResourceInstance: instB}\n   529\u2192\n   530\u2192\tg.Add(b)\n   531\u2192\n   532\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n   533\u2192\troot := state.EnsureModule(addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n   534\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   535\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.A\").Resource,\n   536\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   537\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n   538\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"A\"}`),\n   539\u2192\t\t},\n   540\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   541\u2192\t)\n   542\u2192\n   543\u2192\tif err := (&AttachStateTransformer{State: state}).Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   544\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   545\u2192\t}\n   546\u2192\n   547\u2192\ttf := &DestroyEdgeTransformer{}\n   548\u2192\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   549\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   550\u2192\t}\n   551\u2192\n   552\u2192\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n   553\u2192\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(`\n   554\u2192test_object.A (destroy)\n   555\u2192test_object.B\n   556\u2192  test_object.A (destroy)\n   557\u2192`)\n   558\u2192\tif actual != expected {\n   559\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong result\\n\\ngot:\\n%s\\n\\nwant:\\n%s\", actual, expected)\n   560\u2192\t}\n   561\u2192}\n   562\u2192\n   563\u2192func testDestroyNode(addrString string) GraphNodeDestroyer {\n   564\u2192\tinstAddr := mustResourceInstanceAddr(addrString)\n   565\u2192\tinst := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(instAddr)\n   566\u2192\treturn &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{NodeAbstractResourceInstance: inst}\n   567\u2192}\n   568\u2192\n   569\u2192func testUpdateNode(addrString string) GraphNodeCreator {\n   570\u2192\tinstAddr := mustResourceInstanceAddr(addrString)\n   571\u2192\tinst := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(instAddr)\n   572\u2192\treturn &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{NodeAbstractResourceInstance: inst}\n   573\u2192}\n   574\u2192\n   575\u2192const testTransformDestroyEdgeBasicStr = `\n   576\u2192test_object.A (destroy)\n   577\u2192  test_object.B (destroy)\n   578\u2192test_object.B (destroy)\n   579\u2192`\n   580\u2192\n   581\u2192const testTransformDestroyEdgeMultiStr = `\n   582\u2192test_object.A (destroy)\n   583\u2192  test_object.B (destroy)\n   584\u2192  test_object.C (destroy)\n   585\u2192test_object.B (destroy)\n   586\u2192  test_object.C (destroy)\n   587\u2192test_object.C (destroy)\n   588\u2192`\n   589\u2192\n   590\u2192const testTransformDestroyEdgeSelfRefStr = `\n   591\u2192test_object.A (destroy)\n   592\u2192`\n   593\u2192\n   594\u2192const testTransformDestroyEdgeModuleStr = `\n   595\u2192module.child.test_object.b (destroy)\n   596\u2192  test_object.a (destroy)\n   597\u2192test_object.a (destroy)\n   598\u2192`\n   599\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
153. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"log\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    16\u2192)\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192// PlanGraphBuilder is a GraphBuilder implementation that builds a graph for\n    19\u2192// planning and for other \"plan-like\" operations which don't require an\n    20\u2192// already-calculated plan as input.\n    21\u2192//\n    22\u2192// Unlike the apply graph builder, this graph builder:\n    23\u2192//\n    24\u2192//   - Makes its decisions primarily based on the given configuration, which\n    25\u2192//     represents the desired state.\n    26\u2192//\n    27\u2192//   - Ignores certain lifecycle concerns like create_before_destroy, because\n    28\u2192//     those are only important once we already know what action we're planning\n    29\u2192//     to take against a particular resource instance.\n    30\u2192type PlanGraphBuilder struct {\n    31\u2192\t// Config is the configuration tree to build a plan from.\n    32\u2192\tConfig *configs.Config\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192\t// State is the current state\n    35\u2192\tState *states.State\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192\t// RootVariableValues are the raw input values for root input variables\n    38\u2192\t// given by the caller, which we'll resolve into final values as part\n    39\u2192\t// of the plan walk.\n    40\u2192\tRootVariableValues InputValues\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\n    43\u2192\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\n    44\u2192\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\n    45\u2192\t// initialization step.\n    46\u2192\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192\t// Plugins is a library of plug-in components (providers and\n    49\u2192\t// provisioners) available for use.\n    50\u2192\tPlugins *contextPlugins\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192\t// Targets are resources to target\n    53\u2192\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192\t// ForceReplace are resource instances where if we would normally have\n    56\u2192\t// generated a NoOp or Update action then we'll force generating a replace\n    57\u2192\t// action instead. Create and Delete actions are not affected.\n    58\u2192\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing managed resources\n    61\u2192\tskipRefresh bool\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192\t// preDestroyRefresh indicates that we are executing the refresh which\n    64\u2192\t// happens immediately before a destroy plan, which happens to use the\n    65\u2192\t// normal planing mode so skipPlanChanges cannot be set.\n    66\u2192\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192\t// skipPlanChanges indicates that we should skip the step of comparing\n    69\u2192\t// prior state with configuration and generating planned changes to\n    70\u2192\t// resource instances. (This is for the \"refresh only\" planning mode,\n    71\u2192\t// where we _only_ do the refresh step.)\n    72\u2192\tskipPlanChanges bool\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192\tConcreteProvider                ConcreteProviderNodeFunc\n    75\u2192\tConcreteResource                ConcreteResourceNodeFunc\n    76\u2192\tConcreteResourceInstance        ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n    77\u2192\tConcreteResourceOrphan          ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n    78\u2192\tConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc\n    79\u2192\tConcreteModule                  ConcreteModuleNodeFunc\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\n    82\u2192\tOperation walkOperation\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\n    85\u2192\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\n    86\u2192\t// the actual graph.\n    87\u2192\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\n    90\u2192\t// framework.\n    91\u2192\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192\t// ImportTargets are the list of resources to import.\n    94\u2192\tImportTargets []*ImportTarget\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192\t// forgetResources lists the resources that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed\n    97\u2192\t// from state without destroying.\n    98\u2192\tforgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192\t// forgetModules lists the modules that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed\n   101\u2192\t// from state without destroying.\n   102\u2192\tforgetModules []addrs.Module\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192\t// GenerateConfig tells Terraform where to write and generated config for\n   105\u2192\t// any import targets that do not already have configuration.\n   106\u2192\t//\n   107\u2192\t// If empty, then config will not be generated.\n   108\u2192\tGenerateConfigPath string\n   109\u2192}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192// See GraphBuilder\n   112\u2192func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   113\u2192\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] building graph for %s\", b.Operation)\n   114\u2192\treturn (&BasicGraphBuilder{\n   115\u2192\t\tSteps: b.Steps(),\n   116\u2192\t\tName:  \"PlanGraphBuilder\",\n   117\u2192\t}).Build(path)\n   118\u2192}\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192// See GraphBuilder\n   121\u2192func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {\n   122\u2192\tswitch b.Operation {\n   123\u2192\tcase walkPlan:\n   124\u2192\t\tb.initPlan()\n   125\u2192\tcase walkPlanDestroy:\n   126\u2192\t\tb.initDestroy()\n   127\u2192\tcase walkValidate:\n   128\u2192\t\tb.initValidate()\n   129\u2192\tcase walkImport:\n   130\u2192\t\tb.initImport()\n   131\u2192\tdefault:\n   132\u2192\t\tpanic(\"invalid plan operation: \" + b.Operation.String())\n   133\u2192\t}\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n   136\u2192\t\t// Creates all the resources represented in the config\n   137\u2192\t\t&ConfigTransformer{\n   138\u2192\t\t\tConcrete: b.ConcreteResource,\n   139\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n   140\u2192\t\t\tdestroy:  b.Operation == walkDestroy || b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192\t\t\timportTargets: b.ImportTargets,\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192\t\t\t// We only want to generate config during a plan operation.\n   145\u2192\t\t\tgenerateConfigPathForImportTargets: b.GenerateConfigPath,\n   146\u2192\t\t},\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192\t\t// Add dynamic values\n   149\u2192\t\t&RootVariableTransformer{\n   150\u2192\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n   151\u2192\t\t\tRawValues:    b.RootVariableValues,\n   152\u2192\t\t\tPlanning:     true,\n   153\u2192\t\t\tDestroyApply: false, // always false for planning\n   154\u2192\t\t},\n   155\u2192\t\t&ModuleVariableTransformer{\n   156\u2192\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n   157\u2192\t\t\tPlanning:     true,\n   158\u2192\t\t\tDestroyApply: false, // always false for planning\n   159\u2192\t\t},\n   160\u2192\t\t&variableValidationTransformer{\n   161\u2192\t\t\tvalidateWalk: b.Operation == walkValidate,\n   162\u2192\t\t},\n   163\u2192\t\t&LocalTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n   164\u2192\t\t&OutputTransformer{\n   165\u2192\t\t\tConfig:      b.Config,\n   166\u2192\t\t\tRefreshOnly: b.skipPlanChanges || b.preDestroyRefresh,\n   167\u2192\t\t\tDestroying:  b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,\n   168\u2192\t\t\tOverrides:   b.Overrides,\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192\t\t\t// NOTE: We currently treat anything built with the plan graph\n   171\u2192\t\t\t// builder as \"planning\" for our purposes here, because we share\n   172\u2192\t\t\t// the same graph node implementation between all of the walk\n   173\u2192\t\t\t// types and so the pre-planning walks still think they are\n   174\u2192\t\t\t// producing a plan even though we immediately discard it.\n   175\u2192\t\t\tPlanning: true,\n   176\u2192\t\t},\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192\t\t// Add nodes and edges for the check block assertions. Check block data\n   179\u2192\t\t// sources were added earlier.\n   180\u2192\t\t&checkTransformer{\n   181\u2192\t\t\tConfig:    b.Config,\n   182\u2192\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n   183\u2192\t\t},\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192\t\t// Add orphan resources\n   186\u2192\t\t&OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{\n   187\u2192\t\t\tConcrete: b.ConcreteResourceOrphan,\n   188\u2192\t\t\tState:    b.State,\n   189\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n   190\u2192\t\t\tskip:     b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,\n   191\u2192\t\t},\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192\t\t// We also need nodes for any deposed instance objects present in the\n   194\u2192\t\t// state, so we can plan to destroy them. (During plan this will\n   195\u2192\t\t// intentionally skip creating nodes for _current_ objects, since\n   196\u2192\t\t// ConfigTransformer created nodes that will do that during\n   197\u2192\t\t// DynamicExpand.)\n   198\u2192\t\t&StateTransformer{\n   199\u2192\t\t\tConcreteCurrent: b.ConcreteResourceInstance,\n   200\u2192\t\t\tConcreteDeposed: b.ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed,\n   201\u2192\t\t\tState:           b.State,\n   202\u2192\t\t},\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192\t\t// Attach the state\n   205\u2192\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: b.State},\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192\t\t// Create orphan output nodes\n   208\u2192\t\t&OrphanOutputTransformer{\n   209\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n   210\u2192\t\t\tState:    b.State,\n   211\u2192\t\t\tPlanning: true,\n   212\u2192\t\t},\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192\t\t// Attach the configuration to any resources\n   215\u2192\t\t&AttachResourceConfigTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192\t\t// add providers\n   218\u2192\t\ttransformProviders(b.ConcreteProvider, b.Config, b.ExternalProviderConfigs),\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192\t\t// Remove modules no longer present in the config\n   221\u2192\t\t&RemovedModuleTransformer{Config: b.Config, State: b.State},\n   222\u2192\n   223\u2192\t\t// Must attach schemas before ReferenceTransformer so that we can\n   224\u2192\t\t// analyze the configuration to find references.\n   225\u2192\t\t&AttachSchemaTransformer{Plugins: b.Plugins, Config: b.Config},\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192\t\t// Create expansion nodes for all of the module calls. This must\n   228\u2192\t\t// come after all other transformers that create nodes representing\n   229\u2192\t\t// objects that can belong to modules.\n   230\u2192\t\t&ModuleExpansionTransformer{Concrete: b.ConcreteModule, Config: b.Config},\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192\t\t// Plug in any external references.\n   233\u2192\t\t&ExternalReferenceTransformer{\n   234\u2192\t\t\tExternalReferences: b.ExternalReferences,\n   235\u2192\t\t},\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192\t\t&AttachDependenciesTransformer{},\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192\t\t// Make sure data sources are aware of any depends_on from the\n   242\u2192\t\t// configuration\n   243\u2192\t\t&attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer{},\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192\t\t// DestroyEdgeTransformer is only required during a plan so that the\n   246\u2192\t\t// TargetsTransformer can determine which nodes to keep in the graph.\n   247\u2192\t\t&DestroyEdgeTransformer{\n   248\u2192\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n   249\u2192\t\t},\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192\t\t&pruneUnusedNodesTransformer{\n   252\u2192\t\t\tskip: b.Operation != walkPlanDestroy,\n   253\u2192\t\t},\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192\t\t// Target\n   256\u2192\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: b.Targets},\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192\t\t// Detect when create_before_destroy must be forced on for a particular\n   259\u2192\t\t// node due to dependency edges, to avoid graph cycles during apply.\n   260\u2192\t\t&ForcedCBDTransformer{},\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192\t\t// Close any ephemeral resource instances.\n   263\u2192\t\t&ephemeralResourceCloseTransformer{skip: b.Operation == walkValidate},\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192\t\t// Close opened plugin connections\n   266\u2192\t\t&CloseProviderTransformer{},\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192\t\t// Close the root module\n   269\u2192\t\t&CloseRootModuleTransformer{},\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192\t\t// Perform the transitive reduction to make our graph a bit\n   272\u2192\t\t// more understandable if possible (it usually is possible).\n   273\u2192\t\t&TransitiveReductionTransformer{},\n   274\u2192\t}\n   275\u2192\n   276\u2192\treturn steps\n   277\u2192}\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) initPlan() {\n   280\u2192\tb.ConcreteProvider = func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n   281\u2192\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n   282\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n   283\u2192\t\t}\n   284\u2192\t}\n   285\u2192\n   286\u2192\tb.ConcreteResource = func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n   287\u2192\t\treturn &nodeExpandPlannableResource{\n   288\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n   289\u2192\t\t\tskipRefresh:          b.skipRefresh,\n   290\u2192\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:      b.skipPlanChanges,\n   291\u2192\t\t\tpreDestroyRefresh:    b.preDestroyRefresh,\n   292\u2192\t\t\tforceReplace:         b.ForceReplace,\n   293\u2192\t\t}\n   294\u2192\t}\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192\tb.ConcreteResourceOrphan = func(a *NodeResourceInstanceBase) dag.Vertex {\n   297\u2192\t\treturn &NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\n   298\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   299\u2192\t\t\tskipRefresh:                  b.skipRefresh,\n   300\u2192\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:              b.skipPlanChanges,\n   301\u2192\t\t\tforgetResources:              b.forgetResources,\n   302\u2192\t\t\tforgetModules:                b.forgetModules,\n   303\u2192\t\t}\n   304\u2192\t}\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192\tb.ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed = func(a *NodeResourceInstanceBase, key states.DeposedKey) dag.Vertex {\n   307\u2192\t\treturn &NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n   308\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   309\u2192\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   key,\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192\t\t\tskipRefresh:     b.skipRefresh,\n   312\u2192\t\t\tskipPlanChanges: b.skipPlanChanges,\n   313\u2192\t\t\tforgetResources: b.forgetResources,\n   314\u2192\t\t\tforgetModules:   b.forgetModules,\n   315\u2192\t\t}\n   316\u2192\t}\n   317\u2192}\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) initDestroy() {\n   320\u2192\tb.initPlan()\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192\tb.ConcreteResourceInstance = func(a *NodeResourceInstanceBase) dag.Vertex {\n   323\u2192\t\treturn &NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance{\n   324\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   325\u2192\t\t\tskipRefresh:                  b.skipRefresh,\n   326\u2192\t\t}\n   327\u2192\t}\n   328\u2192}\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) initValidate() {\n   331\u2192\t// Set the provider to the normal provider. This will ask for input.\n   332\u2192\tb.ConcreteProvider = func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n   333\u2192\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n   334\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n   335\u2192\t\t}\n   336\u2192\t}\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192\tb.ConcreteResource = func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n   339\u2192\t\treturn &NodeValidatableResource{\n   340\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n   341\u2192\t\t}\n   342\u2192\t}\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192\tb.ConcreteModule = func(n *nodeExpandModule) dag.Vertex {\n   345\u2192\t\treturn &nodeValidateModule{\n   346\u2192\t\t\tnodeExpandModule: *n,\n   347\u2192\t\t}\n   348\u2192\t}\n   349\u2192}\n   350\u2192\n   351\u2192func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) initImport() {\n   352\u2192\tb.ConcreteProvider = func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n   353\u2192\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n   354\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n   355\u2192\t\t}\n   356\u2192\t}\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192\tb.ConcreteResource = func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n   359\u2192\t\treturn &nodeExpandPlannableResource{\n   360\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n   361\u2192\n   362\u2192\t\t\t// For now we always skip planning changes for import, since we are\n   363\u2192\t\t\t// not going to combine importing with other changes. This is\n   364\u2192\t\t\t// temporary to try and maintain existing import behaviors, but\n   365\u2192\t\t\t// planning will need to be allowed for more complex configurations.\n   366\u2192\t\t\tskipPlanChanges: true,\n   367\u2192\n   368\u2192\t\t\t// We also skip refresh for now, since the plan output is written\n   369\u2192\t\t\t// as the new state, and users are not expecting the import process\n   370\u2192\t\t\t// to update any other instances in state.\n   371\u2192\t\t\tskipRefresh: true,\n   372\u2192\t\t}\n   373\u2192\t}\n   374\u2192}\n   375\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
154. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/graph_builder_apply.go",
  "limit": 200
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n     8\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    15\u2192)\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192// ApplyGraphBuilder implements GraphBuilder and is responsible for building\n    18\u2192// a graph for applying a Terraform diff.\n    19\u2192//\n    20\u2192// Because the graph is built from the diff (vs. the config or state),\n    21\u2192// this helps ensure that the apply-time graph doesn't modify any resources\n    22\u2192// that aren't explicitly in the diff. There are other scenarios where the\n    23\u2192// diff can be deviated, so this is just one layer of protection.\n    24\u2192type ApplyGraphBuilder struct {\n    25\u2192\t// Config is the configuration tree that the diff was built from.\n    26\u2192\tConfig *configs.Config\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\t// Changes describes the changes that we need apply.\n    29\u2192\tChanges *plans.ChangesSrc\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192\t// DeferredChanges describes the changes that were deferred during the plan\n    32\u2192\t// and should not be applied.\n    33\u2192\tDeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\t// State is the current state\n    36\u2192\tState *states.State\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192\t// RootVariableValues are the root module input variables captured as\n    39\u2192\t// part of the plan object, which we must reproduce in the apply step\n    40\u2192\t// to get a consistent result.\n    41\u2192\tRootVariableValues InputValues\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\n    44\u2192\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\n    45\u2192\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\n    46\u2192\t// initialization step.\n    47\u2192\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192\t// Plugins is a library of the plug-in components (providers and\n    50\u2192\t// provisioners) available for use.\n    51\u2192\tPlugins *contextPlugins\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\t// Targets are resources to target. This is only required to make sure\n    54\u2192\t// unnecessary outputs aren't included in the apply graph. The plan\n    55\u2192\t// builder successfully handles targeting resources. In the future,\n    56\u2192\t// outputs should go into the diff so that this is unnecessary.\n    57\u2192\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192\t// ForceReplace are the resource instance addresses that the user\n    60\u2192\t// requested to force replacement for when creating the plan, if any.\n    61\u2192\t// The apply step refers to these as part of verifying that the planned\n    62\u2192\t// actions remain consistent between plan and apply.\n    63\u2192\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\n    66\u2192\tOperation walkOperation\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\n    69\u2192\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\n    70\u2192\t// the actual graph.\n    71\u2192\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\n    74\u2192\t// framework.\n    75\u2192\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192// See GraphBuilder\n    79\u2192func (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    80\u2192\treturn (&BasicGraphBuilder{\n    81\u2192\t\tSteps: b.Steps(),\n    82\u2192\t\tName:  \"ApplyGraphBuilder\",\n    83\u2192\t}).Build(path)\n    84\u2192}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192// See GraphBuilder\n    87\u2192func (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {\n    88\u2192\t// Custom factory for creating providers.\n    89\u2192\tconcreteProvider := func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n    90\u2192\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n    91\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n    92\u2192\t\t}\n    93\u2192\t}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192\tconcreteResource := func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n    96\u2192\t\treturn &nodeExpandApplyableResource{\n    97\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n    98\u2192\t\t}\n    99\u2192\t}\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192\tconcreteResourceInstance := func(a *NodeResourceInstanceBase) dag.Vertex {\n   102\u2192\t\treturn &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{\n   103\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   104\u2192\t\t\tforceReplace:                 b.ForceReplace,\n   105\u2192\t\t}\n   106\u2192\t}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n   109\u2192\t\t// Creates all the resources represented in the config. During apply,\n   110\u2192\t\t// we use this just to ensure that the whole-resource metadata is\n   111\u2192\t\t// updated to reflect things such as whether the count argument is\n   112\u2192\t\t// set in config, or which provider configuration manages each resource.\n   113\u2192\t\t&ConfigTransformer{\n   114\u2192\t\t\tConcrete: concreteResource,\n   115\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n   116\u2192\t\t},\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192\t\t// Add dynamic values\n   119\u2192\t\t&RootVariableTransformer{\n   120\u2192\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n   121\u2192\t\t\tRawValues:    b.RootVariableValues,\n   122\u2192\t\t\tDestroyApply: b.Operation == walkDestroy,\n   123\u2192\t\t},\n   124\u2192\t\t&ModuleVariableTransformer{\n   125\u2192\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n   126\u2192\t\t\tDestroyApply: b.Operation == walkDestroy,\n   127\u2192\t\t},\n   128\u2192\t\t&variableValidationTransformer{},\n   129\u2192\t\t&LocalTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n   130\u2192\t\t&OutputTransformer{\n   131\u2192\t\t\tConfig:     b.Config,\n   132\u2192\t\t\tDestroying: b.Operation == walkDestroy,\n   133\u2192\t\t\tOverrides:  b.Overrides,\n   134\u2192\t\t},\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192\t\t// Creates all the resource instances represented in the diff, along\n   137\u2192\t\t// with dependency edges against the whole-resource nodes added by\n   138\u2192\t\t// ConfigTransformer above.\n   139\u2192\t\t&DiffTransformer{\n   140\u2192\t\t\tConcrete: concreteResourceInstance,\n   141\u2192\t\t\tState:    b.State,\n   142\u2192\t\t\tChanges:  b.Changes,\n   143\u2192\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n   144\u2192\t\t},\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192\t\t// Creates nodes for all the deferred changes.\n   147\u2192\t\t&DeferredTransformer{\n   148\u2192\t\t\tDeferredChanges: b.DeferredChanges,\n   149\u2192\t\t},\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192\t\t// Add nodes and edges for check block assertions. Check block data\n   152\u2192\t\t// sources were added earlier.\n   153\u2192\t\t&checkTransformer{\n   154\u2192\t\t\tConfig:    b.Config,\n   155\u2192\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n   156\u2192\t\t},\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192\t\t// Attach the state\n   159\u2192\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: b.State},\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192\t\t// Create orphan output nodes\n   162\u2192\t\t&OrphanOutputTransformer{Config: b.Config, State: b.State},\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192\t\t// Attach the configuration to any resources\n   165\u2192\t\t&AttachResourceConfigTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192\t\t// add providers\n   168\u2192\t\ttransformProviders(concreteProvider, b.Config, b.ExternalProviderConfigs),\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192\t\t// Remove modules no longer present in the config\n   171\u2192\t\t&RemovedModuleTransformer{Config: b.Config, State: b.State},\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192\t\t// Must attach schemas before ReferenceTransformer so that we can\n   174\u2192\t\t// analyze the configuration to find references.\n   175\u2192\t\t&AttachSchemaTransformer{Plugins: b.Plugins, Config: b.Config},\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192\t\t// Create expansion nodes for all of the module calls. This must\n   178\u2192\t\t// come after all other transformers that create nodes representing\n   179\u2192\t\t// objects that can belong to modules.\n   180\u2192\t\t&ModuleExpansionTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n   181\u2192\n   182\u2192\t\t// Plug in any external references.\n   183\u2192\t\t&ExternalReferenceTransformer{\n   184\u2192\t\t\tExternalReferences: b.ExternalReferences,\n   185\u2192\t\t},\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192\t\t// Connect references so ordering is correct\n   188\u2192\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n   189\u2192\t\t&AttachDependenciesTransformer{},\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192\t\t// Nested data blocks should be loaded after every other resource has\n   192\u2192\t\t// done its thing.\n   193\u2192\t\t&checkStartTransformer{Config: b.Config, Operation: b.Operation},\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192\t\t// Detect when create_before_destroy must be forced on for a particular\n   196\u2192\t\t// node due to dependency edges, to avoid graph cycles during apply.\n   197\u2192\t\t//\n   198\u2192\t\t// FIXME: this should not need to be recalculated during apply.\n   199\u2192\t\t// Currently however, the instance object which stores the planned\n   200\u2192\t\t// information is lost for newly created instances because it contains\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
155. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_apply.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n     8\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    10\u2192)\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192// nodeExpandApplyableResource handles the first layer of resource\n    13\u2192// expansion during apply. Even though the resource instances themselves are\n    14\u2192// already expanded from the plan, we still need to expand the\n    15\u2192// NodeApplyableResource nodes into their respective modules.\n    16\u2192type nodeExpandApplyableResource struct {\n    17\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResource\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192\tPartialExpansions []addrs.PartialExpandedResource\n    20\u2192}\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192var (\n    23\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    24\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    25\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    26\u2192\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    27\u2192\t_ graphNodeExpandsInstances     = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    28\u2192\t_ GraphNodeTargetable           = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    29\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable    = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n    30\u2192)\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) expandsInstances() {\n    33\u2192}\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n    36\u2192\trefs := n.NodeAbstractResource.References()\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192\t// The expand node needs to connect to the individual resource instances it\n    39\u2192\t// references, but cannot refer to it's own instances without causing\n    40\u2192\t// cycles. It would be preferable to entirely disallow self references\n    41\u2192\t// without the `self` identifier, but those were allowed in provisioners\n    42\u2192\t// for compatibility with legacy configuration. We also can't always just\n    43\u2192\t// filter them out for all resource node types, because the only method we\n    44\u2192\t// have for catching certain invalid configurations are the cycles that\n    45\u2192\t// result from these inter-instance references.\n    46\u2192\treturn filterSelfRefs(n.Addr.Resource, refs)\n    47\u2192}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) Name() string {\n    50\u2192\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.Name() + \" (expand)\"\n    51\u2192}\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    54\u2192\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n    55\u2192\t\treturn n.dynamicExpandEphemeral(ctx)\n    56\u2192\t}\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    59\u2192\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n    60\u2192\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\n    61\u2192\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n    62\u2192\t\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(ctx, module)\n    63\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)))\n    64\u2192\t}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192\treturn nil, diags\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192// We need to expand the ephemeral resources mostly the same as we do during\n    70\u2192// planning. There a lot of options than happen during planning which aren't\n    71\u2192// applicable to apply however, and we have to make sure we don't re-register\n    72\u2192// checks which already recorded in the plan, so we create a pared down version\n    73\u2192// of the plan expansion here.\n    74\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) dynamicExpandEphemeral(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    75\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    76\u2192\tvar g Graph\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n    79\u2192\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n    82\u2192\t\tresAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)\n    83\u2192\t\texpDiags := n.expandEphemeralResourceInstances(ctx, resAddr, &g)\n    84\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(expDiags)\n    85\u2192\t}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192\treturn &g, diags\n    88\u2192}\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) expandEphemeralResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, g *Graph) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n    91\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192\t// The rest of our work here needs to know which module instance it's\n    94\u2192\t// working in, so that it can evaluate expressions in the appropriate scope.\n    95\u2192\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\n    98\u2192\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\n    99\u2192\t// to work below.\n   100\u2192\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"testing\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    17\u2192)\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192func TestNodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject_Execute(t *testing.T) {\n    20\u2192\tdeposedKey := states.NewDeposedKey()\n    21\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n    22\u2192\tabsResource := mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_instance.foo\")\n    23\u2192\tstate.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceDeposed(\n    24\u2192\t\tabsResource.Resource,\n    25\u2192\t\tdeposedKey,\n    26\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    27\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectTainted,\n    28\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"bar\"}`),\n    29\u2192\t\t},\n    30\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n    31\u2192\t)\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192\tp := testProvider(\"test\")\n    34\u2192\tp.ConfigureProvider(providers.ConfigureProviderRequest{})\n    35\u2192\tp.UpgradeResourceStateResponse = &providers.UpgradeResourceStateResponse{\n    36\u2192\t\tUpgradedState: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n    37\u2192\t\t\t\"id\": cty.StringVal(\"bar\"),\n    38\u2192\t\t}),\n    39\u2192\t}\n    40\u2192\tctx := &MockEvalContext{\n    41\u2192\t\tStateState:        state.SyncWrapper(),\n    42\u2192\t\tPrevRunStateState: state.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper(),\n    43\u2192\t\tRefreshStateState: state.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper(),\n    44\u2192\t\tProviderProvider:  p,\n    45\u2192\t\tProviderSchemaSchema: providers.ProviderSchema{\n    46\u2192\t\t\tResourceTypes: map[string]providers.Schema{\n    47\u2192\t\t\t\t\"test_instance\": {\n    48\u2192\t\t\t\t\tBlock: &configschema.Block{\n    49\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tAttributes: map[string]*configschema.Attribute{\n    50\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": {\n    51\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tType:     cty.String,\n    52\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tComputed: true,\n    53\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n    54\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n    55\u2192\t\t\t\t\t},\n    56\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n    57\u2192\t\t\t},\n    58\u2192\t\t},\n    59\u2192\t\tChangesChanges: plans.NewChanges().SyncWrapper(),\n    60\u2192\t}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192\tnode := NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n    63\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n    64\u2192\t\t\tAddr: absResource,\n    65\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n    66\u2192\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n    67\u2192\t\t\t},\n    68\u2192\t\t},\n    69\u2192\t\tDeposedKey: deposedKey,\n    70\u2192\t}\n    71\u2192\terr := node.Execute(ctx, walkPlan)\n    72\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    73\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", err)\n    74\u2192\t}\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192\tif !p.UpgradeResourceStateCalled {\n    77\u2192\t\tt.Errorf(\"UpgradeResourceState wasn't called; should've been called to upgrade the previous run's object\")\n    78\u2192\t}\n    79\u2192\tif !p.ReadResourceCalled {\n    80\u2192\t\tt.Errorf(\"ReadResource wasn't called; should've been called to refresh the deposed object\")\n    81\u2192\t}\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192\tchange := ctx.Changes().GetResourceInstanceChange(absResource, deposedKey)\n    84\u2192\tif got, want := change.Change.Action, plans.Delete; got != want {\n    85\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong planned action\\ngot:  %s\\nwant: %s\", got, want)\n    86\u2192\t}\n    87\u2192}\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192func TestNodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject_Execute(t *testing.T) {\n    90\u2192\tdeposedKey := states.NewDeposedKey()\n    91\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n    92\u2192\tabsResource := mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_instance.foo\")\n    93\u2192\tstate.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceDeposed(\n    94\u2192\t\tabsResource.Resource,\n    95\u2192\t\tdeposedKey,\n    96\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n    97\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectTainted,\n    98\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"bar\"}`),\n    99\u2192\t\t},\n   100\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   101\u2192\t)\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192\tschema := providers.ProviderSchema{\n   104\u2192\t\tResourceTypes: map[string]providers.Schema{\n   105\u2192\t\t\t\"test_instance\": {\n   106\u2192\t\t\t\tBlock: &configschema.Block{\n   107\u2192\t\t\t\t\tAttributes: map[string]*configschema.Attribute{\n   108\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": {\n   109\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tType:     cty.String,\n   110\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tComputed: true,\n   111\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n   112\u2192\t\t\t\t\t},\n   113\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   114\u2192\t\t\t},\n   115\u2192\t\t},\n   116\u2192\t}\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192\tp := testProvider(\"test\")\n   119\u2192\tp.ConfigureProvider(providers.ConfigureProviderRequest{})\n   120\u2192\tp.GetProviderSchemaResponse = &schema\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192\tp.UpgradeResourceStateResponse = &providers.UpgradeResourceStateResponse{\n   123\u2192\t\tUpgradedState: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n   124\u2192\t\t\t\"id\": cty.StringVal(\"bar\"),\n   125\u2192\t\t}),\n   126\u2192\t}\n   127\u2192\tctx := &MockEvalContext{\n   128\u2192\t\tStateState:           state.SyncWrapper(),\n   129\u2192\t\tProviderProvider:     p,\n   130\u2192\t\tProviderSchemaSchema: schema,\n   131\u2192\t\tChangesChanges:       plans.NewChanges().SyncWrapper(),\n   132\u2192\t\tDeferralsState:       deferring.NewDeferred(false),\n   133\u2192\t}\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192\tnode := NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n   136\u2192\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n   137\u2192\t\t\tAddr: absResource,\n   138\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n   139\u2192\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   140\u2192\t\t\t},\n   141\u2192\t\t},\n   142\u2192\t\tDeposedKey: deposedKey,\n   143\u2192\t}\n   144\u2192\terr := node.Execute(ctx, walkApply)\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   147\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", err)\n   148\u2192\t}\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192\tif !state.Empty() {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_import.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    14\u2192)\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192type graphNodeImportState struct {\n    17\u2192\tAddr             addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addr is the resource address to import into\n    18\u2192\tID               string                    // ID is the ID to import as\n    19\u2192\tProviderAddr     addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // Provider address given by the user, or implied by the resource type\n    20\u2192\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // provider node address after resolution\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192\tstates []providers.ImportedResource\n    23\u2192}\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192var (\n    26\u2192\t_ GraphNodeModulePath        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n    27\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n    28\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer  = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n    29\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n    30\u2192)\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) Name() string {\n    33\u2192\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (import id %q)\", n.Addr, n.ID)\n    34\u2192}\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\n    37\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {\n    38\u2192\t// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.\n    39\u2192\t// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState\n    40\u2192\t// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the\n    41\u2192\t// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type\n    42\u2192\t// name.\n    43\u2192\treturn n.ProviderAddr, false\n    44\u2192}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\n    47\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) Provider() addrs.Provider {\n    48\u2192\t// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.\n    49\u2192\t// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState\n    50\u2192\t// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the\n    51\u2192\t// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type\n    52\u2192\t// name.\n    53\u2192\treturn n.ProviderAddr.Provider\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\n    57\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) SetProvider(addr addrs.AbsProviderConfig) {\n    58\u2192\tn.ResolvedProvider = addr\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192// GraphNodeModuleInstance\n    62\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\n    63\u2192\treturn n.Addr.Module\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192// GraphNodeModulePath\n    67\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) ModulePath() addrs.Module {\n    68\u2192\treturn n.Addr.Module.Module()\n    69\u2192}\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192// GraphNodeExecutable impl.\n    72\u2192func (n *graphNodeImportState) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    73\u2192\t// Reset our states\n    74\u2192\tn.states = nil\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192\tprovider, _, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n    77\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n    78\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    79\u2192\t\treturn diags\n    80\u2192\t}\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192\t// import state\n    83\u2192\tabsAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(ctx.Path())\n    84\u2192\thookResourceID := HookResourceIdentity{\n    85\u2192\t\tAddr:         absAddr,\n    86\u2192\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\n    87\u2192\t}\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192\t// Call pre-import hook\n    90\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n    91\u2192\t\treturn h.PreImportState(hookResourceID, n.ID)\n    92\u2192\t}))\n    93\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    94\u2192\t\treturn diags\n    95\u2192\t}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192\tresp := provider.ImportResourceState(providers.ImportResourceStateRequest{\n    98\u2192\t\tTypeName: n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n    99\u2192\t\tID:       n.ID,\n   100\u2192\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
158. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_plan_partialexp.go",
  "limit": 150
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/marks\"\n    17\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    18\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange\"\n    19\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    20\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    21\u2192)\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192// nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource is a graph node that stands in for\n    24\u2192// an unbounded set of potential resource instances that we don't yet know.\n    25\u2192//\n    26\u2192// Its job is to check the configuration as much as we can with the information\n    27\u2192// that's available (so we can raise an error early if something is clearly\n    28\u2192// wrong across _all_ potential instances) and to record a placeholder value\n    29\u2192// for use when evaluating other objects that refer to this resource.\n    30\u2192//\n    31\u2192// This is the partial-expanded equivalent of NodePlannableResourceInstance.\n    32\u2192type nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource struct {\n    33\u2192\taddr             addrs.PartialExpandedResource\n    34\u2192\tconfig           *configs.Resource\n    35\u2192\tresolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n    36\u2192\tskipPlanChanges  bool\n    37\u2192}\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192var (\n    40\u2192\t_ graphNodeEvalContextScope = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)\n    41\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource   = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)\n    42\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable       = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)\n    43\u2192)\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192// Name implements [dag.NamedVertex].\n    46\u2192func (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) Name() string {\n    47\u2192\treturn n.addr.String()\n    48\u2192}\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192// Path implements graphNodeEvalContextScope.\n    51\u2192func (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) Path() evalContextScope {\n    52\u2192\tif moduleAddr, ok := n.addr.ModuleInstance(); ok {\n    53\u2192\t\treturn evalContextModuleInstance{Addr: moduleAddr}\n    54\u2192\t} else if moduleAddr, ok := n.addr.PartialExpandedModule(); ok {\n    55\u2192\t\treturn evalContextPartialExpandedModule{Addr: moduleAddr}\n    56\u2192\t} else {\n    57\u2192\t\t// Should not get here: at least one of the two cases above\n    58\u2192\t\t// should always be true for any valid addrs.PartialExpandedResource\n    59\u2192\t\tpanic(\"addrs.PartialExpandedResource has neither a partial-expanded or a fully-expanded module instance address\")\n    60\u2192\t}\n    61\u2192}\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192// ResourceAddr implements GraphNodeConfigResource.\n    64\u2192func (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) ResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource {\n    65\u2192\treturn n.addr.ConfigResource()\n    66\u2192}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192// Execute implements GraphNodeExecutable.\n    69\u2192func (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n    70\u2192\t// Because this node type implements [graphNodeEvalContextScope], the\n    71\u2192\t// given EvalContext could either be for a fully-expanded module instance\n    72\u2192\t// or an unbounded set of potential module instances sharing a common\n    73\u2192\t// prefix. The logic here doesn't need to vary between the two because\n    74\u2192\t// the differences are encapsulated in the EvalContext abstraction,\n    75\u2192\t// but if you're unsure which of the two is being used then look for\n    76\u2192\t// the following line in the logs to see if there's a [*] marker on\n    77\u2192\t// any of the module instance steps, or if the [*] is applied only to\n    78\u2192\t// the resource itself.\n    79\u2192\t//\n    80\u2192\t// Fully-expanded module example:\n    81\u2192\t//\n    82\u2192\t//     module.foo[\"a\"].type.name[*]\n    83\u2192\t//\n    84\u2192\t// Partial-expanded module example:\n    85\u2192\t//\n    86\u2192\t//     module.foo[*].type.name[*]\n    87\u2192\t//\n    88\u2192\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource: checking all of %s\", n.addr.String())\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    91\u2192\tswitch n.addr.Resource().Mode {\n    92\u2192\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n    93\u2192\t\tchange, changeDiags := n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\n    94\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(changeDiags)\n    95\u2192\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceExpansionDeferred(n.addr, change)\n    96\u2192\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n    97\u2192\t\tchange, changeDiags := n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\n    98\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(changeDiags)\n    99\u2192\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportDataSourceExpansionDeferred(n.addr, change)\n   100\u2192\tcase addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:\n   101\u2192\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportEphemeralResourceExpansionDeferred(n.addr)\n   102\u2192\tdefault:\n   103\u2192\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported resource mode %s\", n.config.Mode))\n   104\u2192\t}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192\t// Registering this allows downstream resources that depend on this one\n   107\u2192\t// to know that they need to defer themselves too, in order to preserve\n   108\u2192\t// correct dependency order.\n   109\u2192\treturn diags\n   110\u2192}\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192// Logic here mirrors (*NodePlannableResourceInstance).managedResourceExecute.\n   113\u2192func (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   114\u2192\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192\t// We cannot fully plan partial-expanded resources because we don't know\n   117\u2192\t// what addresses they will have, but in this function we'll still go\n   118\u2192\t// through many of the familiar motions of planning so that we can give\n   119\u2192\t// feedback sooner if we can prove that the configuration is already\n   120\u2192\t// invalid even with the partial information we have here. This is just\n   121\u2192\t// to shorten the iterative journey, so nothing here actually contributes\n   122\u2192\t// new actions to the plan.\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192\t// We'll make a basic change for us to use as a placeholder for the time\n   125\u2192\t// being, and we'll populate it as we get more info.\n   126\u2192\tchange := plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n   127\u2192\t\tAddr:         n.addr.UnknownResourceInstance(),\n   128\u2192\t\tProviderAddr: n.resolvedProvider,\n   129\u2192\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n   130\u2192\t\t\t// We don't actually know the action, but we simulate the plan later\n   131\u2192\t\t\t// as a create action so we'll use that here too.\n   132\u2192\t\t\tAction: plans.Create,\n   133\u2192\t\t\tBefore: cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\n   134\u2192\t\t\tAfter:  cty.DynamicVal, // This will be populated later\n   135\u2192\t\t},\n   136\u2192\t}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.resolvedProvider)\n   139\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n   140\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   141\u2192\t\treturn &change, diags\n   142\u2192\t}\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(validateSelfRef(n.addr.Resource(), n.config.Config, providerSchema))\n   145\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   146\u2192\t\treturn &change, diags\n   147\u2192\t}\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.addr.Resource())\n   150\u2192\tif schema == nil {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

166. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_diff.go",
  "new_string": "\t\tif delete {\n\t\t\t// Destroying always uses a destroy-specific node type, though\n\t\t\t// which one depends on whether we're destroying a current object\n\t\t\t// or a deposed object.\n\t\t\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\n\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addr)\n\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeResourceInstanceBase: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeResourceInstanceBase: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t\tDeposedKey:               dk,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}",
  "old_string": "\t\tif delete {\n\t\t\t// Destroying always uses a destroy-specific node type, though\n\t\t\t// which one depends on whether we're destroying a current object\n\t\t\t// or a deposed object.\n\t\t\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\n\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addr)\n\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}",
  "replace_all": true
}

"   515\u2192func TestDestroyEdgeTransformer_dataDependsOn(t *testing.T) {\n   516\u2192\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n   517\u2192\n   518\u2192\taddrA := mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.A\")\n   519\u2192\tinstA := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addrA)\n   520\u2192\ta := &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{NodeAbstractResourceInstance: instA}\n   521\u2192\tg.Add(a)\n   522\u2192\n   523\u2192\t// B here represents a data sources, which is effectively an update during\n   524\u2192\t// apply, but won't have dependencies stored in the state.\n   525\u2192\taddrB := mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.B\")\n   526\u2192\tinstB := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addrB)\n   527\u2192\tinstB.Dependencies = append(instB.Dependencies, addrA.ConfigResource())\n   528\u2192\tb := &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{NodeAbstractResourceInstance: instB}\n   529\u2192\n   530\u2192\tg.Add(b)\n   531\u2192\n   532\u2192\tstate := states.NewState()\n   533\u2192\troot := state.EnsureModule(addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n   534\u2192\troot.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n   535\u2192\t\tmustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.A\").Resource,\n   536\u2192\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n   537\u2192\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectReady,\n   538\u2192\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"A\"}`),\n   539\u2192\t\t},\n   540\u2192\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n   541\u2192\t)\n   542\u2192\n   543\u2192\tif err := (&AttachStateTransformer{State: state}).Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   544\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   545\u2192\t}\n   546\u2192\n   547\u2192\ttf := &DestroyEdgeTransformer{}\n   548\u2192\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n   549\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n   550\u2192\t}\n   551\u2192\n   552\u2192\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n   553\u2192\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(`\n   554\u2192test_object.A (destroy)\n   555\u2192test_object.B\n   556\u2192  test_object.A (destroy)\n   557\u2192`)\n   558\u2192\tif actual != expected {\n   559\u2192\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong result\\n\\ngot:\\n%s\\n\\nwant:\\n%s\", actual, expected)\n   560\u2192\t}\n   561\u2192}\n   562\u2192\n   563\u2192func testDestroyNode(addrString string) GraphNodeDestroyer {\n   564\u2192\tinstAddr := mustResourceInstanceAddr(addrString)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
