Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"log\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    14\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    15\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    17\u2192)\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192// ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert\n    20\u2192// an abstract resource instance to a concrete one of some type that has\n    21\u2192// an associated deposed object key.\n    22\u2192type ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc func(*NodeResourceInstanceBase, states.DeposedKey) dag.Vertex\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192type GraphNodeDeposedResourceInstanceObject interface {\n    25\u2192\tDeposedInstanceObjectKey() states.DeposedKey\n    26\u2192}\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192// NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject represents deposed resource\n    29\u2192// instance objects during plan. These are distinct from the primary object\n    30\u2192// for each resource instance since they must be either destroyed or forgotten.\n    31\u2192//\n    32\u2192// This node type is also used during the refresh walk to ensure that the\n    33\u2192// record of a deposed object is up to date before we plan to destroy or forget\n    34\u2192// it.\n    35\u2192type NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject struct {\n    36\u2192\t*NodeResourceInstanceBase\n    37\u2192\tDeposedKey states.DeposedKey\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances\n    40\u2192\tskipRefresh bool\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192\t// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions\n    43\u2192\t// for any instances.\n    44\u2192\tskipPlanChanges bool\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\t// forgetResources lists resources that should not be destroyed, only removed\n    47\u2192\t// from state.\n    48\u2192\tforgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192\t// forgetModules lists modules that should not be destroyed, only removed\n    51\u2192\t// from state.\n    52\u2192\tforgetModules []addrs.Module\n    53\u2192}\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192var (\n    56\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDeposedResourceInstanceObject = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    57\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource                = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    58\u2192\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance              = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    59\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable                 = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    60\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer                    = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    61\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable                    = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    62\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer              = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    63\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer           = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    64\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer                     = (*NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n    65\u2192)\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192func (n *NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n    68\u2192\treturn &n.Addr\n    69\u2192}\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192func (n *NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject) Name() string {\n    72\u2192\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (deposed %s)\", n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String(), n.DeposedKey)\n    73\u2192}\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192func (n *NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject) DeposedInstanceObjectKey() states.DeposedKey {\n    76\u2192\treturn n.DeposedKey\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192// GraphNodeReferenceable implementation, overriding the one from NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n    80\u2192func (n *NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n    81\u2192\t// Deposed objects don't participate in references.\n    82\u2192\treturn nil\n    83\u2192}\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192// GraphNodeReferencer implementation, overriding the one from NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n    86\u2192func (n *NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n    87\u2192\t// We don't evaluate configuration for deposed objects, so they effectively\n    88\u2192\t// make no references.\n    89\u2192\treturn nil\n    90\u2192}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192// GraphNodeEvalable impl.\n    93\u2192func (n *NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    94\u2192\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject: planning %s deposed object %s\", n.Addr, n.DeposedKey)\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192\t// Read the state for the deposed resource instance\n    99\u2192\tstate, err := n.readResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx, n.Addr, n.DeposedKey)\n   100\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n   101\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   102\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   103\u2192\t}\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192\t// Note any upgrades that readResourceInstanceState might've done in the\n   106\u2192\t// prevRunState, so that it'll conform to current schema.\n   107\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx, n.DeposedKey, state, prevRunState))\n   108\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   109\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   110\u2192\t}\n   111\u2192\t// Also the refreshState, because that should still reflect schema upgrades\n   112\u2192\t// even if not refreshing.\n   113\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx, n.DeposedKey, state, refreshState))\n   114\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   115\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   116\u2192\t}\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192\tvar forget bool\n   119\u2192\tfor _, ft := range n.forgetResources {\n   120\u2192\t\tif ft.Equal(n.ResourceAddr()) {\n   121\u2192\t\t\tforget = true\n   122\u2192\t\t}\n   123\u2192\t}\n   124\u2192\tfor _, fm := range n.forgetModules {\n   125\u2192\t\tif fm.TargetContains(n.Addr) {\n   126\u2192\t\t\tforget = true\n   127\u2192\t\t}\n   128\u2192\t}\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192\t// We don't refresh during the planDestroy walk, since that is only adding\n   131\u2192\t// the destroy changes to the plan and the provider will not be configured\n   132\u2192\t// at this point. The other nodes use separate types for plan and destroy,\n   133\u2192\t// while deposed instances are always a destroy or forget operation, so the\n   134\u2192\t// logic here is a bit overloaded.\n   135\u2192\t//\n   136\u2192\t// We also don't refresh when forgetting instances, as it is unnecessary.\n   137\u2192\tif !n.skipRefresh && op != walkPlanDestroy && !forget {\n   138\u2192\t\t// Refresh this object even though it may be destroyed, in\n   139\u2192\t\t// case it's already been deleted outside of Terraform. If this is a\n   140\u2192\t\t// normal plan, providers expect a Read request to remove missing\n   141\u2192\t\t// resources from the plan before apply, and may not handle a missing\n   142\u2192\t\t// resource during Delete correctly. If this is a simple refresh,\n   143\u2192\t\t// Terraform is expected to remove the missing resource from the state\n   144\u2192\t\t// entirely\n   145\u2192\t\trefreshedState, refreshDeferred, refreshDiags := n.refresh(ctx, n.DeposedKey, state, ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed())\n   146\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)\n   147\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   148\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   149\u2192\t\t}\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192\t\tstate = refreshedState\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192\t\t// If we refreshed then our subsequent planning should be in terms of\n   154\u2192\t\t// the new object, not the original object.\n   155\u2192\t\tif refreshDeferred == nil {\n   156\u2192\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx, n.DeposedKey, refreshedState, refreshState))\n   157\u2192\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   158\u2192\t\t\t\treturn diags\n   159\u2192\t\t\t}\n   160\u2192\t\t} else {\n   161\u2192\t\t\tdeferred = refreshDeferred\n   162\u2192\t\t}\n   163\u2192\t}\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192\tif !n.skipPlanChanges {\n   166\u2192\t\tvar change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n   167\u2192\t\tvar pDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   168\u2192\t\tvar planDeferred *providers.Deferred\n   169\u2192\t\tif forget {\n   170\u2192\t\t\tchange, pDiags = n.planForget(ctx, state, n.DeposedKey)\n   171\u2192\t\t} else {\n   172\u2192\t\t\tchange, planDeferred, pDiags = n.planDestroy(ctx, state, n.DeposedKey)\n   173\u2192\t\t}\n   174\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(pDiags)\n   175\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   176\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   177\u2192\t\t}\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192\t\tif deferred == nil {\n   180\u2192\t\t\t// Only set the plan deferral if it wasn't already due to be\n   181\u2192\t\t\t// deferred from the refresh step.\n   182\u2192\t\t\tdeferred = planDeferred\n   183\u2192\t\t}\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192\t\tif deferred != nil {\n   186\u2192\t\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, deferred.Reason, change)\n   187\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   188\u2192\t\t}\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192\t\t// NOTE: We don't check prevent_destroy for deposed objects, even\n   191\u2192\t\t// though we would do so here for a \"current\" object, because\n   192\u2192\t\t// if we've reached a point where an object is already deposed then\n   193\u2192\t\t// we've already planned and partially-executed a create_before_destroy\n   194\u2192\t\t// replace and we would've checked prevent_destroy at that point. We're\n   195\u2192\t\t// now just need to get the deposed object removed, because there\n   196\u2192\t\t// should be a new object already serving as its replacement.\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, n.DeposedKey))\n   199\u2192\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   200\u2192\t\t\treturn diags\n   201\u2192\t\t}\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx, n.DeposedKey, nil, workingState))\n   204\u2192\t} else {\n   205\u2192\t\t// The working state should at least be updated with the result\n   206\u2192\t\t// of upgrading and refreshing from above.\n   207\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx, n.DeposedKey, state, workingState))\n   208\u2192\t}\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192\treturn diags\n   211\u2192}\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192// NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject represents deposed resource\n   214\u2192// instance objects during apply. Nodes of this type are inserted by\n   215\u2192// DiffTransformer when the planned changeset contains \"delete\" changes for\n   216\u2192// deposed instance objects, and its only supported operation is to destroy\n   217\u2192// and then forget the associated object.\n   218\u2192type NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject struct {\n   219\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n   220\u2192\tDeposedKey states.DeposedKey\n   221\u2192}\n   222\u2192\n   223\u2192var (\n   224\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDeposedResourceInstanceObject = (*NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   225\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource                = (*NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   226\u2192\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance              = (*NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   227\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer                     = (*NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   228\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD                  = (*NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   229\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable                 = (*NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   230\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer                    = (*NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   231\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable                    = (*NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   232\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer              = (*NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   233\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer           = (*NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   234\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer                     = (*NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   235\u2192)\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject) Name() string {\n   238\u2192\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (destroy deposed %s)\", n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), n.DeposedKey)\n   239\u2192}\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject) DeposedInstanceObjectKey() states.DeposedKey {\n   242\u2192\treturn n.DeposedKey\n   243\u2192}\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192// GraphNodeReferenceable implementation, overriding the one from NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n   246\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n   247\u2192\t// Deposed objects don't participate in references.\n   248\u2192\treturn nil\n   249\u2192}\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192// GraphNodeReferencer implementation, overriding the one from NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n   252\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n   253\u2192\t// We don't evaluate configuration for deposed objects, so they effectively\n   254\u2192\t// make no references.\n   255\u2192\treturn nil\n   256\u2192}\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192// GraphNodeDestroyer\n   259\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n   260\u2192\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n   261\u2192\treturn &addr\n   262\u2192}\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\n   265\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\n   266\u2192\t// A deposed instance is always CreateBeforeDestroy by definition, since\n   267\u2192\t// we use deposed only to handle create-before-destroy.\n   268\u2192\treturn true\n   269\u2192}\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\n   272\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\n   273\u2192\tif !v {\n   274\u2192\t\t// Should never happen: deposed instances are _always_ create_before_destroy.\n   275\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"can't deactivate create_before_destroy for a deposed instance\")\n   276\u2192\t}\n   277\u2192\treturn nil\n   278\u2192}\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192// GraphNodeExecutable impl.\n   281\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   282\u2192\tvar change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n   283\u2192\n   284\u2192\t// Read the state for the deposed resource instance\n   285\u2192\tstate, err := n.readResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx, n.Addr, n.DeposedKey)\n   286\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   287\u2192\t\treturn diags.Append(err)\n   288\u2192\t}\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192\tif state == nil {\n   291\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"missing deposed state for %s (%s)\", n.Addr, n.DeposedKey))\n   292\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   293\u2192\t}\n   294\u2192\n   295\u2192\tchange, deferred, destroyPlanDiags := n.planDestroy(ctx, state, n.DeposedKey)\n   296\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(destroyPlanDiags)\n   297\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   298\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   299\u2192\t}\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192\tif deferred != nil {\n   302\u2192\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, deferred.Reason, change)\n   303\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   304\u2192\t} else if ctx.Deferrals().ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(n.Addr, n.Dependencies) {\n   305\u2192\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq, change)\n   306\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   307\u2192\t}\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192\t// Call pre-apply hook\n   310\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(n.preApplyHook(ctx, change))\n   311\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   312\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   313\u2192\t}\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192\t// we pass a nil configuration to apply because we are destroying\n   316\u2192\tstate, applyDiags := n.apply(ctx, state, change, nil, instances.RepetitionData{}, false)\n   317\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\n   318\u2192\t// don't return immediately on errors, we need to handle the state\n   319\u2192\n   320\u2192\t// Always write the resource back to the state deposed. If it\n   321\u2192\t// was successfully destroyed it will be pruned. If it was not, it will\n   322\u2192\t// be caught on the next run.\n   323\u2192\twriteDiags := n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state)\n   324\u2192\tdiags.Append(writeDiags)\n   325\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   326\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   327\u2192\t}\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(n.postApplyHook(ctx, state, diags.Err()))\n   330\u2192\n   331\u2192\treturn diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\n   332\u2192}\n   333\u2192\n   334\u2192// NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject represents deposed resource\n   335\u2192// instance objects during apply. Nodes of this type are inserted by\n   336\u2192// DiffTransformer when the planned changeset contains \"forget\" changes for\n   337\u2192// deposed instance objects, and its only supported operation is to forget the\n   338\u2192// associated object.\n   339\u2192type NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject struct {\n   340\u2192\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n   341\u2192\tDeposedKey states.DeposedKey\n   342\u2192}\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192var (\n   345\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDeposedResourceInstanceObject = (*NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   346\u2192\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource                = (*NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   347\u2192\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance              = (*NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   348\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable                 = (*NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   349\u2192\t_ GraphNodeReferencer                    = (*NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   350\u2192\t_ GraphNodeExecutable                    = (*NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   351\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer              = (*NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   352\u2192\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer           = (*NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   353\u2192\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer                     = (*NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject)(nil)\n   354\u2192)\n   355\u2192\n   356\u2192func (n *NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject) Name() string {\n   357\u2192\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (forget deposed %s)\", n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), n.DeposedKey)\n   358\u2192}\n   359\u2192\n   360\u2192func (n *NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n   361\u2192\treturn &n.Addr\n   362\u2192}\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192func (n *NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject) DeposedInstanceObjectKey() states.DeposedKey {\n   365\u2192\treturn n.DeposedKey\n   366\u2192}\n   367\u2192\n   368\u2192// GraphNodeReferenceable implementation, overriding the one from NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n   369\u2192func (n *NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n   370\u2192\t// Deposed objects don't participate in references.\n   371\u2192\treturn nil\n   372\u2192}\n   373\u2192\n   374\u2192// GraphNodeReferencer implementation, overriding the one from NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n   375\u2192func (n *NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n   376\u2192\t// We don't evaluate configuration for deposed objects, so they effectively\n   377\u2192\t// make no references.\n   378\u2192\treturn nil\n   379\u2192}\n   380\u2192\n   381\u2192// GraphNodeExecutable impl.\n   382\u2192func (n *NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   383\u2192\t// Read the state for the deposed resource instance\n   384\u2192\tstate, err := n.readResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx, n.Addr, n.DeposedKey)\n   385\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   386\u2192\t\treturn diags.Append(err)\n   387\u2192\t}\n   388\u2192\n   389\u2192\tif state == nil {\n   390\u2192\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"missing deposed state for %s (%s)\", n.Addr, n.DeposedKey))\n   391\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   392\u2192\t}\n   393\u2192\n   394\u2192\t_, forgetPlanDiags := n.planForget(ctx, state, n.DeposedKey)\n   395\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(forgetPlanDiags)\n   396\u2192\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   397\u2192\t\treturn diags\n   398\u2192\t}\n   399\u2192\n   400\u2192\tctx.State().ForgetResourceInstanceDeposed(n.Addr, n.DeposedKey)\n   401\u2192\n   402\u2192\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\n   403\u2192\treturn diags\n   404\u2192}\n   405\u2192\n   406\u2192// GraphNodeDeposer is an optional interface implemented by graph nodes that\n   407\u2192// might create a single new deposed object for a specific associated resource\n   408\u2192// instance, allowing a caller to optionally pre-allocate a DeposedKey for\n   409\u2192// it.\n   410\u2192type GraphNodeDeposer interface {\n   411\u2192\t// SetPreallocatedDeposedKey will be called during graph construction\n   412\u2192\t// if a particular node must use a pre-allocated deposed key if/when it\n   413\u2192\t// \"deposes\" the current object of its associated resource instance.\n   414\u2192\tSetPreallocatedDeposedKey(key states.DeposedKey)\n   415\u2192}\n   416\u2192\n   417\u2192// graphNodeDeposer is an embeddable implementation of GraphNodeDeposer.\n   418\u2192// Embed it in a node type to get automatic support for it, and then access\n   419\u2192// the field PreallocatedDeposedKey to access any pre-allocated key.\n   420\u2192type graphNodeDeposer struct {\n   421\u2192\tPreallocatedDeposedKey states.DeposedKey\n   422\u2192}\n   423\u2192\n   424\u2192func (n *graphNodeDeposer) SetPreallocatedDeposedKey(key states.DeposedKey) {\n   425\u2192\tn.PreallocatedDeposedKey = key\n   426\u2192}\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192func (n *NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject) writeResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject) error {\n   429\u2192\tabsAddr := n.Addr\n   430\u2192\tkey := n.DeposedKey\n   431\u2192\tstate := ctx.State()\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192\tif key == states.NotDeposed {\n   434\u2192\t\t// should never happen\n   435\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"can't save deposed object for %s without a deposed key; this is a bug in Terraform that should be reported\", absAddr)\n   436\u2192\t}\n   437\u2192\n   438\u2192\tif obj == nil {\n   439\u2192\t\t// No need to encode anything: we'll just write it directly.\n   440\u2192\t\tstate.SetResourceInstanceDeposed(absAddr, key, nil, n.ResolvedProvider)\n   441\u2192\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed: removing state object for %s deposed %s\", absAddr, key)\n   442\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   443\u2192\t}\n   444\u2192\n   445\u2192\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n   446\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   447\u2192\t\treturn err\n   448\u2192\t}\n   449\u2192\n   450\u2192\tschema, currentVersion := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(absAddr.ContainingResource().Resource)\n   451\u2192\tif schema == nil {\n   452\u2192\t\t// It shouldn't be possible to get this far in any real scenario\n   453\u2192\t\t// without a schema, but we might end up here in contrived tests that\n   454\u2192\t\t// fail to set up their world properly.\n   455\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode %s in state: no resource type schema available\", absAddr)\n   456\u2192\t}\n   457\u2192\tsrc, err := obj.Encode(schema.ImpliedType(), currentVersion)\n   458\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   459\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode %s in state: %s\", absAddr, err)\n   460\u2192\t}\n   461\u2192\n   462\u2192\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed: writing state object for %s deposed %s\", absAddr, key)\n   463\u2192\tstate.SetResourceInstanceDeposed(absAddr, key, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\n   464\u2192\treturn nil\n   465\u2192}\n   466\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

214. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_deferred.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package terraform\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n     8\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n    11\u2192)\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192// DeferredTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing\n    14\u2192// each of the deferred changes to the graph.\n    15\u2192//\n    16\u2192// Deferred changes are not executed during the apply phase, but they are\n    17\u2192// tracked in the graph to ensure that the correct ordering is maintained and\n    18\u2192// the target flags are correctly applied.\n    19\u2192type DeferredTransformer struct {\n    20\u2192\tDeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc\n    21\u2192}\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192func (t *DeferredTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n    24\u2192\tif len(t.DeferredChanges) == 0 {\n    25\u2192\t\treturn nil\n    26\u2192\t}\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\t// As with the DiffTransformer, DeferredTransformer creates resource\n    29\u2192\t// instance nodes. If there are any whole-resource nodes already in the\n    30\u2192\t// graph, we must ensure they get evaluated before any of the corresponding\n    31\u2192\t// instances by creating dependency edges.\n    32\u2192\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()\n    33\u2192\tfor _, node := range g.Vertices() {\n    34\u2192\t\trn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)\n    35\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n    36\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    37\u2192\t\t}\n    38\u2192\t\t// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\n    39\u2192\t\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\n    40\u2192\t\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.\n    41\u2192\t\tif _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResourceInstance); ok {\n    42\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    43\u2192\t\t}\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192\t\trAddr := rn.ResourceAddr()\n    46\u2192\t\tresourceNodes.Put(rAddr, append(resourceNodes.Get(rAddr), rn))\n    47\u2192\t}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192\tfor _, change := range t.DeferredChanges {\n    50\u2192\t\tnode := &nodeApplyableDeferredInstance{\n    51\u2192\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(change.ChangeSrc.Addr),\n    52\u2192\t\t\tReason:                       change.DeferredReason,\n    53\u2192\t\t\tChangeSrc:                    change.ChangeSrc,\n    54\u2192\t\t}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192\t\t// Create a special node for partial instances, that handles the\n    57\u2192\t\t// addresses a little differently.\n    58\u2192\t\tif change.DeferredReason == providers.DeferredReasonInstanceCountUnknown {\n    59\u2192\t\t\tper := change.ChangeSrc.Addr.PartialResource()\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192\t\t\t// This is a partial instance, so we need to create a partial node\n    62\u2192\t\t\t// instead of a full instance node.\n    63\u2192\t\t\tnode := &nodeApplyableDeferredPartialInstance{\n    64\u2192\t\t\t\tnodeApplyableDeferredInstance: node,\n    65\u2192\t\t\t\tPartialAddr:                   per,\n    66\u2192\t\t\t}\n    67\u2192\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192\t\t\t// Now we want to find the expansion node that would be applied for\n    70\u2192\t\t\t// this resource, and tell it that it is performing a partial\n    71\u2192\t\t\t// expansion.\n    72\u2192\t\t\tfor _, v := range g.Vertices() {\n    73\u2192\t\t\t\tif n, ok := v.(*nodeExpandApplyableResource); ok {\n    74\u2192\t\t\t\t\tif per.ConfigResource().Equal(n.Addr) {\n    75\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tn.PartialExpansions = append(n.PartialExpansions, per)\n    76\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n    77\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n    78\u2192\t\t\t}\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192\t\t\t// Also connect the deferred instance node to the underlying\n    81\u2192\t\t\t// resource node to make sure any expansion happens first.\n    82\u2192\t\t\tfor _, resourceNode := range resourceNodes.Get(node.Addr.ConfigResource()) {\n    83\u2192\t\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, resourceNode))\n    84\u2192\t\t\t}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    87\u2192\t\t}\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192\t\t// Otherwise, just add the normal deferred instance node.\n    90\u2192\t\tg.Add(node)\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192\t\t// Still connect the deferred instance node to the underlying resource\n    93\u2192\t\t// node.\n    94\u2192\t\tfor _, resourceNode := range resourceNodes.Get(node.Addr.ConfigResource()) {\n    95\u2192\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, resourceNode))\n    96\u2192\t\t}\n    97\u2192\t}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192\treturn nil\n   100\u2192}\n   101\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

    "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n)\n\nfunc TestNodeAbstractResourceInstanceProvider(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tAddr                 addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\t\tConfig               *configs.Resource\n\t\tStoredProviderConfig addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n\t\tWant                 addrs.Provider\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",",

    "numLines": 30,

  },
  "type": "text"
}

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance_test.go",
  "newString": "func TestNodeResourceInstanceBaseProvider(t *testing.T) {",
  "oldString": "func TestNodeAbstractResourceInstanceProvider(t *testing.T) {",
  "originalFile": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n)\n\nfunc TestNodeAbstractResourceInstanceProvider(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tAddr                 addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\t\tConfig               *configs.Resource\n\t\tStoredProviderConfig addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n\t\tWant                 addrs.Provider\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"hashicorp\",\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"terraform_remote_state\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\t// As a special case, the type prefix \"terraform_\" maps to\n\t\t\t\t// the builtin provider, not the default one.\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.BuiltInProviderHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: addrs.BuiltInProviderNamespace,\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"terraform\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tConfig: &configs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\t// Just enough configs.Resource for the Provider method. Not\n\t\t\t\t// actually valid for general use.\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t// The config overrides the default behavior.\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"terraform_remote_state\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tConfig: &configs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\t// Just enough configs.Resource for the Provider method. Not\n\t\t\t\t// actually valid for general use.\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t// The config overrides the default behavior.\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"happycloud\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tAddr: addrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.DataResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"null_resource\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"baz\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tConfig: nil,\n\t\t\tStoredProviderConfig: addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tModule: addrs.RootModule,\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t// The stored provider config overrides the default behavior.\n\t\t\tWant: addrs.Provider{\n\t\t\t\tHostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: \"awesomecorp\",\n\t\t\t\tType:      \"null\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, test := range tests {\n\t\tvar name string\n\t\tif test.Config != nil {\n\t\t\tname = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s with configured %s\", test.Addr, test.Config.Provider)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tname = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s with no configuration\", test.Addr)\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tnode := &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n\t\t\t\t// Just enough NodeResourceInstanceBase for the Provider\n\t\t\t\t// function. (This would not be valid for some other functions.)\n\t\t\t\tAddr: test.Addr,\n\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\t\t\t\tAddr:                 test.Addr.ConfigResource(),\n\t\t\t\t\tConfig:               test.Config,\n\t\t\t\t\tstoredProviderConfig: test.StoredProviderConfig,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tgot := node.Provider()\n\t\t\tif got != test.Want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"wrong result\\naddr:  %s\\nconfig: %#v\\ngot:   %s\\nwant:  %s\", test.Addr, test.Config, got, test.Want)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestNodeResourceInstanceBase_WriteResourceInstanceState(t *testing.T) {\n\tstate := states.NewState()\n\tctx := new(MockEvalContext)\n\tctx.StateState = state.SyncWrapper()\n\tctx.Scope = evalContextModuleInstance{Addr: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n\n\tmockProvider := mockProviderWithResourceTypeSchema(\"aws_instance\", &configschema.Block{\n\t\tAttributes: map[string]*configschema.Attribute{\n\t\t\t\"id\": {\n\t\t\t\tType:     cty.String,\n\t\t\t\tOptional: true,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\n\tobj := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\tValue: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n\t\t\t\"id\": cty.StringVal(\"i-abc123\"),\n\t\t}),\n\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n\t}\n\n\tnode := &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"aws_instance.foo\"),\n\t\t// instanceState:        obj,\n\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws\"]`),\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\tctx.ProviderProvider = mockProvider\n\tctx.ProviderSchemaSchema = mockProvider.GetProviderSchema()\n\n\terr := node.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, obj, workingState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", err.Error())\n\t}\n\n\tcheckStateString(t, state, `\naws_instance.foo:\n  ID = i-abc123\n  provider = provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws\"]\n\t`)\n}\n\nfunc TestNodeResourceInstanceBase_refresh_with_deferred_read(t *testing.T) {\n\tstate := states.NewState()\n\tevalCtx := &MockEvalContext{}\n\tevalCtx.StateState = state.SyncWrapper()\n\tevalCtx.Scope = evalContextModuleInstance{Addr: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n\n\tmockProvider := mockProviderWithResourceTypeSchema(\"aws_instance\", &configschema.Block{\n\t\tAttributes: map[string]*configschema.Attribute{\n\t\t\t\"id\": {\n\t\t\t\tType:     cty.String,\n\t\t\t\tOptional: true,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\tmockProvider.ConfigureProviderCalled = true\n\n\tmockProvider.ReadResourceFn = func(providers.ReadResourceRequest) providers.ReadResourceResponse {\n\t\treturn providers.ReadResourceResponse{\n\t\t\tNewState: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n\t\t\t\t\"id\": cty.UnknownVal(cty.String),\n\t\t\t}),\n\t\t\tDeferred: &providers.Deferred{\n\t\t\t\tReason: providers.DeferredReasonAbsentPrereq,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tobj := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\tValue: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n\t\t\t\"id\": cty.StringVal(\"i-abc123\"),\n\t\t}),\n\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n\t}\n\n\tnode := &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"aws_instance.foo\"),\n\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws\"]`),\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\tevalCtx.ProviderProvider = mockProvider\n\tevalCtx.ProviderSchemaSchema = mockProvider.GetProviderSchema()\n\tevalCtx.DeferralsState = deferring.NewDeferred(true)\n\n\trio, deferred, diags := node.refresh(evalCtx, states.NotDeposed, obj, true)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\tt.Fatal(diags.Err())\n\t}\n\n\tvalue := rio.Value\n\tif value.IsWhollyKnown() {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"value was known: %v\", value)\n\t}\n\n\tif deferred == nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected deferral to be present\")\n\t}\n\n\tif deferred.Reason != providers.DeferredReasonAbsentPrereq {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected deferral to be AbsentPrereq, got %s\", deferred.Reason)\n\t}\n}\n",

    "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\"\n\ttfaddr \"github.com/hashicorp/terraform-registry-address\"\n\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/checks\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/marks\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/provisioners\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// NodeResourceInstanceBase represents a resource instance with no\n// associated operations. It embeds NodeAbstractResource but additionally\n// contains an instance key, used to identify one of potentially many\n// instances that were created from a resource in configuration, e.g. using\n// the \"count\" or \"for_each\" arguments.\ntype NodeResourceInstanceBase struct {\n\tNodeAbstractResource\n\tAddr addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// These are set via the AttachState method.\n\tinstanceState *states.ResourceInstance\n\n\tDependencies []addrs.ConfigResource\n\n\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n\n\t// During import we may generate configuration for a resource, which needs\n\t// to be stored in the final change.\n\tgeneratedConfigHCL string\n\n\t// override is set by the graph itself, just before this node executes.\n\toverride *configs.Override\n}\n\n// NewNodeResourceInstanceBase creates a resource instance base graph\n// node for the given absolute resource instance address.\nfunc NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) *NodeResourceInstanceBase {\n\t// Due to the fact that we embed NodeAbstractResource, the given address\n\t// actually ends up split between the resource address in the embedded\n\t// object and the InstanceKey field in our own struct. The\n\t// ResourceInstanceAddr method will stick these back together again on\n\t// request.\n\tr := NewNodeAbstractResource(addr.ContainingResource().Config())\n\treturn &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n\t\tNodeAbstractResource: *r,\n\t\tAddr:                 addr,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) Name() string {\n\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String()\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\n\treturn n.Addr.Module",

  "file": {
    "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// NodeApplyableResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is\n// \"applyable\": it is ready to be applied and is represented by a diff.\n//\n// This node is for a specific instance of a resource. It will usually be\n// accompanied in the graph by a NodeApplyableResource representing its\n// containing resource, and should depend on that node to ensure that the\n// state is properly prepared to receive changes to instances.\ntype NodeApplyableResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeResourceInstanceBase\n\n\tgraphNodeDeposer // implementation of GraphNodeDeposerConfig\n\n\t// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to\n\t// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\n\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\n\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\n\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource     = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance   = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeCreator            = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer         = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDeposer            = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable         = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachDependencies = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n)\n\n// GraphNodeCreator\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) CreateAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\treturn &addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferencer, overriding NodeResourceInstanceBase\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\t// Start with the usual resource instance implementation\n\tret := n.NodeResourceInstanceBase.References()\n\n\t// Applying a resource must also depend on the destruction of any of its\n\t// dependencies, since this may for example affect the outcome of\n\t// evaluating an entire list of resources with \"count\" set (by reducing\n\t// the count).\n\t//\n\t// However, we can't do this in create_before_destroy mode because that\n\t// would create a dependency cycle. We make a compromise here of requiring\n\t// changes to be updated across two applies in this case, since the first\n\t// plan will use the old values.\n\tif !n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\n\t\tfor _, ref := range ret {",
    "filePath": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_apply_instance.go",
    "numLines": 70,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 493
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n)\n\n// NodeDestroyResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is to be\n// destroyed.\ntype NodeDestroyResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeResourceInstanceBase\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance      = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource      = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance    = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer           = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD        = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable       = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer          = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable          = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer    = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) Name() string {\n\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String() + \" (destroy)\"\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) ProvidedBy() (addr addrs.ProviderConfig, exact bool) {\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\n\t\t// indicate that this node does not require a configured provider\n\t\treturn nil, true\n\t}\n\treturn n.NodeResourceInstanceBase.ProvidedBy()\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyer\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\treturn &addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\n\t// State takes precedence during destroy.\n\t// If the resource was removed, there is no config to check.\n\t// If CBD was forced from descendant, it should be saved in the state",

1. EDIT /workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go
Before
// NodeAbstractResourceInstance represents a resource instance with no
// associated operations. It embeds NodeAbstractResource but additionally
// contains an instance key, used to identify one of potentially many
// instances that were created from a resource in configuration, e.g. using
// the "count" or "for_each" arguments.
type NodeAbstractResourceInstance struct {
After
// NodeResourceInstanceBase represents a resource instance with no
// associated operations. It embeds NodeAbstractResource but additionally
// contains an instance key, used to identify one of potentially many
// instances that were created from a resource in configuration, e.g. using
// the "count" or "for_each" arguments.
type NodeResourceInstanceBase struct {
2. EDIT /workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go
Before
// NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance creates an abstract resource instance graph
// node for the given absolute resource instance address.
func NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) *NodeAbstractResourceInstance {
	// Due to the fact that we embed NodeAbstractResource, the given address
	// actually ends up split between the resource address in the embedded
	// object and the InstanceKey field in our own struct. The
	// ResourceInstanceAddr method will stick these back together again on
	// request.
	r := NewNodeAbstractResource(addr.ContainingResource().Config())
	return &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{
		NodeAbstractResource: *r,
		Addr:                 addr,
	}
}
After
// NewNodeResourceInstanceBase creates a resource instance base graph
// node for the given absolute resource instance address.
func NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) *NodeResourceInstanceBase {
	// Due to the fact that we embed NodeAbstractResource, the given address
	// actually ends up split between the resource address in the embedded
	// object and the InstanceKey field in our own struct. The
	// ResourceInstanceAddr method will stick these back together again on
	// request.
	r := NewNodeAbstractResource(addr.ContainingResource().Config())
	return &NodeResourceInstanceBase{
		NodeAbstractResource: *r,
		Addr:                 addr,
	}
}
3. EDIT /workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go
Before
func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance)
After
func (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase)

func TestNodeAbstractResourceInstanceProvider(t *testing.T) {
	tests := []struct {
		Addr                 addrs.AbsResourceInstance
		Config               *configs.Resource
		StoredProviderConfig addrs.AbsProviderConfig
		Want                 addrs.Provider
	}{
		{
			Addr: addrs.Resource{
				Mode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,
				Type: "null_resource",
				Name: "baz",
			}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),
			Want: addrs.Provider{
				Hostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,
				Namespace: "hashicorp",
				Type:      "null",
			},
		},
		{
			Addr: addrs.Resource{
				Mode: addrs.DataResourceMode,
				Type: "terraform_remote_state",
				Name: "baz",
			}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),
			Want: addrs.Provider{
				// As a special case, the type prefix "terraform_" maps to
				// the builtin provider, not the default one.
				Hostname:  addrs.BuiltInProviderHost,
				Namespace: addrs.BuiltInProviderNamespace,
				Type:      "terraform",
			},
		},
		{
			Addr: addrs.Resource{
				Mode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,
				Type: "null_resource",
				Name: "baz",
			}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),
			Config: &configs.Resource{
				// Just enough configs.Resource for the Provider method. Not
				// actually valid for general use.
				Provider: addrs.Provider{
					Hostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,
					Namespace: "awesomecorp",
					Type:      "happycloud",
				},
			},
			// The config overrides the default behavior.
			Want: addrs.Provider{
				Hostname:  addrs.DefaultProviderRegistryHost,
				Namespace: "awesomecorp",
				Type:      "happycloud",
			},
		},
		{
			Addr: addrs.Resource{
				Mode: addrs.DataResourceMode,
				Type: "terraform_remote_state",
				Name: "baz",
			}.Instan
... (truncated)
After

	_ GraphNodeModuleInstance            = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)

	_ GraphNodeAttachProvisionerSchema   = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeAttachProviderMetaConfigs = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeTargetable                = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeOverridable               = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)
	_ dag.GraphNodeDotter                = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)
)
After
var (
	_ GraphNodeModuleInstance            = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeReferenceable             = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeReferencer                = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer          = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer       = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeConfigResource            = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeResourceInstance          = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeAttachResourceState       = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig      = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeAttachResourceSchema      = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeAttachProvisionerSchema   = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeAttachProviderMetaConfigs = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeTargetable                = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ GraphNodeOverridable               = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
	_ dag.GraphNodeDotter                = (*NodeResourceInstanceBase)(nil)
)

81. EDIT /workspace/internal/terraform/transform_diff.go
Before
		if delete {
			// Destroying always uses a destroy-specific node type, though
			// which one depends on whether we're destroying a current object
			// or a deposed object.
			var node GraphNodeResourceInstance
			abstract := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addr)
			if dk == states.NotDeposed {
				node = &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{
					NodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,
				}
			} else {
				node = &NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{
					NodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,
					DeposedKey:                   dk,
				}
			}
After
		if delete {
			// Destroying always uses a destroy-specific node type, though
			// which one depends on whether we're destroying a current object
			// or a deposed object.
			var node GraphNodeResourceInstance
			abstract := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addr)
			if dk == states.NotDeposed {
				node = &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{
					NodeResourceInstanceBase: abstract,
				}
			} else {
				node = &NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{
					NodeResourceInstanceBase: abstract,
					DeposedKey:               dk,
				}
			}

After

Before
func testDestroyNode(addrString string) GraphNodeDestroyer {
	instAddr := mustResourceInstanceAddr(addrString)
	inst := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(instAddr)
	return &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{NodeAbstractResourceInstance: inst}
}
After
func testDestroyNode(addrString string) GraphNodeDestroyer {
	instAddr := mustResourceInstanceAddr(addrString)
	inst := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(instAddr)
	return &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{NodeResourceInstanceBase: inst}
}

}
After
func testUpdateNode(addrString string) GraphNodeCreator {

85. EDIT /workspace/internal/terraform/transform_destroy_edge_test.go
Before
	addrA := mustResourceInstanceAddr("test_object.A")
	instA := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addrA)
	a := &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{NodeAbstractResourceInstance: instA}
	g.Add(a)

	// B here represents a data sources, which is effectively an update during
	// apply, but won't have dependencies stored in the state.
	addrB := mustResourceInstanceAddr("test_object.B")
	instB := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addrB)
	instB.Dependencies = append(instB.Dependencies, addrA.ConfigResource())
	b := &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{NodeAbstractResourceInstance: instB}
After
	addrA := mustResourceInstanceAddr("test_object.A")
	instA := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addrA)
	a := &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{NodeResourceInstanceBase: instA}
	g.Add(a)

	// B here represents a data sources, which is effectively an update during
	// apply, but won't have dependencies stored in the state.
	addrB := mustResourceInstanceAddr("test_object.B")
	instB := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addrB)
	instB.Dependencies = append(instB.Dependencies, addrA.ConfigResource())
	b := &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{NodeResourceInstanceBase: instB}

        Bash Commands (15)
        
1. $ grep -r "NodeAbstractResourceInstance" internal/terraform --include="*.go" | grep -v "NodeResourceInstanceBase" | head -20
2. $ grep -n "NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance" /workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_partial_plan.go
3. $ grep -n "NodeAbstractResourceInstance\|NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance" /workspace/internal/terraform/transform_destroy_edge_test.go
4. $ grep -r "NodeAbstractResourceInstance\|NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance" internal/terraform --include="*.go" | grep -v "NodeResourceInstanceBase" | wc -l
5. $ grep -r "NodeAbstractResourceInstance\|NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance" internal/terraform --include="*.go" | grep -v "NodeResourceInstanceBase" | head -30
6. $ grep -n "NodeAbstractResourceInstance{$" /workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_destroy_deposed_test.go
7. $ grep -r "NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance\|concreteResource.*\*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\|concreteResourceOrphan.*\*NodeAbstractResourceInstance" internal/terraform --include="*.go" | grep -v "NodeResourceInstanceBase" | wc -l
8. $ grep -r "NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance\|concreteResource.*\*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\|concreteResourceOrphan.*\*NodeAbstractResourceInstance" internal/terraform --include="*.go" | grep -v "NodeResourceInstanceBase"
9. $ git diff --stat internal/terraform | head -40
10. $ grep -c "NodeResourceInstanceBase" internal/terraform/*.go
11. $ grep "NewNodeResourceInstanceBase" internal/terraform/*.go | wc -l
12. $ head -50 /workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go | grep -A 5 "^type"
13. $ grep "^func NewNode" /workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go
14. $ find /workspace -name "*.go" -type f | head -20
15. $ go build ./internal/terraform 2>&1 | head -30
